filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signature of the following Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) which will be exchanged during the forthcoming State Visit of the Prime Minister to India to further consolidate the longstanding relations with India, by opening new avenues of cooperation, reinforcing shared values, and providing a solid framework for future collaboration across priority sectors - (a) MoU for the implementation of Phase II of Small Development Projects (High Impact Community Development Projects for the total capital cost of Rs500 million. The projects relate primarily to socio-economic development, particularly creation of infrastructure in the education, basic healthcare or community development sectors. Livelihood activity, skill development, renewable energy, conservation of environmental and cultural heritage, empowerment of women and child welfare, and disaster risk reduction, shall be primary themes of the projects. (b) MoU between the Government of the Republic of India and the Government of the Republic of Mauritius in the power sector covering the following areas of collaboration– (i) cooperation in power sector for energy security and transition; (ii) power planning, power generation, including Liquified Natural Gas transmission, distribution, and grid management; (iii) renewable energy; (iv) energy conservation and efficiency improvement; (v) capacity building and institutional strengthening; and (vi) technology and digitalisation. (c) MoU between the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research of India and the Mauritius Oceanography Institute for research and education in marine science and technology in the following areas of cooperation - (i) matters relating to oceanography and technology; (ii) exchange of scientists and research scholars for the purpose of research, training and consultations; (iii) exchange of expertise and scientific and technical information; (iv) joint identification of scientific and technical problems, formulation and implementation of a joint research programme in areas of Marine Science of common interest; and (v) joint research work on hydrocarbon and seabed minerals in the Exclusive Economic Zone of Mauritius under the co-ordination of the Department for Continental Shelf, Maritime Zones Administration and Exploration. (d) MoU between the Ministry of Public Service and Administrative Reforms and Karmayogi Bharat under the Department of Personnel and Training, India, to work collaboratively towards establishing and strengthening a digital capacity- building ecosystem, aiming at - (i) facilitating knowledge exchange; (ii) adapting and integrating digital solutions; (iii) promoting sustainable capacity building; and (iv) strengthening institutional partnership. Furthermore, two MoUs will also be exchanged between the University of Mauritius and the Indian Institute of Technology, Madras, India; and the University of Mauritius and the Indian Institute of Plantation Management, Bengaluru, India. The following Agreement/MoU will also be renewed - (a) the Technical Cooperation Agreement on the cooperation for the establishment of Telemetry, Tracking and Telecommunications Station for Satellites and Launch Vehicles and for cooperation in the fields of space, research, science and application; and (b) the MoU for cooperation in the field of Hydrography.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Prime Minister in the 45th Summit of Heads of State and Government of the Southern African Development Community (SADC), held in Antananarivo, Madagascar. The theme for the Summit was "Advancing Industrialisation, Agricultural Transformation, and Energy". The Summit agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding in respect of Western Sahara. The Prime Minister signed the Agreement amending the Treaty of the SADC to establish the SADC Parliament. In his address at the Opening Ceremony, the Prime Minister highlighted that peaceful transitions in SADC countries illustrate shared commitment to the values of good governance and the rule of law. He underscored the unwavering commitment of Mauritius to further the vision and objectives of the Community. He also expressed the gratitude of Mauritius to all the members of the SADC for their continuous support in our struggle to recover our sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago including Diego Garcia. He highlighted that this historic victory had been made possible largely due to the unity and solidarity of the entire African continent without which, we would not have reached this significant outcome with the United Kingdom. On the issue of regional security, the Prime Minister expressed the appreciation of Mauritius for the progress made by the SADC mission in Mozambique. In his address on the theme of the Summit, the Prime Minister called for the SADC Community to build resilience especially in the wake of the current unpredictable and changing global economic landscape arising from the imposition of tariffs across several jurisdictions. He enjoined the Community to embrace economic diversification without compromising sustainable development and renew efforts for ensuring food security. He shared how Mauritius has embarked on a transformative journey with structural reforms being brought while putting human development at the core of development. The Prime Minister informed the Summit of the decision to re-establish the Regional Cooperation and Integration Council, as a high-level public-private sector forum, to address regional cooperation and integration issues. He underscored that a common stand and a pooling of efforts were imperative for the Community to withstand external shocks and face the challenges, especially in the context of the renewal of the African Growth and Opportunity Act.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Prime Minister in the 9th Tokyo International Conference on African Development (TICAD-9) held in Yokohama, Japan. The theme of the 9th TICAD was "Co-creating innovative solutions with Africa". At the end of the two-day deliberations, the Conference adopted by acclamation the TICAD-9 Yokohama Declaration entitled "Co-create innovative solutions with Africa" which is broadly aligned to Africa's development priorities as contained in the AU Agenda 2063 and the UN 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. In his address during the plenary session of TICAD-9, the Prime Minister highlighted the crucial role played by the TICAD over the last 30 years in laying down the foundations for long and lasting partnerships between Africa and Japan. He also underscored the setting up of the Mauritius-Japan Business Facilitation Committee and discussions engaged for the conclusion of an Investment Promotion and Protection Agreement. He also called for more cooperation between African Small Island Developing States and Japan in unlocking the potential of ocean economy. Prior to the start of the Conference, the Prime Minister had a bilateral meeting with Prime Minister Ishiba. During the discussions, both counterparts agreed to strengthen relations between Mauritius and Japan at the bilateral and international levels. At the invitation of the Emperor of Japan, the Prime Minister also attended a Tea Ceremony at the Imperial Palace along with other Heads of State and Governments participating in the TICAD-9. During his visit, the Prime Minister inaugurated the Embassy of Mauritius in Tokyo which will be called upon to play an important role in further strengthening the bilateral relations between the two countries and in unlocking Japanese investment opportunities into Mauritius. The Economic Development Board organised a Business Meet with prominent Japanese companies with the participation of several Mauritian companies. This interaction was an opportunity to gauge investment opportunities in Mauritius in areas such as financial services, banking, bunkering, port modernisation and logistics.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that HE Mr Emmanuel Macron, President of France, will undertake an official visit to Mauritius on 21 and 22 November 2025, on his way to attend the G20 Leaders' Summit in Johannesburg, scheduled on 22 and 23 November 2025.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Government engaging in discussions on Electoral Reforms. A document will be considered by Cabinet shortly and thereafter wide consultations will be held with relevant stakeholders.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Mr Meghanathan Pillay, CSK, is being proposed as Executive Chairperson of Airport Holdings Limited.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Prime Minister and the Deputy Prime Minister recently met Mr Franceau Aubret Grandcourt, GOSK, Chief Commissioner of Rodrigues. Discussions during that meeting focused on the political landscape and upcoming development projects in Rodrigues. The Prime Minister and the Deputy Prime Minister will also hold a meeting with Mrs Franchette Gaspard Pierre Louis, CSK, Minority Leader of the Rodrigues Regional Assembly shortly.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that in a letter dated 26 August 2025, the International Olympic Committee has informed the Mauritius National Olympic Committee (NOC) that it is suspending all funding, except for payment to athletes who have benefitted from scholarships under the Solidarité Olympique. This decision has been taken in view of the fact that holding of elections of the Mauritius NOC is still pending, which is not in conformity with the Charte Olympique. This issue has been viewed with serious concern, the moreso as it might lead to the suspension of the Mauritius NOC, which would have serious repercussions on the participation of Mauritian athletes in Olympic competitions. The Minister of Youth and Sports will hold a Press Conference on Saturday 06 September 2025 and thereafter hold a meeting with all National Sports Federations to explain the situation.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Arts and Culture and other Government institutions to commemorate the 125th birth anniversary of Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam to honour his enduring legacy, recall the values of unity and nation building that guided his leadership and sensitise younger generations to his role in shaping the identity and development of Mauritius. Marking this milestone through cultural, educational and institutional activities will not only preserve the memory of his contribution but also reaffirm the importance of civic responsibility, social equity and national cohesion. To commemorate his 125th birth anniversary, the following activities will be held on 18 September 2025 - (a) the launching of an exhibition on the life and legacy of Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam at the Salon Mairal of the Municipal City Council of Port-Louis and the launching of a special commemorative stamp and first day cover on Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam by the Mauritius Post Ltd; (b) a Wreath Laying ceremony at the Statue of Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam at the Port-Louis Waterfront; and (c) a Wreath Laying ceremony at the Bust of Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam at the Memorial Park in Kewal Nagar and cultural programme.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Republic of Mauritius has been officially designated as the Primary Jurisdiction for the Headquarters of the Africa Credit Rating Agency (AfCRA) by the African Peer Review Mechanism. The initiative stems from an African Union Executive Council Decision aiming at establishing a private sector-led, independent and self-sustaining African credit rating agency. The AfCRA is expected to deliver credit assessments that more accurately reflect African economic realities, in contrast to traditional global rating agencies. The designation of Mauritius as the host country for AfCRA is a major milestone that reinforces Mauritius as a continental leader in financial governance and innovation. It reaffirms the credibility of Mauritius as a trusted International Financial Centre, committed to advancing African priorities through transparency, regulatory soundness, and professional excellence. With AfCRA headquartered in Mauritius, our jurisdiction will serve as a focal point for promoting investor confidence, facilitating intra-African financial flows, and deepening local capital markets across the African continent.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of the recommendations of the Report of the Inter-Ministerial Committee on Work Permit Issues. The Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations and comprising the Minister of Tourism; the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation and the Secretary for Home Affairs met on several occasions to look into the difficulties being encountered by private companies in obtaining work and residence permits. The Committee formulated several recommendations, comprising, inter alia – (a) the existing pre-established quota/ratio principle will be replaced by a system based on evidence of inability to recruit locally; (b) the maximum allowable period of stay for foreign workers in all sectors will be 10 years; (c) the Private Recruitment Agencies Regulations will be amended to ensure transparency, efficiency and ethics in recruitment of workers; (d) signing of Memoranda of Understanding with sourcing countries so as to ensure regular, ethical, and orderly recruitment of foreign workers; (e) new regulations for centralised lodging accommodation will be promulgated to provide for grant of Lodging and Accommodation Permit (LAP) to the owner of the building rather than to each employer renting the building as is the case presently; (f) employers will be allowed to submit work permit applications and applications for LAP in parallel to enable simultaneous processing instead of awaiting receipt of the LAP to process the application for work permit; (g) the Work Permit Module on the National E-licensing System will be enhanced by the Economic Development Board to cater for applications and processing of Certificates of Exemption, Employment Permits as well as cancellation of permits, contract vetting, among others, and issue of electronic work permits; (h) in order to cater for the welfare of migrant workers in Mauritius, an amount of Rs500 per migrant worker per year to be transferred by employers to a special fund to be used to pay for living expenses and legal fees to represent workers in case of dispute with their employers; (i) conduct of regular joint crack-down operations with the Passport and Immigration Office to track foreigners working on a tourist visa or working without a valid work permit; and (j) educational institutions will put up a monitoring mechanism to ensure that their foreign students get a valid authorisation to work and the number of hours worked is monitored to ensure compliance with legal provisions. The implementation of some of the recommendations will require further consultations and legislative amendments. A Monitoring Committee will be set up at the level of the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations comprising representatives of the Home Affairs Division of the Prime Minister's Office, the Passport and Immigration Office and the Economic Development Board.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Higher Education Commission implementing the National Credit Value and Transfer System Framework with the following objectives – (a) legislative and institutional arrangements, defining duties and responsibilities of stakeholders and authorities; (b) development and provision of the education and training system; (c) career development, guidance and job placement, information and orientation, including labour market mobility; and (d) developing mutual transparency, trust and cooperation. The National Credit Value and Transfer System closely aligns with the European Credit Transfer and Accumulation System. The implementation of the National Credit Value and Transfer System also complements the Mauritian National Qualifications Framework which defines qualification levels based on learning outcomes and complexity. The Higher Education Commission will organise capacity-building workshops for participants from both public and private higher education institutions to support the effective implementation of the National Credit Value and Transfer System.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Waste Management and Resource Recovery (Registration of Scavenging Contractor) Regulations 2025 which will provide for, inter alia, the following – (a) an application for registration approved by the Minister; (b) ownership of a minimum of two tipper lorries by the scavenging contractor; (c) registration for a period of three years and renewal applicable on expiry of validity for further periods of three years; (d) fees of Rs20,000 imposed for registration and renewal of registration of scavenging contractors; (e) application for renewal at least one month before the date of expiry of the registration and a surcharge of 35% applicable for renewal after expiry date; (f) fine not exceeding Rs25,000 applicable on conviction; and (g) a Certificate of Registration with conditions to be issued to the Scavenging Contractors as per the Third Schedule of the new Regulations. The Waste Management and Resource Recovery (Registration of Scavenging Contractor) Regulations 2025 will come into operation on 01 October 2025. A transitionary period of 60 days will be provided, during which a Certificate of Registration issued under the previous Regulations shall remain valid to allow existing scavenging contractors enough time to submit their applications under the new Regulations.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Local Government (Amendment of Schedule) (No. 3) Regulations 2025 and the Local Government (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 which will provide for the introduction of a new trade, namely "Seller of foodstuffs and non-foodstuffs on islet", together with the applicable corresponding fees.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the National Archives (Access to Public and Destruction of Public Records) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 to enable henceforth, the Responsible Officer of a Ministry or Department to cause records, which have been determined by the Public Records Appraisal Committee, to be shredded for recycling or to be disposed of by any other secure methods as recommended by the Ministry responsible for the subject of environment and in the presence of any officer deputed by him.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the restructuring and strengthening of the Drains Infrastructure Construction Ltd which is responsible for the implementation of the National Flood Management Programme. The restructuring exercise will be carried out in response to a recent internal audit which has revealed significant previous operational shortcomings and governance failures, and to secure its future as a critical vehicle for implementing drains projects.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of the Mauritius Biomass Fuel Aggregation Limited which will be mainly responsible for the sourcing of local and imported biomass in the context of the implementation of the National Biomass Initiative. The Mauritius Biomass Fuel Aggregation Limited, a joint venture between Government and the Independent Power Producers, will be responsible for the procurement and all logistical management related to importation, port handling, aggregation, transportation and warehousing of both local and imported biomass for use by the Independent Power Producers.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius supporting the draft decision tabled at the United Nations General Assembly today to convene the resumed session of the Palestine Conference on 22 September 2025. Mauritius will participate in that session and make an oral statement.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Health and Wellness and the Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences Hospital, Hyderabad, India. The aim of the Memorandum of Understanding will be to strengthen specialised healthcare services through structured collaboration. The Memorandum of Understanding will provide for capacity building of Mauritian healthcare professionals, technical assistance for upgrading local facilities and access to advanced treatments not available in Mauritius. Moreover, it will promote cooperation in high-specialty fields such as neuro-intervention, transplantation and oncology, while ensuring continuous medical education and streamlined referral services for Mauritian patients.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Health and Wellness and Yashoda Group of Hospitals, Hyderabad, India. The Memorandum of Understanding aims to establish a collaborative framework in order to advance several key areas of cooperation, including – (a) enhancing the capacity of Mauritian healthcare professionals; (b) implementing a Medico/Paramedic training programme; and (c) establishing a referral system for patients requiring complex medical procedures that cannot be performed in Mauritius.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the signing of a Treaty on the Transfer of Sentenced Persons between the Republic of Mauritius and the Republic of Kazakhstan. The Treaty sets out, inter alia, the following – (a) the general provisions for the transfer of sentenced persons, whereby the Parties may transfer sentenced persons to execute the sentences imposed by the Sentencing Party against these persons in the territory of the Administering Party; (b) the conditions for transfer include the following – (i) completion of the minimum sentence by the sentenced person; and (ii) the remaining period of time to be served by the sentenced person is at least six months; (c) the documents required to enable the transfer, including a certified copy of the judgment relating to the sentenced person, information on the type of punishment and the duration of the sentence.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the submission to the International Labour Organization of reports on Conventions ratified by the Republic of Mauritius, in line with Article 22 of the International Labour Organization Constitution. Mauritius has as at date, ratified 52 International Labour Organization Conventions, including 10 fundamental and two governance conventions, which are progressively incorporated into national legislation.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the meeting of the National Steering Committee on Gender Mainstreaming recently held under the chair of the Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare. The National Steering Committee on Gender Mainstreaming is a platform for Supervising Officers of Ministries and Departments to report on, coordinate, monitor and evaluate gender mainstreaming initiatives across all sectors. The main objective of the National Steering Committee on Gender Mainstreaming meeting was to review progress made by Ministries and Departments in the implementation of their respective sectoral gender-responsive actions and identify challenges being faced. The Committee also provided an opportunity to members to learn from best practices and benefit from strategic guidance to address gender gaps.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Revenue Authority will host the Tax Administration Diagnostic Assessment Tool (TADAT) Workshop from 08 to 12 September 2025. The workshop will be organised in collaboration with the International Monetary Fund under the theme "Beyond 10 Years of TADAT Assessments: Building Stronger Tax Systems for Tomorrow with the 2015 Field Guide". The aim of the workshop is to enhance understanding of international good practices, provide insights into the updates introduced in the TADAT Field Guide 2025, facilitate discussions on global and regional trends in tax administration, and promote peer learning opportunities among tax authorities.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Health and Wellness, in collaboration with the Hatter Cardiovascular Institute, University College London, UK, the Renal Association of Mauritius and the Cardiovascular Society of Mauritius, will hold an International Conference on "Addressing the crisis in Heart, Kidney and Metabolic Disorders" on 25 and 26 October 2025 in Mauritius. The objective of the conference is to address the crisis in heart, kidney and metabolic disorders and share evidence-based strategies which can be devised for prevention and management of cardio-renal and metabolic diseases along the life-course of an individual. The Conference aims at fostering national and international collaboration in addressing these diseases and formulating practical measures for their prevention and management. Eminent professors, experts, diabetologists, cardiologists, researchers, and epidemiologists from countries such as Australia, France, South Africa and United Kingdom will be present at the conference to deliver lectures and present case studies and research findings on cardiovascular and related diseases. Furthermore, the conference is expected to promote collaboration between different medical specialties, support the sharing of knowledge and best practices, as well as contribute to the strengthening of health systems for more effective prevention and management of non-communicable diseases.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of a high-level workshop on Strategic Communication, tailored specifically for Ministers, Junior Ministers, Members of Parliament, Senior Government Officials and Communication Advisors from 15 to 19 September 2025 by the Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning in collaboration with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP). The workshop follows strategic discussions held with the UNDP as part of broader efforts to support the Vision 2050 and the National Development Plan, and to improve policy uptake, reform delivery, and public confidence through more effective government communication. The workshop will be led by Mr. Simon Lancaster, a globally renowned speechwriter and communication expert. The primary objectives of the workshop are to equip participants with skills to - (a) communicate persuasively and effectively in a range of scenarios; (b) communicate complex ideas quickly in a way that is credible, convincing and compelling; (c) navigate difficult discussions and communicate calmly in crisis and conflict; and (d) craft and deliver world-class speeches that inspire, influence and energise change.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the University of Mauritius will organise an International Conference on Recent Advances in Information Systems from 10 to 12 September 2025 in collaboration with OFFIS, a German research institute and the University of Oldenburg, Germany. The conference will provide a platform to experts from around the world to come together to share ideas and to solve problems related to information systems. The conference will focus on finding solutions to issues such as data privacy, fair access to technology and creating rules for using Artificial Intelligence responsibly.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that L'Université des Mascareignes will organise the 5th International Conference on NanoMaterials for Health, Energy and the Environment (5-ICNM) from 07 to 11 September 2025 in Mauritius. The Conference, of a multidisciplinary nature, is being co-organised by leading international partners from France, the United States of America, Australia, Belgium and several other countries, together with L'Université des Mascareignes. The 5-ICNM will bring together leading scientists and innovators from diverse disciplines and continents to explore cutting-edge breakthroughs in nanomaterials, foster interdisciplinary collaboration and accelerate the development of innovative and sustainable solutions for pressing challenges in health, energy and the environment. The 5-ICNM will serve as a platform to explore the latest nanomaterials with applications in medical diagnostics and treatment, renewable energy systems and environmental sustainability. It will also create the foundation for future international partnerships and the development of multifunctional smart systems tailored to regional needs.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on the occasion of World Maritime Day which will be celebrated on 25 September 2025, the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries will organise an Open Day at the Rajiv Gandhi Science Centre, coupled with the launching of the pre-consultative dialogues for Les Assises de l'Océan. The theme for World Maritime Day this year is "Our ocean, our obligation, our opportunity". The commemoration of World Maritime Day 2025 will comprise, inter alia, the following activities – (a) an Open Day organised in partnership with stakeholders in the maritime industry such as the Mauritius Maritime Training Academy, Acropolis, Polytechnics Mauritius, Chantier Naval de L'Océan Indien and NGOs in the sector, to showcase career opportunities in the maritime sector; and (b) launching of Ocean Dialogues-Pre-Consultative Meetings focused on 10 themes, namely Climate Change, Biodiversity and Conservation; Blue Finance; Green Ports, Maritime Transport and Trade; Fisheries and Aquaculture and Food Security; Education, Marine Jobs, ICT and Innovations; Exploring the Ocean: Youth, Gender and Social/Awareness; Marine Renewable Energy; Applied Research, Exploration and Development in Marine Sector; Sustainable Marine Tourism; and Blue Diplomacy and Legal Framework.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning in the 20th Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) India-Africa Business Conclave jointly organised by the CII in collaboration with the Ministries of External Affairs and Commerce and Industry of India. The Conclave served as a strategic platform for advancing Africa's development agenda and underscored the role of the India- Africa economic and trade partnership in integrating regional and global value chains. In her keynote intervention on financial partnerships and banking services, the Minister highlighted Mauritius' role as a bridge between India and Africa, and encouraged Indian investors to leverage Mauritius as a platform for regional investment. She also promoted the implementation of the Comprehensive Economic Cooperation and Partnership Agreement and new Fintech collaborations to drive inclusive growth. In the sidelines of the Conclave, the Minister held meetings with Indian authorities, including the Foreign Secretary, the Chairman of the Central Board of Direct Taxes, Joint Secretary of Financial Action Task Force (FATF) matters and Vice Chairperson of NITI Aayog and the Indian Venture Capital Association, focusing on the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements ratification, venture capital flows, and Anti-Money Laundering/Combatting the Financing of Terrorism cooperation ahead of Mauritius' 2027 FATF assessment.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Land Transport in the International Transport Summit 2025 held in Russia. The key objectives of the Summit were to establish an international expert platform and framework enabling partner cities to share their experiences in the comprehensive development and modernisation of transport systems and related digital technologies; and to collaboratively devise systematic approaches for addressing challenges in urban mobility and enhancing the quality of life in cities. The Minister held meetings with, inter alia, the Minister of Transport of the Russian Federation, the First Deputy Head of the Department of Transport and Road Infrastructure Development of Moscow, the Director of the Department of Digital Development of the Ministry of Land Transport of the Russian Federation and the Chief Executive Officer of the Compagnie Industrielle des Transports Algériens.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection in the 20th Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) India-Africa Business Conclave, and the high-level meetings with the Ministry of Commerce and Industry and the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution of the Government of the Republic of India. During the Conference, the Minister intervened in three sessions, namely, 'From Farm to Future'; 'Sharing India's Experience, Mobility, Logistics and Supply Chains'; and in the Valedictory Session, alongside other Ministers from Africa. He emphasised Mauritius' position and firm commitment to strengthening itself as a key driver for India-Africa business partnerships. The Minister held a meeting with the Minister of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution of India and his team and engaged in fruitful discussions on the Price Stabilisation Fund and Consumer Protection. The Price Stabilisation Fund is a flagship initiative of the Government of India aimed at mitigating price volatility in essential agricultural commodities, thereby protecting consumers and ensuring fair prices for producers. The Minister also held discussions with the Minister of Commerce and Industry, for expanding commercial partnerships between the two countries through Government-to-Government Agreements.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent official visit of the Minister of Public Service and Administrative Reforms to Rodrigues. The main objectives of the visit were to take stock of the situation in the general administration of the Public Service in Rodrigues, share the projects/initiatives undertaken by the Ministry of Public Service and Administrative Reforms and also take cognisance of the main challenges so as to find ways and means as to how the Ministry could collaborate with and support the relevant Commission in Rodrigues. Meetings were held with the Chief Commissioner, Commissioners, the Acting Island Chief Executive, Departmental Heads, senior officers, staff of the Rodrigues Regional Assembly as well as the representatives of the Trade Unions. Issues relating to, inter alia, Training and Capacity Building; Public Sector Reforms; Human Resource Management; and Welfare of Public Officers in Rodrigues were discussed.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_05_SEPTEMBER 2025_1.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Residential Care Homes Board, with Mrs S. Bahadoor, Permanent Secretary as part-time Chairperson.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_07_March_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities and arrangements being made for the National Day Celebrations 2025 on the theme "As One People As One Nation". HE Shri Narendra Modi, Prime Minister of the Republic of India, would be on a State Visit to Mauritius on 11 and 12 March 2025, on the occasion of the National Day Celebrations 2025. The Prime Minister of the Republic of India would attend several functions during his visit, including, inter alia, a Wreath Laying Ceremony and Tree Planting at Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Botanic Garden, a State Banquet, a Delegation Level Meeting, exchange of bilateral agreements and inauguration ceremonies of bilateral projects. HE Shri Narendra Modi would be the Chief Guest at the Flag Raising Ceremony to be held at noon at Champ de Mars, Port Louis on 12 March 2025. The programme of the Official Ceremony would be followed by a cultural programme by Mauritian artists. Special transport facilities would be provided to the public as follows – (a) bus and metro fares at half rate two hours before and two hours after the celebration to all persons travelling to and from Champ de Mars; and (b) free transportation during the above-mentioned time and route for children aged less than 13 years.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_07_March_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Department for Continental Shelf, Maritime Zones Administration and Exploration and the Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services. The aim of the Memorandum of Understanding would be to create a framework of collaboration with a view to enhancing cooperation for the exchange of information and expertise focused on the technology and standards used in data repositories and visualisation centres. The Memorandum of Understanding would provide, inter alia, for – (a) access to up-to-date technologies that facilitate the availability of and access to marine geospatial data, including the use of data and/or metadata management tools; (b) collaboration on best practices in geospatial data management, including the sharing of standards to ensure data interoperability with local, regional and international geospatial platforms; (c) capacity building in ocean services, including data management, remote sensing, ocean modelling and hydrodynamic modelling of the coastal and marine environment; (d) collaboration in the field of ocean observations; and (e) distribution and sharing of information regarding the coastal and marine environment.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_07_March_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the holding of the 8th Eastern Africa Regional Technical Advisory Committee (ReSCO) on 10 and 11 April 2025 in Mauritius. The events would be organised by the Ministry of Health and Wellness in collaboration with the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). The objective of the Regional Technical Advisory Committee meeting would be to review Africa CDC's Strategic Implementation Progress, discuss current and emerging public health threats such as Mpox, Marburg and Cholera, enhance Health Security and Pandemic Preparedness and formulate recommendations for ReSCO. The mandate of the Regional Ministerial Steering Committee Meeting would be to provide High-Level Policy Oversight, strengthen Regional Emergency Coordination and elect new Regional Ministerial Steering Committee leadership.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_07_March_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a team from the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) would visit Mauritius from 10 to 21 March 2025 to conduct a survey to assess the readiness of the National Productivity and Competitiveness Council (NPCC) for a project to disseminate the Kaizen Approach in Mauritius and neighboring countries. The project would enhance the NPCC's capacity to become a Centre of Excellence for Kaizen dissemination, promote regional integration, generate revenue through consultancy services, and support the Government's objective of boosting productivity and competitiveness. The initiative aligns with recommendations from "Les Assises de L'Industrie" held in February 2025. The visit would culminate in the signing of an agreement between JICA and NPCC, covering key technical aspects such as consultant recruitment, training programs, project selection and a framework for Kaizen dissemination to neighbouring countries.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a Crisis Committee to look into the economic and social impacts of the present conflict in the Middle East, under the chair of the Prime Minister and comprising the following Ministers – (a) the Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change; (b) the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries; (c) the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (d) the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities; (e) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (f) the Minister of Land Transport; (g) the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; (h) the Minister of Tertiary Education, Science and Research; (i) the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; (j) the Minister of Education and Human Resource; and (k) the Minister of Public Service and Administrative Reforms.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Sugar Insurance Fund (Reduced General Insurance Premium and Fire Insurance Premium) Regulations 2026 with a view to exempting sugar cane planters and métayers producing up to 60 tons of sugar from the general and fire insurance premiums payable to the Sugar Insurance Fund Board for Crop 2025. The Regulations will provide for the general insurance premium payable under section 24 of the Sugar Insurance Fund Act and the fire insurance premium payable under section 34 of the Act, by planters and métayers who have produced total sugar accrued not exceeding 60 tons, to be reduced to nil.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries conveying drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for the preparation of the Animal Health, Veterinary Public Health and Livestock Production Bill. The objectives of the Bill will be to, inter alia, - (a) promote and protect animal health and veterinary public health, prevent the introduction and spread of animal diseases and to provide for appropriate sanitary measures for the control of animal diseases; (b) regulate the trade of animals and products of animal origin; and (c) provide for the development of the livestock sector and traceability of livestock and products of animal origin.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to regulations being made under sections 29A, 29B and 124 of the Workers' Rights Act 2019, for the registration of labour contractors and supply of migrant workers by the labour contractors in the agricultural sector. In view of the shortage of skilled and unskilled labour which is severely impacting sugarcane and food production in Mauritius, leading to small planters abandoning their arable land and worsening food insecurity, the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries is proposing to resort to the recruitment of migrant workers, on a pilot basis, through labour contractors. In this context, the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations has held several consultations with the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries and relevant stakeholders. The modalities and legal considerations for the recruitment of migrant workers, on a pilot basis, through labour contractors were agreed upon during a tripartite meeting held at the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the National Research and Innovation Institute Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to provide for the establishment of the National Research and Innovation Institute. Accordingly, the Mauritius Research and Innovation Council Act will be repealed and replaced by this legislation. The National Research and Innovation Institute will be the national centralised body dedicated to advancing scientific discovery, research, innovation and evidence-based policymaking, conducting mission-oriented research addressing pressing national challenges, coordinating the national research ecosystem, and driving the transition of Mauritius into a resilient, competitive and knowledge-powered economy.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent visit of the Minister of National Infrastructure to Rodrigues to assess the situation following the torrential rain which occurred during the period from 14 to 16 March 2026. The Minister held meetings with the Chief Commissioner of Rodrigues along with Commissioners and senior officers who made a detailed presentation of the problems encountered during the torrential rain. Over 200 millimetres of rain had been recorded in many regions, and it was reported that there had been huge impacts on the existing infrastructure and damage had been caused. Recovery operations, post torrential rain, had already started for road clearing in affected areas and clogged drains were being cleared. Damage had been identified in road networks, flooding of hydraulic structures across river courses, flooding in Morcellements and damage to individual houses and associated infrastructure, including retaining walls. The Minister effected several site visits, namely at – (a) Port Mathurin, where it was observed that drain project has to be implemented. The dredging and cleaning of canals had to be done as a short-term measure; (b) Queen Elizabeth Hospital at Crève Coeur, where there is an urgent need to re-align and enlarge the existing drain at the back of the hospital; (c) Quatre Vents, where a retaining wall will need to be constructed in view of soil erosion; (d) La Ferme; (e) Pistaches; (f) Baie Topaze; (g) Port Sud Est; (h) Cascade Victoire; and (i) Mourouk. The Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity will extend appropriate support under the National Empowerment Foundation and the National Solidarity Fund to eligible families affected by the torrential rain. Modalities of the support are being looked into.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Health and Wellness initiating Alternative Dispute Proceedings with Messrs Pack and Blister Distribuciones Farmaceuticas SL and Mediterranean Healthcare Supply Network SL in the case of ventilators procured by the previous Government during the COVID-19 period in 2020, in an attempt to recover the expenses incurred by Government to the tune of EUR2.2 million (Rs118.8 million) for equipment which was never delivered as well as claim damages.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the actual cost of a 12-kg domestic Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) cylinder averages Rs550, whilst the subsidised price is Rs190. The current deficit in the subsidy account of the State Trading Corporation stood at Rs1.9 billion for the period June 2022 - June 2025. Based on the average price structure of a 12-kg LPG cylinder in 2025, the total annual subsidy required for LPG for this financial year is expected to reach approximately Rs2.3 billion. In the wake of the geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, this amount is expected to rise in the coming months, consequently increasing the deficit incurred for subsidising LPG domestic cylinders. Cabinet has agreed to the retail price of domestic LPG cylinders being reviewed from Rs190 to Rs250 per 12-kg cylinder, with a view to reducing the annual fiscal deficit in the subsidy account of the State Trading Corporation.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recommendations made by the Office of Public Sector Governance (OPSG) in its Report regarding the organisational review of the Mauritius Meat Authority (MMA). The MMA has been facing significant challenges impacting its operations and overall effectiveness over the last 10 years. The main issues relate to lack of strategic direction, ineffective management of human resources, poor financial situation, poor sales and marketing strategies, inefficient and ineffective operations over procurement and warehousing, inadequate good governance practices, amongst others. The OPSG formulated several recommendations to review the organisational structure of the MMA comprising, inter alia, - (a) Organisational Set Up; (b) Human Resource Management; (c) Financial Management; (d) Production Department; and (e) Governance. The MMA is preparing an Action Plan to implement the recommendations of the Report of the OPSG, focusing on operational efficiency and compliance with law.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of an Agreement on Cooperation and Mutual Administrative Assistance in Customs Matters between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of India. The objective of the Agreement is to provide a framework for both customs administrations to strengthen, promote and develop cooperation, communication and mutual assistance in customs matters. The Agreement provides for mutual assistance for – (a) the facilitation of trade and proper application of customs legislation; (b) the prevention, investigation and combating of customs offences; (c) the exchange of best practices pertaining to customs, including research, development and application of new customs procedures; (d) the simplification and harmonisation of customs procedures to facilitate legitimate trade; (e) ensuring the security of the international trade supply chain; and (f) mutual capacity building in customs matters, such as training and exchange of personnel.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of an Agreement between the International Trade Centre (ITC) and the Economic Development Board (EDB) for the provision of technical assistance on the EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CS3D) which is a landmark European Union law that introduces the obligation for large companies to conduct appropriate human rights and environmental due diligence with respect to their operations, operations of their subsidiaries, and operations of their business partners in the companies' chains of activities. Mauritian exporters, particularly small and medium enterprises, will have to prepare themselves to meet the requirements under the CS3D. The EDB has engaged with the ITC for technical assistance to be provided to these enterprises to assess their levels of preparedness relative to the CS3D requirements, in the context of the EU-funded project entitled 'Global Public Goods: Providing Trade and Market Intelligence for Informed Trade Decisions'. This will allow the enterprises to take appropriate measures to be in line with the Directives. The technical assistance will cover the following – (a) building capacity of EDB on CS3D; (b) piloting an ITC-developed online self-assessment diagnostic questionnaire related to CS3D; (c) collecting data from participating small enterprises; (d) generating basic Gap Analysis based on responses of participating enterprises; (e) producing technical guidance notes and lessons learned in relation to levels of preparedness; and (f) systematising the most important challenges, opportunities and support measures needed in regard to human rights and environmental rights due diligence.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities supporting and signing an In-Principle Memorandum of Understanding for Procurement under the Small Island Developing States (SIDS) Platform, a joint initiative of the International Solar Alliance (ISA) and the World Bank Group, to facilitate aggregated and efficient large scale solar deployment. The SIDS Platform, led by ISA in partnership with the World Bank Group, is a global initiative aimed at supporting SIDS in accelerating the deployment of solar PV and battery storage through aggregated demand, standardised procurement, and coordinated financial and technical support. The Platform seeks to address structural challenges faced by island economies by creating scale, reducing risk, and enabling timely project execution. By joining the SIDS Platform, Mauritius will benefit from – (a) cost optimisation through competitive, aggregated e-procurement; (b) fiscal stability through the deployment of renewables to lower cost and increased competition between Renewable Energy vendors; (c) reduced diesel dependency and exposure to fuel price volatility; (d) contribution to Sustainable Development Goals and climate commitments; (e) capacity building through knowledge and toolkits embedded in the Platform; and (f) improved data-driven energy planning.
ministries: Ministry Of Energy and Public Utilities

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the University of Mauritius signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Pretoria, South Africa, for collaboration in the following areas – (a) training of undergraduate and postgraduate students in the health sciences; (b) teaching and learning through capacity building, encompassing knowledge and skills transfer as well as structured workplace experience; (c) joint promotion and marketing of programmes to be delivered under the MoU, both domestically and internationally; (d) development of supplementary agreements by both institutions to give effect to specific collaborative activities; (e) joint applications for research and development funding; (f) exchange of academic and non-academic staff and students; and (g) appointment of local and international extraordinary lecturers to support quality assurance in respect of programme content and delivery. The MoU will promote and advance collaborative projects between the two institutions, including joint research initiatives and the delivery of programmes at all levels; deepen collaborative ties in areas of shared interest and mutual benefit, notably through the exchange of students and academic staff; and establish a framework for addressing other matters of common concern as they arise.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the African Regional Intellectual Property Organization (ARIPO) will field a mission to Mauritius from 21 to 23 April 2026 in pursuance of its ongoing initiative to sensitise users in its Member States to the e-filing system launched in 2015. The objective of the mission is to enhance uptake of the e-filing system with a view to facilitating online filing of Intellectual Property titles, online payment of fees and online tracking of applications. The e-filing service sensitisation workshop will target Intellectual Property agents, attorneys and other potential users of the ARIPO system. The Member States module capacity-building session will be held at the Intellectual Property Office for the benefit of the technical staff of the Office.

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the programme of activities being organised to mark Earth Day, which is celebrated annually on 22 April. The theme retained for Earth Day 2026 is "Our Power, Our Planet". The following activities will be organised by the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change in collaboration with relevant stakeholders – (a) a workshop, focused on clean air and air quality, at the Université des Mascareignes, to raise awareness and strengthen stakeholder engagement on air quality monitoring, regulatory compliance, vehicular emissions, and industrial enforcement in Mauritius, while identifying actions to improve air quality in line with environmental priorities; (b) a one-day exhibition at the Université des Mascareignes to showcase practical demonstrations of air quality monitoring equipment, vehicular smoke emission testing, stack emission monitoring devices, and cleaner production technologies; (c) TV broadcast of a special magazine on the theme of Earth Day 2026; and (d) extension of wastepaper collection for local recycling to primary and secondary schools across the island in collaboration with the Ministry of Education and Human Resource, the Private Secondary Education Authority and the Service Diocésain de l'Éducation Catholique. Sensitisation of the public on the theme of Earth Day 2026 will be carried out through talks, radio programmes, and dissemination of a message via the website of the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change and the Government Email Services.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: FINAL_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_27_March_2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the setting up of the Workfare Programme Fund Committee, with Mr Osman Mahmad Badat as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Fixed Penalty Notice) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 which would provide for the review of the penalty fees for Fixed Penalty Notice which aimed at deterring trade malpractices and strengthening consumer protection while promoting accountability and safeguarding consumer rights. The Regulations would provide for, inter alia, - (a) a graduated penalty structure with tiered penalties based on the number of products involved in violations; (b) enhanced enforcement mechanisms; (c) increased penalty thresholds; (d) correction to frequency of penalty multiplication; and (e) refined categorisation of offences for stricter oversight of warehousing practices to enhance accountability. With a view to allowing the industry to adapt to the updated provisions and to ensuring a smooth transition, the regulations would come into effect on 31 March 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Customs (Amendment) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for amendments to requirements for the appointment of a person as Customs House Broker, who had to succeed in a written and oral examination conducted by the Mauritius Revenue Authority. With a view to being fairer to persons who succeeded in the written examinations, a person would be allowed to take part in three subsequent oral examinations within three years from the date of the first oral examination, instead of two subsequent oral examinations within two years from the date of the first oral examinations.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the payment of shared contributions to the SADC Mission in Mozambique and the Democratic Republic of Congo, the SADC Secretariat lifted the sanction on Mauritius imposed thus authorising the country to fully participate in events organised by the SADC.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Higher Education Commission signing a Memorandum of Understanding with the Tanzania Commission for Universities, which would aim at promoting the development of higher education, academic research and training, through equity of access, high quality learning and research, as well as the efficient use of national resources and innovation. Both institutions would pursue the following main activities of mutual interest – (a) exchange of information, including non-confidential policy documents and relevant manuals; (b) sharing of good practices and experiences in the field; (c) staff development, through the organisation of training activities, and participation of technical staff as observers in regulatory processes; (d) use of the partnering institution's list of reviewers and specialists, to strengthen the international dimension of institutional reviews, accreditations and reviews of cross border education; (e) identification of fraudulent awarding bodies or accrediting agencies in the Republic of Mauritius and the United Republic of Tanzania; and (f) mutual recognition of the partnering institution's standards, regulations and procedures.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the University of Technology, Mauritius, signing a Convention Cadre De Coopération with the Université de Toamasina, Madagascar. The Convention Cadre De Coopération would aim at fostering collaboration between the two institutions for the development of the following activities of mutual interest, in the fields of higher education and scientific research - (a) student and faculty exchange programmes; (b) advanced training courses for academic staff and students of either institution; (c) joint research projects and scientific activities, including joint organisation of conferences and seminars; and (d) promotion and publication of research works.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the release of the Family Planning and Demographic Yearbook 2023 which provides data on population trends, vital rates, family planning, and health service points for Mauritius and Rodrigues. The salient features of the yearbook include, inter alia, - (a) the island of Mauritius recorded a population growth rate of -0.09% in 2023; and (b) Mauritius was experiencing a demographic shift with an ageing population due to sustained low fertility rates and increasing life expectancy. In 2023, the number of individuals aged 60 years and above exceeded the number of children under 15 years. Life expectancy had improved from 71.6 years in 1999- 2001 to 73.3 years in 2021-2023. The Ministry of Health and Wellness was committed to enhancing sexual and reproductive health services, including, family planning, through a series of initiatives.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Computer Emergency Response Team of Mauritius (CERT-MU) to mark Safer Internet Day, on the theme "Too good to be true? Protecting yourself and others from scams online", namely - (a) a National Sensitisation Campaign on 17 February 2025 with the objective of sensitising youngsters and raising public awareness about common types of online scams, namely phishing, fake websites, social media fraud, lottery scams, identity theft; (b) train the trainer's programmes on dangers of the internet and online scams to better equip ICT Educators of both Primary and Secondary schools in communicating the overall gist of this campaign to the pupils and students; (c) production of two video clips on online scams to raise awareness and educate viewers in identifying online scams; and (d) awareness sessions in Community Centres and Women Centres.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the Extraordinary Summit of the Assembly of the African Union (AU) on the "Post-Malabo Comprehensive Africa Agriculture Development Programme (CAADP) Agenda" in Kampala, Uganda. The objective of the Extraordinary Summit was to deliberate on and adopt a post- Malabo CAADP Strategy and Action Plan for the period 2026 to 2035 with a view to building strong and resilient agri-food systems on the African continent. The Summit concluded with the adoption of the Kampala CAADP Declaration on "Building Resilient and Sustainable Agri- Food Systems in Africa". During his intervention in the Summit, the Minister highlighted the significant potential of the CAADP in ensuring food security within the African Continental Free Trade Area which aims to create a single market across the AU. He also stressed the importance of promoting agricultural trade among African countries, and the need to encourage south-south cooperation on a larger scale as Africa can be a breadbasket and ensure food security in the continent.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations in the "2nd Global Labour Market Conference" held in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The objective of the Conference was to discuss and foster collaboration between global leaders, policymakers, worker and employer representatives as well as experts in strategies for labour market resilience in the face of global challenges. The Minister had meetings with various Ministers of Labour, including the Minister of Labour, Employment, and Social Security of Nepal and the Minister of Investment of Saudi Arabia.

filename: Final_Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 07 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Land Drainage Board of the Land Drainage Authority, with Dr Dhirajsing Rughoo as part-time Chairperson; and (b) Human Resource Development Council, with Mr Bhooyasheel Akshay Seedoyal as part-time Chairperson. Cabinet further noted the appointment of – (a) Mr William Mario Ayelou as Managing Director of the National Housing Development Co. Ltd; (b) Mr Mohammad Shameem Rassool Cadam Ally, as Managing Director of the New Social Living Development; and (c) Mrs Shalina Maumoondee-Dhallapah as part-time Chairperson of the New Social Living Development Board.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the- (a) payment of a 14th Month Bonus to employees in the public and private sectors deriving a monthly basic salary of up to Rs50,000 to be paid in two instalments as follows - (i) a first instalment of 50 percent in December 2024; and (ii) a second instalment of 50 percent in January 2025. (b) payment of a 14th Month Bonus to beneficiaries of basic pensions and CSG retirement benefit as well as public service pensioners to be paid in December 2024; (c) award of a monthly salary compensation of 3.7 percent of the National Minimum Wage, i.e., Rs610 to employees drawing a monthly basic salary of up to Rs50,000 with effect from 01 January 2025; (d) provision of financial support to SMEs, Export-Oriented Enterprises and such enterprises facing financial difficulties as may be approved, subject to profitability criteria, for the payment of the 14th Month Bonus and of the salary compensation; and (e) reduction in the retail prices of Mogas (Essence) and Gas Oil (Diesel) by Rs5 per litre as from Saturday 14 December 2024.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Employment Relations (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Employment Relations Act to give the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations the power to make regulations for wage adjustment arising from the introduction or review of national minimum wage in the private sector, including any industry or sector of activity not governed by Remuneration Regulations.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Special Allowance Bill into the National Assembly to give effect to the decision regarding the payment of the 14th Month Bonus to employees drawing a monthly basic wage or salary not exceeding Rs50,000.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the impact of the passage of the cyclone Chido over Agalega and immediate actions taken to bring assistance to the inhabitants of the island. A Dornier flight left Mauritius this morning to deliver essential supplies, including food and medicine, to Agalega. A Technician from Emtel Ltd also travelled on board the Dornier to restore communication on the island. The assistance of the Government of India has been sought to deploy a P81 cargo plane from Goa for aerial reconnaissance of both islands and to deliver supplies to Agalega.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the critical financial situation of the Metro Express Ltd and on the need for urgent actions to redress the Company, the gist of which is as follows – (a) as at 30 June 2024, borrowings amounted to Rs17.2 billion; (b) foreign exchange loss accounted for some Rs3 billion over the past five years due to the rapid depreciation of the Mauritian Rupee; (c) the annual deficit was of the order some Rs300 million due to operating expenses exceeding revenue; and (d) an expected average deficit of some Rs2.1 billion yearly was foreseen for the next 10 years. The Office of the Public Sector Governance will be conducting a governance and financial audit at the Metro Express Ltd.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Local Government to look into a thorough reform of the entire Local Government System that reflects the present realities and also enables local authorities to respond effectively to the needs of the local community. The Committee would comprise the - (a) Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change; (b) Minister of National Infrastructure; (c) Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (d) Attorney-General; (e) Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade; (f) Minister of Youth and Sports; (g) Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (h) Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; and (i) Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status regarding the major fire outbreak which occurred recently at the Mare Chicose Landfill and the post-fire monitoring mechanism put in place by the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change. As at 08 December 2024, the fire had been completely extinguished. Ambient air assessments were conducted in numerous places and results revealed that throughout the firefighting period, all toxic gases screened were below the detectable limits of the Portable Gas Analyser at time of assessment and was well below the Lowest Observed Adverse Effect Level. The quality of groundwater and surface water would continue to be monitored. A Comité de Suivi had been set up on a permanent basis to ensure that – (a) the landfilling operations were properly conducted to reduce the risk of fire; (b) a proper surveillance be set up; and (c) the implementation of the firefighting system being closely monitored.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Civil Aviation (Pravasi Bharatiya Divas 2025) (Exemption) Regulations 2024 which provide for the exemption from payment of the Passenger Service Charge, the Passenger Solidarity Fee and the Terminal Expansion Fee in favour of the registered participants of the 18th Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Convention 2025, which would be held from 08 to 10 January 2025 in Bhubaneswar, Odisha, India.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund (Pilgrimage) (Amendment) Regulations 2024 would be promulgated to provide for – (a) an increase in the payment of fee paid by Hadj operators on application for a permit from Rs5,000 to Rs10,000; and (b) an increase in the uniform sum of Rs250,000, being security provided to the Islamic Cultural Centre by the Hadj operators prior to the issue of a permit, to the following categories – (i) to a maximum of 100 Hadjis, a security of Rs500,000 ; (ii) to more than 100 but less than 200 Hadjis, a security of Rs750,000; or (iii) to more than 200 Hadjis, a security of Rs1M. The Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund (Pilgrimage) (Amendment) Regulations 2024 would come into operation on 14 December 2024.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the results of the PSAC Assessment 2024. Out of the 13,866 school candidates of the Republic of Mauritius who took part in the 2024 PSAC assessment for the first time, 10,629 met the requirements for the PSAC representing a percentage of 76.66%. There were 13,117 candidates who took part in PSAC Assessment 2024 in Mauritius, out of whom 10,034 met the PSAC requirements, that is, 76.5%. As for Rodrigues, 749 candidates took part in PSAC assessment and 595 candidates attained the PSAC requirements, that is 79.44%. Out of the 10 school candidates in Agalega who took part in the PSAC Assessment 2024, four attained the PSAC requirements, that is, 40%.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Condavelloo Dorsamy as Managing Director and Mr Jean Hubert Nöel as Deputy Managing Director of the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd for a period of two years.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Zeïnoule Ahbédine Chundoo as part- time Chairperson of the National Housing Development Co Ltd Board.

filename: Final_Highlights-13.12.24.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the National Transport Corporation Board with Mr Nishad Baig, as the part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Municipal City Council election and the Municipal Town Council elections being organised after the National Day Celebrations 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Coordination Committee had been set up at the level of the Prime Minister's Office to ensure the implementation of reconstruction works on the North and South Islands of Agalega. A first meeting of the Coordination Committee was held on 19 December 2024 where the following salient issues were discussed - (a) Extent of the damage in the North and South Islands; (b) Reconstruction works; (c) Relief efforts; (d) Funds would be provided from the Prime Minister's Relief Fund for the repairs and reconstruction works; (e) No oil spill had been reported on the AFCONS tug which was grounded near Grande Caze. A Salvage Team would leave for Agalega by Dornier on 20 December 2024. All efforts were being sustained to avoid oil spilling; and (f) Two officers from EMTEL would be on board MV Mauritius Trochetia and would bring along solar panels and communication-related equipment to restore communication in the South Island. The Prime Minister's Office would set up an Agalega Relief Fund for the raising of funds in favour of Agalega.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Centre would henceforth operate under the Prime Minister's Office. The cyclone warning system of the Mauritius Meteorological Services would be reviewed.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the National Pensions (Amendment of Schedule) (No. 2) Regulations 2024 with a view to giving effect to the increase in the rates of the Basic Retirement Pension, Widow's Basic Pension, Invalid Basic Pension and Orphan's Pension by Rs1,000 as from January 2025. The payment of the increase in pensions for some 311,000 beneficiaries would be effected on 03 January 2025 through banks and from 03 to 16 January 2025 through post offices.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Private Secondary Education (Amendment) Regulations 2024 to provide for the criteria for promotion to Grade 12, from five credits to three credits as from January 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the review of the current Comprehensive Grant Formula and the introduction of a new formula for the financing of Grant-Aided Private Secondary Schools for period 2025-2027. Following representations received from the Federation of Unions of Managers, a Technical Working Group comprising representatives of the Ministry of Education and Human Resource, the Ministry of Finance, the Office of the Public Sector Governance and the Private Secondary Education Authority (PSEA) had been set up to work on the review of the Comprehensive Grant Formula with the following Terms of Reference - (a) to hold consultations with stakeholders for a review of the Comprehensive Grant Formula and examine their proposals; (b) to examine the shortcomings of the present system of the validation exercise and disbursement of grants by the PSEA and make recommendations for the improvement thereof; and (c) to submit its Report for the financing of Grant-Aided Private Secondary Schools for period 2025-2027.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change submitting the first Biennial Transparency Report (BTR1) for the Republic of Mauritius, to the Secretariat of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change.
