The main components of the BTR1 include a National Greenhouse Gas Inventory Report for the period 2017 to 2022 and a National Report on progress made in implementing and achieving the Nationally Determined Contributions, information related to climate change impacts and adaptation, levels of financial support, technology development and transfer as well as capacity building needs and support and areas of improvement.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following representations received from Heads of School/Rectors and Union of Rectors regarding the inability of schools to proceed with urgent and necessary repairs of amenities and the shortage of certain essential requisites, the Ministry of Education and Human Resource would provide an amount of Rs27.1 M to public primary and secondary schools for, inter alia, the maintenance of schools and procurement of urgent requisites and other ancillary purchases/works.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Foundational Programme in Literacy, Numeracy and Skills would be introduced in Lower Secondary Schools and would be run over three years as from 2025. The programme would be conducted in secondary schools (public and private), in collaboration with the Mauritius Institute of Training and Development, the Mauritius Institute of Education, the Mauritius Examinations Syndicate and the National Social Inclusion Foundation. The Curriculum framework for the new programme would constitute of three major parts as follows - (a) Functional literacy which would include Numeracy, English Literacy, French Literacy, Scientific Literacy, Digital Literacy and Kreol Morisien/Kreol Rodrigues/Asian Languages/Arabic; (b) Life skills and values comprising Social and Emotional Well-being, the Arts, Physical Education and Health; and (c) Trade and livelihood skills where the students would be taught culinary skills, Smart Agriculture, Woodwork, Garment Making, Electric Installation and Plumbing.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following representations received, the hours of work of teachers of Municipal/District Council Pre-Schools and Aided Private Pre- Primary Schools had been reviewed to 08 30 hours to 14 30 hours instead of 08 30 hours to 15 00 hours on normal school days as from school resumption in January 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Mauritius Institute of Education co-hosting the 5th World Curriculum Studies Conference together with the International Association for the Advancement of Curriculum Studies from 24 to 27 June 2025, in Mauritius. The theme of the conference would be "Curriculum Studies in the post Anthropocene Era" and would cover issues ranging from language in the curriculum, scientific education, artificial intelligence, early childhood and inclusive education curriculum. The conference would play a key role towards the achievement of UN Sustainable Development Goals since the quality of the curriculum and its delivery affects the quality of education across all levels.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the price of sugar for Crop 2024, had been fixed as follows - Growing Side - Rs24,060/T of sugar at 98.5% Polarisation Milling Side - Rs23,000/T of sugar at 98.5% Polarisation

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that an Electronic Travel Authorisation scheme was being introduced by the United Kingdom (UK) worldwide for 82 countries, including Mauritius, whose citizens currently do not need a visa for short stays of up to six months or who do not have a UK immigration status. As from 08 January 2025, Mauritian nationals travelling to the UK must have a valid Electronic Travel Authorisation to enter or transit through the UK.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Mauritius Oceanography Institute Board, with Dr Mitrasen Bhikajee as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Road Development Authority Board, with Mr Seewoonarain Koonjul as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Construction Industry Board, with Mr Avinash Shree Mojee as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_20.12.2024.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Board of the Tourism Authority, with Mr Jose Arunasalom as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent Israeli strike in Doha, Qatar's capital, condemning without reserve these acts of violence. A message of condolence and solidarity is being sent to the Qatari authorities.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Education and Human Resource engaging in consultations with stakeholders on the proposal for a legislative framework to regulate the private primary education sector.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the meeting held under the chair of the Minister of Youth and Sports with the executive members of the Mauritius Olympic Committee and the presidents/members of National Sports Federations in the presence of the representative of the International Olympic Committee, on 09 September 2025, following the freezing of programmes and funds of the Mauritius Olympic Committee by the International Olympic Committee. The Minister of Youth and Sports highlighted his role as facilitator without interfering with the internal affairs of the Mauritius Olympic Committee and the National Sports Federation with a view to resolving the matter in the interest of the Mauritian athletes. The following two proposals have been retained for further discussions – (a) setting up, by the Mauritius Olympic Committee, of an interim 13-member Managing Committee comprising representatives of both disputants (about 15 National Sports Federations, including the Mauritius Tennis Federation) and members of the current executive committee of the Mauritius Olympic Committee (but excluding the Mauritius Modern Pentathlon Federation and the Mauritius Roller and Skate Federation) to align the statutes of the Mauritius Olympic Committee with local legislation; and (b) holding of the election, without the participation of the Mauritius Modern Pentathlon Federation and the Mauritius Roller and Skate Federation.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised to mark the International Day of Older Persons, which is commemorated on 01 October each year in recognition of the significant contribution of elders to the socio-economic development of the country. This year's theme is "Older Persons Driving Local and Global Action: Our Aspirations, Our Well-Being, Our Rights", which will serve as a platform for older persons to share their aspirations and advocate for their well-being. The Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity will celebrate the International Day of Older Persons by gathering members of some 846 Senior Citizens Associations. The Senior Citizens Council will hold a consultative workshop on 24 September 2025 with representatives of Senior Citizens Associations with a view to gathering insights on the proposed reforms and legal framework, in line with this year's theme.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Health and Wellness and the SRM Institutes for Medical Science Hospitals, Chennai, India. The aim of the Memorandum of Understanding will be to strengthen specialised healthcare services through structured collaboration. The Memorandum of Understanding will provide for capacity building of Mauritian healthcare professionals, technical assistance for upgrading local facilities and access to advanced treatments not available in Mauritius. Moreover, it will promote cooperation in high-specialty fields such as Neuro Interventions, Transplantation, Bone Marrow Transplants, Paediatric Surgeries and Cardiac Electrophysiology. It will ensure continuous medical education and streamline referral services for Mauritian patients.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of the Obesity Action Acceleration Roadmap (2025-2030) which will focus on the following five priority actions with a view to reducing the prevalence of obesity in the Mauritian population - (a) enhance obesity prevention and management across the life course in primary health care settings and community-based programmes; (b) promote and create enabling environments to facilitate physical activity appropriate for all ages in different settings; (c) restrict the marketing of unhealthy, ultra-processed foods and sugar-sweetened beverages; (d) behaviour change communication campaigns related to healthy diets, physical activity, and demand for health services; and (e) increase local agricultural production and accessibility to healthy foods to ensure availability and affordability of safe and quality food products.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the formulation of the Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) 3.0 for the Republic of Mauritius; and preparation being made for the participation of Mauritius to the 30th Session of Conference of Parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change. The NDC 3.0 follows a process established by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change which is underpinned by a transparency guidance and which comprises two distinct phases, namely the formulation of a Roadmap that aims to assess current NDC implementation, identifying gaps, challenges and opportunities as well as defining the key recommendations for the NDC 3.0.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of Les Assises du Travail et de l'Emploi by the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations from 23 to 25 September 2025 at the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Public Service and Innovation. The main objectives of the Assises are to review the challenges in the world of work and to identify solutions or measures to address these challenges. The objectives are aligned to the four pillars of the International Labour Organization's Decent Work Agenda, namely employment creation; social protection; rights at work; and social dialogue. The Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations had invited members of the public, Ministries, non-governmental organisations, private sector associations, representatives of employees, representatives of employers and interested parties to submit proposals for consideration during the Assises. Some 400 proposals from around 70 stakeholders were received and regrouped into the following themes which will be discussed during the Assises - (a) worker's rights and conditions of employment; (b) current Occupational Safety and Health challenges; (c) employment facilitation, migration and labour and skills shortage; (d) industrial relations; and (e) dispute resolution mechanisms.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting _Friday_12_September_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the – (a) appointment of Mr Vinesh Sewraz, as part-time Chairperson of the Agricultural Marketing Board; (b) reconstitution of the Loïs Lagesse Trust Fund Board with Mr Robeensing Baboolall, as part-time Chairperson; (c) reconstitution of the Board of Trustees of the Malcolm de Chazal Trust Fund with Mr Gérard Jean-Max Baya, as part-time Chairperson; and (d) reconstitution of the Committee of the President's Fund for Creative Writing with Mrs Nandini Bhautoo, as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 31 July 2025, the US President issued a new Executive Order with new tariffs underlining that some countries have been willing to negotiate and have shown commitment to address existing trade imbalances with the US. According to the new Executive Order, the new tariff rate applicable to Mauritius will be 15%. The Executive Order will take effect on 07 August 2025. It is to be recalled that on 02 April 2025, the US President had issued an Executive Order introducing country-specific reciprocal tariffs based on trade imbalances, effective as from 09 April 2025. All countries, including AGOA beneficiaries, were initially subject to these measures and Mauritius faced a 40% tariff. Subsequently, the US announced a 90-day pause and reduced the tariff rate to 10% till 09 July 2025 with all trading partners. A further postponement to 01 August 2025 was announced on 07 July 2025, alongside revised rates for several countries. Since the imposition of the tariff, Government has engaged with the US Authorities at various levels. In reply to a correspondence from the Prime Minister, the US President stated that both Mauritius and the US could negotiate a deal that not only addresses the trade imbalances but also enhances the partnership between the two nations. Mauritius will pursue discussions with the US Authorities in the interest of the export- oriented sector.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the re-establishment of the Regional Cooperation and Integration Council, under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister. In 1996, a Regional Cooperation Council was set up by Government, as a high-level public-private sector forum to address regional policy issues. Its objective was also to create a synergy between Government and economic operators to tap into the vast opportunities that our membership into regional bodies offers. Until recently, not enough priority was given to regional cooperation, including the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) which is now in force. As highlighted in the Government Programme 2025-2029, it is important for Mauritius to strengthen regional dynamics and enhance economic integration, particularly with the coastal states of the Indian Ocean while at the same time, supporting the efforts of our partners on the continent to achieve the goals set by the AfCFTA. The Regional Cooperation and Integration Council will also comprise other Ministers and public and private sector institutions including the Economic Development Board. As geo-political and global economic uncertainties make it imperative for Mauritius to embark upon a more innovative and strategic developmental diplomacy, the more so as the region and the continent in general are considered as an emerging economic powerhouse, a coherent and more engaging policy will allow the country to ensure economic prosperity through regional integration.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Attorney-General had convened an Inter-Ministerial Meeting on 31 July 2025 to discuss the report of the Court of Investigation on the grounding of MV Wakashio and avenues open for a claim to be made by the State of Mauritius. Cabinet has agreed to the full report of the Court of Investigation being referred to the Director of Public Prosecutions, as until now, the request he made since 2023 to the then Attorney-General, Minister of Agro-Industry and Food Security has not been entertained. Government intends to make the report public. Cabinet has also taken note that the Wakashio Commemorative Plaque which was installed at the Mahebourg Waterfront, but removed by the previous Government, will be reinstated during a ceremony to be organised on 06 August 2025 by the District Council of Grand Port. Cabinet has further taken note that action is being initiated for the designation of a Deputy Commandant of the National Coast Guard from amongst serving Senior Officers of the Mauritius Police Force.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the company Udaan Aviation recently involved in a fraudulent activity in Madagascar in the aviation sector, has no dealings in Mauritius. However, UDAAN POTENTIALS LTD, a private domestic company incorporated on 28 November 2019, was struck off the Register of Companies on 16 January 2025 under section 309(1)(b) of the Companies Act 2001. UDAAN POTENTIALS LTD was not a licensee of the Financial Services Commission.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius entering into a new Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) with the Republic of Botswana. The objective of this Agreement is to replace the existing DTAA which was signed in September 1995 and entered into force in March 1996, with a new DTAA which complies with the OECD/G20 Base Erosion and Profit Shifting recommendations. The new DTAA will not only clarify the taxing rights of each country on income covered by the Agreement but also - (a) provide certainty to investors in their dealings with Mauritius or Botswana; (b) create a conducive environment for greater cross-border investment flows between our two countries; (c) establish a framework for exchange of information between the tax authorities of Mauritius and Botswana, with a view to combatting tax evasion and other tax malpractices; and (d) provide a mechanism to taxpayers for the resolution of tax disputes.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Mauritius Agricultural Marketing (Controlled Products) Regulations 2025. The Regulations make provision for, inter alia, - (a) by-catch fish removed from foreign non-licenced tuna longliners to be henceforth considered as a controlled product and to mandatorily have to be sold to the Agricultural Marketing Board (AMB); (b) procedures for the removal of by-catch fish from both licenced and non-licenced fishing vessels to be further regulated by empowering the AMB to be responsible for the Standard Operations Procedures for the receipt, delivery, purchase, handling, transportation, processing, storage and sale of by-catch fish; (c) a sub-committee, to be set up in accordance with the Mauritius Agricultural Marketing Act, which would be mandated to annually review the purchase price of by-catch fish and submit its recommendations to the Board of the AMB; and (d) penalties in terms of fines not exceeding Rs1,000 and imprisonment of up to 12 months for offences. The regulations aim to regulate dealings in by-catch fish and are aligned with the recommendation of the Commission of Inquiry on Drug Trafficking. The Mauritius Agricultural Marketing (Price of Controlled Products) (By-catch Fish) (Amendment) Regulations 2004 and the Mauritius Agricultural Marketing (Controlled Products) Regulations 2013 would be revoked following the coming into operation of the Mauritius Agricultural Marketing (Controlled Products) Regulations 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the promulgation of the Indian Ocean Rim Association (Privileges and Immunities) Regulations 2025 under the International Organizations and Conferences (Privileges and Immunities) Act. The Regulations provide for privileges and immunities such as immunity from legal process, inviolability of archives and property of the Indian Ocean Rim Association (IORA) and protection of premises of the IORA Secretariat, amongst others.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the opening of the Embassy of Mauritius in Tokyo, Japan.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the Department for Continental Shelf, Maritime Zones Administration and Exploration (CSMZAE) and the Institute of Geology, Department of Earth System Sciences of the Universität Hamburg, Republic of Germany. The MoU aims to create a framework of collaboration between CSMZAE and the Institute of Geology, regarding Marine Scientific Research in the Exclusive Economic Zone of Mauritius. The areas of cooperation, under the MoU, include the following – (a) development and implementation of joint research; (b) organising joint research cruise; (c) organising joint scientific meetings; (d) distribution and sharing of scientific information resulting from marine scientific research and surveys; (e) education opportunities and exchange of personnel, including high-level qualified researchers and expertise in scientific and exploratory expeditions; (f) exchange of technical information; and (g) access to infrastructures and research platforms within joint projects.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the study of coastal risks undertaken in Mauritius and Rodrigues by the Bureau de Recherches Géologiques et Minières, with financial assistance to the tune of EUR1 million from the Agence Française de Développement. The objectives of the project were to assess the risks associated with coastal erosion and coastal submersion for Mauritius and Rodrigues as well as produce Geographical Information System maps for the current period and for incremental impacts of climate change up to the years 2050 and 2100. The project started in June 2022. Based on findings, the Study has concluded that coastal hazards will increase in the future due to climate change and will have a greater impact on coastal areas. Tourist facilities, residential areas, economic sectors and strategic energy sites will be at risk. Accordingly, the Study has revealed that a number of measures, including the use of nature- based solutions, will have to be implemented with a view to reducing the vulnerability of the coastal zone. The Report and maps will be shared with stakeholders.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in the 1st Global SME Ministerial Meeting held in Johannesburg, South Africa. The International Trade Centre, which is a joint agency of the World Trade Organization and the United Nations based in Geneva, partnered with South Africa, under the country's G20 Presidency, to host the first-ever Global SME Ministerial Meeting. The initiative aimed to raise the political importance of SME development to a new level, by bringing countries to form a new platform for developing the growth of the sector and tackling the issues that impede its full potential. The Meeting centred around the theme "Navigating New Business Frontiers", focusing on how SMEs can drive economic transformation in their countries by enabling three key areas, namely - (i) Access to Finance; (ii) Digital Connectivity; and (iii) Green Transition. During the event, the Minister intervened at the Ministerial Roundtable and presented the perspectives of Mauritius on the theme "Green and inclusive growth for SMEs – climate, nature, circularity and sustainability". He explained how Mauritius is supporting SMEs on the green journey, through innovative policies, financial incentives, capacity-building programmes, and strategic partnerships. At the end of the Meeting, the "Call to Action" document was endorsed by participating Ministers, which emphasised the urgency of finding shared solutions to challenges that SMEs are facing around the world. In the margins of the Global SME Ministerial Meeting, the Minister had the opportunity to meet - (a) Hon Stella Tembisa Ndabeni, Minister of Small Business Development of South Africa; (b) Hon Dr Jumoke Oduwole, Minister of Industry, Trade and Investment of Nigeria; (c) Senator the Hon Lisa Cummins, Minister of Energy and Business of Barbados; and (d) Mrs Pamela Coke-Hamilton, Executive Director of the International Trade Centre.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Ministerial Segment of the 20th Ordinary Session of the African Ministerial Conference on the Environment (AMCEN-20) in Nairobi, Kenya. The Ministerial Conference on the Environment was convened under the theme "Four Decades of Environmental Action in Africa: Reflecting on the Past and Imagining the Future" and its overall objective was to strengthen collaboration to implement national, regional and global environmental frameworks. During the Ministerial Segment, delegates adopted decisions on, inter alia – (a) ocean governance in Africa; (b) strengthening chemicals and waste governance; (c) climate change; and (d) biodiversity. Decisions were also taken on Africa's priorities for the years 2025-2027 and the Tripoli Declaration on "Environmental Action in Africa: Reflecting on the Past and Imagining the Future" was adopted. In his intervention during the Ministerial dialogues, the Junior Minister laid emphasis on access to finance which remains a major challenge. He urged for a transformation of the global financial architecture with flexible modalities for rapid resource mobilisation in response to climate disasters. The need for Africa to build a unified and strategic implementation approach for a collective participation was underscored to ensure that the Agreement on the Conservation and Sustainable Use of Marine Biological Diversity of Areas Beyond National Jurisdiction (BBNJ) delivered on its promise of equitable access, environmental protection and resilient ocean economies.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the 47th Ordinary Session of the Executive Council of the African Union (AU) in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea, which was held under the AU's Theme of the Year for 2025, namely "Justice for Africans and People of African Descent through Reparations". During his intervention at the Ordinary Session, the Junior Minister took the opportunity to reiterate, on behalf of Mauritius, deep gratitude to all AU Member States and the AU Commission for their unwavering support in Mauritius' long-standing struggle to complete its decolonisation process and the obtention of its rightful sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities to be organised to mark World Humanitarian Day 2025. World Humanitarian Day is celebrated on 19 August each year to honour and recognise the dedicated efforts of humanitarian workers worldwide, raise awareness about their crucial role, and advocate for their safety and protection as they risk their lives to help others in need. To mark World Humanitarian Day 2025, the following activities will be organised - (a) an Awareness Session/Workshop on International Humanitarian Law, with resource persons from the Mauritius Prison Service, the Mauritius Police Force, the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Centre and the Mauritius Fire and Rescue Service. The target audiences will be Police Officers, Prison Officers, Fire Fighters and representatives of the International Humanitarian Law Committee members; and (b) a Blood Donation with the collaboration of the National Blood Transfusion Service.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting-Friday_01_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Mauritius Meat Authority Board, with Mr Muhammad Aniis Ibné Mustapha Rostom as part-time Chairperson; (b) Board of the Irrigation Authority, with Mr Salil Roy as part-time Chairperson; (c) Executive Committee of the Senior Citizens Council, with Mr Narraidoo Gava Naraïdoo, OSK, as part-time Chairperson; (d) Board of the Mauritius Renewable Energy Agency, with Dr Khalil Elahee as part-time Chairperson; (e) Board of the Manufacturing Sector Workers Welfare Fund, with Mr Anas Moussa Ismael Rawat as part-time Chairperson; (f) Board of Directors of Polytechnics Mauritius Ltd, with Dr Tomal Chadeea as part-time Chairperson; (g) Board of Trustees of the Conservatoire National de Musique François Mitterrand Trust Fund, with Mr Allan Marimootoo as part-time Chairperson; and (h) Mauritius Museums Board, with Mr Shiam Persand as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the report of the Inter-Ministerial Committee set up under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister and comprising the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection, the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives, the Junior Minister of Finance and the Financial Secretary to look into the strategy to be adopted for the sustainable utilisation of the Price Stabilisation Fund. The members of the Inter- Ministerial Committee will hold a Press Conference on Saturday 16 August 2025 on the recommendations contained in the report.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note, with concern, of an alleged case of sexual assault involving two minor children of British Nationality in a hotel in the northern part of Mauritius. An inquiry has been initiated by the Police and the Child Development Unit, and two officers of the Child Development Unit have been interdicted in the wake of the investigation. The Commissioner of Police will appoint a special team headed by Mr Daniel Monvoisin, Adviser, to conduct the inquiry.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent participation of the Mauritian athletes in the 13th edition of the Jeux de la Commission de la Jeunesse et des Sports de l'Océan Indien held from 01 to 10 August 2025 in Seychelles, and commended the excellent performance of our young athletes. Mauritius secured a total of 114 medals including 35 gold, 35 silver, and 44 bronze. Additionally, Mauritius secured one gold, one silver, and one bronze medal in Esports. The games also witnessed the setting of two national records by – (a) Mr Yesh Bonomally, who completed the 800-metres athletics event in one minute and 55.2 seconds; and (b) Mr Lucas Chan Chuen, who completed the 100-metre swimming (freestyle) event in 53.26 seconds. The Ministry of Youth and Sports is currently working on a Cash Reward Scheme for the Medallists.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Public Service and Innovation Bill into the National Assembly. The objects of the Bill are to – (a) provide for the establishment of the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Public Service and Innovation as a national institution; (b) provide for continuous professional development and learning and research; (c) hold examinations and grant degrees, including certificates, diplomas, honorary degrees and other academic awards to Public Officers; and (d) ensure that every person presently employed at the Civil Service College, Mauritius shall be entitled to be transferred to the permanent and pensionable establishment of the Institute, with unbroken period of service.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the public release of the Land Drainage Master Plan and to outline the key measures to be undertaken to address flood risks and improve the resilience of vulnerable communities and infrastructure. The Land Drainage Master Plan was commissioned in 2022 by the Land Drainage Authority with financial and technical assistance from the Agence Française de Développement. The primary objective of the Land Drainage Master Plan is to reduce the vulnerability of communities and critical infrastructure to increasing flood risks arising from climate change, through an integrated and strategic approach to the management of drainage networks across Mauritius. In February 2025, Government approved the setting up of a Technical Committee to undertake an independent review of the Land Drainage Master Plan with the aim of ensuring that the Plan is technically robust, aligned with national development priorities, and incorporates stakeholder input as well as international commitments on climate resilience and disaster risk reduction. The Technical Committee has consequently proposed that priority be given to the following measures – (a) enhancement of risk mapping; (b) social impact assessment and awareness programmes; (c) development and operationalisation of safety protocols; (d) fast-tracked land acquisition and relocation assistance; and (e) timely implementation of proposed drainage interventions. The Land Drainage Authority will undertake a national dissemination and sensitisation exercise to ensure effective implementation and stakeholder buy-in.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the list of Public Holidays for the Year 2026 as follows – New Year Thursday 01 January New Year Friday 02 January Abolition of Slavery Sunday 01 February Thaipoosam Cavadee Sunday 01 February Maha Shivaratree Sunday 15 February Chinese Spring Festival Tuesday 17 February Independence Day and Republic Day Thursday 12 March Ugaadi Thursday 19 March Eid-Ul-Fitr Saturday 21 March Labour Day Friday 01 May Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary Saturday 15 August Ganesh Chaturthi Wednesday 16 September Arrival of Indentured Labourers Monday 02 November Divali Sunday 08 November Christmas Friday 25 December The exact date of this festival is subject to confirmation as its celebration depends on the visibility of the moon.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Animal Diseases (Veterinary Laboratories) Regulations 2025. The aim of the Regulations is to strengthen the regulatory activities of the Division of Veterinary Services of the Ministry of Agro- Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries to allow the registration and monitoring of the private veterinary laboratories that are involved in the diagnosis and research of animal diseases. The Animal Diseases (Veterinary Laboratories) Regulations 2025 will provide, inter alia, for the following - (a) any person who intends to operate a veterinary laboratory shall make an application for a licence; (b) the licence shall be valid for a period of one year and may be renewed for a further period of one year; (c) in case of lost, defaced or destroyed licence, the Division of Veterinary Services shall issue same marked as "DUPLICATE", against a payment of Rs10,000; (d) every licensee shall keep a register to record particulars such as name of veterinary surgeon responsible for validating test results, experiment or testing carried out, amongst others; (e) the designated personnel responsible for a veterinary laboratory shall ensure that all employees adhere to the conditions specified in the licence and the laboratory manual approved by the Division of Veterinary Services; and (f) any person who contravenes these regulations shall commit an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding Rs1,000 and to imprisonment not exceeding one year.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that with a view to safeguarding the long-term viability of the Mauritian tea industry and to revitalise the tea sector, an increase in the quantum of the winter allowance from Rs4.50/kg of tea leaves to Rs6/kg will be applicable. This increase will help address a number of challenges being faced by the sector, such as - (a) rising production costs resulting from an increase in the cost of fertilizers, labour and fuel; (b) a decrease in the volume of tea leaves plucked due to seasonal yield constraints, a situation which is exacerbated by climatic changes; and (c) the abandonment of tea plots or their conversion to alternate crops.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Government of Mauritius signing the United Nations Convention against Cybercrime which aims at strengthening international cooperation for combating certain crimes committed by means of information and communications technology systems and for the sharing of evidence in electronic form of serious crimes. The purposes of the Convention are to – (a) promote and strengthen measures to prevent and combat cybercrime more efficiently and effectively; (b) promote, facilitate and strengthen international cooperation in preventing and combating cybercrime; and (c) promote, facilitate and support technical assistance and capacity-building to prevent and combat cybercrime, in particular for the benefit of developing countries.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation submitting the candidature of Mauritius in the Plenipotentiary Conference to be held in Qatar, in November 2026, for re-election for the term 2027- 2030 as member of the Governing Council of the International Telecommunication Union Council.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the strategic directions underlying the Blueprint for the Transformation of the Mauritian Education Sector, which is currently under preparation, following recommendations made during Les Assises de l'Éducation 2025. The national consultations brought together a wide range of stakeholders, including educators, learners, parents, members of the civil society, employers, academia as well as international development partners. These exchanges yielded deep insights into the strengths, weaknesses, and aspirations of the education sector in the Republic of Mauritius. During the consultations, there was broad consensus that a fundamental transformation of the education system is necessary in order to meet the global evolving demands of a knowledge-driven and human-centred society, as indicated in the Sustainable Development Goal 4 for the education sector. The transformation proposed in the Blueprint is geared towards a twenty-first century education system that fosters lifelong learning, inclusive development, and active citizenship. The document is intended to serve as a national reference document and implementation tool to guide policy, planning, and investment in the education sector over the next five years and beyond. The document will more specifically outline a comprehensive set of reforms aimed at modernising and strengthening the education system through a holistic approach across the relevant sub-sectors, that is the pre-primary, primary and secondary levels. The proposed reforms will mainly revolve around the three core pillars of the Mauritian education sector, namely - (a) curriculum reform; (b) reinforcement of support systems; and (c) improvement of the legal framework governing education.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the hosting of the 7th Steering Committee of the Commonwealth Climate Finance Access Hub (CCFAH) in Mauritius on 11 and 12 September 2025. The Steering Committee Meeting will pursue the following objectives in line with the agreed Terms of Reference - (a) review achievements, progress, challenges and opportunities for accelerating the scale and impact of the CCFAH; (b) consider and agree on key operational changes for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the CCFAH and its alignment to the 2025-20230 Commonwealth Secretariat Strategy; (c) discuss proposals for enhancing the sustainability of the CCFAH into the future; and (d) celebrate the 10th anniversary of the CCFAH and assert its place as an important vehicle of the Commonwealth Secretariat.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the organisation of the 4th Intergenerational Dialogue Forum in Mauritius by the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa, in collaboration with the Ministry of Youth and Sports from 01 to 03 September 2025. The Forum aims at bringing together the youth and the older generation from the four island member states, namely Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius and Seychelles. The theme for this year's edition is 'Bridging Generations: Fostering Solidarity, Innovation and Inclusion in Small Island States'.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a delegation from the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change led by the Junior Minister of Environment is currently participating in the Second Part of the Fifth Session of the Intergovernmental Negotiating Committee on Plastic Pollution of the United Nations Environment Programme, in Geneva. This Intergovernmental Negotiating Committee is mandated to develop an international legally binding instrument on Plastic Pollution. In the wake of the negotiations, the delegation led by the Junior Minister of Environment has participated actively in meetings of the Alliance of Small Island States for the adoption of a unified stand during the negotiations, emphasising the need for a treaty that addresses the unique challenges faced by Small Island Developing States. The Mauritian delegation also held a bilateral meeting with the Seychelles delegation.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Board of the Agricultural Marketing Board, with Mrs Meela Devi Ramlochun-Bunwaree as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_15_August_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of the following 10 members to the Environment and Land Use Appeal Tribunal– (a) Mr Cheung Kwet Fa Cheung Kai Suet; (b) Mr Raj Kumar Jugroo; (c) Mr Akash Issur; (d) Mr Shardhanand Beehuspoteea; (e) Mr Gowtamsingh Chekhori; (f) Mr Vishwanath Bissessur; (g) Ms Ruwaydah Ruhomutally; (h) Ms Yushreen Shakila Bibi Uteene; (i) Mrs M. L. Fabiola Monty; and (j) Mrs Marie Darlane Utile.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Commission of Experts, set up to make recommendations to reform the various pillars of the pension system, including the revamping of the National Pensions Fund (NPF) to replace the Contribution Sociale Généralisée (CSG), to be constituted as follows - (a) Mr Nureshkumar Prayag, Actuary as Chairperson; (b) Mr Bernard Yen, Actuary; (c) Mr Azad Jeetun, Economist; (d) Mrs Verena Tandrayen-Ragoobur, Economist; (e) Mr Sattar Jackaria, Actuary; (f) Mr Yvan Legris, Actuary; and (g) Ms Li Fa (Aimé) Cheung Kai Suet, Statistician. According to its Terms of Reference which have been worked out in consultation with the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank, the Commission of Experts will review the Portable Retirement Gratuity Fund, the National Savings Fund, the Statutory Bodies Pension Funds, the Civil Service Defined Benefits and Defined Contribution Pension Schemes, the Family Protection Schemes as well as pension schemes for public officials. The Commission will look into the revamping of the NPF to replace the CSG. In addition, the Commission will assist in defining a long-term strategy for the financial sustainability of the pension system and will provide - (a) an Actuarial and Fiscal Sustainability Report, showing the long-term projections of pension liabilities and expenditures over a 20-30-year horizon; and (b) a Cost-Benefit Analysis by comparing the existing contributory pension system with potential alternative models. Cabinet has further agreed to the setting up of a Steering Committee on Pension Reform which will provide guidance to the Commission on the strategic direction and policy priorities for the reform of the pension system; and assess and act on the recommendations of the Commission, under the chair of the Prime Minister and the following members - (a) the Deputy Prime Minister; (b) the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries; (c) the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (d) the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning; (e) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (f) the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; (g) the Junior Minister of Finance; and (h) the Junior Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that in accordance with section 87 of the Constitution, the Prime Minister has advised the President of the Republic to appoint the following Minister Counsellor/Deputy High Commissioner as Ambassador - (a) Mrs Gajjaluxmi Mootoosamy; and (b) Mr Nilkanthsing Jagarnath. The Prime Minister has also advised the President of the Republic to appoint Mr Mohummad Riad Parvez Hullemuth as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of the Republic of Mauritius to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, with residence in Riyadh.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the report of the Committee of Inquiry set up to look into allegations regarding the allocation of Hadj 2025 visas by the Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund will be made public. The Ministry of Arts and Culture will follow up closely with the Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund for the implementation of recommendations and possible disciplinary and legal proceedings as per the advice of the Attorney-General's Office.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the proclamation of section 31, sub-sections (1) and (3), of the Animal Welfare Act, which relates to the registration of dogs. The effective date for the proclamation shall be as from 01 October 2025. The implementation of the regulatory framework for the compulsory registration would allow the Mauritius Society for Animal Welfare to promote sustainable control of dog population in Mauritius, enhance responsible pet ownership, reduce animal cruelty and minimise health hazards associated with stray animals.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Land (Duties and Taxes) (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2025 which will exempt the beneficiary of a housing unit of the National Housing Development Company Ltd from the payment of registration duty and the tax on transfer of leasehold rights in State land, provided that the value of the property does not exceed Rs5 million. The current exemption threshold is set at Rs2 million, with an annual review based on the construction price index.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the promulgation of the following regulations which provide for the implementation of measures mentioned in Budget 2025-2026 relating to revenue laws as well as other policy and technical measures - (a) Customs (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2025; (b) Excise (Amendment) Regulations 2025; (c) Value Added Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2025; (d) Excise (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2025; (e) Customs Tariff (Amendment of Schedule) (No. 4) Regulations 2025; (f) Companies (Payment of Fees to Registrar) (Amendment) Regulations 2025; (g) Limited Liability Partnerships (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2025; (h) Limited Partnerships (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2025; (i) Foundations (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2025; and (j) Income Tax (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2025.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Control of Imports) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 2025 with a view to updating the list of Competent Authorities that are mandated to certify inspection of second-hand motor vehicles prior to shipment.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) 3.0 for the Republic of Mauritius and its submission to the Secretariat of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change. The NDC 3.0 represents a critical opportunity to raise mitigation and adaptation ambitions to the highest degree possible in line with national circumstances and create enabling conditions to accelerate implementation, attract climate finance and establish ownership across sectoral ministries, private sectors and the public at large. The NDC 3.0 highlights the commitments of Mauritius to set out the required framework to ensure a just socio-ecological transition in the country. As a Small Island Developing State, adaptation to climate change impacts is a priority for Mauritius. The adaptation commitments in the NDC 3.0, aligned with the Global Goal on Adaptation, has proposed specific measures to build resilience in vital sectors, namely - (a) water; (b) coastal zone management; (c) tourism; (d) infrastructure; (e) disaster risk reduction; (f) agriculture; (g) terrestrial ecosystem and biodiversity; (h) fisheries and marine ecosystem; (i) health; and (j) gender and social inclusion.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the 38th edition of the Global Financial Centres Index (GFCI 38) which provides evaluation of 120 financial centres around the world. Mauritius has improved its position in the GFCI 38, now ranking 52nd globally and has climbed 6 places from its 58th position in the GFCI 37 (March 2025) and 60th position in the GFCI 36 (September 2024). Furthermore, Mauritius has also made significant progress in its score rating increasing by 13 points. Regionally, Mauritius has strengthened its standing, now ranking 3rd in the Middle East and Africa. Mauritius continues to be recognised among the 15 financial centres most likely to gain significance over the next two to three years.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the publication of the General Notice providing for the date of entry into force of the Protocol amending the Double Taxation Avoidance Convention (DTAC) with the Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh. The main objective of the Protocol is to make the DTAC between Mauritius and Bangladesh compliant with the Base Erosion and Profit Shifting minimum standards of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and increase the flow of investments between the two countries.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Financial Crimes Commission of the Republic of Mauritius and the Basel Institute on Governance. The objectives of the Memorandum of Understanding are, inter alia, to - (a) strengthen the efforts of the Financial Crimes Commission to identify and recover assets which have been obtained through corruption, organised crime, abuse of power, financial or economic crime, drug related offences or other forms of criminal activity; (b) facilitate the development of plans and strategies to effectively investigate offences of corruption, abuse of power, financing of drug dealing and financial or economic crime; and (c) facilitate the obtaining of intelligence and evidence from other jurisdictions, for use in support of domestic investigations, prosecutions or confiscation/ forfeiture applications.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the National Audit Office of the Republic of Mauritius and the National Audit Office of the Republic of Gambia. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding is to create an avenue for the provision of technical assistance and exchange programmes between both institutions to strengthen professional capacity in the fields of - (a) public sector methodologies including emerging areas; and (b) the accrual reporting system of the international public sector accounting standards to staff of the National Audit Office (Gambia).

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised to mark World Heart Day 2025, which is observed globally every year on 29 September to raise awareness about heart diseases, which remain the leading cause of death worldwide. The theme for World Heart Day 2025 is "Don't Miss a Beat," which highlights the need for ongoing heart health awareness, encouraging individuals to pay attention to warning signs, maintain healthy habits, and get regular checkups. To commemorate World Heart Day 2025, the Ministry of Health and Wellness will organise the following activities - (a) launching of the report on the new cardiac registry; (b) celebration of the achievement of the cardiac unit of Dr A. G. Jeetoo Hospital for winning the African Outstanding Kaizen Award for Primary PCI, organised by the Embassy of Japan; (c) screening for Non-Communicable Diseases; (d) demonstration of physical activity; (e) radio talks and TV programmes on the theme of World Heart Day, "Don't Miss a Beat"; (f) talks and sensitisation campaigns at Community Health Centres, Women Centres, Social Welfare Centres, schools, and urban/rural community settings on the risk factors of heart diseases and preventive measures; and (g) exhibitions on healthy lifestyles and diets in collaboration with relevant stakeholders.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Accreditation Service (MAURITAS) will apply to the African Accreditation Cooperation (AFRAC) and, thereafter, to the International Accreditation Forum (IAF), to extend its scope of international recognition to cover the accreditation of conformity assessment bodies offering Information Security Management System (ISMS) certification schemes. MAURITAS is the national accreditation body of Mauritius and provides a national, unified service for the accreditation of laboratories, inspection bodies and certification bodies. MAURITAS draws its international recognition through its membership to the regional and global accreditation organisations such as AFRAC and IAF, respectively. Accreditation granted by MAURITAS is a formal recognition that an organisation is technically competent to perform specific tasks.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status of social housing projects being implemented by the New Social Living Development Ltd and the National Housing Development Company Ltd as follows - (a) the construction of 774 housing units has been completed; (b) the construction of 2,357 housing units is expected to be completed by December 2025; (c) the construction of 5,244 housing units is expected to be completed by July 2026; and (d) the construction of 70 housing units has not yet started on one site. The Ministry of Housing and Lands is in the process of identifying suitable sites for new housing projects.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation will hold a workshop on Cybersecurity in Mauritius with the assistance of Indian Officials from the Maharashtra Cyber Department who will share their knowledge, insights and experience with the objective of enhancing the cyber security eco-system in Mauritius. Representatives of the Prime Minister's Office, the Mauritius Police Force, the Attorney-General's Office as well as officers of the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation, the Computer Emergency Response Team (Mauritius) and the Information and Communication Technologies Authority will be invited to participate in the workshop.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities in the high-level delegation led by the Prime Minister to India, to explore possibilities of cooperation in the energy sector. The Minister had the opportunity to, inter alia, hold bilateral meetings with the Union Minister of Power and Housing and Urban Affairs, the Minister of Power and Renewable Energy, the Chairman and Managing Director of the National Thermal Power Corporation Limited as well as the Director General of the International Solar Alliance. In line with the Memorandum of Understanding between the Government of Mauritius and the Government of India in the field of power sector, both sides underscored the importance of strengthening bilateral cooperation in the energy sector. Priority areas include energy security, renewable energy development, power generation, transmission, grid management, and capacity building.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Tertiary Education, Science and Research in the Global Smart Education Conference 2025 on the theme "Human-AI Collaboration: Reshaping Education for the Future", in China. In addition to his participation in the Ministerial Roundtable, the Minister chaired a dedicated AI-panel at the conference. He also delivered an academic presentation entitled: "The Mission and Strategic Transformation of Universities in the Age of AI". The Minister visited the Huawei ICT Academy and the Alibaba Cloud Academy and engaged in discussions on equipping university students with industry- relevant ICT and AI skills through micro-credential courses.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Tertiary Education, Science and Research in the Eleventh Pan- Commonwealth Forum on Open Learning (PCF II) Botswana on the theme "Innovative Open Education: Fostering Resilient Societies for Sustainable Economic Development". The Minister chaired three Ministerial panels and facilitated strategic deliberations on the particular needs of small states in the digital education sphere. He also participated in the Ministerial Panel on employability and credentialing where he shared Mauritius' pioneering work in micro-credentials, national skills development programme, employability skills, free education and free transport. During the PCF II, the Minister was conferred an Honorary Fellow of the Commonwealth of Learning. The Forum also served as a platform for Mauritius to officially launch the National Open Educational Resources Repository of Mauritius. The Pan Commonwealth Forum concluded with the release of the PCF11 Gaborone Statement which advocates for "One Resilient Common Future", supporting and prioritising a series of collective actions.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation in the high-level delegation headed by the Prime Minister to India. On the sidelines of the high-level meetings and official events, the Minister had several meetings to discuss projects that have been highlighted in the Digital Transformation Blueprint 2025-2029 for Mauritius, with namely the Chief Minister of Maharashtra; officials of the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team; the Centre for Development of Telematics; and the National Informatics Centre. Discussions were centered around, inter alia - (a) support in fighting cyber frauds, scams and other online crimes by using preventive methods and software; (b) establishment of guidelines for proper usage of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and creating public awareness on AI as well as further upgrading of the online crime reporting system; and (c) implementation of an early warning system for better communication and preparation against natural disasters.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Arts and Culture in the 9th International Dharma-Dhamma Conference in India. The Conference was organised by the India Foundation, in collaboration with Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Open University on the theme "Karma, Reincarnation, Transmigration and The Avatar Doctrine". The objective of the conference was to bring together religious, political and thought leaders to ponder over building a philosophical framework for the emerging new world order. The Minister addressed the inaugural session of the Conference, where he underlined Mauritius' identity as a multicultural and multireligious society and highlighted the importance of dialogue and shared values in strengthening international cooperation. In the margins of the Conference, the Minister held discussions on possible avenues of collaboration with cultural and academic representatives, more particularly in the field of music and dance through capacity-building and training programmes in Indian classical dance and folk music with possibilities for masterclasses in Mauritius.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_26_September 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the - (a) appointment of Mr Meghanathan Pillay, CSK as Executive Chairperson of Airport Holdings Limited; and (b) reconstitution of the Professional Architects' Council for the period 2025-2027.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee to look into the strategy to be adopted for the sustainable utilisation of the Price Stabilisation Fund, which has been announced in the Budget Speech 2025-2026, to protect the purchasing power of the population. The Committee will be chaired by the Deputy Prime Minister and comprise – (i) the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; (ii) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (iii) the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; (iv) the Junior Minister of Finance; and (v) the Financial Secretary.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note with concern of the case of the newborn baby found abandoned at Stanley, Rose-Hill. The Ministry of Health and Wellness and the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare are working jointly to provide immediate assistance and care to the baby.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of a Rules-Based Work Permit System, which will be a new revamped system for the issue of work permits, as announced in the Budget Speech 2025-2026. The Rules-Based Work Permit System will be founded on the following three prerequisites - (a) a Standardised Job Classification and Codification, through the establishment of a system to record job grades and all relevant details as outlined in various Remuneration Orders. The system will also generate a template contract, preventing employers from modifying job requirements or conditions of employment, with a view to ensuring equity and discouraging exploitation; (b) an Accredited Employer Framework for the accreditation of employers to ensure mandatory compliance with relevant legislation; and (c) a Labour Market Information System, which will be a single Employment Portal with advanced AI features to assist employers and job seekers. This will provide employers with a transparent mode of advertisement, whereby job seekers will be notified of job openings. A Steering Committee chaired by the Prime Minister's Office and comprising representatives from the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations, the Passport and Immigration Office and the Economic Development Board is being set up to coordinate the active involvement of various stakeholders and ensure implementation of the System. Once the System is operationalised, applications for work permits would transit through the National E-Licensing System. The Non-Citizens (Employment Restriction) Act will also be amended to allow the issuance of a combined Work and Residence Permit.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of Government's ongoing efforts and strategy to advocate for the renewal of the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA), which will expire on 30 September 2025, with a view to coming up with a new form of partnership with the US Administration. These include - (a) holding bilateral meetings with US Authorities at the forthcoming USA-SADC Dialogue in Madagascar; (b) devising a continental strategy on AGOA renewal, including a meeting between interested African countries and the US Administration prior to the AGOA; and (c) engaging with the Bureau of African Affairs of the US State Department to follow up on the discussions regarding AGOA renewal.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the participation of Mauritius in the Call for Proposals to host a Green Climate Fund (GCF) Regional Office in Mauritius. The GCF, headquartered in South Korea since 2013, is the world's largest climate fund dedicated to developing countries. GCF currently manages a portfolio of USD 18 billion to support the transition to low-emission and climate-resilient societies. GCF has so far financed around 300 projects, through grants, concessional financing or guarantees, across more than 140 developing countries for both public and private entities. A GCF regional presence in Mauritius will offer significant advantages to the country by enabling faster and more effective engagement with Government and the private sector on climate finance. This proximity will better support the preparation of project proposals and improve access to finance. It will also attract green investment, promote innovation in sustainable sectors, and generate economic spillovers such as job creation and increased demand for professional services. Collectively, these benefits will support Mauritius to establish itself as a regional climate action hub, play a leading role in climate resilience and sustainable development goals, and influence regional climate finance strategies.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning has submitted an Expression of Interest (EOI) for Mauritius to host the Africa Credit Rating Agency (AfCRA). This initiative follows the African Union's endorsement, at its 44th Ordinary Session in July 2024, of the establishment of AfCRA as a private sector-driven, independent, and self- sustaining credit rating agency. AfCRA is intended to provide more balanced, context-aware, and accurate financial assessments of African economies, complementing or offering an alternative to existing global agencies. The African Peer Review Mechanism (APRM), under the African Union, has been mandated to oversee the creation of AfCRA and all African Union Member States were invited to submit EOIs, highlighting the competitiveness of their jurisdiction in areas such as political stability, regulatory environment, infrastructure, and human capital. Mauritius' submission showcases its robust legal and institutional frameworks, commitment to transparency, and alignment with pan-African financial integration. Mauritius stands to benefit from increased international visibility, greater investor confidence, access to affordable credit ratings for local businesses, job creation, and opportunities for skills transfer and capacity building. In a positive development, the APRM Secretariat has confirmed that its CEO, Ambassador Marie-Antoinnette Rose-Quatre, will be visiting Mauritius on 03 and 04 September 2025 to engage with Government and key financial sector stakeholders on Mauritius' candidacy and future collaboration.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the national efforts to prepare for the next Mutual Evaluation by the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), scheduled for 2027. The evaluation is a critical opportunity for Mauritius to reaffirm its position as a credible and compliant international financial centre. The importance of sustained inter-agency collaboration and timely implementation of measures to ensure Mauritius maintains its international standing was also reiterated. Since its exit from the FATF grey list in 2021, Mauritius has launched a comprehensive stock-take of its legal, regulatory, and institutional Anti-Money Laundering/Combating Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT) frameworks. Key milestones include – (a) completion of the second National Risk Assessment and development of a new National AML/CFT Strategy and Action Plan; (b) ongoing risk assessments on legal persons, non-profit organisations, proliferation financing, and virtual assets; (c) delivery of targeted training programmes with international partners including the Eastern and Southern Africa Anti-Money Laundering Group (ESAAMLG) and the European Union (EU) Global Facility; (d) initiation of a Centralised Information Management System and new enforcement regulations to enhance supervisory powers; and (e) engagement of an international consultant to conduct a mid-term independent review of the national AML/CFT framework. Cabinet has also taken note that Mauritius remains actively engaged with ESAAMLG and FATF processes and is committed to implementing required reforms. A support package of EUR500,000 has also been secured from the EU to assist national authorities.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation initiating action for the preparation of a new legislation, namely the Electronic Identity Management Bill, to provide for an appropriate legal framework and facilitate electronic identity management by regulating electronic identification, identity credentials and the reliability thereof. The proposed legislation will govern the use of the National Authentication Framework, commonly known as "MauPass", which provides citizens and eligible users a secure and user- friendly single sign-on platform to access many Government digital services and has become a critical infrastructure for digital government. The key elements of the proposed Bill are, inter alia, voluntary use of Identity Management services and its legal recognition, the obligations of Identity Management Service Providers including rules for credential lifecycle management, authentication mechanisms, and system reliability, data breach response and obligations of subscribers, amongst others.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Cybersecurity and Cybercrime (Critical Information Infrastructure Designation) Regulations 2025. The Regulations aim to establish a regulatory framework for designating and managing critical information infrastructure within identified key sectors, namely – (a) Financial Services (Banking and Non-Banking); (b) Government Services; (c) Information, Communication and Technology & Broadcasting; (d) Energy and Water Supply; and (e) Transport Industry. By effectively designating and managing critical information infrastructure, Government will be better placed to – (a) support dependency on key digital services through strong cybersecurity resilience, regulatory frameworks, and coordinated national policies to ensure the continuous availability and security of these essential digital services; and (b) provide the necessary protective framework against terrorist attacks through assessment of risks, having in place appropriate intelligence, physical and cyber safeguards as well as emergency planning.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in the field of Sustainable Development between the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change and the Ministry of Environment and Energy Security of the Italian Republic. The Memorandum of Understanding aims at strengthening bilateral cooperation for sustainable development in the aftermath of the MV Wakashio oil spill, as well as international cooperation to contribute to the mitigation and removal of the damage on the marine and coastal environment. It also aims to restore and rehabilitate the ecosystems and livelihoods affected by oil pollution as well as set up, strengthen and maintain an appropriate array of measures and procedures to monitor, prevent and mitigate major accidents of marine oil pollution and hazards such as chemicals and plastic pollution. The joint initiatives that will be undertaken under the Memorandum of Understanding, include the following - (a) development of effective mitigation and adaptation measures, by enhancing resilience to climate change and supporting the implementation, monitoring and reporting of the Nationally Determined Contributions; (b) protection of biodiversity and reduction of environmental degradation; (c) development of circular economy in order to promote sustainable production and consumption and solid waste management; (d) development of public education and awareness campaigns on global climate change and sustainable development; (e) training and capacity building for all actors involved in the management of crisis; and (f) improvement of monitoring systems, risk assessment and disaster management, and prompt intervention with adequate means and equipment.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research signing a Memorandum of Understanding with the International Science, Technology and Innovation Centre for South-South Cooperation (ISTIC), under the auspices of UNESCO Kuala Lumpur. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding is to foster collaboration between the Ministry and ISTIC and formalise a strategic partnership rooted in mutual interests in advancing science, technology and innovation. The overarching objective is to foster capacity building, promote innovation-driven development and support knowledge transfer in a manner that aligns with the global agenda for sustainable development and the aspirations of the global south. Avenues of cooperation to be explored include – (i) promoting interest in utilisation of research and innovation knowledge activities in their respective organisations; (ii) encouraging and conducting capacity building, including joint research supervision and training; (iii) organising symposia, conferences, short courses, and meetings on science, technology and innovation issues; (iv) exchanging expert, scientific knowledge and information; and (v) issuing joint publications pertaining to developments in research at each institution.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the submission of the Ninth Periodic Report of the Republic of Mauritius under the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW), to the CEDAW Committee in Geneva for consideration. The Report addresses the Concluding Observations and Recommendations issued by the CEDAW Committee following Mauritius' last review in 2018 and provides an update on progress made and challenges faced in implementing the Convention. The main areas covered in the Report include – (a) General: Updated demographic and labour statistics disaggregated by gender and progress on gender-sensitive data systems; (b) Awareness & Legal Mechanisms: Training of law enforcement authorities and awareness on women's rights and CEDAW; (c) Legislative Reform: Review of discriminatory provisions; (d) Access to Justice: Legal aid and support for Gender-Based Violence (GBV) survivors and establishment of a GBV Observatory; (e) Institutional Framework: Progress on the implementation of the National Gender Policy (2022–2030) and gender-responsive budgeting; (f) Gender Stereotypes & Harmful Practices: Campaigns and legal reforms to address harmful practices; (g) Gender-Based Violence: Strengthening services and legal frameworks, including through the forthcoming Domestic Abuse Bill; (h) Political Participation: Initiatives to boost women's representation in political and public life; (i) Education: Access to free education and gender-mainstreaming in curricula; (j) Economic Empowerment: Programmes to promote women's employment, entrepreneurship, and close the wage gap; and (k) Health: Expanded maternal health services and provision of sanitary products to vulnerable groups.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_08_August _2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation will chair an Inter-Ministerial Committee to monitor progress regarding the implementation of the Digital Transformation Blueprint 2025-2029. The Committee will comprise the following Ministers – (i) the Minister of Health and Wellness; (ii) the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (iii) the Minister of Land Transport; (iv) the Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare; (iv) the Minister of Education and Human Resource; and (vi) the Minister of Public Service and Administrative Reforms. The Inter-Ministerial Committee which has been mandated to monitor, coordinate, and guide the implementation of the Blueprint, will serve as a critical governance mechanism and ensure that stakeholders move forward efficiently, transparently, and with accountability across the different sectors involved.
