filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Road Traffic (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The main object of the Bill is to make provisions for the re-introduction of the Penalty Points System. Accordingly, with a view to establishing an effective framework to ensure compliance with traffic laws, the Bill – (a) aims to enhance the protection of road users; (b) fosters a culture of responsible driving; and (c) provides for matters connected and related thereto.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the composition of the Constitutional Review Commission is being finalised and that the issue of electoral reform will be examined separately. Cabinet has further noted that the public will be invited to submit proposals on the electoral reform through a communiqué to be issued by the Prime Minister's Office in the days to come. A dedicated desk will be set up at the Prime Minister's Office to receive all proposals.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Local Government (Fees) (Temporary Provisions) Regulations 2025 which provide for occasional fee payable by hawkers for the period 12 December 2025 to 11 January 2026; and to the plan of action to allow hawkers to operate during the festive season. An Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Housing and Lands was set up to look into the activities undertaken by hawkers during the festive season and possible measures to allow them to operate during that period without disrupting normal economic activities and operations. The Inter-Ministerial Committee met on several occasions to analyse the proposals and after discussions, established the General Guiding Principles under which hawkers will be allowed to ply temporarily during the festive period only, that is, from 12 December 2025 to 11 January 2026, against the payment of a daily prescribed fee ranging from Rs150 to Rs500. Only hawkers residing in the jurisdiction covered by the Local Authority will be allowed to operate therein. Appropriate and accessible locations have been identified by the Local Authorities where hawkers will operate. These areas will be equipped with the necessary facilities, including sanitation, security, safety measures against fire and electrical mishaps, and lighting. Uniform structures, namely gazebos, will have to be erected by the hawkers at their own costs to accommodate their wares. Scavenging and cleaning will be carried out on a daily basis immediately after the end of operations, by each Local Authority. Given that Port Louis is the capital city and in view of its specificity, the Municipal City Council of Port Louis will operate a night market from 12 December 2025 to 05 January 2026 between 1800 hours and 0100 hours along Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Street. Stalls will be installed on both sides of the street and vehicles will not be allowed to park alongside the Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Street. Police will monitor the traffic flow. Communiqués will be issued by the respective Local Authorities to inform of arrangements being made for the operation of hawkers during the festive season.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Tripartite Technical Meeting on Additional Remuneration (Salary Compensation) 2026 was held on 26 November 2025 under the chair of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations with representatives of Statistics Mauritius, Ministries, employers' organisations and employees' organisations. Statistics Mauritius, inter alia, informed that the forecasted inflation rate for the year 2025 will be 3.7% and the average monthly household consumption expenditure which was Rs43,400 in 2024 is expected to increase to Rs45,000 in 2025. Additional information sought from Statistics Mauritius at the Tripartite Technical Meeting will be submitted to representatives of employers' organisations and employees' organisations. The Tripartite Committee Meeting will be held under the chair of the Prime Minister next week.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Dheerendra Kumar Dabee, GOSK, as Chairperson of Air Mauritius Ltd.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to public consultations being held on the review of the mode of admission in secondary schools, for inclusion in the forthcoming Blueprint for the transformation of the Mauritian Education Sector. A communiqué will be issued by the Ministry of Education and Human Resource in that respect.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning issuing drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for the preparation of the Anti-Money Laundering, Combatting the Financing of Terrorism and Countering Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF) (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Amendment) Bill with a view to addressing the technical compliance gaps identified following – (a) recent changes to the Financial Action Task Force Standards; and (b) the publication of the Second National AML/TF Risk Assessment Report in May 2025. The Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning will carry out consultations with all competent authorities, including the Financial Services Commission, the Bank of Mauritius, the Financial Intelligence Unit, the Financial Crimes Commission, the Registrar of Companies, the Registrar of Associations and the law enforcement agencies during the drafting of the Bill.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Safety Requirements) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 which will provide that no gas-fired water heater shall be sold to a consumer unless it is accompanied by – (a) a gas exhaust pipe for external venting; (b) a carbon monoxide detector; (c) clear user instructions in English and French; and (d) a safety warning label in both languages prominently displayed on the packaging. The Regulations will mandate that all gas-fired water heaters placed on the market shall comply with the MS EN 26 standard, which sets the technical and safety benchmarks for such appliances. This requirement will become enforceable six months after the date of publication of the Regulations, so as to provide sufficient time for stakeholders to source compliant gas-fired water heaters, obtain the necessary conformity certification, and exhaust any existing stock that does not meet the prescribed standard.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status of the proposed review of the Disability Pension System. The major reforms that were identified include, inter alia, – (a) the introduction of a hybrid assessment model to replace the 100 per cent medical based model; (b) the introduction of a graduated support model to replace the 'all-or-nothing' model; and (c) the introduction of multi-disciplinary assessment panels to replace the Medical Boards. The comprehensive reform of the Disability Pension model will be implemented over two phases, namely – (a) Phase 1 which will include the updating of Medical Guidelines for Disability Assessment 2016 and the introduction of a graduated support model, whereby those deemed not eligible for the Invalid's Basic Pension could qualify for an Inclusive Living Allowance. The Inclusive Living Allowance will replace the Disability Allowance and its quantum will be revised higher; and (b) Phase 2 which will include the introduction of a hybrid assessment model based on both medical and functional criteria, as well as, the replacement of the current Medical Boards with multi-disciplinary assessment panels.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee, under the chair of the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity, to be responsible to oversee, coordinate and monitor the effective implementation of the 73 recommendations made by the United Nations Committee on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities in its Concluding Observations within the framework of the United Nations Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities (UNCRPD). The Inter-Ministerial Committee will comprise, inter alia, the - (a) Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change; (b) Minister of National Infrastructure; (c) Minister of Health and Wellness; (d) Attorney-General; (e) Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade; (f) Minister of Youth and Sports; (g) Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (h) Minister of Land Transport; (i) Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare; (j) Minister of Education and Human Resource; and (k) Minister of Local Government. The Inter-Ministerial Committee will, inter alia, ensure alignment with policies, legislations and national programmes with the framework of the UNCRPD, make recommendations as appropriate and will drive the implementation of the 73 recommendations, including – (i) the formulation of a National Strategy and Action Plan on Disability 2026–2030; (ii) recommendation of Constitutional Amendment(s); and (iii) adherence to the Optional Protocol(s) of the UNCRPD.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the key outcomes of the first Inter-Ministerial Committee on Anti-Money Laundering, Combatting the Financing of Terrorism, and Combatting Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF); and the preliminary findings of the Independent Assessment of AML/CFT/CPF Framework. The findings of the Independent Assessment will be discussed at the next meeting of the Inter-Ministerial Committee and consolidated with the other challenges. The Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning will come up with a comprehensive time-bound action plan to address the identified deficiencies and gaps.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following a serious incident involving three minors at the shelter "L'Oiseau du Paradis" at Cap Malheureux in June 2025, an Enquiry Committee had been set up. The Committee confirmed serious lapses in supervision, and failures in emergency response protocols by the shelter staff on duty. Consequently, disciplinary actions were taken with the suspension of personnel directly responsible. On 30 October 2025, the National Children's Council, as the body currently managing the shelter, suspended the Council Secretary and other staff members. On 11 November 2025, an Officer-in-Charge was subsequently nominated to look into the day-to-day management of the Council. In the same vein, the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare facilitated the interim placement of the children in alternative NGO-run shelters and initiated renovation works which are now nearing completion at the Cap Malheureux Shelter and provided a basic training programme for remaining shelter carers, with the support of Pedostop and officials of the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare. In order to avoid disruption in the continuity of care of the children, the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare will ensure that - (a) the National Children's Council continues to manage the Cap Malheureux Shelter until further review; (b) the shelter will be renamed as "Cap L'Espoir" and will open on 01 December 2025 for carers and on 05 December 2025 for children ; (c) those who had been suspended under the previous Government and who have been cleared from all charges will be requested to resume work as from 01 December 2025 as some of the caregivers have expressed their intention of not resuming their job, on the advice of their counsel; and (d) the shelter complies with the Residential Care Institutions for Children Regulations 2022 through its Licensing and Enforcement Section.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of action being taken by the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives following an Inquiry Report from the Registrar of Cooperative Societies on alleged malpractices at the Vacoas Popular Multi-purpose Co-operative Society Ltd.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the refund of Arrears Pensions and Gratuity being effected to 77 employees of the Ex-Mauritius Tea Factories Company Ltd who were redeployed from Ex- Teafac in 1996 and 1998. Their offer of employment did not contain any clause stipulating that their past service at the Ex-Teafac will count for pension purposes. Hence, their length of service had not been reckoned as pensionable. In a spirit of equity and fairness, the same policy which applied to employees who were redeployed in 1992 will be applied by reckoning their length of service at the Ex-Teafac as pensionable.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change, in its capacity as the Designated National Authority for carbon market, joining the Southern Africa Alliance on Carbon Markets. The Alliance will bring together Government representatives from the SADC region, regional economic block representatives, international agencies, non-governmental organisations and private sector players on a common platform with the aim of advancing a fair implementation of carbon market initiatives in the region.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the use of non-biodegradable single use cups, bowls and trays for local packaging of food products being allowed, with conditions, as from 15 January 2026 for a period of one year. Amendments will be brought to the Environment (Control of Single Use Plastic Products) Regulations 2020 to cater for a Permit System with conditions for the local packaging of food products and imported food products pre-packed in non-biodegradable single use cups, bowls and trays. A Technical Committee will be set up with concerned stakeholders to work on the modalities of the Permit System.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the submission of the updated National Implementation Plan on Persistent Organic Pollutants (POPs) to the Secretariat of the Stockholm Convention, in the context of the Review and Update of the National Implementation Plan (NIP) for the Stockholm Convention on POPs project, in line with Articles 7 and 15 of the Convention. In February 2025, the services of a consulting firm were enlisted to develop an inventory, as well as an Action Plan for the management of POPs in Mauritius. The inventory resulted in seven Reports, which covered several chemicals/sectors, namely Pesticides, Polychlorinated Biphenyls, Unintentional POPs, namely furans and dioxins, Short and Medium Chain Chlorinated Paraffins, Transport, Building and Construction, Electrical and Electronic Equipment and Related Wastes. Furthermore, a critical review of existing legislative and institutional framework for management of POPs was undertaken. A detailed Assessment Report on Legal, Institutional and Regulatory Framework and Socio-Economic Impacts on POPs was also formulated. The Assessment Reports provide the foundation for devising the implementation strategy for the NIP Update Report. The key national priorities and identified areas for actions are – (a) Institutional and Legal Strengthening; (b) Management of POPs Stockpiles; (c) Unintentional POPs Reduction and Waste Management; (d) Contaminated Site Assessment; (e) Monitoring and Research; and (f) Best Available Techniques and Best Environmental Practices for Unintentional POPs and Waste Recycling.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of a child online protection measure through mobile devices and Wi-Fi home routers. The Information and Communication Technologies Authority will issue a directive to holders of public land mobile network licence and Internet Service Providers (ISPs) to implement a child protection measure in the form of features to be incorporated in the Subscriber Identity Module card. The child protection measure will curtail access and exposure to inappropriate content to minors and will include features enabling parents to either manage, monitor or restrict a child's access to online content, applications or services. This enhanced parental control mechanism is expected to promote safer digital access for minors, reduce exposure to harmful content (including pornography, self-harm, violence and bullying), empower parents with accessible oversight tools and strengthen national resilience against emerging online risks to children. The implementation of the measure will not require any change to mobile phone numbers for existing users. The directive will also provide that, in the medium to long term, the ISPs provide easy-to- use parental control solutions, both through mobile devices and Wi-Fi home routers. The ISPs will engage in a communication campaign to provide clear information on the measure being implemented as part of their commercial plans.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the mission undertaken by a delegation from the European Commission's Directorate General for Maritime Affairs and Fisheries from 07 to 11 July 2025 in Mauritius. In-depth discussions, as well as site visits to the various institutions which have a direct impact on the issue of Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated fishing, were held over the five days. The delegation discussed and formulated recommendations on the following – (a) the legal framework; (b) the fleet management system in Mauritius; (c) monitoring, control and surveillance; (d) infringements and enforcement; and (e) the traceability system in Mauritius. The Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries has initiated actions to reinforce the systems and measures have been put in place in line with these recommendations.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Arts and Culture submitting nomination forms for the possible inscription of the Mauritius World War Records (1914-2018) and Records of Chinese Presence in Mauritius (1826-2022), on the Memory of the World International Register of the Memory of the World Programme of UNESCO. The two proposed collections represent the next logical step in showcasing the breadth and diversity of our documentary legacy. Both collections illuminate major historical processes that shaped the island and contributed to global historical narratives. The Mauritius World War Records (1914-2018) collection contains a wide range of archival materials including 582 manuscripts, official correspondences with the War Office and the Secretary of the State, military despatches, ordinances, speeches, maps, photographs and publications. These document the participation of Mauritius and its people during the First and Second World Wars, as well as events thereafter. The records capture personal experiences, military organisation, local mobilisation and the societal impacts of the global conflicts on Mauritius. The Records of Chinese Presence in Mauritius (1826-2022) collection consists of 170 manuscripts, printed documents, maps and plans, one lithograph, and other historical materials that trace the migration, settlement, cultural evolution and socioeconomic role of the Chinese Community in Mauritius. The documents highlight trade activities, community structures, integration processes, cross-cultural exchanges and the long-term contribution of the Chinese diaspora to the island's multicultural identity.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Health and Wellness in the context of World AIDS Day 2025, which is observed on 01 December to raise awareness about HIV and AIDS and provide an opportunity for people living with HIV to share experiences and challenges faced in their daily lives. The theme chosen by the United Nations for this year is "Overcoming Disruption, Transforming the AIDS Response". To mark World AIDS Day, the Ministry of Health and Wellness will officially launch a booklet of 'Frequently Asked Questions' on the HIV Self-Test and Directly Assisted HIV Self- Test on 01 December 2025 and the following activities - (a) a Beach Testing at Flic en Flac Beach on 30 November 2025; (b) a march in Mahebourg organised by the J. Nehru Day Care Centre for Immunosuppressed in collaboration with the Rotary Club of Mahebourg, including talks and awareness activities at the Mahebourg Social Welfare Centre, on 06 December 2025; (c) a customer care training for staff dealing with people infected with or affected by HIV to equip them with the skills to provide respectful, stigma-free, and person- centred services that build trust and improve health outcomes for people living with HIV; (d) awareness/testing sessions in different regions around the island, targeting vulnerable women and street children and those in shelters; and (e) communication campaigns on HIV/AIDS.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outbreak of Marburg Virus Disease reported in Ethiopia and the national preparedness and surveillance measures being implemented by the Ministry of Health and Wellness to mitigate the risk of importation and ensure timely response capability. Surveillance activities have been strengthened through the dissemination of updated case definitions to all health facilities, both public and private, including Rodrigues. The National Hotline "8924" service has been sensitised to flag any unusual health events. At ports of entry, enhanced screening of travellers from Ethiopia has been implemented and a 21-day follow-up mechanism has been activated. In addition, laboratory preparedness is being consolidated through verification of PCR reagent availability, inactivation kits and specimen transport pathways. Infection Prevention and Control (IPC) preparedness is being reinforced through the review of isolation rooms, confirmation of Personal Protective Equipment stock levels and the circulation of IPC guidance to regional hospitals.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Arts and Culture in collaboration with the Conservatoire National de Musique François Mitterand (CNMFM), the Nelson Mandela Centre for African Culture (NMCAC), and Le Morne Heritage Trust Fund (LMHTF), to celebrate Christmas at national level. The following activities will be organised during the month of December 2025 – (a) two-week Christmas Programme by the Centre de Lecture Publique et D'Animation Culturelle from 15 to 23 December 2025 in the 14 Centres de Lecture et D'Animation Culturelle including "Do-It-Yourself Workshops", Christmas tree decoration, and gift distribution to children and young people aged between 5 to 20; (b) "Symphony of Stars - Christmas in Song (Enn Sinfoni Zetwal)" by the NMCAC on 13 December 2025 as from 1900 hours at the Caudan Waterfront. The event will feature five renowned Mauritian artists and children aged 7 to 17 in an intergenerational cultural concert blending Christmas carols and creole rhythms; (c) an introduction to Apiculture and a visit to a beehive for some 20 pupils of Grade 5 by LMHTF on 15 December 2025; (d) visit to Odysseo Oceanarium Mauritius for pupils of Grades 4 and 5 on 19 December 2025 followed by a picnic at Wolmar public beach and distribution of educational materials and gifts to the children by LMHTF; (e) open-air cinema screening on 20 December 2025 for under-privileged children of Le Morne Village by LMHTF; (f) a mini concert for 360 under-privileged children on 20 December 2025 at the Caudan Arts Centre; and (g) '28th Edition of the Songs of the Season 2025' by the CNMFM on 20 and 21 December 2025 at the Caudan Arts Centre.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Arts and Culture in collaboration with Le Morne Heritage Trust Fund, the Nelson Mandela Centre for African Culture (NMCAC), the Intercontinental Slavery Museum Mauritius Ltd, the National Heritage Fund (NHF) and the National Art Gallery (NAG) to commemorate the 191st Anniversary of the Abolition of Slavery. The abolition of slavery is commemorated every year on 01 February to acknowledge the sufferings endured by enslaved Africans and their descendants and pay tribute to the enduring legacy of slavery while recognising the fight of maroons for freedom. The theme "Justice for Africans and Peoples of African descent through reparations" has been retained. To commemorate this symbolic event, the following activities will be organised - (a) a wreath laying ceremony on 31 January 2026 at 'Bassin des Esclaves', Pamplemousses; (b) a wreath laying ceremony on 31 January 2026 at 'Monument aux Esclaves', Pointe Canon, Mahebourg; (c) a wreath laying ceremony on 31 January 2026 at Frederik Hendrik Historical Site, Vieux Grand Port; (d) a wreath laying ceremony on 01 February 2026 at the International Slave Route Monument, followed by a cultural programme at Le Morne Public Beach; (e) an exhibition titled "Esclavage: Réparations et Restitution" by the NMCAC in collaboration with the NAG from February to March 2026 and a painting contest as well as a slam competition on this theme at Lespas Lar, Pointe aux Sables; (f) the launching of a book written by Nicholas Couronne namely "Le Regard de l'Ancêtre Esclave" on 02 February 2026, a dual exhibition from 02 to 13 February 2026 on Furcy Madeleine and Constance Couronne; and (g) an educational exhibition on the abolition of slavery in schools and regional centres for the whole month of February 2026 by the NHF.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the University of Mauritius will organise the 3rd Emerging Countries International Conference on Business, Finance and Economics 2025 in collaboration with the Faculty of Economic and Management Sciences of the Stellenbosch University, South Africa and the University of Ghana Business School on 03 and 04 December 2025 in Mauritius. The International Conference aims at providing a platform for research dissemination to obtain constructive feedback and creating opportunities for international collaboration and networking among peers in emerging countries and globally. The Conference is expected to enhance the visibility of Mauritius in terms of research at regional level. It is also aligned with the country's vision with regard to internationalisation and the research agenda.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the International Labour Organization in collaboration with the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations will hold a Ministerial Roundtable in Mauritius on 17 and 18 December 2025 with a view to reinforcing the cooperation and exchange experience in addressing labour and employment challenges among Seychelles, Madagascar, Comoros and Mauritius (including Rodrigues). The aim of the Ministerial Roundtable is to encourage those countries to work together to promote decent work on themes such as recycling of waste, climate change and just transition, digital transformation and Artificial Intelligence and job creation, particularly for the youth. Some 125 participants comprising Ministers of Labour and Employment, together with their senior officers and the representatives of employers and employees from their respective countries are expected to participate in the Roundtable.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Tertiary Education, Science and Research to the United Kingdom (UK) where he participated in the Going Global 2025 Conference in Westminster, London. The conference was hosted under the theme "Volatility and Change: A New Era for Global Higher Education". A series of high-level meetings were convened during the mission to strengthen Mauritius' partnerships with leading UK universities and institutions, with a view to advancing national priorities in higher education, research, and innovation.

filename: Final_Highlights_of_Cabinet_Meeting_Friday_28_November_2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent missions of the Junior Minister of Finance to the United Kingdom (UK) for investment promotion and to Washington, D.C. to participate in the 2025 International Monetary Fund/World Bank Group Annual Meetings. The Junior Minister had meetings with various leading companies in UK with the objective of strengthening existing relationships and attracting new players in the financial services landscape. In Washington, the Junior Minister participated in the Annual Meetings of the International Monetary Fund and World Bank Group, and various meetings organised in the margins of the Annual Meetings. The Junior Minister participated in the 2025 Annual Meetings Plenary and the African Caucus meeting. The African Caucus reaffirmed its commitment to safeguarding macroeconomic and financial stability while advancing policies that enhance living standards through improved access to social services, job creation, and the promotion of sustainable growth. The Junior Minister also attended the Commonwealth Finance Ministers Annual Meeting. He had fruitful discussions with, inter alia, the International Monetary Fund and World Bank Officials.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recommendations made by the Pay Research Bureau (PRB) in its 2026 Report on Pay and Grading Structures and Conditions of Service in the Public Sector (Civil Service, Parastatal and other Statutory Bodies, Local Authorities and Rodrigues Regional Assembly) and Private Secondary Schools. The Bureau has had wide consultations with all stakeholders in the preparation of its report. It has, when making its recommendations, remained mindful of national affordability while paying regard to the need for a fair and reasonable pay for Public Sector employees. The Bureau has also considered, inter alia, the following - (a) the necessity to ensure that the starting salary of the lowest paid is not less than the National Minimum Wage and addressing the relativity distortion where necessary; (b) the monetary value of the array of non-monetary benefits that form part of the total remuneration package which accrue to public sector employees; (c) the recruitment and retention of the right mix of employees who are motivated to deliver high-quality public services; (d) the key principles of compensation including equity, transparency, competitiveness and employee performance; and (e) the loss in purchasing power. The salary ratio of the General Worker to the Permanent Secretary which was 1:6.2 in the 2021 PRB Report, is at around 1:5.4 in the 2026 PRB Report. Moreover, the salary ratio of the General Worker to the Senior Chief Executive now works out to 1:6.7. The average weighted salary increase on gross salary December 2025 is 15.3%, compared to 7.95% in the 2021 Report. Recommended 2026 Salary for Selected Grades - Grade 2021 Basic Salary 2026 Basic Salary Initial Top Initial Top Senior Chief Executive 163,250 206,000 Permanent Secretary 132,000 166,000 Educator (Secondary) 27,400 62,700 37,290 77,750 Technical Officer 21,850 47,675 31,365 60,600 Nursing Officer 19,525 44,800 29,215 57,400 Police Constable 18,650 38,400 27,905 50,100 Management Support Officer 18,925 37,450 28,225 49,000 Tradesman 15,745 28,225 24,545 38,215 General Worker 10,250 21,150 17,975 30,615 The recommendations contained in the Report inclusive of salary compensation payable as from 01 January 2026 and the interim allowance of 5% integrated in salary, are estimated to cost Government around Rs10.9 billion yearly. Given the challenging current economic and fiscal context, Cabinet has decided that the Report will have to be implemented in two phases, that is, 50 per cent as from January 2026 and 100 per cent as from January 2027. The Report is available on the websites of the Pay Research Bureau, the Government Information Service and the Ministry of Public Service and Administrative Reforms.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the fixed-quantum subsidy scheme under the Price Stabilisation Fund being extended to two additional essential commodities, namely canned mackerel/pilchards and adult diapers, and to the introduction of a fixed subsidy on anti-hypertensive, cardiovascular and anti-diabetic medicines, for medicines retailing at Rs100 or above. The fixed subsidy will be provided as follows - (a) Rs10 per can of canned mackerel/pilchards; (b) Rs50 per 10 adult diapers; and (c) Rs50 per box of anti-hypertensive, cardiovascular and anti-diabetic medicine. The effective date for the measures has been set to 15 January 2026. Cabinet has further agreed to the implementation of the above subsidies in Rodrigues.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Kunal Naik as part-time Chairperson of the National Agency for Drug Control. Dr F. Sulliman, Director of the National Agency for Drug Control will hold a meeting with Mr Naik on Saturday 20 December 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that former Chief Justice Y. K. J. (Bernard) Yeung Sik Yuen, GOSK has been designated as Chairperson of the Constitutional Review Commission.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that an Inter-Ministerial Committee on the Strategy for Port Development will be set up under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister, comprising the - (a) Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries; (b) Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (c) Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning; (d) Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (e) Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; (f) representatives of the Mauritius Ports Authority; (g) representatives of the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd; (h) representatives of the private sector, namely the Mauritius Chamber of Commerce and Industry, Business Mauritius and the Mauritius Export Association.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Merchant Shipping (Manning of Tugs and Certification of Tug Personnel) Regulations 2025 to provide for a dedicated regulatory framework governing the manning of tug and certification of tug personnel. The Regulations will, inter alia, - (a) ensure the safe and efficient operation of tugs within the Port and territorial waters of Mauritius by establishing clear requirements for the manning, training and certification of tug personnel; (b) set mandatory standards for minimum safe manning levels, prescribe procedures for the issuance of Certificates of Competency and Proficiency and outline responsibilities for companies operating tugs; (c) require the owner of the tug to ensure that a tug is manned by certified tug personnel who should receive regular and continuous training, including towing operations; (d) outline offences regarding persons who are assigned on tugs and do not hold the appropriate qualification, competence or experience as well as make it an offence for a company or tug master to allow any function or have any capacity on a tug to be performed by a person who does not possess the appropriate certificate and training; and (e) enhance maritime safety, protect the marine environment and uphold professional standards in tug operations within the port sea of Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Non-Citizens (Employment Restriction) Exemptions (Amendment) Regulations 2025 to allow the issue of Certificates of Exemption to German nationals coming to Mauritius under the Working Holiday Programme. Under the Working Holiday Programme, young Mauritians and Germans aged between 18 and 31 years would be able to travel to Germany and Mauritius respectively for holiday and take up employment as an incidental activity for the purpose of supplementing their travel funds and to gather work experience. The Working Holiday Programme is an opportunity for Mauritian youth to expand their knowledge, widen their horizon as well as enhance their chances of employment abroad.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the National Protocol for Heavy/Torrential Rain for Mauritius, which will be applicable to both the public and private sector, is being finalised. The private sector is being invited to follow suit on measures that may be implemented for the public sector, pending the finalisation of the National Protocol for Heavy/Torrential Rain for Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, in the context of the pipe replacement project being implemented by the Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities under the Indian Line of Credit, a Consultant has been appointed. The project comprising the replacement of 115 km of pipes will be implemented in two phases. Phase 1 comprising the replacement of 43.6 km of pipes at a cost of Rs1.08 billion will be implemented in 2026 in the following regions - (a) from Deux Frères to Petit Sable; (b) Beau Bassin/Rose Hill; (c) from Trou aux Biches to Pointe aux Piments; and (d) Georges Guibert - Floreal and Laterals.
ministries: Ministry Of Energy and Public Utilities

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Information and Communication Technologies (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2025 and the Information and Communication Technologies (Licensing and Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2025. The purpose of these Regulations is to provide for a new category of licence, namely the Mobile Number Portability Service Provider Licence and to cater for the fees payable by a Mobile Number Portability Service Provider. The Mobile Number Portability system will allow consumers to retain their existing mobile numbers when transferring from one service provider to another.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that in accordance with section 87 of the Constitution of the Republic of Mauritius, the Prime Minister has advised the President of the Republic of Mauritius to appoint Mr Rabin Gungoo as High Commissioner of the Republic of Mauritius to the Mauritius High Commission, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia with residence in Kuala Lumpur.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare signing a "Protocole d'Accord" with Expertise France, with the objective of formalising the collaboration between the parties within the framework of the "Lumin'îles Regional Programme". Lumin'îles is a cooperation platform supported by the "Agence Française de Développement" that helps island countries improve public policies and institutions through studies, expertise and capacity building. It aims at empowering women and is being implemented in Mauritius, Madagascar and the Comoros. Through this Agreement, Mauritius will benefit, inter alia, from - (a) targeted technical assistance and capacity-building initiatives in key areas relating to gender equality, childcare development, and the welfare of women and children; (b) coordinated implementation of activities aimed at addressing challenges faced by women, particularly those relating to childcare; and (c) reinforced evidence-based policymaking through improved availability and use of gender-disaggregated and childcare-related data.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the main outcomes of the 16th round of negotiations between the European Union (EU) and the Eastern and Southern Africa (ESA-5) (Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, Seychelles and Zimbabwe) on the deepening of the Interim EPA (iEPA) and the 10th Meeting of the EPA Implementation Committee, held in Seychelles. Discussions focused on the outstanding issues in the various chapters of the Agreement, namely Trade in Services, Investment Liberalisation and Digital Trade, Customs and Trade Facilitation, Rules of Origin, Trade and Sustainable Development, Dispute Settlement, Institutional Provisions and General and Final Provisions. As a way forward, both the EU and ESA-5 agreed to exchange information with a view to resolving all remaining issues, which are not major stumbling blocks, by 31 December 2025. With regard to the 10th Meeting of the EPA Implementation Committee, both Parties reviewed their status of implementation of tariff liberalisation, assessed the trade performances under the iEPA and identified trade barriers, amongst others. They agreed to continue exchanging information on these matters. The meeting also examined and concluded discussions on the Concept Note on the Joint Monitoring and Evaluation Framework for the iEPA.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that pursuant to the meeting held in Japan between the Prime Minister and Mr Ishiba, the former Prime Minister of Japan on 20 August 2025, the Government of Japan has informed, through its Embassy in Mauritius, that Japan is agreeable to discussions being initiated for a Bilateral Investment Promotion and Protection Agreement with the Government of Mauritius. A comprehensive assessment of factors, including the prospects for future expansion of Japanese Investment in Mauritius and the request of Japanese business circles will be required.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 12 December 2025, a first local confirmed case of Chikungunya was notified in a 68 year-old Mauritian lady. On 15 December 2025, another local case was confirmed in a 59-year old Mauritian lady. All public health response measures, including contact tracing, fever survey, fogging and larviciding were promptly initiated and are ongoing. In addition, a multisectoral technical meeting at the level of the Ministry of Health and Wellness was held on 17 December 2025, emphasising amongst others, strengthening intersectoral coordination, aligning response activities, and ensuring timely implementation of preventive, surveillance, vector-control, and risk- communication measures. Ongoing entomological surveillance is being conducted in the affected and surrounding areas to monitor vector density and guide targeted control measures. Sensitisation and risk-communication campaigns through radio and television has already been initiated over the past two weeks to enhance public awareness on Chikungunya prevention. The situation is being closely monitored through enhanced epidemiological, entomological and laboratory surveillance.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the Blue Justice Conference held in Copenhagen, Denmark and the International Sugar Organisation (ISO) Seminar held in London, United Kingdom. The Blue Justice 2025 Conference held under the theme "United in Action Against Fisheries Crime", aimed at reaffirming Member States' commitment to combat illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing and transnational organised crime in the global fishing industry. During his intervention, the Minister seized the opportunity to underline Mauritius' efforts to address maritime crime through the implementation and strengthening of legal frameworks and monitoring systems. The ISO Seminar was held in under the theme of "Sugar: So Much More Than a Sweetener". Speakers at the ISO conference highlighted the rapid decline in global prices below production costs in most producing countries as surprising and worrying. The Minister participated in an "intergovernmental panel" jointly with Baroness Gloria Hooper, Member of the House of Lords and Executive Director of ISO, Mr José Orive. During his intervention, he highlighted the prompt decisions and actions taken by the Mauritian Government with the implementation of the Multi Annual Adaptation Sugar Strategy Action Plan 2006-2015 in July 2006 after the announcement of a 36% reduction by the EU in the price of sugar.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities to Morocco, where he participated in the 19th World Water Congress. The Congress which was held under the theme "Water in a Changing World: Innovation and Adaptation", featured - (a) a Ministerial Roundtable on the theme "Accelerating SDG 6 in a changing world" with the aim of accelerating progress toward SDG 6 by 2030 and achieving water security as a system interacting with a larger Nexus of Water, Energy, Food, Health and Ecosystem; and (b) an exhibition showcasing innovative and cutting-edge water technologies, and promoting best practices in water governance. The Minister also had bilateral meetings with the Minister of Equipment and Water of Morocco, the Deputy Minister of Agriculture and Forestry of Türkiye as well as the President of the World Water Council.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the 120th Session of the Council of Ministers of the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS) held in Brussels. The Council met in the context of a severe financial crisis affecting the Organisation, arising mainly from low level of payment of assessed contributions by Member States, mismanagement of funds by the previous management which resulted in the European Commission decision to scale back its contribution and limiting it to funding a few activities linked to the implementation of the Samoa Partnership Agreement. In his general statement to the Council, the Minister recalled the vital role played by the Organisation since its inception in supporting the socio-economic development of African, Caribbean and Pacific States, including Mauritius. He underscored the need to rethink the strategic vision of the OACPS with a view to achieving tangible impacts. Mauritius was commended for its decision to ratify the Samoa Partnership Agreement. The instrument of Ratification will soon be submitted to the European Council which is the depositary Organisation. In the margins of the Council, the Minister had bilateral discussions with the Secretary-General of the OACPS and had meetings with, inter alia, the European Commission.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade participated virtually in an extraordinary meeting of the SADC Council of Ministers and Summit of SADC Heads of State and Government held on 17 December 2025. During his intervention, at the Extraordinary Summit, the Minister informed that Mauritius aligns itself with the initiatives of the SADC aimed at restoring peace, stability and constitutional normalcy in Madagascar. The Extraordinary Summit, inter alia, - (a) reaffirmed its commitment to remain engaged with the political and security situation in Madagascar until an elected government is established through electoral processes; (b) approved the report of the chairperson of the SADC Organ; and (c) mandated the undertaking by March 2026 of shuttle diplomacy missions by the SADC Panel of Elders and SADC Mediation Reference Group, with the support of the SADC Secretariat to engage the Government of Madagascar on the national inclusive dialogue, the reforms process and the holding of elections. The Extraordinary Summit appointed HE Mr Hichilema, President of Zambia as the Deputy Chairperson of SADC.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Land Transport to Riyadh, Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, in the context of the CoMotion Global Summit 2025. The Minister took part as a guest speaker in the panel discussion on Public– Private Partnerships in mobility including physical infrastructure and digital services. The session explored how governments and private partners can build effective governance structures that support not only the delivery of assets but also the integration of technologies such as payment systems and data platforms. Strategic opportunities for future Public–Private Partnerships engagement in Mauritius were also discussed during the Summit, including the integration of solar photovoltaic systems to power transport infrastructure, the development of additional urban terminals, and the implementation of a nationwide cashless ticketing system aligned with global best practices and digital transformation trends. In the margins of his mission, the Minister also held a series of bilateral meetings focused on the transport challenges faced by Mauritius and the opportunities for cooperation with Saudi authorities and institutions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in the Fourth Edition of the African Startup Conference, which was held in Algiers, Algeria. The Minister intervened during the Ministerial Summit whereby he welcomed the African Union (AU) report on African best practices for building startup ecosystems and acknowledged strong models emerging from Tunisia, Senegal, Rwanda, Algeria, Egypt, and South Africa. He noted with satisfaction that in the report on 'Tax Framework for Startups, Innovation and R&D in Africa: A Comprehensive Analysis', Mauritius is cited as a leading African example in terms of its sophisticated tax framework for innovation through gradual policy evaluation. During his intervention, the Minister highlighted that Mauritius would explore opportunities which reflect Mauritius' interest in continued regional collaboration and alignment with African best practices. The Minister also intervened during the Plenary session of Champions–Government Summit, which presented a unique dialogue format connecting high-growth startups with policymakers. In the margins of the conference, the Minister had fruitful bilateral meetings with, inter alia, the Minister of Knowledge Economy, Startups and Micro-enterprises of the People's Democratic Republic of Algeria, the Minister of Small and Medium Enterprise Development of the Republic of Zambia and the Minister for Industry and Enterprise of the Republic of Seychelles.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister, Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change in the Seventh Session of the United Nations Environment Assembly (UNEA 7) held in Nairobi, Kenya. The theme set for UNEA 7 was "Advance sustainable solutions for a resilient planet". At the High-Level Segment, the Junior Minister made a statement wherein she laid emphasis on the implementation of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)'s Medium-Term Strategy 2026-2029 and to continued partnerships with UNEP and friendly nations to unlock finance, technology and capacity-building. The Assembly considered a diversity of Resolutions, from safeguarding coral reefs and soundly managing minerals and metals critical for the energy transition, to developing sustainable solutions through sport. The Mauritian delegation also attended the following side events - (i) Improving Global Environmental Governance to Jointly Build a Clean and Beautiful World organised by the China mission in Nairobi; (ii) Ministerial Breakfast Meeting of African Ministers of Environment; and (iii) Critical Energy Transition Minerals: Empowering women for a responsible, just and equitable minerals sector. Bilateral meetings were also held with several senior officials.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decision on 19 December 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the - (a) reconstitution of the Board of Trustees of the Trade Union Trust Fund with Mr Sateedanand Jeewooth as part-time Chairperson; and (b) appointment of Dr (Mrs) Marie Christinne Clarisse as Director of the Conservatoire National de Musique François Mitterrand Trust Fund.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Youth and Sports will organise the National Sports Award 2025 on 06 February 2026 at the Côte d'Or National Sports Complex. The National Sports Award will be relaunched with a fresh, inclusive, and transparent framework aligned with international best practices, with no distinction made between Olympic and non-Olympic sports disciplines. For the 2025 edition, the National Sports Award will feature 14 award categories. The Most Outstanding Sports Performance of the Year, which will be awarded for the first time, aims at recognising achievements at major events of international standing, such as the Olympic Games, Youth Olympic Games, Paralympic Games, Deaflympics, World Championships, and recognised world-level competitions only. Additionally, a special Lifetime Sports Achievement Award will be conferred to a sportsperson in recognition of long-standing contributions to the sports sector.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Inter-Ministerial Committee set up under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister to look into the strategy for port development will hold its first meeting on Wednesday 21 January 2026. Cabinet has further taken note that following a request from Government, the European Union has agreed to the mobiisation of a Technical Assistance Facility to identify opportunities for European Union - Mauritius partnership for the development and modernisation of the port. In this context, a team of two experts will be in Mauritius for a six-day field mission as from 26 January 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the organisation of the 9th Indian Ocean Conference from 10 to 12 April 2026, in Mauritius, in collaboration with the India Foundation. The Conference will, inter alia - (a) provide a high-level platform for dialogue on issues of common interest to Indian Ocean States; (b) promote Mauritius as a hub for diplomacy, maritime cooperation and the blue economy; (c) foster collaboration on maritime security, trade, climate change adaptation and sustainable ocean governance; and (d) strengthen regional and international partnerships. The theme of the 9th Indian Ocean Conference is "The Collective Stewardship for Indian Ocean Governance". The Conference will comprise an inaugural session, plenary discussions and thematic panels, as well as related side meetings. The plenary sessions will focus over three main themes, namely- (a) Freedom of Navigation; (b) Maritime Laws and Governance; and (c) Maritime Security.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 12 January 2026, the United States (US) House of Representatives approved the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA) Extension Bill, extending the programme up to December 2028 and renewing duty- free access for eligible African exports. The Bill will now proceed to the US Senate for consideration, after which it will require Presidential approval and signature to enter into force. Mauritius will continue the active engagement regarding the future US- Mauritius trade relations including the conclusion of a Bilateral Trade Agreement.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Education and Human Resource is undertaking public consultations on, inter alia, the review of the mode of admission of students in secondary schools, with a view to integrating one of the proposals in the Blueprint for the transformation of the Mauritian Education Sector. Several consultations have been held with relevant stakeholders, including the Service Diocésain de l'Éducation Catholique, the Private Secondary Education Authority, the Mauritius Examinations Syndicate, the Mauritius Institute of Education, the Special Education Needs Authority, as well as the Unions of the primary and secondary education sub-sectors. A consultative meeting was also held on 15 January 2026 with Ministers, Junior Ministers, Members of the National Assembly, and Members of the Rodrigues Regional Assembly. Consultations will be held with the Parents-Teachers Associations, Student Councils, Prefects as well as stakeholders in Rodrigues. In order to further assess public opinion, a survey form has been posted on-line on the website of the Ministry of Education and Human Resource as from today. The deadline for the consultation exercise has been extended up to 13 February 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Housing and Lands issuing drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for the Mauritius Hydrographic Services Bill, which will have, inter alia, the following objectives - (a) to set up the Mauritius Hydrographic Services; (b) to regulate hydrographic surveys and charting within Mauritian waters; (c) to produce and maintain official nautical charts and publications in accordance with international standards; (d) to compile, preserve and manage hydrographic and marine geospatial data and to act as the national custodian of such data; (e) to regulate data exchange, access control, and data sharing; (f) to ensure the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive hydrographic data in line with applicable laws and protocols; (g) to regulate the hydrographers' and nautical cartographers' professions; (h) to issue authorisations and permits for hydrographic surveys within Mauritian jurisdiction; and (i) to fulfill international obligations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation conveying drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for amendments to be brought to the Data Protection Act 2017. The proposed amendments cover, inter alia, the following areas - (a) financial independence of the Data Protection Office to make it financially self-sustaining; (b) strengthening of enforcement powers so that the Data Protection Commissioner is able to impose administrative fines for any breach of the Data Protection Act; (c) setting up of a dedicated Prosecution Unit with Police Officers for effective enforcement to align with General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR); (d) review of powers of the Data Protection Commissioner to allow for investigative powers; (e) provision for written contracts between controllers, processors and sub- processors as this will ensure enhanced accountability chains; (f) appeals by an aggrieved person to be made directly to the Supreme Court to avoid any conflict with the powers of the Director of Public Prosecutions under section 72 of the Constitution and to ensure effective judicial redress; and (g) inclusion of a new section on data portability similar to the GDPR.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the second meeting of the Inter- Ministerial Committee on Anti-Money Laundering, Combatting the Financing of Terrorism and Combatting Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF), co-chaired by the Attorney-General and the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning. The meeting reviewed the preliminary findings of the Mid-Term Independent Assessment conducted by an international consultant and gave policy directives to address deficiencies identified in the AML/CFT regime. The Committee took note of key challenges affecting the effectiveness of Mauritius' AML/CFT/CPF framework as identified by the Mock Evaluation. The Committee also took note of progress made in thematic risk assessments, the training delivered by ESAAMLG Experts and efforts towards the operationalisation of the Real Estate Agent Authority. It also discussed proposed reforms under the draft AML/CFT/CPF (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Amendment) Bill, including proposals to regulate cash transactions for second-hand motor vehicles to mitigate money laundering risks.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the launching of National Consultations for the formulation of Vision 2050. The consultations aim to provide stakeholders an opportunity to provide inputs for the formulation of Vision 2050. Stakeholder engagement will focus on both inclusiveness, through regional engagement, and through thematic discussions, with a view to ensuring broad national ownership of Vision 2050. The consultations will be conducted from January to April 2026 and will include 12 regional fora across all Municipal and District Councils, targeting a broad cross-section of citizens including youth, students, community leaders, women's groups, non-governmental organisations and economic operators. In parallel, a series of thematic and sectoral dialogues will be organised with selected public and private sector stakeholders, including non-governmental organisations, senior citizens, youth, students, women's groups and academia as well as trade unions, to deepen discussions on critical cross-cutting areas. The preliminary results of the consultations will be shared by mid-2026, with a draft Vision 2050 document to be made available for public comments in the second half of the year.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the promulgation of the Road Traffic (Crop Season) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 in view of the extension of the Crop Season 2025- 2026 to 31 January 2026. The Regulations provide that a licence may be taken out in respect of any vehicle for which the issue of a carrier's licence for the transport of sugar cane and sugar cane crop requisites has been approved by the National Land Transport Authority, for a specified period.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Medical Council (Medical Institutions) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 to provide for the recognition and listing of 30 additional professional bodies.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Pharmaceutical Products) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2026 to review the implementation date for the application of the subsidy under the Price Stabilisation Fund on selected pharmaceutical products, namely on anti- hypertensive, cardiovascular and anti-diabetic medicines with effect from 01 February 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the award made by the Employment Relations Tribunal concerning the dispute entered by Government Medical and Dental Officers Association regarding the decision taken in 2022 for provision of specialised services in all hospitals on a 24/7 basis. The Tribunal has awarded in favour of the disputants. The Ministry of Health and Wellness will now examine the award and review the working arrangements of the Specialists/Senior Specialists, accordingly.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Dr Mohamed Yakub Janabi, World Health Organization (WHO) Regional Director for Africa will be on official visit to Mauritius from 18 to 22 January 2026, in the context of the International Health Symposium 2026 organised by the Charles Telfair Education Campus. The visit of the WHO Regional Director also forms part of his inaugural programme to engage with all Member States in the WHO African Region and aims to further consolidate the strong collaboration between the WHO and the Government of Mauritius by identifying key avenues for enhanced cooperation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a delegation from Crossrail International Ltd, a public body of the United Kingdom (UK) government's Department for Transport, engaged through the UK's Green Cities, Infrastructure and Energy Programme, will effect a Country Visit to Mauritius from 19 to 23 January 2026, to provide technical assistance to the Government of Mauritius. The technical assistance trails from the wider UK-Mauritius Strategic Partnership Framework within the Growth, Trade and Investment Agenda, and Crossrail International Ltd will, therefore, support the Office of the Public Sector Governance to undertake an Operations and Maintenance review of the Metro Express Limited. The country visit will fundamentally be in relation to the wider benefits of infrastructure schemes and in particular, the Light Rail System of the Metro Express Ltd and other appropriate infrastructure projects.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of an Information Security Management System (ISMS) by Ministries and Departments based on standards developed by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO). The ISMS will establish the following controls - (a) Organisational Controls, which focuses on governance, policies, third party management and access; (b) People Controls, which addresses human factors through measures such as training and security awareness; (c) Physical Controls, relating to controls for the protection of physical assets and environments; and (d) Technological Controls, ensuring information security continuity in IT and communication systems. The implementation of an ISO-certified ISMS across Government is expected to bring significant operational, governance and strategic benefits.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius jointly publishing, with the International Trade Centre, the discussion paper entitled "Trade and Climate Change in Mauritius". In June 2023, Mauritius was selected as a beneficiary of the Climate Competitiveness Project, funded by the European Union and implemented in partnership with the International Trade Centre, along with seven other countries. The project aims to support developing countries' engagement in the Trade and Environmental Sustainability Structured Discussions at the World Trade Organization by working with six countries and/or country groupings to assess the role that trade and trade policy can play in helping them meet climate mitigation and adaptation objectives. The discussion paper examines the impacts of climate change on trade, the role of trade in supporting climate policy objectives and potential market opportunities arising from the green transition. The paper identifies opportunities for Mauritius to position itself in the growing market for green goods and services by leveraging sustainability standards in key export sectors such as sugar, seafood, clothing, essential oils and tourism. It further highlights the importance of multilateral engagement at the World Trade Organization, regional cooperation with neighbouring island States, and targeted unilateral measures, supported by aid-for- trade and climate finance with a view to addressing market access risks, renewable energy challenges and circular economy initiatives.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of Memoranda of Understanding between - (a) the Ministry of Health and Wellness and Medanta Hospital, Gurugram, India; and (b) the Ministry of Health and Wellness and Sri Kauvery Medical Care Limited. The objectives of the Memoranda of Understanding are as follows - (a) strengthening of specialised healthcare services through structured collaboration; (b) provision of capacity building to Mauritian healthcare professionals and technical assistance for upgrading of local health facilities; (c) access to advanced treatments not available in Mauritius; and (d) promotion of cooperation in high-specialty fields such as neuro- intervention, transplantation, bone marrow transplant, paediatric surgery, cardiac electrophysiology, while ensuring continuous medical education and streamlined referral services for Mauritian patients.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research signing a Memorandum of Understanding with the School of Oriental and African Studies (SOAS), University of London to foster international collaboration to position Mauritius as a regional hub for knowledge and innovation. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding is to facilitate collaboration between public higher education institutions and the SOAS in the following areas - (a) joint degree programmes, professional development programmes, executive course, joint research initiative and co-supervision of PhD projects; (b) exchange of non-confidential information including exchange of teaching and research documentation; and (c) exchange of faculty members for research, lectures and discussion.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Revenue Authority will host - (a) a Regional Training Workshop on Cybercrime, Virtual Assets and Digital Evidence, jointly organised and facilitated by the US Department of Justice, Office of Overseas Prosecutorial Development, Assistance and Training and the US Federal Bureau of Investigation, from 19 to 23 January 2026; and (b) a Regional Seminar on Developing a Risk-Based Compliance Plan for Customs, organised by IMF AFRITAC South, from 02 to 06 February 2026. The Regional Workshop on Cybercrime, Virtual Assets and Digital Evidence will enhance the investigative and prosecutorial capacities of Mauritian and regional institutions. It will strengthen cross-border cooperation, improve the handling and admissibility of digital evidence, and build skills to effectively investigate and disrupt cyber-enabled and transnational financial crimes. Moreover, it will reinforce Mauritius' position as a regional hub for combating cybercrime. The Regional Seminar on Developing a Risk-Based Compliance Plan for Customs aims to support Customs Administrations in - (a) understanding the concept and importance of an Integrated Risk Management (IRM) approach as part of Customs Governance; (b) identifying the key component required to implement an effective Integrated Risk Management Framework; (c) developing a risk-based compliance improvement plan, using IMF's Fiscal Affairs Department technical note as a foundation; (d) establishing, monitoring, evaluating and refining effective selectivity criteria; and (e) enhancing the flow and use of risk information across enforcement and compliance units, including mechanisms for acting on intelligence and reporting outcomes.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Health and Wellness in the Second Global Summit on Traditional Medicine in India, organised by the World Health Organization (WHO) in collaboration with the Government of India. The WHO Global Summit on Traditional Medicine focused on the role of traditional medicine through scientific validation, regulatory frameworks, health system integration, innovation and sustainability. During the visit, discussions focused on the significant progress made towards the establishment of a Centre of Excellence on Traditional Medicine in Mauritius, with the technical, institutional and financial support from the Government of India. The Centre will be a regional hub for evidence-based integration of traditional medicine into the public health system, research, education and training for Mauritius, Africa and Small Island Developing States. The Minister participated in various inter-ministerial meetings and discussed with the Minister of Ayush, reaffirming Mauritius' position as a strategic partner of India in traditional medicine, health systems strengthening and capacity building. The Minister discussed future collaboration under the WHO Global Traditional Medicine Strategy 2025-2034, including regulatory harmonisation, research partnership, human resource development and knowledge exchange. The Minister also had discussions with the Director of the All India Institutes of Medical Science to explore the possibility of cooperation in the medical field.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent visit of the Minister of Local Government to Nanchang City, Jiangxi Province, China. The Minister conducted a series of economic, educational, and institutional exchanges in the context of the World Green Development Investment and Trade Expo and China Green Food Expo which is an international event, focusing on green development, digital empowerment, and low-carbon economic cooperation and attracts regional and international participation from government bodies and the public and private sectors alike. The primary objectives of the visit were to participate in a high-level international green development platform, engage with Chinese state-owned and central enterprises on concrete cooperation opportunities, explore the possibilities of education and cultural cooperation to support long-term bilateral relations and assess the feasibility of translating cooperation intentions into implementable projects. The Minister also participated in the China (Jiangxi) – Africa Investment and Cooperation Exchange Event. In the margins of the visit, the Minister carried out working sessions with several major Chinese enterprises.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Decisions 16 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Second Edition of Business Forum on the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) in Marrakesh, Morocco, under the theme 'Together for an Efficient Implementation of the AfCFTA'. In his intervention during the forum, the Junior Minister highlighted several key elements, notably the importance of preserving special and differential treatment for developing countries and least developed countries, in order to guarantee inclusive and sustainable development. The Junior Minister also participated in the panel discussion titled 'Status of the AfCFTA Implementation and Prospects' along with other African ministers.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - 14 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Representation of the People (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill would be to amend the Representation of the People Act so as to provide for the compilation of a supplementary register of electors for the forthcoming Municipal City Council and Municipal Town Council elections, or for such other election as may be prescribed. The Representation of the People Act currently provides that a person cannot be registered as an elector in any year unless he has attained or will have attained the age of 18 on 15 August in that year. The register of electors compiled in a year comes into force on 16 August in that year and remains in force until the register next compiled comes into force. As the law currently stands, the name of a person who attained the age of 18 after 15 August would only be entered in the register of electors during the next registration exercise. That person would not be able to vote although he had already attained the age of 18 and be a resident and domiciled in Mauritius. Cabinet noted that, despite the efforts of the Office of the Electoral Commissioner to register electors, some 1,941 persons, out of whom 1,205 resided in the City of Port Louis and the towns, could not vote during the last National Assembly Elections since their names did not appear on the register of electors. The amendments would allow qualified unregistered persons to submit their claims to be registered and have their names included in a supplementary register for them to be able to vote for the forthcoming Municipal City Council and Municipal Town Councils elections, and stand as candidate. The supplementary register of electors would be an addendum to the register of electors which came into force on 15 August 2024 and would be compiled immediately after the issue of writs of elections. This would ensure that every eligible voter has the opportunity to engage in the electoral process, and that no one is deprived of his constitutional right to vote. A person would therefore be entitled to have his name entered in a supplementary register where – (a) his name was not entered on the register of electors; (b) he fulfils the criteria to be registered as an elector; and (c) he has attained or would have attained the age of 18 prior to nomination day. However, a person whose name was already entered in a register of electors for an electoral area would not be entitled to have his name entered in a supplementary register of electors. The supplementary register would come into force on the eve of the nomination day.
