filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the judgment of the Supreme Court of India in the Tiger Global case, which had raised concerns among investors regarding the potential application of General Anti-Avoidance Rules (GAAR) to pre-2017 investments, the Prime Minister had raised the matter with the Indian Prime Minister during his recent visit to India. Prime Minister Modi gave the assurance of the continued stand of India of not taking any action that would undermine the benefits that Mauritius currently enjoys under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement. On 31 March 2026 the Central Board of Direct Taxes of India has issued the Income Tax (Amendment) Rules 2026, providing further clarification on the application of the GAAR under the Indian Income Tax Act. The new Rules specify that GAAR shall apply to arrangements made on or after 01 April 2017, while investments undertaken prior to that date will remain outside its scope, thereby providing clarity on the treatment of legacy investments. The new Rules are expected to provide reassurance and certainty to foreign investors and private equity funds with respect to the taxation of exits from such investments.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Government of Mauritius and the Government of India on the modalities for the implementation of projects financed under the Special Economic Package 2025 for an amount of USD655 million. The Memorandum of Understanding provides the operational and administrative modalities for the implementation of the following projects – (a) Grant of USD215 million for the following Projects - (i) New Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam National Hospital; (ii) Ayurvedic Centre of Excellence (AYUSH); (iii) Veterinary School and Animal Hospital; and (iv) Provision of helicopters. (b) Blended Finance of USD440 million, comprising a Grant of USD8 million and a Line of Credit of USD432 million, for the following projects - (i) New Air Traffic Control Tower at the SSR International Airport; (ii) Motorway M4; (iii) Ring Road Phase II project; and (iv) Port equipment by the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd. The Memorandum of Understanding will be signed by Dr S. Jaishankar, External Affairs Minister of India, who will be in Mauritius from 09 to 11 April 2026 in the context of the Indian Ocean Conference.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the setting up of the Crisis Committee on the economic and social impacts of the present conflict in the Middle East, an Inter-Ministerial Committee was chaired by the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities on 01 April 2026. The Prime Minister will chair a meeting of the Crisis Committee on Wednesday 08 April 2026 to examine the recommendations of the Inter-Ministerial Committee.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Mr Kunal Naik has resigned as Chairperson of the National Agency for Drug Control (NADC) and has been appointed as the Chief Executive Officer of the NADC. Ms Nadia Peerun has been appointed as new Chairperson of the agency. Cabinet has further noted that the Prime Minister will chair a meeting of the National Drug Control Commission on Thursday 16 April 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the recent torrential rain in Rodrigues, an amount of Rs795,000 will be provided by the National Empowerment Foundation and an amount of Rs2 million by the National Solidarity Fund to eligible families affected by the torrential rain. An amount of Rs15,000 will be provided to 16 households each as housing support, an amount of Rs5,000 will be provided to 49 students each for school materials and an amount of Rs5,000 will be provided to 62 households each for basic needs by the National Empowerment Foundation. The National Solidarity Fund will provide ad hoc assistance to 200 families as follows – (a) an amount of Rs5,000 to a household with no child; (b) an amount of Rs6,000 to a household with one child; (c) an amount of Rs7,000 to a household with two children; and (d) an amount of Rs8,000 to a household with three children and more.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the road accident which occurred on Tuesday 31 March 2026 involving a heavy vehicle and several other vehicles at the Caudan Roundabout, the Commissioner of Police will ensure compliance with General Notice No. 2670 of 2009 pertaining to prohibition for heavy vehicles from passing through the following roads between 0700 hours and 0930 hours on weekdays – (a) Motorway M1 – from Phoenix Roundabout to Quay D Roundabout, Northbound; (b) Road A1 – from St Jean Roundabout to Place d'Armes, Port Louis via Rose Hill, Beau Bassin and Coromandel; and, from Place d'Armes, Port Louis via Coromandel, Beau Bassin and Rose Hill to St Jean Roundabout; (c) Road A14 – Rose Hill Trianon Road from Vandermersch Roundabout to M1 at Trianon via Ebène, in both directions; (d) Road B1 – Julius Nyerere Avenue from Rose Hill Police Station to M1 at Réduit via Ebène in both directions; and (e) Road B120 – Martindale Road from University of Mauritius Roundabout to M1 passing in front of the entrance to the State House, in both directions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Fonds de Solidarité Africain (Privileges and Immunities) Regulations 2026 to give effect to the privileges and immunities to which the Fonds de Solidarité Africain is entitled. The Fonds de Solidarité Africain contributes to the economic development and social progress of African Member States by facilitating its intervention techniques, access to the financial resources necessary for the implementation of investment projects and other income-generating activities. An Accord de Siège was signed in January 2023 between the Ministry of Finance and the Fonds de Solidarité Africain, providing the legal framework for the presence and operations of the Fonds de Solidarité Africain Resident Mission in Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Grant Fund Agreement between the Mauritius Meteorological Services and the Met Office, United Kingdom. The Grant Fund Agreement is an initiative of Weather and Climate Information Services (WISER) for Africa supported by the World Meteorological Organization. The objective of the initiative is to enable countries to work together to co-produce data/information for better weather/climate forecasting. Financial support to the tune of GBP1,880,000 (approx. Rs117 million) has been granted for the WISER Programme for Africa, out of which Mauritius will benefit GBP80,000 (approx. Rs5 million) to implement WISER Action preliminary activities, namely co-production of climate services, capacity building of meteorologists in advance forecasting techniques and to improve the early warning system.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of a Government Support Agreement (GSA) to improve the bankability of Renewable Energy Projects thereby contributing towards accelerating the green energy transition. The GSA is a risk-mitigation instrument intended to facilitate mobilisation of private and multilateral capital in respect of a major Renewable Energy (RE) project, meant to supply energy to the Central Electricity Board (CEB) under a Power Purchase Agreement (PPA). It is a contract between Government, represented by the Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities, the CEB and a project developer/Seller where the Government provides comfort to investors on the bankability of the approved project under the PPA. The objectives of the GSA are to - (a) improve bankability of the RE projects; (b) attract private and foreign investment; (c) protect investors and lenders against specific political and regulatory risks; (d) secure more competitive electricity tariffs; and (e) ensure continuity of power supply and enhanced long term viability of RE project.
ministries: Ministry Of Energy and Public Utilities

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Higher Education Commission (HEC) signing a Memorandum of Understanding with the University Grants Commission (UGC) of the Republic of India. The Memorandum of Understanding establishes the following key areas of collaboration – (a) UGC sharing comprehensive information on the quality assurance, accreditation, and regulation of higher education institutions in India, enabling HEC to make well-informed and reliable decisions on Indian qualifications and institutions operating in Mauritius; (b) UGC facilitating HEC's engagement with other key sector bodies in India; (c) UGC and HEC working together to strengthen quality assurance in Transnational Education, supporting the growth of Mauritius as a credible, high- quality regional higher education hub; (d) exchange of best practices in quality assurance, accreditation processes, quality culture, and regulatory frameworks; and (e) staff development, training programmes, workshops, and professional exchange to build institutional capacity on both sides.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of a new regulatory framework for authorised dealers of second-hand motor vehicles to ensure compliance with enhanced service standards to consumers and uphold fair and transparent trade practices. A Ministerial Committee was set up to review the conditions governing the issuance of licences to authorised dealers and to propose a revised framework that promotes fair competition, strengthens consumer safeguards, and supports a sustainable business environment. The Committee recommended the following – (a) Introduction of a Licensing Board/ Committee; (b) Revised Conditions for Authorised Dealer's Licences; (c) Revised Age Criteria for Importation of Second-Hand Motor Vehicles; (d) Importation of Special Purpose Vehicles; (e) Restriction on Transfer of Licences; and (f) Handling of Non-Compliant Vehicles Imports. The Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection will come up with new regulations, accordingly.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius submitting its National Report on the Implementation of the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action (BPfA) for the period 2019–2024 to the United Nations Commission on the Status of Women. The BPfA provides a comprehensive global policy framework for the advancement of women and the achievement of gender equality across 12 critical areas of concern, namely women and poverty; education and training of women; women and health; violence against women; women and the economy; women in power and decision-making; institutional mechanisms for the advancement of women; human rights of women; women and the media; women and the environment; women and armed conflict; and the girl-child. The Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare held a national review process for Mauritius to assess progress achieved and challenges encountered in consultation with relevant Ministries, Departments, civil society organisations and the private sector. The national review reflects progress made in strengthening legal and policy frameworks, reinforcing institutional mechanisms for gender equality, addressing discriminatory social norms and stereotypes, enhancing investment to address resource gaps, strengthening accountability mechanisms, improving capacity-building and monitoring systems, and fostering cooperation and partnerships at national and international levels. Progress made in the legal and policy environment, including the enactment and implementation of key legislative and policy instruments, has also been highlighted. The National Report will be made public.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of an incident which occurred at St Pierre Roman Catholic Aided Primary School where two pupils of Grades 4 and 5 displayed serious disruptive behaviour and a clear disregard of the school's authority. The pupils have subsequently been temporarily suspended by the Minister of Education and Human Resource. Given that such cases of pupils' misbehaviour are recurrent across schools, the Ministry of Education and Human Resource will re-examine the current legislative provisions in order to set up inter-institutional protocols to provide the necessary support to schools, families and pupils indulging in unsafe and inappropriate behaviours. In this context, a committee will be set up at the level of the Ministry of Education and Human Resource, comprising representatives of, inter alia, - (a) the Ministry of Health and Wellness; (b) the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (c) the Attorney-General's Office; (d) the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare; (e) the Ombudsperson for Children; (f) the Mauritius Police Force; and (g) the Probation and After Care Service. The Committee will examine proposals to amend the Children's Act, the Education Act and other relevant legislation, with a view to addressing cases involving pupils beyond control, including those related to extreme violence, sexual abuse, misuse of social media platforms, substance abuse, bullying and other related issues and will also consider setting up the relevant mechanisms for managing these pupils.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised in the context of World Health Day 2026, which is observed annually on 07 April. The theme for this year is "Together for health. Stand with science." To commemorate World Health Day 2026, the Ministry of Health and Wellness will launch the Prévention Nationale pour Maladies Non-Transmissibles Programme across communities throughout the island on 11 April 2026 at the Mediclinic, Bel Air Rivière Sèche. The activities will include non-communicable disease screening, such as diabetes testing, blood pressure monitoring, BMI and vision tests, and medical consultations. They will also comprise breast and cervical cancer screening, the display of healthy food plate models and health education sessions. The activities aim to bring essential health services closer to the population, promote early diagnosis, and encourage individuals to take proactive steps towards better health.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the holding of the Mauritius Climate Investment Forum on 06 and 07 May 2026 in Mauritius. The Forum will play an instrumental role in – (a) showcasing high-impact climate-related projects and enhance visibility on concrete actions being taken by Government to achieve the relevant sector targets and objectives as spelt out in the Government Programme 2025-2029 and the Nationally Determined Contributions 3.0; (b) connecting Government and private sector entities with development partners as well as multilateral and commercial banks to attract co-financing on a concessional basis, grants for blending and technical assistance for improving project implementation and adoption of appropriate technology; and (c) leveraging on private sector investment in public goods, which will be critical in view of the constrained fiscal situation. The theme of the Forum is "Unlocking Finance for Climate Related Projects in Mauritius". Roundtable and boardroom discussions will be organised into thematic cells, namely – (a) Renewable Energy and Water Management Cell; (b) Food Security and Blue Economy Cell; (c) Transport Sector Cell; (d) Environment and Disaster Risk Management Cell; (e) Innovative Financing Cell; (f) Mitigation and adaptation for climate-related victims; and (g) Digital Sustainability.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Secretary-General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of the Union of Comoros recently led a delegation of 13 members for a technical mission in Mauritius. The objectives of the mission were to assess the status of ongoing bilateral cooperation between Mauritius and Comoros; review pending bilateral agreements and explore new avenues of collaboration in sectors of mutual interest. The Comoros delegation engaged in meetings and working sessions with various Ministries, Departments and Institutions, namely the Prime Minister's Office, the Ministry of Finance, the Ministry of Agro- Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries, the Ministry of Health and Wellness, the Attorney-General's Office, the Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection, the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research, the Ministry of Education and Human Resource, the Mauritius Ports Authority, the Mauritius Revenue Authority, Airport Holdings Ltd and Air Mauritius. A Joint Communiqué on the outcomes of the technical mission was issued on 30 March 2026.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Minister for Finance, Economic Planning, Trade and Investment of the Republic of Seychelles led a delegation to Mauritius from 23 to 26 March 2026, comprising representatives of the Seychelles Pension Fund and the Seychelles Trading Company. The ministerial delegation held meetings with the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection, the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity as well as representatives of public institutions, namely, the Agricultural Marketing Board and the State Trading Corporation and private stakeholders.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities in the Bharat Electricity Summit in India on the theme 'Electrifying Growth, Empowering Sustainability and Connecting Globally'. The Summit brought together policymakers, global experts, utilities, innovators, investors, and technology leaders to shape the future of India's electricity ecosystem. The Minister actively participated in discussions which centered mainly around clean energy transition, power infrastructure modernisation, smart grids and digital utilities and global energy cooperation and power sector policy. In the margins of the Summit, the Minister had a meeting with the Minister of New and Renewable Energy during which he made a request for assistance from the Indian Government for the large-scale deployment of hybrid solar photovoltaic systems for 100,000 households in Mauritius. The Minister also had a meeting with the Minister of Power, during which he requested the support of the Indian government for the setting up of a Combined Cycle Gas Turbine Liquefied Natural Gas Power Station of capacity 450-500 MW, with a view to decarbonising the electricity mix and safeguarding our energy supply for the future. The Minister also had the opportunity to review progress on implementation of the 17.5 MW Floating Solar PV Project with a 12 MW Battery Energy Storage System at Tamarind Falls, being executed by National Thermal Power Corporation Limited under a Government- to-Government agreement. The Minister had a meeting with the Director General of the International Solar Alliance in the course of which they reviewed collaboration under a Country Partnership Framework.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent visit of the Minister of Youth and Sports to Rodrigues, following an invitation from the Commission for Youth and Sports, and Others of Rodrigues, in the context of the National Day celebrations. The Minister participated in several events, including the – (a) march against drugs on the theme "Choose Your Future, Not Drugs" and a pledge ceremony; (b) official ceremony marking the 58th anniversary of the Independence of Mauritius; and (c) final of the Republic Cup 2026. The Minister also had meetings with the Commissioner for Youth and Sports, and Others, sports cadres, including coaches, advisers, presidents of regional sports committees, and representatives of the Mouvement Solidarité Rodrigues to discuss several issues, such as the Rod Trail, and homologation of the Camp du Roi Stadium, the challenges Rodriguan athletes face in participating in competitions in Mauritius, and the need for specialised equipment, amongst others. Sports and recreation equipment were donated to the Commission for Youth and Sports to support local communities.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the – (a) appointment of Mrs Dooroovamanee Moher as the new Chairperson of the Sugar Industry Labour Welfare Fund, following the resignation of the former Chairperson; (b) reconstitution of the Board of the Mauritius Digital Promotion Agency, with Mr Shashi Kumar Koonja as part-time Chairperson; and (c) reconstitution of the Board of the Mauritius Emerging Technologies Council, with Dr Harris Neeliah as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note, with serious concern, of the findings of the report of the fact- finding inquiry set up by the National Human Rights Commission on the disturbances which occurred at Melrose Eastern High Security Prison on 17 July 2025. The report is being made public and referred to the Commissioner of Prisons and Commissioner of Police for any action deemed appropriate, including disciplinary actions against those Prison Officers and Police Officers involved in that serious incident. The report will also be referred to the Director of Public Prosecutions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outstanding performances of Mauritian athletes in the Para-Athletics World Championships 2025 in New Delhi, namely – (a) Ms Noemi Alphonse, MSK, won the gold medal in 100 m (T54) clocking her best season with an impressive time of 16.07 seconds; and (b) Ms Anaïs Angeline won the silver medal with a jump of 4.59 m, establishing a new African record at each of her attempts, an achievement that testifies to her remarkable talent and determination.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the report of the Committee of Inquiry, set up to look into allegations regarding the allocation of Hadj 2025 visas by the Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund, is being released today. The report has been referred to the Commissioner of Police and the Director of Public Prosecutions in view of alleged criminal offences highlighted in the report. It has also been referred to the Board of the Islamic Cultural Centre Trust Fund for any disciplinary action as appropriate against officers involved in the alleged malpractices.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Land Transport issuing drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for the preparation of a draft Bus Services Bill, intended to reform and modernise the bus services sector. The Bus Services Bill is expected to transform the bus service network into a modern, efficient and accountable system. Key elements will include - (a) clear obligations on operators and crews (drivers, conductors) as well as passenger rights and behaviour expectations; (b) code(s) of conduct for crews; (c) reviewed procedures to grant and revoke operator licences; (d) streamlined disciplinary and appeal procedures to reduce delays; (e) licensing and regulation of Stand Regulators and Traffic Officers; (f) operationalisation of a Fleet Management System to monitor ridership, performance and compliance of operators and enhancing disbursement of subsidies; (g) provision of CCTV cameras in buses; (h) creation of an integrated Bus Information System for passengers (timetables and real-time bus tracking); (i) migration to a cashless fare collection system; and (j) access of the Ministry of Land Transport and the National Land Transport Authority to accounts of bus operators for verification and audit purposes.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the implementation of an Energy Saving and Energy Efficiency Campaign for the summer period 2025-2026 in order to manage peak power demand and avoid any power shortage. The Energy Efficiency Management Office and the Central Electricity Board developed a sustained awareness plan for demand management which comprises the following areas of thrust – (a) cooperation with the Mauritius Broadcasting Corporation to promote responsible energy consumption among the public through engaging campaigns on the TV, radio and social media platforms; (b) radio and digital campaigns for social media visibility on Radio Plus, Radio 1 and Top FM; (c) collaboration with La Sentinelle Group to share a clip produced by the latter entitled 'Gérer l'énergie au quotidien' on several media platforms; (d) partnering with PopTV to promote energy-saving practices particularly during evening peak demand periods; (e) collaboration with Metro Express Ltd for engagement with commuters through airing of pre-recorded audio messages across all metro trains; (f) outreach programmes in shopping malls meant for awareness of the general public and distribution of flyers; and (g) SMS notifications by Mauritius Telecom to its subscribers urging them to curtail energy consumption, in ultimate strained situations when CEB anticipates that electricity demand would exceed available supply. The CEB has devised an Energy Alert Warning System, similar to the Cyclone Warning System, comprising a colour code (Green, Yellow, Red) based on its reserve margin. The system aims at spreading awareness and encouraging voluntary demand reduction. The alert system will be displayed on MBC TV and other communication platforms. An awareness campaign on the Colour-coded Energy Alert Warning System on all media would start in the coming weeks.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, following the proclamation of section 31, sub-sections (1) and (3), of the Animal Welfare Act, the registration and microchipping of dogs will be conducted by the Mauritius Society for Animal Welfare (MSAW) in different regions across Mauritius. The MSAW is issuing a communiqué to inform the public of the different locations where microchipping and registration services will be available.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of a fire outbreak at La Chaumière Transfer Station in the evening of 30 September 2025. The Mauritius Fire and Rescue Services was mobilised on site and by midnight, the fire was subdued and one team stayed on site overnight to monitor the situation. The Technical Enforcement Unit of the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change and the Police de l'Environnement were mobilised as well. The situation is being closely monitored by the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of consultations held with representatives of major bus companies and the Mauritius Bus Owners Cooperative Federation in relation to the implementation of measures announced in the Budget 2025-2026 to ensure the sustainability of the Bus Companies Recovery Account. The Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity, the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations, the Minister of Land Transport, the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives and the Junior Minister of Finance, met the representatives of the five major bus companies and the representatives of the Mauritius Bus Owners Cooperative Federation to discuss the issues pertaining to the implementation of the Budget measures.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the SME Mauritius Ltd signing with the Small and Medium Enterprises Corporation Malaysia a Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in the field of micro, small and medium enterprises. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding is to enable both countries to strengthen, promote and develop cooperation in the field of micro, small and medium enterprise (MSME) development through the - (a) sharing of policies, expertise and information for improving the competitiveness of Mauritian and Malaysian MSMEs in global markets; (b) exchange of information especially in the areas of digitalisation and market access; (c) implementation of promotional activities such as business fora, workshops, trainings, seminars, conferences and study visits to facilitate the development of MSMEs; (d) provision of facilities for holding of conferences and seminars in the respective countries; and (e) participation in business fairs, conferences, seminars and other events in Mauritius and Malaysia, where MSMEs would have an opportunity to establish business relations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the State Department of the United States of America has released the 25th edition of the Trafficking in Persons (TIP) Report, which provides an annual status update on the state of global anti-trafficking efforts. Each country is graded through a tier system on the strength and effectiveness of its efforts to end human trafficking within its borders. The grading of Mauritius has been maintained to Tier 2, categorised as a country that does not meet the US minimum standards but is implementing substantial efforts to align and comply with them. The 2025 TIP Report covers Government's efforts undertaken from 01 April 2024 to 31 March 2025 to the extent concurrent reporting data is available. Mauritius demonstrated overall increasing efforts to combat trafficking in persons through a series of measures, which include, inter alia, – (a) the operationalisation of the specialised Trafficking in Persons Unit in the Mauritius Police Force; and (b) the review of all employment contracts and conduct induction sessions with foreign workers upon their arrival.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Information and Communication Technologies Authority issuing a national framework for the phasing out of 3G services; and actively monitoring the phasing out by enforcing obligations on the Mobile Network Operators (MNOs). The Information and Communication Technologies Authority will adopt a regulatory approach to ensure that MNOs adhere to their respective phasing out plans while upholding their responsibilities towards consumers throughout the transition period. As part of the regulatory approach, MNOs would be required to provide continuity for voice services via 2G and provide trade-in programmes to encourage customers using only 3G handsets to upgrade to 4G/5G-capable devices. Moreover, MNOs would have to guarantee 4G and 5G network coverage or rely on 2G network coverage to prevent any degradation in service quality or coverage as 3G is phased out.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status of implementation of the Foundation Programme in Literacy, Numeracy and Skills (FPLNS) for students who have not met the minimum requirements to be awarded the Primary School Achievement Certificate. The FPLNS was introduced as a new three-year Foundation Programme in replacement of the Extended Programme, which failed to achieve the desired results. The FPLNS blends academic and vocational components, creating clearer pathways toward the Technical and Vocational Education and Training stream. The structure of the programme places emphasis on basic literacy, numeracy, and practical skills with a view to reducing the dropout rate. The overarching aim of the FPLNS is to provide a supportive learning environment that enhances students' self-esteem, confidence and skills through a curriculum adapted to their needs and aspirations. The FPLNS uses a competency-based approach for curriculum and instructional materials. It sets realistic and achievable learning outcomes tailored to students' individual learning pace and ability. Students will be continuously evaluated using rubrics that define performance at Basic, Intermediate, or Proficient level. These performance levels describe specific actions and evidence that demonstrate mastery of learning outcomes. All subjects will be graded according to these levels. At the end of the three-year Foundation Programme, students will be awarded the National Foundation Certificate of Education, as approved by the Mauritius Qualifications Authority. Teaching and learning materials, including students' worksheets and teacher guides for all subjects are available on the website of the Mauritius Institute of Education. These materials are printed and distributed to students by their respective schools.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of a Regional Training for Trainers Course for Radiation Protection Officers from 27 to 31 October 2025 in Mauritius. The training course is being organised by the International Atomic Energy Agency under its Technical Cooperation Project "RAF 9070: Enhancing the Radiation Safety Infrastructure". The objective of the course is to train potential trainers and to provide them with the necessary knowledge and materials that may be used for the training of Radiation Protection Officers. The training will contribute in upgrading their national radiation safety infrastructure and enhancing safety culture. The Regional Training Course will bring together participants from 21 French-speaking African Member States namely Algeria, Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros, Congo, Côte d'Ivoire, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Gabon, Madagascar, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Senegal, Togo and Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of National Infrastructure in the Global Infrastructure Cooperation Conference 2025 held in Seoul, South Korea. The main objectives of the Conference were to – (a) strengthen cooperation in the field of infrastructure development between South Korea and the participating countries; (b) share experiences, best practices, innovative ideas and latest trends in sustainable infrastructure development projects; and (c) facilitate networking between participating countries and financing agencies that could fund infrastructure projects. The Minister participated in a session on Railways and Roads Infrastructure including the use of Artificial Intelligence. He also participated in a high-level multilateral meeting which involved representatives of the South Korean government, several foreign Ministers and other eminent personalities. Discussions focused on government-to-government cooperation and strategies to advance collaboration for infrastructure development.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the High-Level Segment of the 80th Session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York under the theme "Better Together: 80 years and more for peace, development and human rights". In his statement during the High-Level General Debate, the Minister welcomed the International Court of Justice's advisory opinion on the Chagos Archipelago, which paved the way for an agreement with the United Kingdom. The Minister participated in the following meetings – (a) High-level International Conference for the Peaceful Settlement of the Question of Palestine; (b) 1st Biennial Summit for a Sustainable, Inclusive and Resilient Global Economy; (c) Special High-Level Event on Climate Action Convened by the UN Secretary- General; and (d) High-Level Meeting of like-minded Global South Countries hosted by India. The Minister also held meetings with the UN Secretary General, the US Deputy Secretary, the Minister of State of the United Arab Emirates, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Moroccan Expatriates of the Kingdom of Morocco, the Ministre Délégué pour les Partenariats Internationaux of France, the Vice Minister of Foreign Affairs of Germany, and the Executive Secretary of the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa. The Minister signed a Memorandum of Agreement with the President and Chief Executive Officer of the Cooperate Council of Africa to formally announce that Mauritius will host the 18th US-Africa Business Summit in August 2026. The US Africa Business Summit will bring together African Heads of State and Government, Ministers, senior US and African government officials, and top executives from leading American and African companies to explore and expand trade and investment opportunities.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 03 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the official visit of the Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare to Rodrigues in the context of the handing over of the Gender Policy for Rodrigues to the Commissioner for Women's Affairs and Others. The Gender Policy for Rodrigues, which was developed by a Consultant from the European Union in 2024, marks a significant milestone in promoting gender equality and guiding policy formulation in Rodrigues. In her address during the handing over ceremony, the Minister emphasised that the Policy will be instrumental in, inter alia, shaping future actions to improve women's socio-economic empowerment and in the combat against gender-based violence. The Minister further highlighted that accelerating efforts towards gender equality will ultimately enhance family welfare and benefit the community at large. In the margins of the ceremony, the Minister paid courtesy visits on the Chief Commissioner and the Commissioner for Women's Affairs and Others, where they engaged in fruitful discussions on strategic actions to promote gender equality, children's rights and family welfare in the local Rodrigues context. They also shared experiences on reporting and monitoring of gender-based violence cases and discussed areas of collaboration to improve service delivery. The Minister attended a consultative meeting with representatives of the Commission for Women's Affairs and Others and the Police Department on Child Day Care Centres in Rodrigues. Moreover, a working session on the draft Domestic Abuse Bill and draft Adoption Bill was held with representatives of the Commission for Women's Affairs and Others and the Police Department. The Minister participated in an interactive session organised by the National Women Entrepreneur Council with women entrepreneurs in Rodrigues.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Constitution (Amendment) Bill and the Criminal Code (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Constitution (Amendment) Bill would be to repeal section 7(2) of the Constitution so as to provide that any law which authorises the infliction of any description of punishment, such as torture or inhuman or degrading treatment, would be held to be inconsistent with, or in contravention of, the Constitution. This amendment to the Constitution had been rendered necessary with a view to implementing certain recommendations made by the United Nations Committee against Torture at its meeting held on 30 November 2017, during the review of Mauritius on its fourth periodic report. The Criminal Code (Amendment) Bill would also be introduced to implement certain recommendations made by the United Nations Committee against Torture. Accordingly, manslaughter committed by any person on his spouse, as well as on his accomplice, at the very moment he finds them in the act of adultery would no more be an excusable offence.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Vallée d'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation (Repeal) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill would be to provide for the repeal of the Vallée d'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation Act. The National Parks and Conservation Service would, under the Native Terrestrial Biodiversity and National Parks Act, take over the functions and powers of the Vallée d'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation and the Vallée d'Osterlog Endemic Garden would henceforth be managed, administered, conserved, maintained and developed by the National Parks and Conservation Service. The Bill would also provide for matters connected and related thereto and, consequently, the Native Terrestrial Biodiversity and National Parks Act would be amended.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the – (a) promulgation of the Income Tax (Financial Assistance for Payment of Special Allowance) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 to provide financial support to employers of the transportation sector, including Metro Express Ltd, facing financial difficulties for the payment of the 14th Month Bonus; and (b) payment of salary compensation 2025 to employers in the transportation sector, including Metro Express Ltd, facing financial difficulties for the period January to June 2025. The amount of financial support for the payment of 14th Month Bonus payable to the enterprises would be as follows – (a) full amount of the 14th Month Bonus paid to full-time employees if the enterprise had incurred losses or its profits for the year of assessment 2023-2024 were reduced by more than 50% when the impact of the increased National Minimum Wage in January 2024, Salary Compensation 2024 and 14th Month Bonus were factored in; and (b) 50% of the 14th Month Bonus paid to their full-time employees if the enterprise's profits were reduced by more than 10% but not exceeding 50% when impact of the increased National Minimum Wage in January 2024, Salary Compensation 2024 and 14th Month Bonus were factored in. The amount of financial support for the payment of salary compensation 2025 would be, inter alia, as follows – (a) Rs610 monthly per employee where the employer had incurred losses or whose profits for the year of assessment 2023-2024 would be reduced by more than 50% after appropriate adjustments including payment of the 14th Month Bonus and salary compensation 2025 were factored in; and (b) Rs305 monthly per employee where the employer profits for the year of assessment 2023-2024 would be reduced by more than 10% but not exceeding 50% after appropriate adjustments including payment of the 14th Month Bonus and salary compensation 2025 were factored in.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the outbreak of Chikungunya in Mauritius since 15 March 2025, 19 local cases and seven imported cases had been reported as at 01 April 2025. As regards Rodrigues, three cases had been detected, two of which were imported. The Ministry of Health and Wellness in collaboration with other Ministries, Departments, Parastatal Bodies and private sector entities, was taking all necessary public health and containment measures to minimise the spread of the disease in Mauritius and Rodrigues. In view of the prevailing situation regarding Chikungunya, control measures in Mauritius and Rodrigues were being reinforced. Cabinet further noted that the Chikungunya (Health Assessment of Incoming Passengers from Reunion Island) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for any person travelling to the Republic of Mauritius from Reunion Island, except citizens or residents of Mauritius, airline and vessel crew and transit passengers to produce a PCR test certificate certifying a negative result for Chikungunya, prior to embarkation. The regulations would also provide that all incoming passengers from Reunion Island may, at the health counter of the Port and Airport in Mauritius or Rodrigues, be required to – (a) answer questions about his health or relevant circumstances, including travel history and information about other persons with whom he might have travelled or had contact with; (b) produce any document and give any information that might assist in assessing his health; and (c) provide his contact details or such other sufficient and precise information so that he might be contacted immediately. Any person who contravenes the regulations shall commit an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine not exceeding Rs10,000 and to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years. The regulations would be effective as from 09 April 2025 and be valid until 08 May 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of an Executive Order by the President of the United States implementing a "Reciprocal Tariff Policy" aimed at addressing large and persistent annual United States trade deficits in goods. The Executive Order imposes a baseline 10% tariff on all imported goods as from 05 April 2025. In so far as Mauritius is concerned, the tariff, based on the trade deficit between Mauritius and the United States, has been fixed at 40%. A Working Group had been set up at the level of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade to discuss the possible impact of that policy measure and the way forward for Mauritius, in consultations with relevant public and private stakeholders, including the Mauritius Chamber of Commerce and Industry, the Mauritius Export Association and Business Mauritius.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Dental Council (Medical Institutions) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for the recognition and listing of three additional dental institutions, namely Barkatullah Vishwavidyalaya and Kaloji Narayana Rao University of Health Sciences of India as well as Centro Escolar University of Philippines.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Health and Wellness was reviewing the mechanism regarding cases of alleged medical negligence within Public Health Institutions. A Medical Negligence Committee, chaired by Mrs Rajamanee Veerapen, Senior Adviser, was being set up to investigate cases of suspected medical negligence. The Medical Negligence Committee would convene any officer concerned and the complainant(s) for further explanations and submit a report with recommendations to the Senior Chief Executive of the Ministry of Health and Wellness within two weeks. In addition, the following measures were being introduced to improve patients' safety in the healthcare system – (a) weekly mortality meetings chaired by the Regional Health Directors to review cases of death reported in the hospitals, identify preventable causes, and address lapses in care. Monthly reports would be submitted to the Director of Health Services for follow-up; and (b) clinical auditing across all health regions to identify errors with a view to reducing medical negligence. In the same line, healthcare providers would be trained in auditing, and findings would be incorporated into the Continuing Professional Development Programme. Monthly audit reports would be submitted to Regional Health Directors to track progress and ensure continuous improvement in care.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of the First Mauritius–European Union Partnership Dialogue on 10 April 2025 in Mauritius. Partnership Dialogues between the European Union and each member of the African, Caribbean, and Pacific States Organisation are provided for under the Samoa Agreement. It seeks to modernise the partnership to address contemporary global issues and aims at transforming the traditional donor-recipient model into a partnership of equals. The agenda for the Partnership Dialogue would include, inter alia, Economic and Trade Issues, Ocean Affairs and Maritime Security, Climate Change, Biodiversity and Food Security, European Union Global Gateway Initiative, Human Rights and Governance as well as Research, Innovation and Artificial Intelligence.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the African Senior Weightlifting Championships would be held from 20 to 28 April 2025 in Mauritius. Participants from some 20 countries and Mauritius would be represented by 16 senior weightlifters at the Championships.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the performance of the Export-Oriented Enterprises sector over the period January to December 2024, compared to the corresponding period in 2023. The Export-Oriented Enterprises sector comprises 228 firms employing some 31,400 workers, including expatriates. After a rebound in 2022 (post COVID-19) to reach an all-time high of Rs49.9 billion in exports value, the Export-Oriented Enterprises sector experienced two subsequent years of decline. The value of exports of the Export-Oriented Enterprises sector, which stood at Rs48 billion over the period January to December 2023, decreased by 5.3% to reach Rs45.5 billion (Rs-2.5 billion) during the corresponding period in 2024. The Export-Oriented Enterprises sector comprises several sub-sectors, regrouped into two main categories, namely "Textiles & Clothing" and "Non-Textiles". Only the "Live Primates", "Textile (Yarn & Fabrics)" and "Medical Devices" sub-sectors registered an increase in the value of exports in 2024. All other sub-sectors experienced decline in the value of exports where the "Jewellery and Precious Stones" sub-sector was the hardest hit during that period.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of Les Assises de L'Industrie 2025, held on 13 February 2025, which aimed at identifying challenges and constraints hindering industrial growth and proposing remedial actions to reinforce the reputation of Mauritius as a regional manufacturing hub. Some 120 representatives from public and private sector organisations, having a major stake in supporting industrial development in the country participated in the Assises. The recommendations emanating from Les Assises de L'Industrie 2025 were being examined by the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in consultations with relevant stakeholders.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in the 21st General Session of the African-Asian Rural Development Organization (AARDO) Conference held in New Delhi. The Minister intervened during the Country Statements by Ministers and expressed his gratitude to AARDO for its collaboration for more than 50 years, through the provision of a wide range of courses to enhance the knowledge and upgrade the skills of human resources. In the margins of the Conference, the Minister had bilateral meetings with the Minister of State for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises of the Republic of India, the Minister of State of Rural Development and Communication of the Republic of India and the Minister of Agriculture, Hydraulic Resources and Fisheries of the Republic of Tunisia.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation in the AI Action Summit in Paris. The objective of the AI Action Summit was to establish scientific foundations, solutions and standards for sustainable AI for collective progress and in public interest. The Minister intervened as speaker in the Ministerial Panel on AI Readiness Assessment in Action on the theme "Preparing AI for the World. Preparing the World for AI." His intervention related to the key lessons and best practices learnt in readying Government policies, strategies, and people for the AI transition and the challenges and opportunities available. In the side-line of the Summit, the Minister had several meetings with, inter alia, the Secretary General of the International Telecommunications Union, the Cabinet Secretary of the Ministry of Information, Communications and Digital Economy of the Republic of Kenya, the Chief Executive Officer of Google, the Permanent Delegate of Latvia to UNESCO and the Chief of the Small Island Developing States Section of UNESCO.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation in the Global Digital Summit 2025 under the theme "Digital Pathways for All", in Washington D.C. The objective of the Global Digital Summit 2025 was to have under one roof, the major partners and clients of the World Bank, including representatives from both the public and private sectors, for knowledge sharing, open exchange and valuable discussions about working together on cutting-edge digital technologies. In the side-line of the Summit, the Minister had several meetings with, inter alia, the Coordinator for International Communications and Information Policy of the Bureau of Cyberspace and Digital Policy of the United States State Department, the Secretary General of the International Telecommunications Union, the Director General and the Director-General Elect of the International Telecommunications Satellite Organization and representatives of the World Bank.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 04 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Council of the Mauritius Society for Animal Welfare, with Ms Anuja Ghoorah as part-time Chairperson; (b) Pharmacy Board, with Dr Ashwamedsing Dinassing, Acting Director General, Health Services as part-time Chairperson; (c) Dangerous Chemicals Control Board, with Dr (Mrs) S. Gaya, Head Occupational Health Unit as part-time Chairperson; (d) Board of the National Women's Sports Commission, also known as the Commission Nationale du Sport Féminin with Mrs Noëlle Sindy Christian as part-time Chairperson; (e) Licensing Board of the National Land Transport Authority, with Mr Mohammad Hisham Ahmed Oozeer as part-time Chairperson; (f) Appeal Committee of the National Land Transport Authority, with Mr Sandilen Calliapen as part-time Chairperson; (g) Motor Vehicle Insurance Arbitration Committee, with Mr Nadeem Hyderkhan as part-time Chairperson; (h) Executive Committee of the National Women Entrepreneur Council with Mrs Rehema Fatuma Koylash as part-time Chairperson; (i) Board of the National Women's Council, with Mrs Anoo Murachpersad–Lanfray as part-time Chairperson; and (j) Board of the National Adoption Council, with Mrs Marie Corina Catherine Christ as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Law Reform Commission Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to repeal the Law Reform Commission Act and re-enact a more appropriate and modern legislative framework. The Law Reform Commission under this new legislation will, in keeping with the laws of Mauritius under review, have wider functions and powers to propose reforms aimed at simplifying, modernising and consolidating the laws of Mauritius. In addition, the Law Reform Commission will be empowered to conduct post-legislative analysis of any enactment which has been wholly or partly based on its recommendations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the release of a communiqué by the Prime Minister's Office, inviting all stakeholders, including political parties, the academia, the Mauritian diaspora, trade unions, NGOs, civil society organisations as well as the public at large to submit their proposals and suggestions on electoral reforms by Friday 30 January 2026. Thereafter, a document will be published.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Social Integration and Empowerment (School Materials Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 to amend the "School Materials" Scheme so that financial assistance in terms of grant shall be provided for the purchase of school materials such as bags, uniforms, shoes, copybooks and stationery to any student between three to 23 years of a household on the Social Register of Mauritius. For Academic Year 2026, it is expected that around 11,565 students (7,141 in Mauritius and 4,424 in Rodrigues) of households under the Social Register of Mauritius would be eligible to benefit from the "School Materials" Scheme.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the payment of pension to beneficiaries for the month of January 2026 will be effected on 31 December 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Housing and Lands to follow up on the implementation of the Government Programme 2025-2029. The Committee will comprise – (a) the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (b) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (c) the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; and (d) the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee under the co-chair of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade and the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives, to look into the organisation of the US-Africa Business Summit to be held in Mauritius from 26-29 July 2026. Cabinet has further taken note that a technical committee from the Corporate Council on Africa was in Mauritius this week to discuss the arrangements being made for the Summit.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed, in relation to the night markets to be held during the festive season in different localities, to allow shop owners to display their products in front of their premises, against payment of the relevant occasional fee applicable to hawkers.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of a 'Course de l'Unité' by the Minister of Youth and Sports, in collaboration with several stakeholders, on Sunday 07 December 2025 on the theme 'Morisien mobilize kont ladrog'. The objectives of the event are – (a) to sensitise the population about the scourge of drugs; (b) to promote an active and healthy lifestyle; (c) to provide a platform for athletes, para-athletes and citizens to compete in a national relay; (d) to foster national unity and social cohesion by involving citizens from towns, villages, and Rodrigues; (e) to encourage inclusivity, since the event is open to men and women, including persons with disabilities; and (f) to strengthen institutional partnerships through the involvement of several governmental institutions in the furtherance of a common and noble goal. The 'Course de l'Unité' will bring together some 1,500 participants from Mauritius and Rodrigues and will comprise the following activities – (a) a main relay race of 46.6 km, starting from the Town Hall of the Municipal Council of Curepipe; (b) a wheelchair race of 3.8 km for some 15 para-athletes and which will start at the Plaza, Rose Hill; (c) a 'Fun Race' of 2.1 km for some 130 women of the National Women's Sports Commission; and (d) a march of approximately 1.2 km with approximately 1,000 participants, including licensees from national sports federations. The first three winners of the main relay race (46.6 km) will receive cash prizes of Rs100,000, Rs75,000, and Rs50,000, respectively.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities that the Human Rights Division of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade will organise in the context of the Human Rights Day 2025, which is celebrated around the world every year on 10 December. The Human Rights Day marks the day when the United Nations General Assembly adopted, on 10 December 1948, the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. The theme chosen by the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights is "Human Rights, Our Everyday Essentials", with emphasis that human rights are positive, essential and attainable. An awareness session will be held on gender-based violence for women and girls attending/working in empowerment centres, in line with the UN Secretary-General's "UNiTE to End Violence against Women" campaign. The Convention on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination, which has been translated in "Kreol Morisien" and published in the form of a small booklet, will also be launched.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the release of sterile male mosquitoes in Triolet with a view to reducing the mosquito population of Aedes albopictus, as a measure to combat dengue and chikungunya outbreaks. The Ministry of Health and Wellness will proceed with the upscaling of the sterile mosquito production as part of the national preventive strategy against dengue and chikungunya. Following a multi-criteria decision analysis approach to select localities for the release of those sterile males, the region of Triolet (Winner's area) was identified as a priority area, given its previous distribution of dengue and chikungunya cases and the current Aedes albopictus density. As such, some 80,000 sterile male mosquitoes will be released as from 05 December 2025 for a period of at least two months. Sensitisation of the residents in Triolet has started on 01 December 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Research and Innovation Council has been honoured with the "Startup Research and Development Initiative of the Year" award at the Africa Startup Ecosystem Builders Summit & Awards 2025, in recognition of its leadership in bridging research and commercialisation for supporting innovation from concept to market. The Africa Startup Ecosystem Builders Summit & Awards is a continental initiative that brings together entrepreneurs, investors, ecosystem developers, and policymakers to recognise excellence in ecosystem-building across Africa.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the "United Nations Convention against Cybercrime: Strengthening International Cooperation for Combating Certain Crimes Committed by Means of Information and Communications Technology Systems and for the Sharing of Evidence in Electronic Form of Serious Crimes" was signed by the Permanent Representative of Mauritius to the United Nations in New York on 19 November 2025 at the UN Headquarters in New York. 73 countries have signed the Convention so far.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that at its 93rd General Assembly held in Marrakech, Morocco, from 24 to 27 November 2025, INTERPOL adopted a Resolution entitled "Supporting the Ratification and Implementation of the United Nations Convention against Cybercrime and Promoting the Role of INTERPOL", which calls upon INTERPOL's 196 member States to, inter alia, - (a) recognise the need for a unified international approach to cybercrime and continue to cooperate and share information through INTERPOL; (b) consider signing and ratifying the Convention in accordance with the applicable national laws and procedures; and (c) strengthen the use of INTERPOL's secure communication systems and tools along with capacity-building and operational assistance for relevant national units.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the setting up of an Artificial Intelligence Unit under the aegis of the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation. In accordance with the "Blueprint for Mauritius: A Bridge to the Future - Digital Transformation 2025–2029", the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation has initiated a Public–Private–People Partnership consultation process to develop a strategy which will cover the following aspects – (a) defining principles for the ethical and responsible development, deployment and use of Artificial Intelligence; (b) development of an effective regulatory framework; (c) training and awareness; (d) implementation of intelligent automation, virtual assistants and predictive analytics in government services; and (e) establishing the framework for research partnerships with universities, think tanks and the private sector. The Artificial Intelligence Unit will be responsible for the implementation of the strategy and will, in due course, be transformed into an Artificial Intelligence Office.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to all Ministries and Departments using the Digital Interactive Virtual Assistant (DIVA) as the official chatbot. The Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation is presently working on the revamping of the existing Government chatbot. The revamped Chatbot, which will still be named DIVA, will introduce a future-ready ecosystem of AI agents. These agents will autonomously support citizens and businesses by providing personalised guidance, proactive notifications, automated form-filling and real-time case updates. The Chatbot will be rolled out in a phased manner, starting with 15 Ministries and Departments in phase I. Additional features such as proactive alerts and notifications as well as document processing and other agents will be included in subsequent phases.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Mauritian delegation, led by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade to the 30th Conference of Parties (COP30) to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, held in Belém, Brazil. The Conference adopted a political package entitled the "Global Mutirão: Uniting humanity in a global mobilisation against climate change", which inter alia, calls for urgent action across mitigation, adaptation, loss and damage, and implementation without, however, prescribing detailed roadmaps. In the sidelines of the Conference, Mauritius co-hosted with the Commonwealth Secretariat and Sri Lanka, a side event on "Scaling inclusive climate finance: Delivering the USD1.3T Roadmap for People, Economies and Nature", which attracted high attendance with discussions focusing on the need to seek innovative sources of funding without doing away with concessional and grant-based funding. Cabinet has also taken note of the immediate benefits to Mauritius following its participation in COP30, including – (a) support under the Adaptation Fund and Green Climate Fund to help entities from both the public and private sectors for accreditation; and (b) the NDC Partnership hiring a Technical Coordinator, who is expected to join the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change before the end of December 2025, to help match climate funds with projects and develop bankable proposals to access same.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection to the 30th Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) Partnership Summit 2025, held in India. The Minister attended the Inaugural Session of the Summit along with Ministers of Commerce from across the globe and addressed the Opening Session of the Summit as well as the Plenary Session on Global Financial Architecture. He also attended the Valedictory Session of the CII Partnership Summit. The Minister also had a bilateral meeting with the Minister of Commerce and Industry of the Republic of India, with the aim of deepening the enhanced strategic partnership between India and Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Education and Human Resource to Reunion Island. The purpose of the visit to Reunion Island was to enable the Ministry of Education and Human Resource to gain a deeper understanding of the functioning of the French education system and for - (a) strengthening the use of French language in a multilingual context - a shared challenge and opportunity for the two territories; (b) the development of modern vocational and technical training, aligned with the needs of the green and digital economies, which constitutes a shared strategic objective; and (c) innovation and experiments that are central to the transformation of our systems, with possible focuses on digital education, the school climate, education for sustainable development or assessment practices. The Minister met with the Préfet of Reunion Island and the Recteur of l'Académie de La Réunion during the visit. He also signed an Accord Cadre de Coopération Éducative, to strengthen research and cooperation on bilingual education.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 05 DECEMBER 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Board of Trustees of La Clinique Mauricienne for the year 2026; (b) Tourism Employees Welfare Fund Board, with Mr Rupesh Gujadhur as part-time Chairperson; and (c) Mauritius Tourism Promotion Authority Board, with Mr Dinesh Burrenchobay as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 10 APRIL 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of a series of measures to mitigate the adverse impacts of the conflict in the Middle East on the Mauritian economy and society. A meeting of the Crisis Committee on the economic and social impacts of the present conflict in the Middle East was held, under the chair of the Prime Minister, on 08 April 2026. The Committee noted that on the same day, Iran and the United States had agreed to a conditional two-week cease-fire, during which shipping traffic will be allowed through the Strait of Hormuz. The Prime Minister pointed out that we should not be lulled into complacency as it is a fragile cease-fire. Although the situation might improve, the Committee resolved that immediate, medium-term and long-term strategies would have to be adopted to mitigate the ripple effects of the conflict on the economy, households and vulnerable groups. In this context and keeping in mind that due to the legacy left by the previous government, with a high budget deficit and an elevated debt level, there is limited fiscal space and a balancing act is required. Cabinet has, thus, agreed to the implementation of the following measures - (a) Energy Security  Continue with the frequent reporting on fuel and gas stocks, incoming vessels and potential shortages for better planning and proactive measures;  Intensify the public awareness campaign on energy-saving (lights, AC, motors);  Finalise the regulations to impose restrictions on usage of grid power for superfluous activities that are not required for safety or security such as -  Operating illuminated advertising boards, billboards or digital displays;  Illuminating building facades for aesthetic purposes and decorative/ornamental lighting; and  Inefficient operation of air-conditioning systems in commercial and institutional premises.  Introduce Minimum Energy Performance Standard for refrigerators and air conditioning;  Encourage work-from-home and flexi time arrangements;  Encourage carpooling to maximise vehicle occupancy and reduce fuel consumption;  Maximise the use of coal and biomass fuels to generate electricity and reduce usage of heavy fuel oil;  Plan the maintenance schedules of power stations to make optimum use of coal and biomass generation;  Finalise a G-to-G arrangement with the Indian Government to secure a supplier for the supply of heavy fuel oil;  Maintain a minimum strategic fuel reserve (e.g., 30–45 days);  Explore pooled procurement with neighbouring countries to benefit from economies of scale;  Allow industries to swap from heavy fuel oil to marine fuel oil;  Provide additional incentives to households and businesses for reducing peak-hour consumption;  Encourage use of self-produced renewable energy and promote new renewable energy usage;  Unlock the various renewable energy projects of a total of 405 MW in the pipeline;  Fast-track approvals and grid-connection waivers for rooftop solar on public buildings, hospitals, and critical infrastructure, with upfront financing or on-bill mechanisms;  Development Bank of Mauritius (DBM) Ltd to be requested to consider low interest loans for renewable energy scheme for households and SMEs;  Fast track approval for projects under the CEB Agrivoltaics Scheme; and  Increasing electricity tariffs by 15% with effect from 01 May 2026, while excluding those on the Social Register of Mauritius, consumers falling under the 110A, 215 and 315 tariff categories as well as SMEs. A total of 128,800 consumers out of 541,127 will be excluded. Cabinet has also taken note that the Agreement with Indian Oil Corporation Ltd for the procurement of petroleum products is being finalised following discussions held during the visit of the External Affairs Minister of India, Dr Subrahmanyam Jaishankar. (b) Food Security  Set up a centralised database to monitor stock level of food supply, seeds, fertilizer, and meat products to enable timely corrective actions, including identification of alternative sources;  Secure the supply of fertilizers from different sources, including India;  Develop a calendar of production and supply of local food commodities to better plan production;  Diversify sources of food supplies, including livestock from Madagascar, Namibia and Kenya, to avoid disruption in distribution;  Enhance food and livestock production in Rodrigues;  Negotiate G-to-G agreement with friendly countries (e.g., India and France) to secure supplies of food products at affordable cost;  Intensify site inspections to prevent abusive pricing practices and artificial withholding of stocks; and  Implement a national campaign against food wastage. (c) Tourism  Seat capacity has been restored through additional direct flights and temporary frequency increases (6 supplementary flights by MK for period 23 March to 29 April 2026 to Paris, and 3 additional flights per week by Turkish Airlines for a period of one year);  Intensify destination marketing to reassure travellers;  Support rapid redeployment of marketing efforts towards better- performing markets such as South Africa, India, Australia and Reunion Island; and  Follow-up on the resumption of flights by air lines during the cease fire. (d) SMEs  Introduce an SME Resilience, and Import & Export Continuity Support Scheme to provide targeted and time-bound support to affected SMEs, including maintenance of the existing Freight Rebate Scheme;  Implement measures to ease liquidity constraints and support business continuity, including provision of a moratorium period or rescheduling of loans extended by the DBM Ltd and other relevant financial institutions;  A Working Capital Loan Scheme by the DBM Ltd to enable SMEs to meet short-term working capital requirements will be considered; and  Fast-track the operationalisation of the VOSTRO Account arrangements with India to facilitate settlement of trade transactions in local currencies. (e) Vulnerable Groups  Extension of the list of eligible commodities under the Price Stabilisation Fund to include other essential products whose prices have increased significantly. Cabinet has further taken note that the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity in consultation with the Ministry of Finance is looking into the review of the income threshold to determine the eligibility of a household to be placed on the Social Register of Mauritius.
