The Minister had the opportunity to meet with the Federal Minister for Economic Cooperation and Development of Germany, the President of the Higher Council for the Rights of Persons, the President of the International Disability Alliance, Ministers from Barbados, Nigeria, the United Kingdom, Eswatini, Malawi, Nigeria as well as Ministers from the Francophone African Countries. He also held bilateral meetings with the Minister of Persons with Disabilities and Vulnerable Groups of the Democratic Republic of Congo and with the representatives of delegation from Kenya and Fiji.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 18 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the 39th Council of the Indian Ocean Commission in Victoria, Seychelles. The Council of Ministers was held in preparation of the 5th Heads of State and Government Summit of the Indian Ocean Commission to be held on 24 April 2025 in Madagascar.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 18 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the 11th session of the Africa Regional Forum on Sustainable Development (ARFSD) in Kampala, Uganda. The overall objective of the Regional Forum was to follow up on and review the progress made towards the achievement of the Sustainable Goals of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the aspirations, goals and targets of Agenda 2063 - The Africa We Want, of the African Union by 2030. The Junior Minister participated as a panelist in the session on "Ocean Preservation and Sustainable Use" and chaired a panel discussion on "Life below water", whereby participants reflected on African progress towards the achievement of Sustainable Development Goal 14. During the 11th session of the ARFSD, election of the new bureau of the Regional Forum was held, with a mandate of one year. The new Bureau had been constituted with Uganda as chair and Mauritius as first vice-chair.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 18 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the – (a) appointment of Mr France Christophe Lactive, as Vice-President of the Redundancy Board; (b) reconstitution of the Clinical Research Regulatory Council, with Dr Rajendranath Goordoyal, as part-time Chairperson; (c) reconstitution of the Board of the Bus Industry Employees Welfare Fund, with Mr Ballenden Brian Tandrayen as part-time Chairperson; and (d) reconstitution of the Mauritius Film Development Board, with Mr Ramanand Mattadeen as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent stand of the President of the United States of America regarding the Chagos issue and the status of the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill in the United Kingdom House of Lords. Government is following up closely on the matter. Cabinet has also taken note that four Chagossians have landed on Ile du Coin, part of Peros Banhos atoll in the Chagos Archipelago on 16 February 2026. Subsequently, the British authorities issued a removal/eviction order. Lawyers representing the Chagossians have applied for an injunction to prevent their eviction. Cabinet has further taken note that the delegation from the United States of America which was proposing to visit Mauritius on 24 and 25 February 2026 for the Mauritius-US partnership talks has postponed its mission.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the current situation in South Africa with regard to the Foot and Mouth Disease affecting cattle and which has been declared a national disaster by the South African Government. In this context, Cabinet has decided that no new import permits for cattle from South Africa will be granted for the moment. Arrangements are being made for importation of cattle from other countries.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Fédération Mauricienne de Cyclisme will organise the Tour de Maurice 2026 from 02 to 05 June 2026 with the participation of 16 international teams and four national teams. The first Meeting of the organising committee will be chaired by the Deputy Prime Minister on 27 February 2026, in the presence of the Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change, the Minister of Tourism, the Minister of Youth and Sports, the Minister of Land Transport, the Minister of Local Government, the Junior Minister of Youth and Sports, the representatives of District and Municipal Councils as well as the Fédération Mauricienne de Cyclisme.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that tenders for the Rivière des Anguilles Dam Project have been launched and the procurement exercise is expected to be completed by August 2026, following which works are scheduled to start by October 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of the Intergovernmental Body Allocation contract between UNESCO and Le Morne Heritage Trust Fund (LMHTF) regarding the allocation of USD39,850 to LMHTF for consultancy services for the revision of the Local Economic Development (LED) Plan of Le Morne Cultural Landscape World Heritage Property. The grant allocated by the UNESCO World Heritage Centre will be used to enlist an international expert, a national expert, a facilitator and field workers for the preparation of the LED Plan within a period of six months. The project lays emphasis on the protection, conservation and sustainable valorisation of national heritage, while promoting inclusive socio-economic development at community level. The revision of the LED plan of Le Morne Cultural Landscape World Heritage Property will strengthen the integration of heritage conservation with local economic development, enhance community participation and capacity-building.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following recommendations of the Director of Audit, the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity is enquiring into cases of female spouses having contracted a registered marriage in accordance with Muslim rites and who are entitled to the payment of a surviving spouse allowance. Payment in 15 such cases had been stopped in December 2025 and will now be restored pending the outcome of the inquiry.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Monitoring Committee has been set up to look into the problem of sea pollution in the region of Port Louis following the lagoonal monitoring study in respect of the quality of seawater and fish from Pointe aux Sables to Baie du Tombeau conducted during the period January 2021 to December 2024. The report on the lagoonal monitoring study in the region of Port Louis (from Baie du Tombeau to Pointe aux Sables) will be made public.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the setting-up on an Inter-Ministerial Committee on Pig Breeding under the chair of the Minister of Housing and Lands, and comprising the Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change, the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries, the Minister of Energy and Public Utilities and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following recent cases of leptospirosis in Mauritius, a Monitoring and Coordination Committee chaired by the Minister of Health and Wellness, and comprising representatives of the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change, the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries, the Ministry of Local Government as well as local authorities, has been set up to ensure that adequate measures are being taken for rodent control.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations is currently enquiring into industrial relations issues reported by Trade Unions on behalf of employees of Princes Tuna (Mauritius) Ltd.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a new Municipal Gymnasium will be inaugurated on 21 February 2026 at Ebène. The Gymnasium will provide a modern sports infrastructure to inhabitants of Quatre Bornes, whilst also serving as a hub to encourage sports for different age groups through the promotion of physical fitness, sports and community well-being. The Municipal Gymnasium will be named as "Sir Satcam Boolell Municipal Gymnasium" and the complex housing the gymnasium as "Sir Satcam Boolell Municipal Sports Complex".

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the second Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment of Non-Profit Organisations (NPOs) in Mauritius. For the purpose of the risk assessment, a dedicated working group was established comprising representatives, inter alia, from the Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning, the Financial Services Commission, the Registrar of Associations, the Registrar of Companies, the Registrar of Foundations, the Board of Waqf Commissioners, the Financial Intelligence Unit, the Mauritius Police Force, the Bank of Mauritius, the Mauritius Revenue Authority, the Attorney-General's Office, the Financial Crimes Commission and NPO representatives. A multi-sectoral approach was adopted, with the active participation of relevant competent authorities such as the NPO regulators, the Financial Services Commission, the Mauritius Police Force, the Financial Intelligence Unit, and representatives from the NPOs and the banking sector. An important goal of the risk assessment is to ensure that all relevant stakeholders fully understand the identified risks and take effective measures to mitigate them. The Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning will launch and disseminate the public report of the Second Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment of Non-Profit Organisations in Mauritius during a workshop which will be facilitated by the European Union Global Facility Experts on 24 and 25 February 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the recommendations of the International Advisory Committee on Cardiovascular, Diabetes and Renal Diseases which was established in October 2025 to support the implementation of evidence-based treatment strategies tailored to the specific needs of the Mauritian healthcare system, namely - (a) requesting the World Health Organization at the forthcoming World Health Assembly to update global recommendations regarding screening for prediabetes and diabetes, and review of the diagnostic criteria for prediabetes and diabetes; (b) establishing an Inter-Ministerial Committee on Social Determinants of Health; (c) introducing structured health-literacy and lifestyle-education programmes across all school levels; (d) re-establishing the Non-Communicable Disease, Health Promotion & Research Unit under qualified medical-scientific leadership; (e) establishing a National Centre of Excellence for diabetes, cardiovascular and renal care; (f) creating a Research Trust Fund under the Ministry of Health and Wellness; (g) linking the national screening programme to a structured Diabetes Prevention Programme and a multidisciplinary National Diabetes Remission Programme; (h) developing a transparent, risk-adjusted Mauritius Scorecard for patient protection; (i) linking career progression at all levels to meaningful participation in clinical audit, research, and quality-improvement initiatives; (j) modernising the National Retinal Screening Programme with technical guidance from Professor David Owens; (k) implementing a National ST Elevation Myocardial Infarction and Stroke Network with a centralised hotline integrating SAMU, 24/7 Percutaneous Coronary Intervention and 24/7 thrombolysis for stroke patients; and (l) implementing a national chronic kidney disease prevention programme.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the renewal of the Bilateral Agreement between the Government of Mauritius and the Government of Monaco relating to the implementation of an Exchange Programme between the Ecole Hôtelière Sir Gaëtan Duval and the Lycée Technique et Hôtelier de Monaco, now known as Lycée Rainier III de Monaco, with a view to improving students' skills in the hotel and restaurant sector through international exposure, and enhancing trainers' knowledge in the areas of cooking, pastry and wine service. The Exchange Programme provided in the Agreement caters for – (a) three Mauritian students to benefit from courses and internship in the fields of cooking, pastry and wine service for an academic year consisting of nine months; (b) three Monegasque students to benefit from an internship at Ecole Hôtelière Sir Gaëtan Duval for a period of four months per academic year; (c) either one lecturer from Monaco to conduct a "training of trainers" at Ecole Hôtelière Sir Gaëtan Duval or for a lecturer from the latter school to attend a training session in Monaco for a period of one week; and (d) each Government to bear the cost of airfare of their respective students and trainer, while the cost of accommodation of the latter is borne by the host countries.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the two Committees set up in the context of the Reform of the Pension System as announced in the Budget Speech 2025-2026 will submit their recommendations the following week. Thereafter, a Special Cabinet Meeting will be held to discuss the matter.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Gambling Regulatory Authority (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The objective of the Bill is to amend the Gambling Regulatory Authority Act with a view to revamping the horse racing activities. The Gambling Regulatory Authority Act will be amended to review and spell out clearly the roles and responsibilities of the Gambling Regulatory Authority in connection with its regulatory, licensing, monitoring and enforcement functions regarding the horse racing activities as well as the horse racing organiser. Amendments are also being made to bring changes to other provisions relating to horse racing. Provision is also being made for a new administrative set up at the Gambling Regulatory Authority with seven divisions to be under the control of the Chief Executive of the Gambling Regulatory Authority, one of which will be the Horse Racing Integrity Division which will be responsible to protect the integrity of horse racing and ensure that horse racing is fair, clean and free from corruption or malpractice.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Letter of Agreement between the Department of Civil Aviation and Airservices Australia with a view to ensuring smooth and continuous progress of air traffic across the Mauritian airspace.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the National Preparedness Plan for Viral Haemorrhagic Fever: Ebola Virus Disease and Marburg Virus Disease. The purpose of the plan is to prevent and control the Ebola and Marburg Virus Diseases in Mauritius, including Rodrigues and the Outer Islands. The plan consists of three main parts, namely, the preparedness phase, the mitigation phase and the recovery phase, thus providing the strategic orientations for actions to be taken for the prevention and containment of Ebola and Marburg Virus Diseases.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Cane Industry Authority (Costs of Transport of Canes for Small Planters) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for transport charges payable by small sugarcane planters. The transport charges would be applicable as from Crop 2025. The guiding principle of the mechanism is to set a maximum chargeable price for a fixed range of distances, taking into consideration the lower road rate for the transit of cane during harvest season which is determined by the Control and Arbitration Committee of the Mauritius Cane Industry Authority in the month of January of each year using the Consumer Price Index. The lower road rate is paid by millers if the distance from the small planters' field to the mill or the collection site is in excess of 6.4 Km. The lower road rate for Crop 2025 has been fixed at Rs9.14/Km.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, as from 01 July 2025, owners of private vehicles and company vehicles would be required to renew their Motor Vehicle Licence through the enhanced online Motor Vehicle Licence system. Persons who do not have the necessary IT facilities may call at the post offices for assistance to renew their Motor Vehicle Licence through the online system.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of a Sales Exhibition for Women Entrepreneurs by the National Women Entrepreneur Council at the Main Alley of the Victoria Urban Terminal, Port Louis from 25 to 27 June 2025, with a view to encouraging the development and growth of women entrepreneurs in Mauritius. This initiative would be beneficial to women entrepreneurs by providing them with the opportunity to uplift their businesses, promote their locally made products, meet potential buyers, contract orders and build business networks. Local and handmade agro items, handicrafts, recycled crafts, textile products, amongst others would be on sale at the exhibition.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 20 June 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Board of the Financial Services Commission; (b) Pharmacy Council of Mauritius; (c) Traditional Medicine Board, with Mr Parmanand Mawah, Permanent Secretary as part-time Chairperson; (d) Mental Health Commission, with Mr Rajesh Unnuth as part-time Chairperson; (e) Taxi Operators Welfare Fund, with Mr Ka Yee Leung Tak Wan as part-time Chairperson; and (f) Road Transport Advisory Board, with Mr Ham Sharma Kowlessur as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the Judgment of the Supreme Court in the judicial review of the decision of the Local Government Service Commission to terminate the employment of officers in the Workmen's Class of the Municipal Town Council of Vacoas-Phoenix. The stand of the Commission that the appointments were legally invalid and could accordingly be terminated has been upheld by the Supreme Court. The application for judicial review has, therefore, been dismissed.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister to look into the Strategy for Port Development had two meetings on 21 and 22 January 2026. The first meeting was held with the members of the Committee and the representatives of the Prime Minister's Office and the Economic Development Board, while the second meeting was held with the representatives of the private sector. A third meeting with Trade Unions will be held on 26 January 2026. The urgency to modernise the port was highlighted during the meetings. Discussions focused on key strategic themes including review of governance model, acquisition of port equipment, training and employee relations and the implementation of the Island Container Terminal Project. Progress was also reported on accelerated workforce training, with ongoing training of Rubber Tyred Gantry operators and the anticipated delivery of a training simulator from India in March 2026. Updates were provided on the Island Container Terminal Project, which would increase container handling capacity. The development of a new Fishing Port at Fort William, for which land has already been earmarked, was also discussed as part of efforts to spearhead the development of the fishing sector.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Kreol Morisien being offered as a main subject at Higher School Certificate level as from academic year 2026. The Minister of Education and Human Resource will meet the Creole Speaking Union on Saturday 24 January 2026 to discuss the modalities of implementation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Special Cabinet Meeting will be held on Monday 26 January 2026 to finalise the recommendations in respect of the implementation of the Pay Research Bureau Report 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives issuing drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for the preparation of the Industry Bill. The objective of the Bill will be to provide for a modern legislative framework to reclaim the country's industrial and export base, thereby, repositioning Mauritius as a globally competitive manufacturing hub and export platform. The proposed Bill formally re-anchors industrialisation as a national priority rather than a derivative outcome of investment promotion. It will provide statutory authority for industrial planning, prioritisation of strategic high-potential sectors such as advanced manufacturing, bio-industries, pharmaceuticals, precision engineering and green industries, while embedding performance benchmarks and sustainability standards. The Bill also proposes to introduce a result-oriented culture in the industrial governance, with clear strategic directions, monitoring and accountability mechanisms. The Bill will also align with national development objectives, including achieving higher productivity, greater value addition, heightened economic resilience and smooth integration into regional and global value chains, particularly in the context of the African Continental Free Trade Area and post-African Growth and Opportunity Act trade realignments.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (Jersey) (Amendment) Regulations 2026. The amendments to the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) relate to the following – (a) to reiterate the common intention of both countries to eliminate double taxation without creating opportunities for non-taxation or reduced taxation through tax evasion or avoidance, including through treaty shopping arrangements, in accordance with Base Erosion and Profit Shifting Action 6; (b) to allow a taxpayer, in case of disagreement with the tax authority of either Mauritius or Jersey regarding the application of the DTAA, to initiate a Mutual Agreement Procedure by presenting the case to the tax authority of either country; and (c) to allow the tax authority of either Mauritius or Jersey to deny benefit under the DTAA to an investor of the other country where there is treaty-shopping, i.e., it is found that the principal purpose of carrying out the investment is mainly to obtain the benefits of the DTAA.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Allied Health Professionals Council (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 2026. The Regulations will provide a transitional mechanism for the registration of persons who were practising prior to the enactment of the Allied Health Professionals Council Act or before the Allied Health Professionals Council (Amendment of Schedules) Regulations 2024. In accordance with the Regulations, the Allied Health Professionals will have to submit an application for consideration by an Evaluation Committee, which will make a competency- based assessment of the application.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Central Water Authority (Water Supply for Domestic Purposes) (Amendment) Regulations 2026. In view of the financial difficulties of the Central Water Authority and in line with the recommendation of the National Audit Office, only consumers listed on the Social Register of Mauritius and consuming six cubic metres or less of water monthly will be exempted from payment of meter rent and water charges.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Trade in Bunker Fuels) Regulations 2026, with a view to transforming Mauritius into the preferred bunkering hub of the region. The Regulations will encapsulate the provisions under the existing legislation and the following new requirements – (a) the yearly Trade in Bunker Fuel Licence fee will be reviewed to Rs200,000; (b) licensed traders shall ensure that their vessels are seaworthy and comply with International Maritime Organization Conventions (SOLAS, MARPOL) as applicable; (c) licensed traders shall maintain an adequate third-party liability insurance coverage at all times; (d) in accordance with international Conventions, the crew members shall be provided with the required number of hours of rest; (e) licensed traders shall report the quantity of bunker fuels that is being transported; (f) as per the obligation under the Merchant Shipping (Liability and Compensation for Oil Pollution Damage) Act 2024, licensed traders carrying bunker fuels exceeding 150,000 tons shall settle every annual outstanding claim from the International Oil Pollution Compensation Fund in accordance with the 1992 Fund and the Supplementary Fund Protocol 2003; (g) provision of a duplicate licence to traders against a fee of Rs10,000 in case of loss, theft or destruction; and (h) in case of breaches of any condition of his licence or contravention of the regulation, the trader shall commit an offence and on conviction, he shall be liable to a fine not exceeding Rs100,000 and to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to amendments being brought to the Consumer Protection (Control of Imports) Regulations 2017 to enable the importation of used tyres for the purpose of re-treading for export only, with a view to promoting a circular economy by encouraging recycling and re-use of products.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of a National Data Strategy (2025-2029) which will be the national plan guiding the Government, people and businesses on how to handle/process data safely and smartly, turning it into helpful tools for better lives and stronger growth, while at the same time protecting privacy. The National Data Strategy is supported by four key implementation tools, namely – (a) Data Governance Framework Template which provides a comprehensive model for data governance, including rules, processes, roles, and pillars to ensure data integrity, security, compliance, and effective management; (b) Data Sharing Policy Template which outlines guidelines and legal requirements for responsible data sharing; (c) Data Sharing Protocol Template which details procedures, principles, and responsibilities for secure, lawful information sharing across organisations, including consent and security measures; and (d) Data Retention Policy which outlines guidelines for the storage, retention, and secure disposal of personal data, ensuring lawful processing, privacy protection, and compliance with the Data Protection Act 2017.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of the Assises de l'Agriculture by the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries under the theme "SMART agriculture for a resilient Mauritius", on 27 and 28 January 2026. The main objectives of the Assises, are to develop, inter alia, strategies to – (a) strengthen food sovereignty and resilient domestic production; (b) promote inclusive and competitive value chains; (c) foster innovation, knowledge systems and youth engagement; (d) ensure sustainable natural resource management and eco-system health; (e) enhance nutritional security, food safety and public health linkages; and (f) strengthen governance, coordination and financing mechanisms. The Assises will also be used as a forum to encourage a national shift towards the principle of "Eat what we produce and produce what we eat". Moreover, the Assises will highlight the necessity to adopt water-efficient irrigation technologies, the cultivation of climate-resilient food crops, the deployment of early warning systems for extreme weather events, the modernisation of the agricultural sector and the sustainable use of land.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the launching by the Central Electricity Board (CEB) of a new Renewable Energy (RE) Scheme for Major Commercial Customers with Battery Energy Storage System and an option for Time-of-Use Tariff. The CEB has revisited its RE Scheme applicable to large commercial customers under Electricity Tariff Codes 225 and 225A, by allowing them to generate RE to offset their energy consumption. These customers, which include shopping malls, banks and hotels, would be given the choice between either Gross-Metering or Quasi-Net-Metering. Under the quasi-net- metering option, an unbundled Time-of-Use tariff structure would apply. It will be mandatory for subscribers to the new Scheme to install a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS). The installation of BESS (onsite or offsite) will be mandatory and will be used to manage and offset peak energy demand as well as enable participation of these consumers in Demand-Side Management initiatives. For the initial phase of the Scheme, the CEB intends to accommodate 70 MW capacity of solar photovoltaic projects with at least 11.5MW battery energy storage systems from the Commercial Customers registered under the Electricity Tariff Codes 225 and 225A.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Mauritius has scored 0.944 in the GovTech Maturity Index 2025 of the World Bank which offers a comprehensive assessment of public sector digital transformation in 197 economies across four key dimensions through 48 indicators. The score of Mauritius has improved considerably over the years, from 0.574 in 2020, 0.864 in 2022 to 0.944 in 2025. Mauritius is in Group A as an "Extensive GovTech Maturity" economy.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a Special Committee to look into the overall aspects and mechanisms for effective handling of cases of Children with Serious Behavioural Concerns (CSBC) under the chair of Mrs S. Ganoo-Arekion, Magistrate and comprising Mrs K. Ah Nien-Kotadoo and Mr Vincent Mandrou as Assessors. The objects of the Special Committee will be to – (a) assess the overall functioning of existing institutional mechanism, in terms of, their set-up; profile of children and the procedures entailed prior to their placement/admission, in an institution under the Probation of Offenders Act and the Reform Institutions Act; human resources; rehabilitation and re-integration programmes; (b) look into provisions of existing legislation, namely the Children's Act 2020; the Probation of Offenders Act; the Reform Institutions Act, including existing regulations made, and whether these are responsive to the needs, care and protection of CSBC; (c) make recommendations in relation to the above on the basis of its findings, including specific amendments to be brought to the legislation; and the appropriate intervention pathways and facilities to cater for CSBC; and (d) provide policy-makers with future orientations for better service delivery to CSBC and those with psychiatric problems.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Mauritius Revenue Authority and the International Trade Centre regarding the exchange of information, specifically customs data. The Memorandum of Understanding provides for the establishment of a framework for collaboration and cooperation for the exchange of information, specifically customs data, on an anonymised basis for trade analysis, capacity building and reporting. The International Trade Centre has, in collaboration with the African Union Commission, developed an online platform, namely the African Trade Observatory. The objective of the Observatory is to compile datasets from across African countries and regions in order to provide timely and reliable trade information to policy-makers and businesses. This will allow them to - (a) compare trade opportunities and requirements to identify promising markets in Africa; (b) explore latest trends, market conditions and potential partners across the continent; and (c) monitor trade, market access, border performance and the impact of the African Continental Free Trade Area through some 20 indicators.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius signing a Double Taxation Avoidance Convention (DTAC) with the Kingdom of the Netherlands, in respect of Curaçao. The DTAC complies with the commitments to the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development on the Base Erosion and Profit Shifting tax treaty related measures. The DTAC will clarify the taxing rights of each country on income covered by the Convention and will also – (a) provide certainty to investors in their dealings with Mauritius or Curaçao, as the case may be; (b) create a conducive environment for greater cross-border investment flows between our two countries; (c) establish a framework for exchange of information between the tax authorities of Mauritius and Curaçao, with a view to combatting tax evasion and other tax malpractices; and (d) provide a mechanism to taxpayers for the resolution of tax disputes.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of a ruling of the Supreme Court of India, Civil Appellate Jurisdiction in the case of The Authority for Advance Rulings (Income Tax and Others) versus Tiger Global International II Holdings, Tiger Global International III Holdings and Tiger Global International IV Holdings which are global companies incorporated in Mauritius. The Supreme Court of India has, on 15 January 2026, set aside the judgment of the High Court of Delhi delivered in August 2024 on grounds that the Tax Residency Certificate issued by Mauritian Tax authorities is not sufficient evidence to demonstrate the eligibility of a company to apply the provisions of the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement between Mauritius and India where an interposed entity is alleged to be a conduit for tax avoidance. Given the systemic importance of the global business sector to the Mauritian economy, Cabinet was apprised of measures under consideration to provide comfort to investors and preserve the credibility of the Mauritius International Financial Centre.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a professional music group from Chennai, comprising seven musicians will be travelling to Mauritius to perform during the Thaipoosam Cavadee 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised in the context of World Cancer Day 2026 under the theme "United by Unique". World Cancer Day is observed on 04 February every year in order to raise awareness on cancer and to encourage its prevention, early detection and treatment. The Ministry of Health and Wellness will hold a series of activities during the month of February 2026, namely – (a) a sensitisation march under the theme "United by Unique", in collaboration with the University of Mauritius; (b) the launching of the National Cancer Registry Report; (c) the launching of a mass Breast and Cervical Cancer Screening Campaign targeting working women aged between 25 to 65 years across Mauritius; (d) radio talks and social media awareness campaign; (e) screening activities and sensitisation campaigns at Community Health Centres, Women Centres, Social Welfare Centres, schools, urban and rural communities, and workplace settings on cancer risk factors and preventive measures; and (f) distribution of pamphlets and posters.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that HE Ms Veronique Laporte, Minister of Transport, Ports and Civil Aviation of the Republic of Seychelles, will lead a fact-finding mission to Mauritius from 26 to 28 January 2026. The objective of the mission is to allow the Seychelles authorities to engage with relevant Mauritian authorities and stakeholders to gain first-hand insights into Mauritius' successful implementation of electric buses in public transport, as well as into safety requirements for artisanal and semi-industrial fishing vessels, with particular emphasis on Very High Frequency radio carriage and operational requirements and the potential use and regulation of personal location beacons for fishing vessels.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the Peer-to-Peer Learning Exchange Visit on blue financing in Cabo Verde. The Junior Minister led the Mauritian delegation, comprising representatives of the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries, the Ministry of Finance, the Bank of Mauritius and the Stock Exchange of Mauritius to participate in the Peer-to-Peer Learning Exchange on blue financing in Cabo Verde. The Mauritian delegation had courtesy meetings with representatives of key Ministries and Institutions, namely the Minister for Agriculture and Environment, the Minister of Sea and the Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of Stock Exchange. The Junior Minister visited key Government bodies and institutions in the blue economy sector, namely the Baixo Water Desalination Plant, the Institute of the Sea and the Cruise Port.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 23 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of – (a) the Board of Governors of the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Public Service and Innovation, with Dr Nicolas Ragodoo, as part-time Chairperson; and (b) Le Morne Heritage Trust Fund Board, with Mrs Marie Diana Isobelle Bablee, as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note, with concern, of the case reported to the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations, by two former employees of legal firm ENSafrica Mauritius, alleging that they had been laid off for wearing hijab at work. The matter has been referred to the Employment Relations Tribunal for the latter to look into their possible reinstatement. The possibility of action being taken against the law firm is being examined. Proposals for amendments to the relevant legislation to address such cases are also being examined.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Civil Appeal Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to clarify, simplify and standardise the procedures for all appeals in civil matters made – (a) to the Court of Civil Appeal from the final decision of the Supreme Court in the exercise of its original jurisdiction in civil matters; and (b) to the Supreme Court in the exercise of its appellate jurisdiction in civil matters from the final decision of the subordinate court, such as the District Court, the Intermediate Court, the Industrial Court, the Master and Registrar, the Judge in Chambers or a Tribunal, and to bring together such appeal procedures into one consolidated legislation. The Bill has been rendered necessary with a view to bringing clarity and certainty in this area of the law, being given that the procedures for making appeals in civil matters are scattered in different pieces of legislation and are not uniform. In addition, the Bill imposes an obligation on any Court, immediately after it gives judgment in a civil matter, to notify any party to the case of his right to appeal. Consequently, the Court of Civil Appeal Act and the Court of Civil Appeal Rules 1965 are being repealed, and the Courts Act, the District and Intermediate Courts (Civil Jurisdiction) Act, the Environment and Land Use Appeal Tribunal Act, the Equal Opportunities Act, the Industrial Court Act and the Sale of Immovable Property Act are being amended.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Optical Council (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Optical Council Act 2021 so as to – (a) empower the Optical Council of Mauritius to regulate, in addition to the profession of opticians, the profession of orthoptists; (b) review the composition of the Optical Council of Mauritius with a view to addressing the anomaly with regard to public sector representation; and (c) abolish the pre-registration examination for opticians and, therefore, no pre- registration examination will be required for orthoptists.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that in March 2023, the General Assembly of the United Nations had approached the International Court of Justice to give its Advisory Opinion on the questions as follows - (a) What are the obligations of States under international law to ensure the protection of the climate system and other parts of the environment from anthropogenic emissions of greenhouse gases for States and for present and future generations? (b) What are the legal consequences under these obligations for States where they, by their acts and omissions, have caused significant harm to the climate system and other parts of the environment, with regard to – (i) States, including, in particular, Small Island Developing States (SIDS), which due to their geographical circumstances and level of development, are injured or specially affected by or are particularly vulnerable to the adverse effects of climate change?; and (ii) peoples and individuals of the present and future generations affected by the adverse effects of climate change? Cabinet has further taken note that in a landmark Advisory Opinion delivered on 23 July 2025 on the obligations of States in respect of climate change, the Court has ruled on the above questions as follows – (i) States have an obligation to protect the environment from Greenhouse Gas emissions and act with due diligence and cooperation to fulfil this obligation; (ii) compliance with climate treaties is not necessarily sufficient to fulfil all relevant obligations. Various other sources of most directly relevant law including the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea, human rights and customary international law should be considered; and (iii) if States breach these obligations, they incur legal responsibility and may be required to cease the actions causing this, offer guarantee for non-repetition and make full reparation. Additionally, the Advisory Opinion specifies that, even countries which are not Party to the Paris Agreement or which want to leave the Agreement are still required to protect the climate and the environment.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Land Transport initiating action, in consultation with the Attorney-General's Office, for the preparation of a Bill for the re-introduction of an improved version of the penalty points system with a view to addressing reckless driving and curbing fatal accidents on the roads. Further to the meeting of three sub-committees which had been set up to formulate proposals that could be considered for the re-introduction of the penalty points system, an improved version of the penalty points system has been recommended. The list of traffic offences and corresponding penalty points will also be included in the Bill.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Income Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2025 will be promulgated, to provide for amendments to the Statement of Emoluments and Tax Deduction to include - (a) the date the employee started employment with the employer; and (b) the amount of Portable Retirement Gratuity Fund (PRGF) contributed by the employer for the year. The proposed declaration by the employer of the date the employee started employment and the amount of PRGF contributed in the Statement of Emoluments and Tax Deduction will - (a) provide greater transparency and visibility to employees regarding PRGF contributions made by the employer to the Mauritius Revenue Authority; (b) strengthen compliance by employers; (c) facilitate the computation of gratuity payable upon retirement, death, cessation, or termination of employment; and (d) avoid the risk of disputes arising from the non-disclosure or under-declaration of PRGF contributions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of new measures to - (a) restrict the sale of domestic Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) cylinders to ensure consistent availability to households, reduce the risk of hoarding and discourage the redirection of subsidised cylinders to non-household purposes; (b) prevent the storage of domestic LPG cylinders on trade premises not involved in their commercialisation to prevent the improper use of subsidised cylinders by businesses; and (c) amend the relevant regulations to provide for the issuance of a formal warning to first-time offenders prior to establishing a contravention or issuing a Fixed Penalty Notice. Relevant legislative amendments would be brought to give effect to the above measures.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Price and Supplies Control) (Coupons and Gift Vouchers) Regulations 2025 so as to regulate the fair issuance, use, and redemption of coupons and gift vouchers in Mauritius in line with international best practices. The regulations will – (a) provide precise and legally sound definitions for "coupon" and "gift voucher" in order to eliminate ambiguity and ensure clarity for both traders and consumers; (b) require that all coupons issued by traders clearly indicate their terms and conditions of use, including applicable restrictions, limitations, and expiry dates; (c) stipulate that all gift vouchers for which payment has been made by the consumer shall remain valid for a minimum of six months from the date of issue; (d) impose a proactive obligation on traders to notify the holder of a gift voucher at least one month before the expiry of the gift voucher, using any available and practical means of communication, thus ensuring that consumers are adequately informed and able to exercise their rights; (e) explicitly prohibit unfair trading practices such as the refusal to honour valid and legitimate coupons or gift vouchers, the dissemination of misleading or deceptive promotional claims, and the use of bait advertising tactics where the advertised goods or services are not reasonably available; and (f) establish a clear and proportionate penalty structure to deter non-compliance, including a minimum fine of Rs10,000 and a maximum of Rs100,000 for a first offence, and a fine of not less than Rs25,000 and not more than Rs250,000 along with a possible term of imprisonment not exceeding 12 months for repeat or aggravated breaches.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to amendments being brought to the Electronic Transactions Act 2000 to provide, inter alia, for expanding the scope of electronic signatures, incorporating provisions for smart contracts and recognising foreign Certificate Authorities and electronic signatures which will enhance business facilitation by providing robust legal certainty, international interoperability and operational efficiency.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Information and Communication Technologies Act being amended in order to better address the new realities of the sector. The objectives of the amendments will be to – (a) bring clarity to consumers on the services which fall under the mandate of the Information and Communication Technologies Authority (ICTA); (b) clarify the functions of ICTA as regulator and licence provider of radio communication apparatus that are operated on board ships and aircrafts; (c) clarify the functions of ICTA with respect to the conduct of examinations and issuance of certificates for the operation of radio communication services; (d) lighten the procedure for licensing of engineering licences in favour of business facilitation; and (e) introduce a mechanism for compounding technical ICT offences for efficient resolution of certain violations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the proclamation of the English-Speaking Union Act 2015. The objects of the Union will be to, inter alia – (a) promote the English language both in its written and spoken form; (b) promote understanding and friendship between the English-speaking peoples of the world and to engage in any educational work designated to further that objective; (c) provide facilities for the interchange of views and discussion concerning any question affecting the interests, well-being, development, relationships and common problems of the English-speaking peoples of the world; (d) promote and encourage education in any form amongst English-speaking peoples, for the benefit of both adults and young persons, but with special reference to the historical, cultural, artistic, economic, diplomatic and social development of those peoples; (e) promote and organise the interchange of books, pamphlets, periodicals and other information between colleges, schools and other educational establishments or group of members of the Union or persons situated in another country; and (f) promote and encourage correspondence, debates, visits and other activities between universities, colleges, schools, and other bodies situated in Mauritius and in another country.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Financing Agreement, between the European Commission and the Republic of Mauritius for the implementation of the Circular Economy Roadmap. This Financing Agreement to the tune of EUR3.5 million will be implemented over some 48 months with the overall objective of strengthening socially inclusive and environmentally sustainable development in the Republic of Mauritius and the specific objectives are as follows – (a) improving the institutional and regulatory frameworks for the implementation of the national Circular Economy Roadmap and Action Plan; and (b) increasing effectiveness of circular economy initiatives within the built environment value chain, prioritising women entrepreneurs in the circular economy. The Action Plan will contribute principally to the achievement of SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production), and also, indirectly, to SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), SDG 13 (Climate Action), SDG 15 (Life on Land), SDG 14 (Life below Water), SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities), SDG 5 (Gender Equality), SDG 8 (Decent Work and Economic Growth), SDG 1 (No Poverty), and SDG 17 (Partnerships for the Goals).

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Data Protection Office and the Bank of Mauritius. The collaboration will be beneficial to the business environment, inter alia, in the following ways – (a) the Bank of Mauritius can streamline or devise requirements that a local bank will have to abide by for the collection of Know Your Customer (KYC) data to comply with Anti-Money Laundering (AML) legal frameworks while ensuring that the appropriate mechanism is in place mandating strict privacy measures for handling of personal data. Thus, the sharing of information between the Bank of Mauritius and the Data Protection Office will ensure that the bank's KYC procedures both satisfy AML requirements and comply with data privacy laws; and (b) the Data Protection Office, in collaboration with the Bank of Mauritius, can integrate specialised training on data protection best practices into the broader financial compliance programmes and the Data Protection Office can also offer such training to the employees of financial institutions regulated by the Bank of Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Open University of Mauritius signing an Accord Cadre de Coopération with L'Université de Toamasina, Madagascar. The Accord Cadre de Coopération aims to strengthen institutional ties through mutual cooperation in the technical and scientific fields with Mauritius benefitting from the joint publication of scientific results and outcomes. The main areas of cooperation are as follows - (a) joint research activities; (b) academic/staff and student exchanges; (c) exchange of PhD students and joint scientific supervision of thesis work; (d) exchange of scientific and technical publications; (e) joint publication of scientific results and educational documents; and (f) organisation of mutual courses and joint seminars, face-to-face or distance learning.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the "Review of the Oversight Mechanism for the Operation of Child Day Care Centres", carried out by the Office of Public Sector Governance (OPSG). Based on the recommendations made by the OPSG, the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare will implement an Action Plan and has worked out tentative timeframes for the implementation thereof. To address ambiguities and challenges being encountered in the application of the existing Child Day Care Centres Regulations 2022, the Ministry will review the Regulations.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of two Committees as follows – (i) an Independent Committee of Enquiry to look into alleged mismanagement and industrial relations issues at the New Educational College; and (ii) an Independent Technical Committee to assess the infrastructural integrity of the building of Alpha College.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the finals of the Inter-College Sports Competition 2025 held from 14 to 18 July 2025. A total of 2,568 students coming from 116 colleges, including four from Rodrigues, took part in the finals, which comprised five sports disciplines, namely athletics, badminton, football, table tennis and volleyball. The overall champion of this year's edition is St Mary's College having secured eight gold, one silver, and two bronze medals. This performance placed St Mary's College at the top across all five sports disciplines based on medal count. The exceptional performance of Néhémie Clair, from Marechal College of Rodrigues, in the Under-16 years' category javelin event, with a throw of 56.92m was noted.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of arrangements being made by the Ministry of Youth and Sports for the participation of the Mauritian delegation in the Jeux de la Commission de la Jeunesse et des Sports de l'Océan Indien to be held in Seychelles from 01 to 10 August 2025. Mauritius will participate in all the 11 sports disciplines, namely athletics, para- athletics, badminton, basketball, boxing, football, judo, swimming, volleyball, yachting and weightlifting. The games will also feature a youth component consisting of an Assemblée des Jeunes, Danse et Théâtre, Esports and the Salon des Jeunes Entrepreneurs. The Mauritian delegation will comprise 234 members with 192 athletes, including 27 from Rodrigues. In order to ensure optimal preparation of the selected athletes for the Games, the Ministry of Youth and Sports is providing the following facilities and support – (a) access to the High-Performance Centre at the Côte d'Or National Sports Complex for physical preparation; (b) participation in local and regional competitions; (c) meal after training sessions; (d) transport facilities; (e) intensive training camps tailored for each sport; (f) provision of general and specific equipment; and (g) medical and para-medical facilities, such as massage, physiotherapy, vitamins and supplements, blood tests, and dental check-up.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the implementation of the Anou Transform Nou Landrwa project by the Ministry of Youth and Sports which seeks to foster the development and promotion of sports and cultural activities in socially vulnerable localities across the island. The project was implemented on a pilot basis at Karo Kalyptis by engaging youth and community members in meaningful, structured and inclusive activities. A series of community- building activities were organised, including street football, boxe éducative, painting and fresques murales, minor games and jeux d'antan and cultural evenings. The project was replicated at Batterie Cassée and Résidence Briquetterie. The next event is scheduled to take place at Roche Bois, with further expansion planned in other localities which are being identified around the island.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the Action Plan 2025-2029 of the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare, developed in alignment with the Government Programme 2025- 2029. It defines the strategic direction of the Ministry over the next five years and provides a coordinated framework in fulfilling the Ministry's mandate. It includes the promotion of gender equality, protection of children, advancement of family welfare, and strengthening of community well-being. The main areas under the Action Plan 2025-2029 include – (a) Treaty Compliance and Legislative Action The Ministry will pursue necessary legal and procedural steps to ratify key regional and international instruments; (b) Institutional Reform and Strategic Planning The Child Protection Services will be revamped and restructured for better service delivery and improved community outreach; (c) Women's Empowerment and Leadership Key initiatives will be undertaken to enhance women's participation in leadership, decision-making, and economic activity; (d) Preventing and Addressing Gender-Based Violence Measures will be taken to reinforce legal frameworks and protection mechanisms for victims of Gender-Based Violence; and (e) Strengthening Family Resilience and Social Welfare Initiatives will be rolled out to promote family cohesion, prevent intergenerational poverty, and foster positive parenting.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of a Workshop on the theme "Adolescence and Cybercrime", to be held on 28 and 29 July 2025 with a focus on teenagers, cybercrime and family well-being. The Workshop aims to - (a) raise awareness among adolescents, parents, and key institutions about cyber threats; (b) sensitise stakeholders on the psychological and social consequences of cybercrime; (c) share best practices and preventive strategies for online safety; and (d) facilitate dialogue among professionals from multiple sectors and generate recommendations for a national response.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Osaka Expo 2025, where Mauritius has a pavilion for the period from 13 April to 13 October 2025. The theme for the Osaka Expo 2025 is "Designing Future Society for Our Lives" and the sub-theme chosen by Mauritius for its pavilion is "Empowering Lives". The Minister had a bilateral meeting with the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Japan. The meeting was an opportunity to engage in the enhancement of our bilateral ties, and to discuss areas of cooperation. The Minister conveyed his appreciation for Japan's continued cooperation across various sectors while highlighting the cordial and longstanding relations between the two countries. The Minister also had a working session with the Japan Association of Corporate Executives, during which various issues pertaining to the economic and trade relations between Mauritius and Japan were discussed.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection in the Ninth United Nations Conference to Review All Aspects of the Set of Multilaterally Agreed Equitable Principles and Rules for the Control of Restrictive Business Practices, in Geneva. During the Conference, the Minister emphasised Mauritius' firm commitment to strengthening consumer protection as a key driver of sustainable development. He highlighted that the existing legal framework forms the backbone of the consumer protection system. He further underlined the increasing relevance of digital markets and artificial intelligence to the national agenda, and informed the Conference of his Ministry's ongoing work on new legislation to regulate digital commerce. The main outcomes of the Conference include the adoption of a draft Resolution for the first-ever United Nations Principles for Consumer Product Safety and a Declaration on Cross-Border Dispute Resolution and Redress. In the margins of the Conference, the Minister held bilateral meetings with – (a) the Egyptian delegation headed by the Minister of Supply and Internal Trade; (b) the Indian delegation which was headed by Mrs Nidhi Khare, Secretary Department of Consumer Affairs, Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution; and (c) the delegation of the COMESA Competition Commission headed by its Director and Chief Executive Officer.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcomes of the meeting of the Committee of SADC Ministers of Justice/Attorneys-General, that was held virtually and was hosted by Zimbabwe on 11 July 2025. The Committee considered the benchmarking exercise carried out by the SADC Secretariat to - (a) introduce a hierarchy of sanctions to be imposed on defaulting Member States; (b) be specific about the last sanction to be imposed under level four sanctions; and (c) determine whether the provisions of Article 33(4) have to be maintained or be amended to enable Council to approve a decision to impose a sanction against a defaulting Member State prior to the Secretariat issuing a notification to the defaulting Member State.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 25 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Board of Mauri-Facilities Management Co. Ltd with Mr Sadaseeven Sooben as part-time Chairperson; (b) Veterinary Council of Mauritius; and (c) Council of the National Social Inclusion Foundation, with Mrs Mary-Anne Sharon Caroline Fitz-Gibbon as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note, with concern, that the legislative process with regard to the Diego Garcia Military Base and British-Indian Ocean Territory Bill in the United Kingdom (UK) has been delayed and will be activated as soon as an agreement between the UK and the United States is reached. Government is following-up closely on the matter. Cabinet has further taken note of the recent stand taken by the Government of the Republic of Maldives whereby it no longer recognises the sovereignty of the Republic of Mauritius over the Chagos Archipelago and is now objecting to the UK-Mauritius Agreement. In view thereof, Cabinet has decided to immediately suspend all diplomatic relations with the Republic of Maldives. Cabinet has also taken note that the injunction filed by the "BIOT Citizens" led by Mr Misley Mandarin has been called before the British-Indian Ocean Territory Administration (BIOTA) Court on Thursday 26 February 2026 and the Court has, on the undertaking of the UK Government, extended the stay of the Removal Order up to 13 March 2026, when the matter will be heard on its merits. The BIOTA Court has also acceded to a request by the UK to order that no new persons be transported on the Chagos Islands.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Prime Minister in the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Impact Summit, at the invitation of HE Shri Narendra Modi, Prime Minister of India, in New Delhi. The Mauritian delegation comprised the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation, the Mauritian High Commissioner in India, senior officials from the Prime Minister's Office, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade, as well as the Chief Executive Officer of Mauritius Telecom. The Summit was on the theme "AI for All and Welfare for All" and witnessed the participation of some 20 Heads of State and Government. During his address at the Leaders' Plenary, the Prime Minister stressed the urgent need to ensure that the gap in AI divide does not widen between developed and developing countries and called for the transformative power of AI to be at the service of humanity at large. He urged for dedicated initiatives, including the development of an enabling environment to foster a level-playing field so that countries, particularly Small Island Developing States, do not lag behind. The Prime Minister enjoined leaders to consider implementing an equitable international governance framework that promotes safe and trusted use of AI tools. He highlighted the need to foster partnerships among nations so that AI works towards the common good and acts as a socio-economic enabler. The Prime Minister took the opportunity of his participation in the AI Summit to promote the Special Economic Zone, to be set up at Côte d'Or, and which will be designed to attract investment and geared towards promoting the economy of the future, including the digital economy and AI. He emphasised that in line with the Digital Transformation Blueprint 2025- 2029, Mauritius will accelerate its transition towards a high-income innovation driven and digitally enabled economy and is looking forward to entering into strategic partnerships with high-end companies in the field of AI, Data Centres, Cloud Infrastructure and Fintech. The Prime Minister had a one-to-one meeting with Shri Narendra Modi, Prime Minister of India at the Hyderabad House in New Delhi and discussed several issues of bilateral, regional and multilateral interests, including – (a) the forthcoming Indian Ocean Conference to be hosted by Mauritius from 10 to 12 April 2026; (b) assistance in the Port Sector; (c) the India-Mauritius Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA). Prime Minister Modi assured of the continued stance of India of not taking any action that would undermine the benefits that Mauritius currently enjoys under the DTAA; (d) the setting up of a Special Economic Zone at Côte d'Or; and (e) the America India Connect Cable Connection project announced by the Chief Executive Officer of Google during the Summit. The Prime Minister also met with Dr S. Jaishankar, External Affairs Minister of India who reiterated the readiness of India to enhance bilateral cooperation as per the outcomes of the official visit of Prime Minister Modi to Mauritius in March 2025 and the Prime Minister's State Visit to India in September 2025. In the margins of the Conference, the Prime Minister met with HE Mr Bharrat Jagdeo, Vice President of Guyana, Dr Kristalina Georgieva, Director General of the International Monetary Fund and Mr Natarajan Chandrasekaran, Chairman of the TATA Group of Companies. The Minister of Information Technology, Communications and Innovation intervened in the ICT Minister's Forum where he presented the AI Vision Strategy for Mauritius which is articulated on a fair, accountable, inclusive and responsible AI framework. He also advocated for AI safety through regulatory and governance mechanisms and stressed for institutional readiness and appropriate oversight structures. In the margins of the summit, the Minister of Information Technology, Communications and Innovation held bilateral meetings with, inter alia, the Minister of State for Electronics and IT, representatives of the Centre for Development of Telematics, Mistral AI, NVIDIA, Google, Indian Cybercrime Coordination Centre and VisionPlus.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the provision of Rs1.1 billion voted in the 2025-2026 Budget for the payment of passage benefits to public officers was exhausted by end November 2025. As a result, a significant number of applications for encashment of passage benefits have been kept in abeyance since December 2025. In order not to deprive public officers of their dues, Cabinet has agreed to – (a) an additional budgetary provision of Rs50 million being made for the payment of passage benefits to public officers who had already submitted their applications by end-December 2025, as well as to new applicants, provided that these are for travel, medical and educational purposes supported by appropriate documentary evidence; and (b) the deferment of payment of passage benefits for pending cases and new applications for purposes other than medical, educational, and travel to July 2026. Those who had already applied by end-December 2025 would be paid the passage benefits in July 2026 on the same terms as prior to the implementation of the 2026 PRB Report, i.e., they would be paid in full.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Health and Wellness effecting part payment of Rs170 million for the outstanding claim in relation to Johnson and Johnson COVID-19 vaccines to African Vaccine Acquisition Trust. The Commitment Undertaking and the Participation Agreement signed by the previous regime in May 2021 for the procurement of around 1.1 Million doses of Johnson and Johnson COVID-19 vaccines created an obligation on Government for the payment of the total amount due and that there was no possibility of substitution. Only 439,200 doses of Johnson and Johnson COVID-19 vaccines have been procured and there was an outstanding balance of 691,200 doses which have never being procured but for which payment was still required. An outstanding amount USD5,421,697.41 (around Rs243 million) with interest compounded on a daily basis was due in respect of these 691,200 doses of Johnson and Johnson COVID-19 vaccines. Cabinet further noted that an amount of USD5,999,040 (around Rs277 million) was also due in respect of 599,040 doses of Pfizer vaccines. The contract agreement had been signed with Pfizer Inc. and BioNTech Manufacturing in October 2022 for the procurement of 999,140 doses of Pfizer vaccines. Only 400,140 doses had been supplied in December 2022 and the payment for the outstanding balance is still due. Cabinet has also taken note that, on the other hand the Russian authorities have agreed to waive an amount of USD11,243,500 (around Rs521 million) which was due for the Sputnik V vaccines. Cabinet has noted, with concern, that no procurement procedures have been followed for the procurement of COVID-19 vaccines during that period. It is understood that the decision for the supply of vaccines was taken during the High Level Committee on COVID-19 chaired by the former Prime Minister. However, no records of such decisions are available.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Land Transport issuing drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office to amend the Road Traffic (Amendment) Regulations 2021 so as to extend the moratorium on the expiry date of Provisional Driving Licenses (Learner) for autocycle/motorcycle, currently scheduled to lapse on 01 March 2026, as transitional relief pending broader reforms to the driving test framework. The validity period of the Provisional Driving Licences (Learner) will be extended for a period of one year.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Yogendranath Servansingh, Mr Noor-Ud-Diin Ruhomaun and Dr Guy Rivaltz Julien Quenette as members of the National Road Safety Council.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Optical Council (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Optical Council Act 2021 so as to – (a) empower the Optical Council of Mauritius to regulate, in addition to the profession of opticians, the profession of orthoptists; (b) review the composition of the Optical Council of Mauritius with a view to addressing the anomaly with regard to public sector representation; and (c) abolish the pre-registration examination for opticians as well as orthoptists.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Higher Education (Use of Artificial Intelligence) Regulations 2026 which will apply to the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the higher education sector in Mauritius. The regulations will confer upon the Higher Education Commission the authority and the duty to - (a) issue guidelines, directives and standards to regulate the use of AI across all higher education institutions operating in Mauritius; (b) prohibit uses of AI that may lead to bias, breach of academic integrity, plagiarism, or violation of data protection, intellectual property, copyright and cybersecurity laws; (c) appoint ad hoc working groups drawn from higher education institutions, professional bodies and other stakeholders to advise the Commission; (d) promote academic research on the use of AI in higher education; (e) conduct periodic reviews to ensure that policies remain current and aligned with best practice; and (f) require institutions to take corrective measures within prescribed timeframes where non-compliance is identified. The regulations will also impose clear obligations on every higher education institution.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status reached in the Local Government Reforms being undertaken with a view to conducting a thorough review of the entire local government system, so as to ensure that it reflects present realities and enables Local Authorities to respond more effectively to the needs of local communities. The Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Local Government met on four occasions and came up with proposals. In order to ensure that the reform framework is inclusive, coherent and aligned with national priorities and constituency realities, consultations were held with all the elected members, both at local and national levels. A Local Government Reform Cell has been set up at the level of the Ministry of Local Government. The Cell is currently analysing proposals received from the public and various organisations following the public consultation exercise. In addition, all views and comments received during the Consultative Workshop and the Consultative Meeting are currently being examined.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee on the Price of Bread under the chair of the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection and comprising the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity and the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of complaints regarding alleged inappropriate behavior of Mr Frédéric Pavaday, a former music teacher of Loreto Junior School at Curepipe. The nature of the allegations relates to abusive conduct of the teacher against two students during their time at the school. Senior officials of the Ministry of Education and Human Resource had a meeting with the President of the Parent Teacher Association and a parent on 24 February 2026 to discuss how the Ministry of Education and Human Resource can extend its support to the students and parents of the aggrieved parties. The Ministry of Education and Human Resource proposed to offer psychological assistance to students and parents requiring same. The support of the Ministry of Education and Human Resource aims to ensure the emotional safety and well-being of students during this period of heightened public concern regarding child protection protocols, mandatory reporting, and institutional responsibilities in educational settings.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting - Friday 27 Feb 2026.pdf
