The new framework aims to - (a) ensure that any expansion of dealership licences fosters a healthier competitive environment, resulting in better prices and improved service standards for consumers; (b) consider the possibility to streamline procedures for industry operators and SMEs to import vans, dual-purpose vehicles, goods vehicles (trucks and lorries), motorbuses, and special purpose vehicles, while addressing the current demand–supply gap; (c) promote a sustainable business model that benefits all stakeholders over the long term; (d) strengthen consumer protection measures proportionate to the value of the purchase made; and (e) prevent the unauthorised entry of imported second-hand motor vehicles.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Medical Council (Medical Institutions) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for the change of appellation of the 'Kasturba Medical College, Manipal University' into that of 'Manipal Academy of Higher Education, Karnataka, formerly known as Kasturba Medical College, Manipal University'.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the existing legislative framework for regulating the organisation of Trade Fairs being reviewed to - (a) provide for licensing framework to authorise the holding of trade fairs; and (b) introduce more effective and stringent measures to ensure the economic stability of the retail industry. The Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection will introduce a licensing framework for companies organising Trade Fairs in Mauritius. The framework will aim at ensuring that such activities take place in a well-regulated environment that minimises market disruptions while promoting a level playing field for all retailers.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the findings of the report of the Internal Enquiry Committee on the absconding of three minors from Shelter L'Oiseau du Paradis, Cap Malheureux on 01 June 2025. The report of the Internal Enquiry Committee comprise, inter alia, the following observations - (a) there was lack of security monitoring; (b) the absconding was due to alleged gross negligence from the caregivers; (c) the 'prise en charge' of the minors was unsatisfactory; (d) overzealousness of certain staff of the shelter was left unchecked by the National Children's Council; (e) the was no effective supervision on the shelter by the National Children's Council; (f) there was an abuse of overtime which may amount to dilapidation of funds; (g) illegal video recording of minors was a frequent feature; and (h) command and control were absent from the supervising staff of the National Children's Council. The Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare is following up on the recommendations made by the Committee with the National Children's Council.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Health and Wellness signing a Clinical Training Framework Agreement with the IOMIT'S SSR Medical College. The aim of the Clinical Training Framework Agreement is, inter alia, to - (a) establish a structured collaboration to facilitate the clinical training of medical students from IOMIT'S SSR Medical College at Victoria Hospital and specialised hospitals; (b) ensure the clinical training is carried out under appropriate supervision, in line with the guidelines of the Ministry of Health and Wellness and the Higher Education Commission; and (c) set out the responsibilities of the Ministry of Health and Wellness, IOMIT'S SSR Medical College and the students as well as the terms and conditions for the conduct of the training.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research and the Department of Science and Technology of the Republic of India. The following activities will be undertaken, in line with the objectives of the Memorandum of Understanding - (a) experts and officials of the Department of Science and Technology will assist the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research and concerned stakeholders in the elaboration of a National Science and Technology Strategy; (b) concerned stakeholders will collaborate to develop the roadmap for the implementation of the National Science and Technology Strategy in Mauritius, with actionable steps, timelines and budgets; and (c) officials of the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research will benefit from the expertise and advice of the Indian party for the setting up of a Science and Technology Directorate, and also defining its mission and tasks.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of the 15th Best Practices Forum including the 31st Directors Joint Consultative Committee of the East, Central and Southern Africa Health Community from 04 to 06 August 2025, in Mauritius. The Best Practices Forum will bring together senior officials of the Ministry of Health of the participating Member States, policy makers, researchers health professionals from the private sector, representatives of regional and international organisations and universities, Non-Governmental Organisations, and other development partners to showcase, share as well as scale up best practices with a view to improving health outcomes in the East, Central and Southern Africa region.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of members of the Commission for Conciliation and Mediation for a period of two years.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 18.07.2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken of the reconstitution of the - (a) Board of Trustees of the Rajiv Gandhi Science Centre Trust Fund, with Mr Partish Chumroo as part-time Chairperson; (b) Standards Council of the Mauritius Standards Bureau, with Mr Abdool Hakim Fakira as part-time Chairperson; (c) Executive Council of the Hindi-Speaking Union, with Dr Anjalee Chintamunnee as President; (d) Executive Council of the Marathi-Speaking Union, with Mr Bhalchandra Govind as President; (e) Executive Council of the Tamil-Speaking Union, with Mr Egambrum Nagapen as President; (f) Executive Council of the Arabic-Speaking Union, with Mr Kalim Oullah K. Wahedally as President; (g) Executive Council of the Creole-Speaking Union, with Prof. Louis Arnaud Carpooran as President; (h) Executive Council of the Chinese-Speaking Union, with Mr Marc Eric You Fook Lim Kian Siang, MSK as President; and (i) Executive Council of the Urdu-Speaking Union, with Ms Abidah Zainab Alladee as President.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that His Excellency Shri Narendra Modi, Prime Minister of the Republic of India, will be the Chief Guest for the National Day Celebrations 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Prime Minister in the 38th Ordinary Session of the Assembly of the African Union held in Addis Ababa. The theme adopted by the Summit "Justice for Africans and People of African Descent Through Reparations" was of direct relevance to the struggle to exercise the sovereignty of Mauritius over the Chagos Archipelago. In his statement, the Prime Minister gave an account of some of the measures that Mauritius had taken to redress the negative impact of slavery and indentured servitude in Mauritius. The Prime Minister expressed his gratitude to the African Union for its unwavering support in the long struggle for the exercise of sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago. Mr Louis Olivier Bancoult, GCSK, Chairperson of the Chagossian Welfare Fund, who had fought relentlessly for the rights of the Chagossians, was part of the Mauritian delegation. The Prime Minister pointed out that Africa cannot be fully decolonised until and unless the Chagos Archipelago is fully restored under our sovereignty. In his address to the Summit as one of the newly elected Heads of State and Government, the Prime Minister pointed out that democracy, good governance and integrity would be the bedrock on which Mauritius would be rebuilt. He highlighted the fact that the Government Programme 2025-2029 which centred around People, Planet and Prosperity mirrors in many ways the African Agenda 2063, more particularly in terms of inclusive and sustainable development for our people. He also paid tribute to late President Sam Nujoma of Namibia. The Summit took cognisance of various Reports pertaining to the activities of the Peace and Security Council and the state of peace and security in Africa. The Summit elected Mr Mahmoud Ali Youssouf of Djibouti as the new Chairperson of the African Union Commission and Ambassador Mrs Selma Malika Haddadi of Algeria as Deputy Chairperson. In the margins of the Summit, the Prime Minister had a number of bilateral meetings with, inter alia, the President of the Union of Comoros, the President of Madagascar, a number of Heads of State and Government including those of Algeria, Botswana, Djibouti, Kenya and Mauritania, the Secretary-General of the United Nations, the Director-General of the World Trade Organisation, the Chief Executive Officer of Ethiopian Airlines, the President of the African Development Bank and the Secretary General of the Organisation Internationale de La Francophonie. The Prime Minister also met the Mauritian community working at the African Union and in other African Organisations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status of implementation of the small development projects financed under the Indian Grant Assistance to the tune of Rs400 million for the implementation of small development projects across Mauritius, including Rodrigues and Agaléga. Out of 96 projects, 51 projects had been inaugurated, 21 projects were completed and 24 projects were ongoing.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Energy Efficiency (Minimum Energy Performance Standards for Regulated Machinery) Regulations 2025 to provide for the introduction of mandatory Minimum Energy Performance Standards for air conditioners with a view to enhancing effectiveness of control on the energy performance of imported air conditioners. In the first instance, the "Regulated Machinery" would be "Air Conditioner" and would apply to split, double duct and single duct portable air conditioners having a cooling capacity of 12Kw or less. The Regulations would come into operation on 01 March 2025 and a moratorium of six months would be provided to dealers.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Sugar Insurance Fund (Reduced General and Fire Insurance Premiums) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated with a view to exempting sugar cane planters and métayers producing up to 60 tonnes of sugar from the general and fire insurance premiums payable to the Sugar Insurance Fund Board for crop 2024.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to amendments being brought to the Consumer Protection (Consumer Goods) (Maximum Mark-Up) Regulations 1998 to include frozen packed vegetables and canned vegetables under a Maximum Mark-Up Mechanism. This mechanism was expected to stabilise the market, bring immediate relief to households and reinforce Government's commitment to curbing the rising cost of living.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the 17th edition of the Public Service Excellence Award would be launched by the Ministry of Public Service and Administrative Reforms on the theme "Delivering Results and Building Trust through an Innovative, Sustainable and People-Centric Public Service". The competition would be open to four categories of institutions, that is, Ministries/Departments, Disciplined Forces, Local Authorities and Parastatal Bodies, and winners under each of these categories would be awarded cash prizes. The participating public organisations would be assessed on the basis of the following criteria - (a) Transformational Leadership; (b) Operational Efficiency to meet Customers' Needs and Aspirations; (c) Adoption of Innovative and sustainable initiatives; (d) Optimal use of Resources; (e) Fostering Good Governance, Accountability and Transparency; and (f) Promoting a culture of Safety and Health at Work.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the 29th Volleyball Championship for the Zone VII region would be held from 01 to 08 March 2025 in Mauritius. The competition would be organised by the Mauritius Volleyball Federation and the Confédération Africaine de Volley Ball. Clubs from Mauritius, Reunion Island, Madagascar, Comoros, Seychelles and Mayotte would participate in the competition.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Public Service and Administrative Reforms in the World Governments Summit meeting held in Dubai. The programme focused on shaping a better future for humanity and accordingly, plenary sessions of the Summit dealt with varied themes ranging from Governments in the Intelligent Age, Re-imagining Technology in Government, Global Political influence, Investments in Emerging markets to Financing the future and Effective Governance. The Minister participated in discussions on "Effective Governance for Crisis Resilience". After the meeting, the Minister had the opportunity to network with the Minister of Public Service of Uganda, the Director General of the African Training and Research Centre in Administration and Development, as well as the Malagasy delegation and the representatives of the African Association of Public Administration and Management.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 February 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the - (a) Medical Council of Mauritius; (b) Dental Council of Mauritius; (c) Allied Health Professionals Council; and (d) National Heritage Fund Board, with Mr Ricardo Lalande as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Public Inquiries Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to repeal the Commissions of Inquiry Act and replace it with a modern, more transparent and legally robust framework for the conduct of public inquiries into matters of significant public importance. In lieu of a Commission of Inquiry, a Board would be set up with wider powers. The purpose and scope for the setting up of a Board of Inquiry would be broadened. The Bill would make new provisions, inter alia, for – (a) consultations to be held with the person who had been appointed as Chairperson of a Board of Inquiry, or the person who was being proposed to be appointed as Chairperson thereof, prior to the terms of reference of the Board of Inquiry were finalised; (b) fixing a deadline by which a Board of Inquiry shall complete its inquiry and submit its findings; (c) apprising the National Assembly when a Board of Inquiry had been set up or would be set up; (d) enabling the public to obtain and view a record of evidence given, or produced, at an inquiry; (e) enabling any person who was of the opinion that his reputation was likely to be prejudicially affected by a public inquiry of being heard and to give evidence, and produce any document, in his defence; and (f) for making it mandatory to lay a copy of the findings of a public inquiry before the National Assembly and for its publication in the Gazette.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Customs Tariff (Amendment of Schedule) (No. 2) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for the implementation of the fifth round of customs duty reduction by Mauritius under the Comprehensive Economic Cooperation and Partnership Agreement (CECPA) between Mauritius and India with effect from 01 April 2025. The CECPA was signed in February 2021 and implementation of the respective market access preferences started on 01 April 2021 and customs duty reduction was to be effected over a total of 15 years. The CECPA comprised three components namely, Trade in Goods, Trade in Services and Economic Cooperation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Shipping Corporation Ltd would deploy the MV Peros Banhos to Agalega on 29 March 2025 for the supply of commodities to Agalega. Necessary arrangements had been made to ensure safe berthing of MV Peros Banhos. The Mauritius Ports Authority arranged for a qualified tug master to travel to Agalega on Board the MV Peros Banhos to assist the Tugmaster in Agalega for the proper berthing and unberthing of the vessel. The Outer Islands Development Corporation was liaising with the Indian Authorities for the only tug available in Agalega to be manned by a qualified tug master and additional fenders to be fixed on the jetty.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius joining the Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) Partnership, which had been established to facilitate collaboration in order to provide timely support for accelerated climate action. Mauritius, being a Party to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change and in line with the Paris Agreement, had a commitment to prepare and submit its Nationally Determined Contributions, whereby targets were set for the reduction of its greenhouse gas emissions. By joining the NDC Partnership, Mauritius would gain access to a global network of knowledge and resources to support the work in climate action.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the accession of the Republic of Mauritius to the Harare Protocol on Patents and Industrial Designs which would guarantee protection for patents and industrial designs and utility models by allowing registration of Intellectual Property. The objective of the protocol was to foster social, economic and technological growth in Member States. Mauritius acceded to the membership of the African Regional Intellectual Property Organization (ARIPO) under the Lusaka Agreement in September 2020. The ARIPO administers five treaties on intellectual property, including the Harare Protocol on Patents and Industrial Designs. Accession to the Harare Protocol on Patents and Industrial Designs would ensure an effective engagement and enable Mauritius to benefit from the services of ARIPO.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of new minimum entry requirements for eligibility to apply for a seat to study at public universities, in alignment with the decision to allow students of secondary schools who achieve at least three credits and at least a pass in English Language at a single sitting at School Certificate level or equivalent to progress to Higher School Certificate level.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Education and Human Resource signing a Letter of Exchange with the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) Institute for Information Technologies in Education for the implementation of the Education Management Information System (EMIS) in Mauritius. The two institutions would explore opportunities to establish close cooperation on matters of mutual interest on educational aspects of information and communication technology applications within the scope of their respective objectives and functions. Among the topics of interest, focus would be made on national capacities in the use and management of quality data that accurately reflects changes in the education system. The EMIS, an open-source system, would provide real-time quality and accurate data at all levels of education administration for better planning and decision-making. It would be implemented on a pilot basis in 21 secondary schools for 12 months and would eventually be rolled out to all state secondary schools.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the 48th session of the International Fund for Agricultural Development Governing Council, held in Italy, on the theme "Catalysing Investment at the First Mile." The Council primarily focused on funding mechanisms to integrate small farmers into the economic mainstream. The necessity for Public-Private Partnerships was highlighted, with calls for funding from multilateral agencies and active participation from non-governmental organisations. The Council also addressed critical issues such as climate change mitigation and adaptation. The Minister had a bilateral meeting with the Director-General of the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) where discussions centered on FAO's support in advancing strategic planning to enhance food security and reduce dependency on food import.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Attorney-General to Singapore to discuss avenues of collaboration with the Singaporean authorities regarding judicial reform, as outlined in the Government Programme 2025- 2029. Discussions were also held on anti-financial crime measures and prosecutorial cooperation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that HE Dr Milan Jaya Nyamrajsingh Meetarbhan, GOSK, Permanent Representative to the United Nations, would be accredited to the International Seabed Authority.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 21 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Board of the Food and Agricultural Research and Extension Institute, with Mr Ritesh Sumputh as part-time Chairperson; (b) Sugar Investment Trust Board; (c) Mauritius Cane Industry Authority Board, with Dr Dhanandjay Kawol, Senior Chief Executive as part-time Chairperson; (d) Sugar Insurance Fund Board, with Mr Kailash Bheenick as part-time Chairperson; (e) Law Reform Commission, with Mrs Yanilla Moonshiram as part-time Chairperson; and (f) Board of Trustees of the Mauritius Marathi Cultural Centre Trust, with Mr Danirow Bhiwajee as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the message of condolences that the Prime Minister had conveyed to Shri Narendra Modi, Prime Minister of the Republic of India in the aftermath of the heinous and brutal terror attack in Jammu and Kashmir Pahalgam region, resulting in the loss of lives and causing injuries to many innocent people. Cabinet also associated itself with the plea of the Prime Minister that such dastardly and despicable acts committed by terrorists must be condemned without reserve.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to instructions being conveyed to the Attorney-General's Office for the drafting of the Domestic Abuse Bill which would eventually repeal the existing Protection from Domestic Violence Act. The salient features of the new Domestic Abuse Bill would be – (a) definitions would be broadened to include other relationships such as intimate partners not living under the same roof; (b) introduction of a mandatory reporting clause whereby professionals suspecting acts of domestic abuse would have the mandatory obligation to report the matter; (c) definition of the different forms of abuse including physical, financial, sexual and psychological abuse; (d) introduction of a Protection Notice to be issued by the Police for cases reported during the night or weekend to protect the victim from further acts of domestic violence pending the issue of a Protection Order; (e) Enforcement Officers would be empowered to make application for Orders on behalf of victims who are unable to do so; (f) Police Officers would be able to access into the place of residence and accompany the victim inside to take his/her belonging; (g) a statement may be made on the case in any Police Station, irrespective of where the incident of violence occurred; and (h) counselling and rehabilitation programme would be made mandatory for perpetrators.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Monday 05 May 2025 would be declared a "No School Day" in view of the Municipal City Council and Municipal Town Elections 2025 scheduled on Sunday 04 May 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a contract for cleaning of Ile aux Bénitiers islet had been awarded. Cleaning works were ongoing and were expected to be completed within 20 days. Some 50 operators would, thereafter, be authorised to carry out their activities anew on the islet without erecting any permanent structure. The Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change would be responsible for the management of the islet pending the finalisation of a Masterplan by the Ministry of Housing and Lands. A Ministerial Delegation led by the Deputy Prime Minister would effect a site visit on the islet on 27 April 2025 to take stock of the situation.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Pomponette Beach would be proclaimed as Public Beach, in accordance with section 2 of the Beach Authority Act.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 25 April 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Land Transport had set up three sub- committees to formulate proposals that could be considered for the re-introduction of an improved version of the Penalty Points System, which had been replaced by the Cumulative Road Traffic Offences System in July 2015, as follows – (a) the first Sub-Committee would review the provisions of the Road Traffic (Amendment) Act which then dealt with the Penalty Points System. Amendments would be proposed, where necessary, to make the legislation more responsive to present needs in terms of traffic behaviour, and to ease the administration of the system to be introduced; (b) the second Sub-Committee would look into the IT issues relating to the introduction of the system, as well as in the administrative process and distribution of the Penalty Points Certificate, commonly known as the Driving Licence Counterpart, and legislative amendments that would be required in relation thereto; and (c) the third Sub-Committee would review the schedule of traffic offences and the points allocation in relation thereto, and propose an amended schedule to be considered in the revamped Penalty Points System.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the National Agency for Drug Control Bill into the National Assembly. The Bill would provide for the establishment of the National Agency for Drug Control (NADC) as an apex body responsible for drug use prevention, as well as rehabilitation and drug control issues. NADC would have broader responsibilities and powers than the defunct National Agency for the Treatment and Rehabilitation of Substance Abusers (NATReSA), and would therefore be responsible - (a) for the demand reduction of drugs - (i) through drug use prevention; (ii) by coordinating and monitoring the treatment and rehabilitation of persons who use drugs, and their social reintegration; (iii) through harm reduction of drugs, by minimising the negative health and social consequences on persons who use drugs and by minimising the transmission of HIV and AIDS and Hepatitis C among those persons; and (b) to coordinate the supply reduction of drugs by preventing, reducing or disrupting the entry, production and availability of drugs. NADC would be administered and managed by the Drug Control Board which would have the responsibility to make policy decisions on the functioning of NADC, under several Divisions, such as a Policy, Research and Strategy Division, a Treatment and Rehabilitation Services Division, a Prevention and Public Awareness Division, an HIV and AIDS Prevention and Care Coordination Division and a Supply Reduction Coordination Division. The Bill makes further provisions for the establishment of the National Drug Control Commission, chaired by the Prime Minister and comprising the Deputy Prime Minister and other Ministers. The National Drug Control Commission would be mainly responsible for, inter alia - (a) setting national objectives and goals, and determining policies and priorities with regard to drug control issues; (b) overseeing the overall coordination for all drug control activities in Mauritius for greater collaboration and synergy among stakeholders; and (c) monitoring and evaluating the implementation of the National Drug Control Master Plan and the National Action Plan for HIV and AIDS.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Speaker of the National Assembly had set up, as a matter of utmost priority, consultative meetings with a view to looking into the practical hurdles that needed to be cleared before introducing the use of Kreol Morisien in the National Assembly.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Municipal City Council and Municipal Town Council Elections (Amendment) Regulations 2025 would be promulgated to provide for, inter alia, same day counting and revision of the hours of poll from "7 am to 6 pm" to "7 am to 5 pm" for the forthcoming Municipal City Council and Municipal Town Council Elections.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Ministerial Delegation led by the Deputy Prime Minister and comprising the Minister of Housing and Lands, the Minister of National Infrastructure, the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives, Hon M. E. Juman, as well as Officials of the Prime Minister's Office, the Ministry of Housing and Lands, the Ministry of National Infrastructure, the Outer Islands Development Corporation, the Mauritius Ports Authority and the Mauritius Police Force, would proceed to Agalega on 03 and 04 April 2025 to carry out a survey of all Government buildings and conduct an assessment of the status of infrastructure following the passage of Cyclone Chido. Thereafter, a plan would be worked out for the maintenance of these infrastructure.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the second National Risk Assessment exercise carried out to assess the National Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Risk in Mauritius. The findings of the National Risk Assessment exercise would be disseminated to all stakeholders and the National Risk Assessment Public Report would be launched and made public. The Report covers the period January 2018 to December 2024 and provides - (a) a detailed snapshot of Money laundering/ Terrorist Financing activity, at national and sectoral levels for the period January 2018 to June 2022; and (b) major developments, including updated assessments on emerging threats for the period July 2022 to December 2024.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcomes of the 15th round of technical negotiations between the European Union and Eastern and Southern Africa States (ESA-5: Comoros, Madagascar, Mauritius, Seychelles and Zimbabwe) on the deepening of the interim Economic Partnership Agreement. The technical negotiations were conducted in parallel sessions and covered the following chapters: Trade in Services, Investment Liberalisation, and Digital Trade; Intellectual Property Rights, including Geographical Indications; Rules of Origin; Dispute Settlement & Institutional Provisions; and Trade and Sustainable Development.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the entry into force of the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) between Mauritius and the United Arab Emirates on 01 April 2025. The CEPA which marks a significant step towards the consolidation of bilateral economic and trade ties between the United Arab Emirates and Mauritius, covers Trade in Goods, Trade in Services, Economic Cooperation, Technical Barriers to Trade, Sanitary and Phytosanitary measures, Small and Medium Enterprises, Trade Remedies, Investment Facilitation, Intellectual Property Rights, Digital Trade among others.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the 59th SADC Trade Negotiating Forum (SADC TNF) and TNF Retreat would be held from 05 to 09 May 2025, in Mauritius. The meeting would be organised by the SADC Secretariat. The SADC TNF technical meeting would consider a number of trade-related issues, namely progress made to implement Summit decisions and the 33rd SADC Committee of Ministers of Trade decisions, covering Trade, Customs, Sanitary and Phytosanitary issues, Technical Barriers to Trade issues, amongst others. The meeting would look into the implementation and consolidation of the SADC Free Trade Area.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of an Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Côte d'Ivoire on reciprocal exemption of entry visas for holders of diplomatic and official passports. Nationals of both countries, holding valid diplomatic or service passports would be exempted from visa entry requirements for stays of less than 90 days. This would be applicable to persons who are assigned to a Diplomatic Mission, Consular Post, Permanent Mission and Organisation in the other State or attending meetings and conferences. Such exemption of entry visas would thus facilitate travel for diplomatic staff and government officials of both countries.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius maintaining its candidature for the position of Vice-Chair of the Indian Ocean Rim Association for the period 2025 to 2027 and the position of Chair for the period 2027 to 2029.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the submission of the "Voluntary Pledges and Commitments" document of the Republic of Mauritius to the United Nations Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights, in the context of its candidature for a seat on the Human Rights Council for the period January 2026 to December 2028. The elections would take place in October 2025 at the United Nations General Assembly in New York. The "Voluntary Pledges and Commitments" document was premised on the commitment of Government to enhance the protection of fundamental rights of citizens, including the constitutional right to privacy, workers' rights, new generation rights relating to the environment, technology, health, education, other economic, social and cultural rights provided under international treaties, and also the rights of Nature.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the transfer of all activities of the Vallée D'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation to the National Parks and Conservation Service. Following the conduct of a performance review by the Office of Public Sector Governance, with a view to redressing the current situation at the Vallée D'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation, the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries had agreed that the transfer of all activities of the Vallée D'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation to the National Parks and Conservation Service was the best course of action. The Vallée D'Osterlog Endemic Garden Foundation Act would be repealed and amendments would be brought to the Native Terrestrial Biodiversity and National Parks Act, accordingly.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of an Application Fee of Rs100,000 for a Strategic Environmental Assessment and the review of the existing Application Fees for the Preliminary Environmental Report from Rs3,000 to Rs10,000 and for the Environmental Impact Assessment from Rs15,000 to Rs50,000. The Environment Protection (Applications for PER Approval and EIA Licence) (Processing Fees) Regulations 2011, would be revoked and replaced with new set of Regulations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the water level in the reservoirs had reached 47.5% of their capacity. The Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities, the Central Water Authority and the Water Resources Unit were monitoring the situation on a daily basis.
ministries: Ministry Of Energy and Public Utilities

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Polytechnics Mauritius Ltd signing a Memorandum of Understanding, jointly with King's College London and the Guy's and the St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust, London. The Memorandum of Understanding would aim at facilitating and enhancing academic collaboration among the three institutions in the following areas of mutual benefit - (a) development of articulation and Top-Up programmes of King's College undergraduate and postgraduate qualifications in the fields of Nursing, Midwifery and Palliative Care to be run in the Republic of Mauritius, in collaboration with the Guy's and the St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust; (b) development of programmes in the fields of Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Informatics, in collaboration with the Faculty of Natural, Mathematical & Engineering Sciences of King's College; (c) offer of observerships, fellowships, placements and other clinical initiatives with the Guy's and the St Thomas' NHS Foundation Trust; (d) development of Professional Education, capacity building programmes, micro-credentials modules and other programmes for students as well as for upskilling of Polytechnics Mauritius Ltd staff and other in-service professionals; and (e) offering students of either institution with mobility opportunities.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Quality Assurance Authority, Mauritius, signing Letters of Intent with the Quality Assurance Agency for Higher Education of the United Kingdom and the Council on Higher Education of the Republic of South Africa. The aims of the Letters of Intent would be to - (a) facilitate the exchange of information and promote the sharing of best practices in the field of quality assurance; (b) provide the Quality Assurance Authority with networking opportunities within the global higher education community and thus enhance the visibility and credibility of the Authority on the international level; and (c) empower the Quality Assurance Authority to improve its quality assurance processes and enhance its international standing, hence contributing to the development of a robust higher education system in Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research signing a Memorandum of Understanding with the Edith Cowan University, Australia. The Memorandum of Understanding would aim at fostering collaboration between the institutions. The Memorandum of Understanding would provide for the following avenues of cooperation - (a) capacity building, curriculum development, academic and articulation agreement in designated areas of mutual interest; (b) professional development courses in the areas of teacher education, nursing, paramedics and other areas as mutually agreed by the parties; (c) tuition-waiver for PhD seats in the field of education; (d) research collaboration; (e) student and staff mobility; and (f) facilitating the enrolment of beneficiaries of scholarships from the Mauritian Government or other sponsoring bodies in Mauritius and signified interest to pursue their studies at Edith Cowan University.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives signing with the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa, an Implementation Agreement with a view to completing the activities under a Technical Assistance Facility project to further enhance the development of the leather value chain in Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status regarding measures taken to phase out the use of coal in electricity generation by the Independent Power Producers, through an increased utilisation of biomass.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the SADC Extraordinary Summit of Heads of State and Government which was held virtually. The Agenda of the Summit related to the security situation in the Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo. The Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade led the Mauritius delegation to the Council.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, in accordance with Section 87 of the Constitution, the President of the Republic of Mauritius had conveyed his approval to the appointment of - (a) Dr Rajeshwar Jeetah as High Commissioner of Mauritius to the United Kingdom with residence in London; (b) Ms Sarojini Seeneevassen as High Commissioner of Mauritius to Australia with residence in Canberra; (c) Mr Brian Neil Joseph Glover as Ambassador and Permanent Representative of Mauritius to the United Nations in Switzerland with residence in Geneva; (d) Mr Bruno Clifford Cunniah as Ambassador of Mauritius to the Republic of Madagascar with residence in Antananarivo; and (e) Mrs Anne Marie Danielle Perrier as High Commissioner of Mauritius to the Republic of Mozambique with residence in Maputo.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting 28 March 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the - (a) Board of the Small Farmers Welfare Fund with, Mr Mahendrah Goonniah as part-time Chairperson; (b) Seafarer's Welfare Fund Board, with Mr Jacques Patrick Bouquet as part- time Chairperson; (c) Board of Trustees of the Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Botanic Garden Trust, with Mr Rigg Singh Needroo as part-time Chairperson; (d) Board of Trustees of the Trust Fund for Specialised Medical Care, with Dr Pravish Rai Sookha as part-time Chairperson; (e) Board of the National Council for Sports in Schools and Universities, with Mr Shailendra Dhuromsingh as part-time Chairperson; (f) National Productivity and Competitiveness Council, with Mr Arshad Duymun as part-time Chairperson; (g) Board of Trustees of the Mauritius Tamil Cultural Centre Trust, with Mrs Vimala Sawmy as part-time Chairperson; and (h) Board of Trustees of the Mauritius Telugu Cultural Centre, with Mr Mootaloo Poolovadoo as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recommendations of the two Committees set up to look into possible support to be provided to those persons who will not be eligible for the Basic Retirement Pension (BRP) and who rely mainly or solely on the BRP, and has agreed to the following - (a) persons who reach the age of 60 years as from 01 September 2025 and who will become ineligible for the BRP will benefit from a monthly Income Support of Rs10,000, including an end-of-year bonus, subject to an income criterion; (b) persons will be eligible for the Income Support provided that the monthly income does not exceed Rs20,000 for a couple and Rs10,000 for a single person household; (c) for a single person, the monthly income will include emoluments, income from business or profession, and contributory retirement benefits, but will exclude the lump sum payable on retirement and other benefits (including the Invalidity Allowance) provided by the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (d) for a married person, the monthly income will include emoluments, income from business or profession, and contributory retirement benefits but exclude the lump sum payable on retirement and other benefits (including the Invalidity Allowance) provided by the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity as well as the basic invalidity pension, the Income Support in lieu of the BRP or the BRP being drawn by the spouse; (e) beneficiaries of the Basic Invalidity Pension, the Basic Widow's Pension and the Invalidity Allowance, who turn 60 years, will continue to receive their respective pensions until they qualify for the BRP. Those benefitting from the Basic Invalidity Pension and the Basic Widow's Pension will not be concerned by the Income Support; (f) beneficiaries will include housewives, retirees, employed and self-employed that meet the criteria; and (g) the period during which the person reaching the age of 60 years on 01 September will be paid the Income Support is as follows –  one year for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2025 up to 31 August 2026;  two years for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2026 up to 31 August 2027;  three years for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2027 up to 31 August 2028;  four years for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2028 up to 31 August 2029;  five years for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2029 up to 31 August 2030; and  five years for those reaching age of 60 years on 01 September 2030 up to 31 August 2031. The Mauritius Revenue Authority (MRA) will be responsible for putting in place and administering an IT system for the payment of the Income Support. Beneficiaries will have to submit a one-time application for the Income Support to the MRA unless there is a change in circumstances. The Income Support will be credited directly to the bank account of the beneficiaries on the first working day of every month. Around Rs8.7 billion will be disbursed in respect of income support to eligible beneficiaries over a period of five years. The Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity will carry out a holistic review of the invalidity pension system and make appropriate recommendations for reform. A Commission of Experts will be set up to look into the reform of the three pillars of the pension system, as well as the revamping of the National Pension Fund to replace the Contribution Sociale Généralisée. The Terms of Reference of the Commission are being worked out. The Chairperson and members of the Commission will be appointed soon.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that Mauritius has been elected as member of the Executive Council of the UNESCO Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission for the term 2025-2027.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of the finals of the Inter-College Sports Competition, commonly known as the Jeux Inter-Collèges, from 14 to 18 July 2025. The competition will serve as a platform for discovering young talents, fostering camaraderie and instilling a deep sense of pride and belonging among our youth. The competition will comprise five sports disciplines, namely athletics, badminton, football, table tennis and volleyball. 2,568 students, including 40 from Rodrigues, qualified for the national competition. The finals for football and athletics will take place at the Côte d'Or National Sports Complex whereas those for badminton will be held at the National Badminton Centre in Rose Hill. The finals of the table tennis competition are scheduled at the National Table Tennis Centre in Beau-Bassin, and volleyball at the Pandit Sahadeo Gymnasium in Vacoas. An overall champion of the Jeux Inter- Collèges will be declared based on the cumulative points obtained by each college in the five sports disciplines during the finals. The winners will be rewarded with medals and trophies.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the continued collaboration of the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare with the United Nations Fund for Population Activities (UNFPA) for the period 2024-2028, through the implementation of the Annual Workplan for the year 2025, in line with the United Nations Sustainable Development Cooperation Framework, signed between the United Nations and the Republic of Mauritius. Following the signature of an Implementing Partner Agreement, funds to the tune of Rs303,000 will be disbursed by the UNFPA for the year 2025 and the following projects will be implemented – (a) elaboration of a new Domestic Abuse Bill for Mauritius; (b) development of an Executive Package on Gender-Based Violence Case Management and Referral Pathways; and (c) training of Trainers Programme on Inter-Agency Response to Gender-Based Violence, facilitated by the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation sending to the International Telecommunication Union Radiocommunication Bureau a Letter of Agreement on behalf of the Republic of Mauritius for Angola to act as the International Telecommunication Union Notifying Administration for the SADC Shared Satellite Network.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries and the SBM Bank (Mauritius) Ltd (SBM) to establish a framework of cooperation between the Forestry Service of the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries and the SBM. The SBM will collaborate with the Forestry Service on the sustainable management of forests with a view to contributing to the economic development of the country in accordance with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. The Memorandum of Understanding will cover, inter alia, the following areas of strategic collaboration – (a) increase in the area of protected, restored or sustainably managed forests; (b) reduction of the annual rate of deforestation of natural forests; (c) dissemination of information and sharing of experiences in the field of sustainable forest management and land use in the dry tropical forests; (d) improving the management, protection, rational use and diversification of forest resources; (e) protection of Environmentally Sensitive Areas in forests to ensure a continuous supply of clean and fresh water for domestic and other purposes; (f) preservation of our rich heritage of native species of plants, birds and animals; and (g) promotion of the overall welfare and wellbeing of the population by improving access and facilities in our forests for leisure and recreation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the signing of an agreement between the University of Mauritius and the University of Waterloo, Canada under which an amount of around Rs6 million will be allocated for a research project titled "Resilience to Climate Vulnerability and Environmental Risk" (RECOVER). The RECOVER project is an innovative, timely and needs-driven North-South initiative aimed at addressing climate change adaptation through a multi-risk and multi-sector approach. Pilot projects will be conducted in three countries, namely Fiji, Maldives and Mauritius, represented respectively by the University of Fiji, the Maldives National University and the University of Mauritius. The objectives of the project will be met through multisectoral partnerships and combined scientific excellence of multiple disciplines that will engage stakeholders in co-creating knowledge and developing place-based solutions. The collaborative approach will enhance transformative capacity in small islands by promoting inclusive development, building partnerships and creating communities of practice.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the National Productivity and Competitiveness Council (NPCC) and the Japan Productivity Center, for capacity-building, networking and promoting Kaizen in neighbouring countries in Africa. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding, which is being signed in the context of the African Kaizen Initiative, is to establish the terms and conditions for the execution of a project submitted by the NPCC in January 2025 to assist enterprises operating in the Agro- processing and Tourism sectors of Madagascar. The project will entail, inter alia, - (a) the holding of a public webinar introducing the concept of Kaizen to a Malagasy audience; (b) a call for interest of Malagasy enterprises that are interested in the project; (c) the conduct of a training for the interested enterprises on lean management tools and techniques; (d) a diagnosis of the enterprises' readiness to implement Kaizen lean management techniques; (e) the implementation of the Kaizen project in the selected enterprises which is expected to be done virtually over a period of two months; and (f) the organisation of a dissemination workshop to share the outcome of the project.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in the 17th United States-Africa Business Summit held in Luanda, Angola, which was organised by the Corporate Council of Africa (CCA). The Minister participated in the Roundtable on Pathways to Prosperity: A shared vision for Trade by Investment. In his address, the Minister called for an enhanced partnership between Africa and the United States (US), through the Africa Continental Free Trade Area, which can unlock the door for US businessmen to a unified market of over 1.4 billion people. He also underscored the crucial role played by Small and Medium Enterprises, which also includes a women empowerment dimension given the active participation of African women in businesses, in bolstering trade ties between Africa and US. The Minister had a bilateral meeting with the Chief Executive Officer of the CCA and Ambassador Troy Fitrell, Deputy Head of the US delegation to the Business Summit. The Minister also participated in the Closing Plenary on the Future of US-Africa Partnership.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives in the Commonwealth Trade Ministers Meeting (CTMM) which was held in Namibia. A Commonwealth Business Summit was also organised in the margins of the CTMM and aimed at enhancing public-private sector dialogue and partnerships and unlock untapped opportunities. The Minister was invited to speak on the plenary panel of the Business Summit entitled "Navigating Global Trade Uncertainty - The Commonwealth Advantage". The CTMM considered the recommendations made by Senior Trade Officials and examined the 2025 Commonwealth Trade Ministers Meeting Outcome Statement as well as the Commonwealth Statement on the Multilateral Trading System. The Minister also had bilateral meetings with the Secretary General of the Commonwealth, the Assistant Minister for Immigration and Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs and Trade of Australia as well as the Minister of State for Trade and Industry and National Development of Singapore, and Board member of the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Board of the Mauritius Society of Authors will proceed with the reinstatement of Mr Antoine Eugène Gérard Louise to the post of Director of the Mauritius Society of Authors.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 04 July 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Rose Belle Sugar Estate Board, with Mr Rajendra Kumar Ujoodha, GOSK as part- time Chairperson; (b) Council of Registered Professional Engineers of Mauritius; and (c) Mauritius Research and Innovation Council with, Dr Khemduth Singh Angateeah, as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the stand taken by the President of the United States of America following discussions he has had with the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom (UK) regarding Diego Garcia. President Trump expressed his support in favour of the UK Prime Minister's decision of proceeding with the signature of the Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland concerning the Chagos Archipelago including Diego Garcia. Cabinet has further taken note that the UK is going ahead with the parliamentary process regarding the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill and that debates in the House of Lords will resume shortly.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the National Day 2026 being celebrated under the theme "In Peace, Justice and Liberty". The Flag Raising Ceremony, followed by a cultural show will be held at Champ de Mars on 12 March 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare will organise a national event to celebrate International Women's Day on Sunday 08 March 2026 at the Côte d'Or National Sports Complex on the theme "Rights. Justice. Action. For ALL Women and Girls". The International Women's Day celebration in Mauritius will bring together around 1,500 women from diverse backgrounds and sectors. The celebration aims to reaffirm Government's commitment to promoting gender equality and women's empowerment and also recognise the contribution of women and girls to national development.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Minister of Justice of the Republic of Madagascar is currently on official visit in Mauritius. The Minister paid a courtesy call on the Prime Minister and had meetings with the Deputy Prime Minister, the Attorney-General and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade, during which issues of collaboration between the two countries, including in the Judiciary, were discussed.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Budget measure for the provision of free internet access to families registered with the Social Register of Mauritius (SRM) has now entered its operational phase. Internet Gratis, is a government initiative aiming to guarantee equitable access to digital technology by the most vulnerable households, both in Mauritius and Rodrigues, and to strengthen their social, educational, and economic inclusion. Rs85 million has been allocated to finance this measure annually in spite of the economic and financial constraints. The measure will concern more than 7,000 families, covering nearly 31,000 persons. The three Internet Service Providers have agreed to provide specific internet packages approved by the Information and Communication Technologies Authority for this scheme. The State Informatics Limited has devised a platform to allow the Internet Service Providers to verify whether the applicant is on the SRM database. Notices have been issued by the National Empowerment Foundation to invite its beneficiaries to avail of the free internet from any one of the Internet Service Providers. Notices will also be issued to eligible persons on the SRM database by the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Waste Water (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2026. The Wastewater Management Authority subscribers consuming six cubic metres or less and listed on the Social Register of Mauritius will be exempted from the payment of wastewater charges.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 03 February 2026, the House of Representatives of the United States of America passed the modified H.R 7148 (Consolidated Appropriations Act 2026) as approved by the US Senate. The Minibus Legislation included the extension of the African Growth and Opportunity Act until 31 December 2026 with retroactive effect from 30 September 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the launching of the National Cooperative Excellence Awards 2026 by the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives. The National Cooperative Excellence Awards are intended to serve as a long-term benchmark for Mauritian cooperative societies, promoting performance excellence through the adoption of internationally recognised norms, standards, and quality management systems using the Baldrige Excellence framework. The Awards constitute a national recognition framework for outstanding performance within the cooperative sector. The competition is open to all Cooperative Societies and Federations in Mauritius and awards will be given to the best Primary Cooperative Society and best Secondary (Federation) Cooperative Society. One prize will be dedicated especially to the cooperative societies in Rodrigues. Moreover, with a spirit of promoting sound management practices, awards will be presented to the most performing cooperative society on aspects of 'good governance', 'financial sustainability', 'digitalisation', 'creativity and innovation' and 'adoption of sustainable green practices'. The award ceremony will be held in April 2026 under the theme "Cooperatives: the power to act for a sustainable and inclusive future".

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of the recommendations of a project entitled "Supply Chain Analysis for Plastic Pellets", undertaken by the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change under the "Implementing Sustainable Low and Non-Chemical Development in Small Island Developing States" (ISLANDS) Project, which is being implemented in 33 Small Island Developing States worldwide and financed by the Global Environment Facility. To address plastic pollution through the development of a strategic framework for the progressive reduction of virgin plastic pellets within the national economy, a comprehensive supply chain analysis of plastic pellets was undertaken with a view to coming up with the formulation of a strategic framework aimed at progressively reducing the use of virgin plastic pellets within the national economy. The main recommendations of the study are as follows – (a) the regulation of plastic pellets as a controllable item with a registration process; and (b) improvement in Customs Data Access and Reporting through a collaboration with the Mauritius Revenue Authority.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the establishment of a framework of collaboration between Fairtrade Africa and the Mauritius Cane Industry Authority and the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the two parties. The Memorandum of Understanding aims at improving the resilience of small sugar cane planters through the adoption of Good Agricultural Practices in sugar cane production to boost productivity per unit area, increase planters' revenue and ensure the subsequent sustainability as well as the compliance of Fairtrade Certified Producers in Mauritius. The scope of the Memorandum of Understanding relates to the following – (a) implementation of Good Agricultural Practices; (b) training of trainers for members of Fairtrade Cooperative Credit Societies; (c) interpretation of soil analysis results for planters who are opting for new plantation; and (d) provision of technical advice.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare and the European Family Justice Center Alliance (EFJCA), which is a legal entity and the officially recognised network of Family Justice Centers and related multidisciplinary models in Europe. As an umbrella organisation, the EFJCA facilitates the exchange of knowledge, experiences, and good practices among its members. Its core focus is the development of a multidisciplinary approach to gender-based violence, domestic violence, sexual violence, and child abuse. The Memorandum of Understanding will be beneficial to the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare in further strengthening family welfare and protection services through technical cooperation, exchange programmes, consultancy support, and the sharing of best practices. The Memorandum of Understanding will also contribute to reinforcing collaboration with European partners in areas related to the prevention of and response to gender-based and domestic violence.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a Steering Committee under the chair of the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation to facilitate the development of the Super App "KOREK". Presently, citizens can access details of their driving licence and number of points accumulated under the Penalty Point System through the App "KOREK". The Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation proposes to integrate services scattered over a number of apps like Lespwar, BesafeMoris, MoRendezvous and MoKloud into one single app. The Super App will include a wide spectrum of other citizen-centric and business-oriented services, including digital driving licence, driving licence renewal, vehicle registration, civil status and mobile identity services, appointment booking at public health centres, trade fee payments, application for character certificates, student support and scholarship applications. Thus, "KOREK" will evolve into a Super App to become a single digital gateway to all public services, accessible through only one secure login. The Super App constitutes a pivotal advancement to Government's vision of building a modern, inclusive, and citizen-centric digital state. It aims at placing citizens at the centre of service delivery, reducing administrative burdens, and rendering government services simpler, faster, and more attuned to everyday real-life requirements.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the findings and recommendations made by Dr Bernard Gruson, Honorary Director General of the Hôpitaux Universitaires de Genève, following his visit to Mauritius from 16 December 2025 to 05 January 2026. Dr Bernard Gruson conducted a mission with a view to establishing cooperation with the Hôpitaux Universitaires de Genève. His assessment covered aspects including the selection of hospital managers, improvement of hospital management, assessment of healthcare risks and opportunities, and alignment with the Government's strategic objectives for hospital and ambulatory care. The Ministry of Health and Wellness will examine the recommendations made in the report and assess the feasibility of implementation, in consultation with relevant stakeholders.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade, in collaboration with the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) of the United Kingdom (UK) and the British High Commission in Mauritius will be organising a one-day validation workshop on the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Protocol on Digital Trade in Mauritius. The aim of the workshop will be to validate the findings of the draft Report entitled 'Implementing the AfCFTA Protocol on Digital Trade in Mauritius'. The Report, which undertakes an assessment and alignment requirements of Mauritius' digital trade regulatory framework in the context of the AfCFTA Protocol on Digital Trade, is part of the 'Supporting Investment & Trade in Africa' (SITA) programme, launched by the FCDO. The SITA programme is part of the UK's efforts to support trade, investment and development on the African continent. It further aims to support AfCFTA negotiations, assist on country implementation and undertake relevant policy analysis. The workshop will bring together participants from different ministries as well as regulatory bodies such as the Bank of Mauritius, the Financial Services Commission and the Information and Communication Technologies Authority of Mauritius.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent participation of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations in the annual Ministerial Roundtable of the Global Labor Market Conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. The theme of the Ministerial Roundtable was "From Policy to Practice: Opening immediate pathways to work amid rapid change". In his intervention, the Minister spoke about the impact of generative Artificial Intelligence on the job market and the need to skill, reskill and upskill our labour force. He highlighted some of the initiatives taken by Government to address this issue in consultation with trade unions and employers' organisations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Optical Council of Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that HE Dr Patrick Herminie, President of the Republic of Seychelles will be on State Visit in Mauritius from 11 to 14 March 2026, on the occasion of the National Day Celebrations 2026. The President of the Republic of Seychelles will be the Chief Guest at the Flag Raising Ceremony at the Champ de Mars, Port Louis on 12 March 2026. During the visit, the President will, inter alia, - (a) pay a courtesy call on the President of the Republic of Mauritius; (b) have a tête-à-tête with the Prime Minister, followed by a Delegation Level Meeting ; (c) witness the exchange of agreements and make a joint statement to the Media; (d) attend the State Banquet; (e) participate in a Wreath Laying Ceremony and Tree Planting at Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Botanic Garden; and (f) visit the Aapravasi Ghat World Heritage Site and the Intercontinental Slavery Museum. Cabinet has agreed to the signing of the following Agreements, Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) and Programme of Cooperation which will be exchanged during the State Visit – (a) Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Seychelles on Access for Seychelles Vessels to the Mauritius Fishing Zone pertaining to the promotion and implementation of responsible fishing by Seychelles vessels in the Mauritius Fishing Zone based on the principle of non-discrimination between the different fleets fishing in those waters; (b) Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Seychelles on Access for Mauritius Vessels to the Seychelles Fishing Zone relating to the promotion and implementation of responsible fishing by Mauritius vessels in the Seychelles Fishing Zone based on the principle of non-discrimination between the different fleets fishing in those waters; (c) MoU between the Republic of Mauritius and the Republic of Seychelles in Agriculture, Sanitary and Phytosanitary Issues for the establishment of a long-term relationship that utilises the Parties' complementary research expertise, impact pathway skills and institutional and capacity development strength; (d) MoU between the Mauritius Fire and Rescue Service of the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Seychelles Fire and Rescue Services Agency of the Government of the Republic of Seychelles to foster cooperation with a view to creating the necessary framework and mechanisms to allow a better understanding of issues common to both States related to fires and other emergencies; (e) MoU between Statistics Mauritius of the Republic of Mauritius and the National Bureau of Statistics of the Republic of Seychelles on Cooperation in the Area of Statistical Development to establish a practical framework for cooperation between Mauritius and Seychelles with regard to improvement in the production and dissemination of high-quality official statistics and to strengthen institutional relations as well as capacity building; (f) Twinning MoU between Le Morne Heritage Trust Fund of the Republic of Mauritius and the National Heritage Resource Council of the Republic of Seychelles which defines the general framework for cooperation through twinning and the base of an economic, social and cultural heritage management partnership between the two regions and parties concerned; and (g) Programme of Cooperation in the fields of Arts and Culture for the Years 2026- 2029 between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the Republic of Seychelles which encourages the exchange of information on cultural policies and promotes activities relating to folklore and tradition between Mauritius and Seychelles.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that in response to the recent escalation of hostilities and mounting tensions throughout the broader Middle East, following the intervention of the Prime Minister with the Saudi Authorities, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade established direct contact with Mauritian citizens across the Gulf region with the support and coordination of the diplomatic Missions in Riyadh and Islamabad, and the Mauritius Consulate in Dubai. An alert notice was issued on 28 February 2026 with a view to conveying the emergency contact details of the Missions and the crisis cell of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade. The notice also advised all Mauritians to defer any travel to Iran and countries of the Gulf region. Around 121 Mauritian nationals, who are in Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have established contact with the Mission in Riyadh in view of their return to Mauritius. Through robust partnerships and consultations with Emirates and other airlines, rapid protocols were activated. 20 Mauritian nationals, mainly pilgrims, departed Jeddah for Mauritius on 02 March 2026. In addition, 39 Mauritian nationals safely returned to Mauritius on 03 March 2026 via Nairobi. Three flights have been scheduled by Emirates for the Dubai–Mauritius route from 05 to 07 March 2026. Out of the 72 Mauritian nationals currently registered in Dubai, approximately 80% are expected to depart Dubai on these flights by 07 March 2026. Arrangements are also being made for one Mauritian national who is currently in Iran to travel to Mauritius via Turkey. Reaffirming its diplomatic stance, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade issued a Communiqué on 03 March 2026, calling for an immediate cessation of hostilities and the resumption of dialogue between the involved parties. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade also expressed its official appreciation to the United Arab Emirates and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia for their invaluable logistical and diplomatic support in facilitating the repatriation of Mauritian nationals.
ministries: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recommendations of the Inter-Ministerial Committee on the Strategy for Port Development under the chair of the Deputy Prime Minister which met on five occasions and during which discussions were held with the representatives of the Prime Minister's Office, the Economic Development Board, the Mauritius Ports Authority, the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd, the private sector and the Trade Unions of both the Mauritius Ports Authority and the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd. Discussions were focused on key strategic themes, including review of the port governance model, acquisition of port equipment, training and employee relations, opening of share capital to Shipping Lines as well as the implementation of the Island Container Terminal Project which was raised by the Prime Minister during his recent mission to India. It was unanimously agreed that there is extreme urgency to devise a new strategy for the survival of the port. Cabinet has agreed to the Inter-Ministerial Committee pursuing negotiations with two strategic shipping lines for the latter to enter the capital of the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd alongside Government, as minority shareholders. The shipping lines will then bring commercial discipline, operational expertise, provide mobile cranes as required, critical direct incentive to route vessels through Port Louis and grow traffic volumes. Discussions with Trade Unions will also be pursued. Cabinet has further agreed to the Mauritius Ports Authority refocusing its role as the sole authority to regulate and control the port sector and provide marine services; and the Cargo Handling Corporation Ltd as the unique operator of the Port.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
