text: Cabinet has agreed to drafting instructions being conveyed to the Attorney-General's Office for the inclusion of Road Rage incidents in the draft Road Traffic (Amendment) Bill. Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Land Transport will collaborate with Cerebro Ltd and Etiquette Africa, a pan-African initiative dedicated to fostering a culture of respect and a civility non-profit organisation promoting behavioural excellence and civility across the African continent, for the conduct of a National Survey on Road Civility and Behaviour. The objective of the survey is to capture the experiences, perceptions, and attributes of motorists, pedestrians and public transport users to, inter alia, measure the perceived level of civility and identify key friction points on Mauritian roads and to evaluate perceived public responses and etiquette in accident scenarios. The survey is expected to provide insights that can guide public awareness campaigns, policy refinement and infrastructural planning. Specifically, the survey will delve into the daily commuting experience of citizens and capture their perceptions and experiences across a range of critical scenarios.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of allegations of malpractices against Mr J. Arunasalom, Chairperson of the Mauritius Tourism Authority and has decided that – (a) an enquiry be carried out into the allegations made; and (b) actions be initiated for the suspension of Mr J. Arunasalom, with immediate effect.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of a new scheme under the National Solidarity Fund (NSF) for the payment of an allowance for a period not exceeding two months to victims of domestic violence residing in shelters. The scheme will be applicable to victims of domestic violence who are issued a Protection Order from the Court Magistrate, and residing in shelters run by Non-Governmental Organisations recognised by the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare. Assistance will be granted for the first two months of stay of the victim in a shelter. The objective of the scheme is to promote the economic empowerment of victims of domestic violence. It will be a critical step towards helping them to regain their autonomy, security and dignity in the society. It will also play a transformative role by allowing victims to address their immediate personal needs and create a pathway for their long-term stability. The eligibility criteria, guidelines and mode of operation for the new scheme have been worked out by the NSF.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the National Pensions (Non-Contributory Benefits) (Amendment) Regulations 2026. The objective of the Regulations is to pay in full the basic pensions under the National Pensions Act, to beneficiaries whose stay in public hospitals is in excess of three consecutive months, excluding inmates of Brown Sequard Mental Health Care Centre.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Customs Tariff (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2026 which provide for the implementation of customs duty reductions by Mauritius in view of its preferential market access commitments under - (a) the sixth round for the Comprehensive Economic Cooperation and Partnership Agreement with India; and (b) the second round for the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement with the United Arab Emirates.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the updated National Oil Spill Contingency Plan (NOSCP) of the Republic of Mauritius (2025), which adopts the Incident Command Structure for managing an oil spill response. The NOSCP (2025) adopts a new tiered response whereby the environmental sensitivity of an affected area and the severity of impact in case of an oil spill are taken into consideration. The Plan makes provision for the establishment of environmental pre-spill baseline data collection to assess and monitor post-spill damage to the environment and to support claims for compensation against damage to the marine ecosystem as well as the determination of clean- up endpoints to assess whether the clean-up response has achieved the desired results. The strategies to be adopted for oil spill response and recovery are also defined.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius endorsing, on a voluntary basis, the Declaration on Information Integrity on Climate Change, which was launched at the 30th Session of the Conference of Parties (COP30) to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) held in November 2025 in Brazil. The purpose of the Declaration is to combat climate misinformation, denialism, and attacks on climate communicators by establishing shared commitments for Governments, technology platforms, media and academia to promote accurate, evidence-based climate information, protect journalists/scientists and foster media resilience for effective climate action. Under the Declaration, signatories commit to the following – (a) promote the integrity of information related to climate change in line with international human rights law, including freedom of expression standards; (b) support the sustainability of a diverse and resilient media ecosystem to ensure accurate and reliable coverage on climate and environmental issues; (c) support the inclusion of information integrity commitments into Action for Climate Empowerment Agenda under the UNFCCC; (d) promote informed and inclusive climate action by advancing equitable access to accurate, evidence-based and understandable information for all; and (e) foster cooperation and capacity building to address threats to information integrity, safeguarding those reporting and researching climate issues.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 20 February 2026, the United States Supreme Court ruled tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act as being unlawful. Consequently, the United States Administration has introduced a universal 10% for a period of 150 days, with a possible increase to 15%. On 02 March 2026, the United States Administration's 2026 Trade Policy Agenda confirmed Agreements of Reciprocal Trade as its primary market-access tool. Mauritius is following up with the United States Administration for negotiation on that issue. A formal letter will be sent to the United States Trade Representative to confirm our interest and readiness to discuss an Agreement on Reciprocal Trade, highlighting concrete steps already taken by Mauritius to address trade imbalance. A high-level delegation made up of high-level officials and the private sector will proceed to the United States in the months to come to secure high-level engagements.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the guidelines on best practices for roadside weed management using herbicides in Mauritius. The guidelines provide detailed instructions across several critical domains. The guidelines also address critical environmental concerns such as water contamination, soil degradation, development of herbicide-resistant weeds and recommendation on alternatives such as bio-herbicides in sensitive zones. The guidelines emphasise the establishment of untreated buffer zones to protect the environment from herbicide contamination. Specific recommended buffer zones mentioned in the guidelines comprise – (a) water bodies (rivers and canals): A buffer of at least 100 feet is recommended near rivers, aligning with the provisions of the Rivers and Canals Act, for the control of activities near such water bodies; and (b) agricultural zones: A minimum 10-meter untreated buffer is recommended around agricultural zones.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the endorsement, by the Global Environment Facility, of a grant to the tune of USD4,532,164 in respect of the project entitled "Realising Energy Savings and Climate Benefits of Implementing Mandatory Energy Auditing in the Republic of Mauritius." The project primarily aims to address the fundamental challenge of Mauritius' heavy dependence on imported fossil fuels. Following the coming into operation of the Energy Efficiency Act in 2011 and subsequent introduction of the Energy Efficiency (Energy Consumer and Energy Audit) Regulations 2017, energy audits have been mandated for large public and private sector entities. However, the effective implementation of these energy audits has faced persistent barriers. The project has been designed to address the barriers in an integrated way, creating a sustainable ecosystem that will enable and incentivise the full implementation of the national energy audit scheme. The project directly supports the national target of increasing overall energy efficiency by 10% by 2035 relative to a 2019 baseline. The successful implementation of the project is expected to yield approximately one million tonnes of direct avoided CO2 emissions over the lifetime of the measures implemented.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of export permits for second-hand motor vehicles to the African region and to amendments being brought to the Consumer Protection (Export Control) Regulations 2000. The Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection will introduce an export permit for the exportation of the used vehicles and relevant conditions will be applicable.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the designation of the Old Prison Buildings, located in Maillard Street, Port Louis, as a National Heritage. The Old Prison Buildings constitute one of the largest and most significant colonial prison complexes constructed in Mauritius. The prison remained operational for around 114 years and was officially closed in 1953 by the British Government. The buildings were constructed through the labour of apprentices, prisoners and some ex- indentured labourers, thereby bearing testimony to significant phases of the colonial, penal and labour history of Mauritius. Although part of the prisons complex was destroyed in the late 1990s, substantial elements remain intact, including the detention centre, the female wing, prison cells and the gallows used for executions. The structures which remain represent rare and tangible evidence of the colonial penal system at that time. In view of its scale, age, rarity and historical associations, the site satisfies the criteria of cultural and historical significance under the National Heritage Fund Act. Its designation as National Heritage would ensure statutory protection and facilitate appropriate conservation of the site.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the third meeting of the Inter-Ministerial Committee on Anti-Money Laundering, Combatting the Financing of Terrorism and Countering Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF), co-chaired by the Attorney-General and the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning. Since the last meeting, a number of activities have been completed, namely the Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment of Non-Profit Organisations and the National Strategy for Combatting Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism. Some of the actions required, such as the updates relating to the National Risk Assessment on Money Laundering/Terrorism Financing, the Proliferation Financing National Risk Assessment and the Risk Assessment of Legal Arrangements are progressing well and are expected to be finalised in the coming months. Significant progress has also been made in the setting up of a Centralised Information Management System and the operationalisation of the Real Estate Agency Authority. A comprehensive action plan has been developed by the National Committee on AML/CFT/CPF to address the shortcomings identified in the Mid-Term Independent Assessment ahead of the Mutual Evaluation scheduled in 2027.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Mahatma Gandhi Institute and the Rabindranath Tagore Institute jointly signing a Memorandum of Understanding with Sofia University, St Kliment Ohridski, Bulgaria. The Memorandum of Understanding provides for the following avenues of cooperation - (a) student and staff exchanges; (b) academic programmes; (c) joint research activities; (d) exchange of academic materials and information; (e) scholarly activities such as seminars, conferences, and symposia; and (f) short attachments for academic, management, and administrative staff.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities of the University of Mauritius signing a Student Exchange Agreement with the Faculty of Arts and Science of the Université de Montréal. The Agreement is a focused, operational instrument establishing a structured Student Exchange Programme between the two Faculties. Eligible students enrolled in full-time at their Home Institution may undertake a full-time, non-degree study stay at the Host Institution for one to two study sessions, without paying tuition fees at the Host Institution. The Agreement provides for one exchange spot per study session on each side, while maintaining a balance of student flow.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of a workshop on the Chagos Archipelago Marine Protected Area (CAMPA) from 31 March to 02 April 2026 organised by the Department for Continental Shelf, Maritime Zones Administration and Exploration of the Prime Minister's Office in collaboration with the Zoological Society of London. The workshop aims at bringing together national stakeholders, international practitioners and technical specialists to exchange knowledge and practical experience to inform the planning, implementation and long-term management of CAMPA. The workshop will be of significant importance for the establishment and future management of CAMPA. It will support the development of shared technical understanding, build national capacity, and promote the adoption of best international practices in marine protected area governance. Particular emphasis will be placed on strengthening capacity- building, enhancing institutional coordination, and leveraging modern technologies for surveillance, enforcement, research and monitoring, thereby laying a sound foundation for the effective and sustainable management of CAMPA in the future.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the present situation regarding infectious diseases in Mauritius and the public health and sanitary measures being taken to control the spread of the diseases. The Minister of Health and Wellness has chaired a multi-sectoral meeting on 23 February 2026 with relevant stakeholders to take stock of the actions being taken and to emphasise on further action required to reinforce sanitary measures. The Ministry of Health and Wellness has released sterile Aedes albopictus males in Triolet, Roches Brunes and Beau Bassin with a view to controlling the mosquito population. Rodent control activities are regularly carried out at all public health institutions, medical stores, Sea Port and selected premises at the Airport. Mist blowing and larviciding activities are being strengthened to eliminate mosquito larvae at identified breeding sites. House to house inspections and indoor residual survey are ongoing. Cleaning campaigns of bare lands are being conducted in collaboration with the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change and the Ministry of Local Government.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Drains Infrastructure Construction Ltd proceeding with the implementation of the project "Construction of drain at Morcellement Domah under Phase 1 - Construction of cut-off drain at the upstream periphery over a stretch of 250 metres".

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Drains Infrastructure Construction Ltd proceeding with the construction of culvert across Higginson Road as part of the implementation of the project "Flood Mitigation Measures at L'Unité".

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Energy and Public Utilities in collaboration with the Agence Française de Développement, in the context of World Water Day 2026 which is observed on 22 March every year. The theme for this year is "Water and Gender - Where Water Flows, Equality Grows". A calendar of activities, geared toward raising awareness of stakeholders on the importance of water as a sine qua non resource to sustain life, has been prepared comprising - (a) an awareness session at the Rehabilitation Centre of the Petit Verger Prison; (b) a tree planting initiative at La Nicolière Water Treatment Plant aiming to link water conservation to environmental sustainability by promoting greenery around key water infrastructure; (c) an educational tour for school students at La Marie and Bagatelle Water Treatment Plants, aiming at building early awareness of water treatment, conservation and environmental responsibility; (d) Public Open Days at La Marie and Bagatelle Water Treatment Plants to promote transparency and public trust by allowing citizens to observe water treatment processes, quality control measures, and the expertise behind safe water supply; and (e) a seminar on the theme of World Water Day highlighting the vital role in water management, public health, household decisions and community resilience.
ministries: Ministry Of Energy and Public Utilities

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent visit of the Minister of Education and Human Resource to Rodrigues regarding progress achieved under the Foundation Programme in Literacy, Numeracy and Skills. The Minister participated in a roundtable discussion organised by the Deputy Chief Commissioner of the Rodrigues Regional Assembly, along with the Departmental Head (Education), Rectors, Deputy Rectors and Senior Educators of the Secondary Schools. The deliberations focused on the philosophy of the Foundation Programme, the importance of the Foundation Programme School Monitoring Committee and recruitment of one Coordinator to monitor the teaching and learning of the foundation programme in Rodrigues.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 06 March 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Ms Bhawna Atmaram as part-time Chairperson on the Committee of the President's Fund for Creative Writing.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Police (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Police Act so as to empower the Commissioner of Police to retain the services of foreign specialised investigative officers to carry out, under the direction and control of the Commissioner of Police, complex or serious criminal inquiries. The specialised investigative officers will have no powers of arrest.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Courts (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Courts Act with a view to simplifying and streamlining the procedure for an application for judicial review. Accordingly, an application for leave to apply for judicial review shall be made, inter partes, to the Judge in Chambers and the Judge shall determine the application for leave on the papers alone, but the Judge may, where he deems necessary, request for written submissions on any point and, in exceptional circumstances, require an oral hearing in Chambers. A time limit is also being prescribed on the Judge in Chambers to determine the application for leave to apply for judicial review and it is further being provided that where leave is granted, the application for leave shall stand as the application for judicial review. Consequently, several other enactments are being amended so as to align them with the new judicial review provisions of the Courts Act.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the zoning framework for the Chagos Archipelago Marine Protected Area (CAMPA) and the roadmap for the stewardship and resettlement framework under CAMPA. CAMPA will cover approximately 645,835 km2 and will be divided into four zones, each aligned with the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) categories to ensure effective conservation and management. The IUCN provides an internationally accepted framework for classifying protected areas according to their management objectives, which is widely adopted under the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) to guide national and regional conservation efforts. A roadmap has been elaborated for the stewardship and resettlement framework under CAMPA. The roadmap outlines a five-year plan with the following objectives – (a) establishing an effective institutional and legal framework for CAMPA as a statutory body under the Prime Minister's Office, working in close coordination with the Department for Continental Shelf, Maritime Zones Administration and Exploration; (b) implementing a zoning and management framework consistent with the IUCN classification system; (c) facilitating phased resettlement in the designated Traditional Zone for Resettlement on Peros Banhos and Salomon Islands; (d) developing scientific research plans, management plans, operational protocols, and budgetary framework; (e) promoting sustainable infrastructure and livelihoods through renewable energy, circular waste systems, and eco-friendly housing; and (f) strengthening scientific monitoring, conservation enforcement, and community participation to safeguard biodiversity.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the National Housing Development Company Ltd introducing a new scheme, based on a "Rent-to-Own" mechanism, to cater for beneficiaries who are not able to qualify for existing financing schemes.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a new body which will be responsible for the management and preservation of islets around the island and to drafting instructions being conveyed to the Attorney-General's Office for the preparation of the required legislation for the setting up of the proposed new body. The new entity will, inter alia, be responsible for - (a) the development and implementation of Master Plans for the sustainable management of the islets; (b) the day-to-day management of the islets to ensure that they are properly maintained; (c) the provision of the necessary facilities and infrastructure as well as regular maintenance thereof; (d) overseeing the zoning, infrastructural development and restoration/conservation projects within islet boundaries; (e) ensuring the collaboration with relevant stakeholders in the management and regulation of activities on the islets; (f) the formulation of guidelines for access, usage and preservation/ conservation of the islets while ensuring measures to be taken on ecological threats such as invasive species, pollution or climate-related risks and for the preservation of the existing biodiversity of the islets; and (g) facilitating the implementation of ecological studies and collection of data.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Immigration (Permits) (Prescribed Fees, Guarantees and Application Form) (Amendment) Regulations 2025. The Immigration Act currently provides for the holder of a work and residence permit to make a deposit with, or provide a bank guarantee to the Immigration Officer to indemnify Government for any expense or charge likely to be incurred for his maintenance, support or removal. The Immigration Act has been amended so that, in lieu of a Bank Guarantee, the employer shall pay an annual non-refundable prescribed fee per non-citizen worker. Accordingly, the Immigration (Permits) (Prescribed Fees, Guarantees and Application Form) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 will provide for - (a) the replacement of the existing bank guarantee with a non-refundable annual fee of Rs500 payable by the employer for each non-citizen worker; and (b) applicants of an occupation permit, a residence permit as retired non-citizen or a short-term occupation permit will also have to pay an application fee of USD50.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Sale of Essential Goods During Emergency Period) Regulations 2025 and the Consumer Protection (Price Label) Regulations 2025. The Consumer Protection (Sale of Essential Goods During Emergency Period) Regulations 2025 will provide a dedicated legal framework for regulating the sale and pricing of essential goods during officially declared emergencies. During such emergency periods, the regulations will prohibit retailers from hoarding or refusing to sell essential goods, increasing prices without justification, or applying discriminatory pricing. Retailers will also need to refrain from contravening provisions of the Fair Trading Act and Consumer Protection legislation. The Consumer Protection (Price Label) Regulations 2025 aim to modernise price display requirements and strengthen consumers' ability to make informed comparisons. The regulations will prescribe clear formatting and visibility standards for price boards in retail outlets.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee on Anti-Money Laundering, Combatting the Financing of Terrorism, and Combatting Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF) co-chaired by the Attorney-General and the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning. The Committee will oversee and coordinate the preparatory work for the forthcoming Mutual Evaluation of Mauritius' AML/CFT/CPF framework, which will be conducted by the Eastern and Southern Africa Anti-Money Laundering Group (ESAAMLG) in 2027. The Committee will provide the necessary governance mechanism to ensure that all relevant Ministries, regulatory bodies, and law enforcement agencies work in concert towards a unified national strategy and evidence-based results. The Committee will provide high-level guidance on priority reforms, ensure inter-agency coordination with the 16 enforcement agencies and oversee the implementation of the national AML/CFT/CPF Agenda. The Inter-Ministerial Committee will provide strategic direction to the National Committee on AML/CFT/CPF matters to monitor implementation progress on the National AML/CFT/CPF Strategy and for co-ordination among its Immediate Outcome sub-committees and resolve policy or operational bottlenecks.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of progress made regarding the implementation of the integrated e-Social Security (eSS) system to cater for the payment of basic and contributory pensions as well as other benefits. In order to ensure the smooth implementation of the eSS system, some preparatory projects have been executed in parallel to guaranteeing organisational and technical readiness while ensuring a seamless and effective transition to the new system. The Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity has already initiated actions for a comprehensive data quality assessment, bulk scanning of beneficiary files and the alignment of Standard Operating Procedures and Business Rules to the new system in order to promote consistency and transparency. The implementation of the eSS is expected to bring a radical change in the Mauritius Social Security landscape as this will have an impact on processes, people and technology.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the school calendar for Primary and Secondary schools for the academic year 2026 as follows – First Term Second Term Third Term Monday 12 January 2026 to Friday 03 April 2026 Monday 12 January 2026 Admission: Grade 1 in Primary schools, Grade 7 in Regional Schools and Grade 10 in Academies Tuesday 13 January 2026 Resumption of all classes Monday 20 April 2026 to Friday 17 July 2026 Monday 17 August 2026 to Friday 30 October 2026

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius signing two Agreements developed by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) with a view to aligning with the highest standards regarding transparency and exchange of information, namely – (a) the Multilateral Competent Authority Agreement on Automatic Exchange of Information pursuant to the Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework; and (b) an addendum to the existing Multilateral Competent Authority Agreement on Automatic Exchange of Financial Account Information. The Agreements will provide a mechanism for enhanced exchange of information that will also capture transactions involving crypto-assets. This will enable tax authorities across the world to co-operate in the fight against tax evasion.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Mauritius Institute of Professional Accountants (MIPA) and the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI). The collaboration between the two institutions is aimed at fostering mutual cooperation for the benefit of their members while strengthening the global reputation of both institutions. The partnership seeks to advance knowledge and professional growth through joint efforts in taxation, quality support, research, and capacity building for Small and Medium Practitioners, as well as through exchange programmes and Continuous Professional Development (CPD). Both institutions will organise and participate in joint seminars, workshops, and conferences, with CPD recognition governed by their respective rules. They will also facilitate student and member exchanges to enhance global professional exposure. Additionally, ICAI will assist MIPA with updates, IT capacity building, and quality assurance frameworks, while MIPA will support ICAI by providing updates and promoting the profession within Mauritius. Together, both parties will extend mutual support in engaging with regional and international accountancy bodies, thereby strengthening the profession at national, regional, and global levels.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the University of Mauritius signing a Memorandum of Understanding with CGC University, Mohali, India, which aims to establish opportunities for collaboration in the following areas – (a) academic staff collaboration in research, teaching and learning in areas of common interest; (b) general academic collaboration including the exchange of educational resource materials and publications that are of mutual interest; (c) assessment of units for credit for prior learning for academic programmes offered at each institution; (d) development and implementation of student mobility programmes; (e) development and implementation of academic staff exchange programmes; (f) identification of special short-term academic programmes and projects of mutual benefit to both institutions; and (g) any other collaborative efforts the parties consider to be appropriate from time to time.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Higher Education Commission signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), with Le Haut Conseil de l'Evaluation de la Recherche et de l'Enseignement Supérieur (Hcéres). The purpose of the MoU is to set out the general terms for cooperation in the field of quality assurance in higher education and research, in order to gain a better understanding of the higher education and research systems of both countries, to contribute to their improvement and to promote academic cooperation, particularly with French- speaking higher education institutions. The MoU outlines the following key areas of cooperation – (a) exchange of information and documents pertaining to French institutions evaluated by Hcéres and wishing to establish themselves in Mauritius; (b) exchange best practices on topics related to evaluation and quality of higher education and research, through webinars or joint presentations at international conferences; (c) capacity building of Higher Education Commission staff; (d) exchange programmes between institutions of both countries; and (e) exchange of information related to quality assurance in Mauritius, France and other regions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Tertiary Education, Science and Research signing a Déclaration d'Intentions Communes, with the Government of France, to foster collaboration in higher education and research between the two countries to, inter alia, – (a) promote the mobility of students, teachers and researchers within higher education institutions and scientific research centers in both countries, as well as at seminars and scientific events; (b) promote degree courses in areas of common interest for the social and economic development of both countries; (c) promote capacity building of academic staff and researchers from Mauritian and French higher education institutions and scientific research centers; (d) foster collaboration between French and Mauritian agencies responsible for the evaluation and accreditation of higher education courses and institutions, with a view to guaranteeing the quality of higher education and strengthening mutual collaboration; (e) foster collaboration between the agencies responsible for the recognition of courses and qualifications; (f) promote the establishment of French higher education institutions in Mauritius; (g) promote collaboration for joint research and development projects; and (h) enhance the quality of higher education and research systems, thereby contributing to the strengthening of the academic and scientific potential of both countries.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the football ground at Royal Road, Beau Vallon, being named the 'Jacques Clency Bauluck Football Ground'.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Health and Wellness will introduce the Sexually Transmitted Infections Management Guidelines for guidance of healthcare professionals in the management of sexually transmitted infections. The Sexually Transmitted Infections Management Guidelines have been developed by a multidisciplinary team of healthcare professionals in line with the framework and recommendations from the Southern African Development Community, the World Health Organization, and Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and customised to the local epidemiological context. The aim of the guidelines is to improve the quality, consistency and effectiveness of care, thereby enhancing healthcare outcomes for patients. The overall objectives are, inter alia, to - (a) provide up-to-date, evidence-informed clinical and practical recommendations for the management of individuals with or without symptoms of sexually transmitted infections; and (b) support clinicians in making timely, evidence-based decisions that optimise patient care and resource utilisation. The Sexually Transmitted Infections Management Guidelines will be disseminated to all healthcare professionals involved in the management of sexually transmitted infections.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of the 8th High-Level Meeting of the Djibouti Code of Conduct and its Jeddah Amendment from 12 to 14 November 2025 in Mauritius. The 8th High-Level Meeting aims at enhancing cooperation and coordination in maritime security activities. The meeting will build on the outcomes of the 7th High-Level Meeting held in Tanzania in November 2024 to review progress and chart the next phase of regional cooperation in the maritime domain and in securing the Western Indian Ocean and Gulf of Aden.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities which will be organised by the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare in collaboration with the National Children's Council in the context of the Universal Children's Day 2025 celebrated on 20 November each year to promote children's rights, education and well-being. The theme for this year is 'zanfan viv zot drwa dan lazwa ek linite'. The Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare will organise a recreational Fun Day at the Anse La Raie Outdoor Recreation Centre on 20 November 2025 as well as the following activities – (a) sensitisation on environment by the Mauritius Wildlife Foundation; (b) organisation of nautical and beach fun games; (c) setting of Creative Corners, that is, Art therapy, Bracelet Making, Face painting and Mehendi; and (d) organisation of sports activities and challenges such as Zumba and Tug of war amongst others.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised by the Ministry of Health and Wellness to mark World Diabetes Day, which is commemorated worldwide on 14 November. The theme for 2025, "Diabetes Across Life Stages", underscores that diabetes affects individuals throughout their lives and highlights the importance of integrated care, supportive environments, and policies that promote health and dignity at every stage of life. The official launching ceremony of World Diabetes Day 2025 will be held on 14 November 2025 in Ebene. The programme will include the following activities, namely - (a) screening for Non-Communicable Diseases for public officers, including HbA1c testing; and (b) an exhibition on Non-Communicable Diseases and other health-related topics.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning in the promotional mission to London. The Minister delivered a keynote speech during the Africa Investment Summit where she emphasised Mauritius' role as a credible and forward-looking gateway to Africa. The Minister also delivered an address during the Africa Venture Capital Association Sustainability Summit on three key themes, namely, Africa as a land of opportunity; the importance of bridging the investment gap; and how Mauritius, as an African International Financial Centre, offers a neutral, trusted, and enabling platform to channel sustainable finance into the continent. The Minister also participated in a fireside chat for the Financial Times Africa Summit 2025 where discussions revolved around Mauritius being a model for sustainable growth and innovation in the Continent. In the margin of the Summits, the Minister held a number of bilateral meetings and events with, inter alia, the London Stock Exchange Group; the Vice President and Minister of Finance of Botswana; the UK Deputy Trade Commissioner for Africa; the Secretary General of the Commonwealth; the Financial Conduct Authority; and the Standard Chartered Bank UK.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Education and Human Resource in the G20 Education Ministers' Meeting held in South Africa on the theme of "Solidarity, Equality, Sustainability". The Ministerial Meeting was preceded by Education Working Group meetings where discussions focused on the following priority areas – (a) Priority 1 - Quality foundational learning: The role of Early Childhood Care and Education to improve the quality of foundational learning; (b) Priority 2 - Mutual Recognition of Qualifications in a Global Context; and (c) Priority 3 - Education Professional Development for a Changing World. During his interventions, the Minister spoke about the endeavours carried out by the Government of Mauritius in each of the three priority areas and stressed on how the meeting was a unique opportunity to reaffirm the collective commitment to strengthening foundational learning and lifelong skills development, early childhood education and high-quality teacher education which are the cornerstones for sustainable economic growth, social cohesion and equitable opportunities. On the sidelines of the G20 Education Ministers' Meeting, the Minister had the opportunity to network with a number of high dignitaries to explore future opportunities for collaboration.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 07 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Board of the Conservatoire National de Musique François Mitterrand Trust Fund has renewed the contract of Mrs Claudie Marie Patricia Ricaud as Director.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Prime Minister will, at the invitation of the Prime Minister of the Republic of India, attend the India AI Impact Summit 2026 in New Delhi from 17 to 22 February 2026. During his visit, the Prime Minister will have a working session with Prime Minister Modi.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the debates on the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill are expected to resume in the House of Lords of the United Kingdom on Monday 23 February 2026. Government is following up closely on the issue.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the widespread damage caused by cyclone Gezani following its passage in Madagascar in the evening of 10 February 2026. Both the Prime Minister and the Deputy Prime Minister have addressed letters of sympathy to the President and the Prime Minister of the Refoundation of the Republic of Madagascar respectively to express sadness and solidarity with the Malagasy population. Cabinet has further agreed that a donation of USD100,000 will be made to the Republic of Madagascar.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Land Transport conveying drafting instructions to the Attorney-General's Office for amendments to be brought to relevant legislation to provide for - (a) impounding of vehicles in cases of driving under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol; and (b) the Commissioner of Police to be empowered to make an ex-parte application to a Judge in Chambers for immediate suspension of driving licence in cases of driving under the influence of drugs and/or alcohol.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of a Special Economic Zone over 83 Arpents of land belonging to Landscope (Mauritius) Ltd at Côte d'Or. A Steering Committee will be set up to oversee the modalities for the implementation of the Special Economic Zone project. The setting up of the Special Economic Zone is of strategic importance to our economic development, particularly in attracting greater and more productive foreign direct investment, generating employment opportunities, boosting exports, and enhancing economic growth. It will contribute towards our strategy for economic renewal and transition to a New Economic Model. Given the importance of attracting high-value investment from different countries in strategic sectors in Mauritius, the Special Economic Zone will be designed to attract investment and will be geared towards promoting the economy of the future, including the digital economy and Artificial Intelligence.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the adoption of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Strategy, AI for Mauritius, which will serve as the national guiding policy framework for the development and implementation of AI in Mauritius. The National AI Strategy sets out how Mauritius will approach and use AI development over the coming years to boost economic growth and improve efficiency across various sectors of the economy and society. The AI Strategy is supported by a FAIR (Fair, Accountable, Inclusive and Responsible) guideline document, which is a practical document providing for responsible adoption, development and use of AI across sectors by taking into consideration the Mauritian realities while aligning with international practices. The Guidelines will evolve into AI Regulations with the maturity of the ecosystem.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Ministry of Education and Human Resource is making necessary arrangements to pay the applicable fee for first-time students entering for Kreol Morisien at Advanced Subsidiary Level. In accordance with existing arrangements applicable to all subjects at Higher School Certificate Level, Government pays the examination fees for students attempting the examinations for the first time. The same arrangement is being extended to school students sitting for Kreol Morisien at Advanced Subsidiary Level under the National School Certificate.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Town Planners' Council (Code of Practice) Regulations 2026 had been promulgated to bring into operation a Code of Practice for registered Town Planners in Mauritius. The Code of Practice, inter alia, - (a) sets out the professional standards governing conduct and responsibilities; (b) requires town planners to act with honesty, integrity, competence and independence to avoid conflicts of interest, maintain confidentiality, and refrain from accepting inducements or engaging in misleading practices; (c) requires town planners to maintain and update their professional knowledge, work within their areas of expertise, and provide impartial advice based on reliable evidence; (d) establishes proper professional practice, including clear terms of engagement with clients, professional indemnity insurance, and respect for colleagues; and (e) promotes professional behaviour, equity, inclusion, and non-discrimination, ensuring that town planners uphold the dignity and reputation of the profession at all times.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Mauritius Digital Promotion Agency will launch a communication campaign to increase public awareness on the use of the app "KOREK!". Presently, citizens can access details of their driving licence and number of points accumulated under the Penalty Points System through the app "KOREK!". In order to access the app, a citizen must register through the MauPass authentication platform. To kickstart the communication campaign, awareness sessions targeting 3,000 citizens will be held in 75 Sugar Industry Labour Welfare Fund community centres. The awareness sessions will enable users to understand basics of smart phones utilisation, how to access services online, how to conduct online payments and security precautions against phishing and scamming.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding on cooperation in the field of the youth between the Republic of Mauritius and the State of Qatar. The Memorandum of Understanding aims to promote cooperation and facilitate communication between youth organisations of the two countries and set the framework for a programme of cooperation in the field of youth, based on the principle of reciprocity. Under the Memorandum of Understanding, the two countries will strengthen their mutual cooperation in the following areas – (a) exchange visits of youth delegations, members of the youth centres and specialists in the fields of youth; (b) development of national youth policies; (c) exchange experiences and research related to the youth sector; (d) participation in the meetings, conferences, lectures, seminars and academic forums on topics dealing with youth held in both countries; (e) exploring opportunities to set up, alternately in both countries, youth camps; (f) youth entrepreneurship; (g) training of youth in the area of management of projects; and (h) any other cooperative fields in youth sector agreed on between the participants.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Tourism in the Outbound Travel Market 2026, which was held in Mumbai, India. The Outbound Travel Market is the leading travel/trade exhibition held annually in the Asian region as it brings together tourist destinations, airlines, tour operators, hotel groups, and media organisations from across the world. The Mauritian delegation comprising officials from the Ministry of Tourism, the Mauritius Tourism Promotion Authority, as well as representatives from the private tourism sector, including hoteliers and destination management companies, networked, held meetings, conducted business negotiations and promoted the Mauritian destination while putting emphasis on our unique offering such as golf, wellness, and cultural tourism, among others. During the mission, the Minister also held discussions with leading Indian tour operators, travel agents, corporate travel managers, and destination wedding planners.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Public Service and Administrative Reforms in the World Governments Summit meeting held in Dubai under the theme "Shaping Future Governments". The World Governments Summit is a leading global platform that convenes world leaders, policymakers, and industry pioneers to address critical challenges and shape the future of governance. The main objective of the Summit is to foster international cooperation and identify innovative solutions to meet future challenges of the public service, ultimately inspiring and empowering the next generation of governments to deliver efficient and people-centred service. The Minister participated in discussions during Ministerial Roundtables on "Shaping Future Governments: Achievements and Challenges in the Global South". During the Ministerial Roundtable, the Minister presented the achievements and progress that Mauritius has made in the modernisation of government operations and reinforcement of its institutional standing with international norms.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the Board of the National Anti-Doping Organisation, with Dr (Mrs) Rubina Ashika Bhujoo as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 13 February 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of members of the Employment Relations Tribunal, in accordance with section 85 of the Employment Relations Act 2008.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that HE Emmanuel Macron, President of the French Republic will be on Official Visit to Mauritius on 20 and 21 November 2025. During the visit, the French President will, inter alia, pay a courtesy call on the President of the Republic of Mauritius, have a tête-à-tête with the Prime Minister, visit the vessel 'Plastic Odyssey' as well as the 'Champlain' Vessel, meet the Mauritian and French economic operators in the field of artificial intelligence, make a joint statement to the press together with the Prime Minister and inaugurate the French Embassy at Telfair, Moka. In the context of the forthcoming Official Visit, Cabinet has agreed to the signing of the following documents - (a) Déclaration d'intention pour la coopération sur l'économie bleue between the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries and L'Institut de Recherche pour le Développement. The Déclaration d'intention pour la coopération sur l'économie bleue provides for mutual agreement and cooperation in the fields of, inter alia, campaigns at sea; marine biology and biotechnologies; marine genomics and biotechnology applications for aquaculture, pharmaceuticals and sea-derived products; detection of illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing; research in physical oceanography; and earth observation by satellite imagery; (b) Fonds d'EXpertise Technique et d'Echanges d'expériences (FEXTE) Partnership Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius, the Agence Française de Développement and Electricité de France in the field of renewable energy in Mauritius. The Partnership Agreement seeks to strengthen dialogue and the exchange of expertise between French and Mauritian stakeholders in the field of renewable energy integration in Mauritius. The Tripartite FEXTE Partnership Agreement covers, inter alia, modernisation of the dispatching system; system studies and grid integration; and partnership with Réunion Island; (c) Déclaration d'intention pour accompagner la République de Maurice dans la gestion durable de l'eau between the Republic of Mauritius, the Agence Française de Développement, and the European Union. The Déclaration d'intention sur la gestion durable de l'eau represents a strategic partnership to advance Sustainable Development Goal 6 regarding Clean Water and Sanitation. The document is aligned with the Government Programme 2025-2029 and the Marshall Plan on the Water Sector. The Agence Française de Développement is already supporting Government through a Budget Support Loan of EUR200 million and a grant of EUR2 million. Government has sollicited the Agence Française de Développement and the European Union for the financing of the Water Investment Programme directed toward modernising infrastructure, enhancing water governance, and promoting sustainable resource management; (d) Déclaration d'intention pour accompagner la République de Maurice dans la transition énergétique between the Republic of Mauritius, the Agence Française de Développement and the European Union. The Déclaration d'intention sur la transition énergétique provides a strategic framework for accelerating our energy transition in alignment with Sustainable Development Goal 7. Through this partnership, the Agence Française de Développement and the European Union will mobilise both financial resources and technical expertise to enhance the country's renewable energy production, grid integration, and energy efficiency; and (e) Déclaration d'intention commune relative au développement d'un enseignement bilingue francophone et à l'appui de la politique linguistique dans le système éducatif Mauricien between the Republic of Mauritius and the French Republic. The objective of the Déclaration d'intention commune which will be fully funded by the Ministry of Europe and Foreign Affairs of the French Republic, is to develop cooperation for the preparation of a pilot project for bilingual and plurilingual education in Mauritius. It is intended to strengthen bilingual competencies in French and Kreol Morisien within the national education system.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the revised Law Practitioners (Disciplinary Proceedings) Bill into the National Assembly. Following concerns raised by some members of the Bar, the Attorney-General held further consultations with the Bar Council and the Mauritius Law Society, on the Bill. Most proposals made were retained, more specifically the institution of an Appointment Committee, comprising the – (a) Attorney-General, as Chairperson; (b) Chairperson of the Bar Council; (c) President of the Law Society; and (d) Chairperson of the Chamber of Notaries. The main object of the revised Law Practitioners (Disciplinary Proceedings) Bill is to make provisions for simplified and codified procedures, and provide clarity and transparency, with regard to – (a) the conduct of an independent investigation into an alleged act of professional misconduct by a law practitioner in the exercise of his profession; and (b) the institution and conduct of disciplinary proceedings against a law practitioner before the Supreme Court. The Bill provides for the establishment of the Law Practitioners Complaints Commission, which will be the sole body empowered to investigate into an alleged act of professional misconduct by a law practitioner, and decide upon the institution of disciplinary proceedings, if any, against the law practitioner before the Supreme Court. Accordingly, neither the Attorney-General, nor the Bar Council, the Chamber of Notaries and the Mauritius Law Society Council will henceforth investigate into an alleged act of professional misconduct by a law practitioner in the exercise of his profession, and none of these bodies will have the power to inflict any sanction on a law practitioner. As a result of these new provisions, consequential amendments are being brought to the Courts Act, the Law Officers Act, the Law Practitioners Act, the Mauritius Bar Association Act, the Mauritius Law Society Act and the Notaries Act.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Agence Française de Développement (Privileges and Immunities) (Amendment) Regulations 2025 aiming at extending privileges and immunities to "Expertise France", a subsidiary of the Agence Française de Développement which implements technical assistance projects and mobilises international expertise to support public policies in the areas of governance, environment, maritime security, and pandemic response.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Higher Education (Transitional Provisions Relating to the Accreditation of Institutional Accreditation Process) Regulations 2025 with a view to providing for a transitional provision, up to 31 August 2027, for higher education institutions to develop and seek accreditation for their institutional accreditation process. The Higher Education Act was amended in August 2025 and section 17 of the Act was repealed and replaced by new provisions for Accreditation of Institutional Accreditation Process, in accordance with guidelines issued by Higher Education Commission (HEC). Pending the new guidelines and the accreditation by the HEC, higher education institutions shall continue to seek the approval of the HEC for the accreditation of their programme of study. The Regulations will provide for accreditation of any programme granted by the HEC to a higher education institution under the repealed section 17 of the Act to remain valid until 31 August 2027.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Tourism organising the Festival Internasional Kreol 2025 from 05 to 07 December 2025. The Festival Internasional Kreol showcases the authenticity of the Mauritian Creole culture, promotes community wellness, celebrates cultural diversity and supports local arts. The theme of this year's celebration is Otantisite nu kiltir kreol. The following activities have been scheduled with the collaboration of the Association des Hôteliers et Restaurateurs de l'Ile Maurice and several hotels – (a) an opening ceremony on 05 December 2025 at Mont Choisy public beach; (b) Sega tipik, on 06 December 2025 at Four à Chaux, Trou d'Eau Douce; and (c) Regat, culinary exposition and musical show on 07 December 2025 at Mahebourg Waterfront.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities which will be organised by the Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity to mark the International Day of Persons with Disabilities on 03 December 2025. This year's theme is "Fostering disability inclusive societies for advancing social progress". The focus for this year's celebration will be to raise awareness about the abilities and contributions of persons with disabilities in every aspect of political, social, economic and cultural life, support their rights, dignity and well-being, as well as, promote social inclusion and equal opportunities. The Ministry of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity will celebrate the International Day of Persons with Disabilities in the presence of around 850 guests, comprising persons with disabilities from various non-governmental organisations on 03 December 2025 at the Auditorium of the Mahatma Gandhi Institute.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the participation of a Mauritian delegation in the fourth African Youth Games, scheduled from 10 to 20 December 2025, in Angola. The main objectives of the African Youth Games are talent identification and development, youth empowerment and inclusion, as well as continental integration. Mauritius will participate in 11 sports disciplines, namely Athletics, Badminton, Beach Volleyball, Beach Wrestling, Boxing, Cycling, Judo, Swimming, Table Tennis, Tennis, and Weightlifting.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Youth and Sports undertaking the necessary preparatory works for the establishment of a National Cadet Corps (NCC) under its purview. The proposed NCC in Mauritius would have the following key objectives – (a) to instil discipline, patriotism, social cohesion, and civic responsibility among Mauritian youth; (b) to develop leadership and resilience through structured training, camps, and community engagement; (c) to enhance national disaster preparedness and first aid response capacity through trained youth; (d) to complement existing youth and volunteer programmes (e.g. Scouts, Duke of Edinburgh, Red Cross, National and Regional Youth Councils); and (e) to encourage youth participation in national events and cultural activities.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius endorsing, on a voluntary basis, the Call to Action on Integrated Fire Management and Wildfire Resilience and the Declaration on the Launch of the Tropical Forest Forever Facility during the 30th Session of the Conference of Parties (COP30) to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change being held from 10 to 21 November in Brazil. The Call to Action on Integrated Fire Management and Wildfire Resilience aims to provide a platform for willing countries to converge around shared principles and practical cooperation, in full respect of national sovereignty and in complementarity with existing frameworks, including national climate and adaptation plans, disaster risk reduction policies, biodiversity and forest strategies, and related multilateral processes. The Tropical Forest Forever Facility aims to permanently fund the conservation of tropical forests by creating a sustainable financial mechanism which makes it more profitable to preserve forests than to destroy them.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the forthcoming launching of national consultations on Vision 2050 - a long-term strategic planning exercise, in line with the Government Programme 2025-2029 to lay the foundations for uplifting the country from an upper- middle-income to a high-income country with an inclusive and sustainable agenda. The national consultations will seek to capture a diversity of voices from across society, ensuring inclusiveness, intergenerational equity, and a strong sense of national ownership. A series of sectoral engagements and public outreach sessions will be organised nationwide during the forthcoming months. Vision 2050 will serve as a roadmap that will provide a coherent and forward- looking policy framework guiding socio-economic transformation, fostering long-term resilience, and driving inclusive growth. The 25-year horizon is consistent with UN guidelines that such exercises typically cover one generation to allow for deep structural transformation. Vision 2050 will define the long-term goals and development priorities of Mauritius towards an inclusive, resilient and sustainable future. A 10-Year Development Plan will also be prepared to translate the strategic ambitions into actionable milestones, with key performance indicators to support monitoring and evaluation. Three key pillars of national development have been identified as overarching themes for consultations, as follows - (a) Prosperity - economic transformation and innovation; (b) Sustainability - environmental resilience and infrastructural development; and (c) Inclusiveness - social equity, cohesion and well-being.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Health and Wellness signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Sunlife Trust. The MoU aims to establish a mutually beneficial arrangement for a specific and dedicated project concerning the development and enhancement of the new Paediatric Oncology Ward at the National Cancer Centre with the shared objective of promoting the health, comfort and overall well-being of Paediatric patients. The collaboration will pursue a renewed vision for the Paediatric Ward within the Cancer Unit, with space equipped with the necessary amenities and features to create a nurturing, child-friendly and emotionally uplifting environment. The enhanced environment will contribute significantly to the healing and well-being of young patients, while also providing comfort and reassurance to parents.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the Financial Services Commission and the Securities and Commodities Authority of United Arab Emirates. The main objective of the MoU is to enhance mutual cooperation and exchange of information in the fields of supervision and surveillance on securities markets and financial services providers. The main areas of collaboration provided in the MoU are as follows - (a) general cooperation on financial markets supervision and surveillance, including Anti-Money Laundering and Combatting the Financing of Terrorism, and regulatory compliance to improve transparency and enforcement of regulations; (b) joint investigation on investors' complaints and reports; and (c) organisation of joint trainings on the subject of regulatory supervision and surveillance.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the decision of the United Kingdom (UK) to renew the automatic tuna derogation under the UK–Eastern and Southern Africa (ESA) Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA) for a further period of five years, from 01 January 2026 to 31 December 2030, with an annual quota of 10,000 tons of preserved tuna and 340 tons of tuna loins available for export by the ESA States. The UK-ESA EPA provides, inter alia, for an automatic derogation of 6,300 tons of preserved tuna and 340 tons of tuna loins annually, normally renewable every five years by the UK-ESA EPA Committee. This derogation is shared among the ESA tuna- exporting States, namely Mauritius, Seychelles, and Madagascar. The derogation allows the sourcing of raw tuna from third countries for processing in the ESA States and export to the UK under preferential terms. For the year 2025, further to a request made by the ESA States, the UK exceptionally agreed to increase the quota for preserved tuna from 6,300 tonnes to 10,000 tonnes, while maintaining the tuna loins quota at 340 tonnes. This derogation is due to expire on 31 December 2025. The renewal of the automatic derogation will provide a boost to the Mauritian canned tuna industry as it would enable the sourcing of additional raw tuna from third countries for processing and export to the UK, thereby maintaining a competitive edge on that market.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius extending duty free tariff treatment to goods originating from Tunisia following its accession to the COMESA Free Trade Area.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius hosting the Africa Kaizen Annual Conference 2026 at the request of the Japan International Cooperation Agency, in collaboration with the African Union Development Agency–New Partnership for Africa's Development. The Africa Kaizen Annual Conference is an annual central activity of the African Kaizen Initiative comprising panel discussions based on selected themes and visits to local organisations which have demonstrated high level of Kaizen implementation. The conference will culminate in the Africa Kaizen Awards and the designation of Centres of Excellence. It is a premier continental platform that brings together policymakers, academics, and quality and productivity improvement practitioners to share knowledge and experiences to promote the Kaizen approach in the region, through policy advocacy, mutual learning and co-creating African wisdom to enhance firm capabilities and competitiveness.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the hybrid training session on "Greening your business: Leveraging Environmental Data and the Green Performance Toolkit", will be held on 25 November 2025 in the context of the Project on Climate Competitiveness, in collaboration with SME Mauritius, targeting enterprises within the SME network. The training aims to help SMEs understand the business value of environmental data and develop practical skills to collect, analyse, and use it to improve environmental performance. Participants will also learn to apply International Trade Centre's Green Performance Toolkit to monitor and enhance their environmental footprint. Around 30 SMEs are expected to participate in the hybrid training session.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the Report of the Office of Public Sector Governance (OPSG) on the Organisational Review at the National Transport Corporation, and the Action Plan of the National Transport Corporation for the implementation of the recommendations contained therein. The OPSG Report has highlighted a number of weaknesses and shortcomings, essentially in the following areas and formulated several recommendations to improve the situation at the National Transport Corporation - (a) Organisational Set-up; (b) Traffic Department/Route Operations; (c) Engineering; (d) Financial Management; (e) Human Resource Management; (f) Procurement; (g) Information Technology; (h) Internal Audit; (i) Corporate Governance; and (j) Overall Efficiency and Cost Rationalisation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries in the Global Maritime Forum (GMF) Annual Summit 2025 and the India Maritime Week 2025 which were held in Antwerp, Belgium and in Mumbai, India, respectively. The GMF is an international platform that brings together global maritime leaders, Governments, shipping companies, international organisations and financial institutions to deliberate on the future of global seaborne trade. The Minister was a lead participant, together with the Secretary General of the International Maritime Organisation in a Working Group and also participated in a high-level session themed "Charting a Course Amid Complexity", together with global leaders, industry experts, and policymakers to discuss strategies to make the maritime and shipping sectors stronger, safer, and more sustainable. The India Maritime Week 2025 was organised by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways of India on the theme "Uniting Oceans, One Maritime Vision". The event aimed at facilitating global conversations pertaining to strategic partnerships and investment opportunities in the advancement of innovation, sustainability, and a globally connected maritime future. During his intervention in the inaugural session, the Minister emphasised the close relations between Mauritius and India, notably in the maritime domain and the imperative of addressing the adverse effects of climate change through greener shipping practices and the importance for the international community to come together in reshaping the Blue Economy. The Minister participated in the Leaders Conclave session, the Great Oceans Dialogue and a panel discussion entitled "Oceans of Opportunity: Reimagining the Global Blue Economy". During the India Maritime Week, the Minister had bilateral meetings with several foreign dignitaries on potential areas of bilateral cooperation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Tourism in the 46th Edition of the World Travel Market held in London. The main objective of the participation of Mauritius at the World Travel Market was to, inter alia, strengthen the country's visibility on that market, consolidate trade partnerships, and sustain growth in tourist arrivals. The Mauritian stand comprised major hotel groups together with key Destination Management Companies and Air Mauritius, all of whom showcased the island's tourism offering. The Minister participated in the annual Ministers' Summit, organised in association with the BBC and the UN World Tourism Organization. The Summit, which had as theme "Reimagining Tourism Investment Models: Building Next-Generation Incentives," gathered over 50 Tourism Ministers and senior officials. Deliberations focused on innovation, diversified investment strategies, human capital development, and digital transformation to enhance the resilience and sustainability of the tourism sector. The Minister also had the opportunity to hold discussions with major trade and media partners.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Second World Summit for Social Development held in Doha, Qatar. The Summit saw the adoption of the Doha Political Declaration, which reaffirmed global solidarity and social justice as the basis for sustainable progress. During his intervention in the plenary session of the World Social Summit, the Minister highlighted the vulnerabilities of Mauritius as a Small Island Developing State. He recalled that the Government Programme 2025-2029 underlines the commitment of the Government to build a fair, inclusive and sustainable economy based on merit and opportunity for all and that the 2025-2026 Budget supports this new vision through three main pillars, Economic Renewal, A New Social Order and Fiscal Consolidation. During his stopover in Seychelles, the Minister had a meeting with the Minister of Foreign Affairs and Diaspora of Seychelles and discussed several issues of bilateral interests.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations in the Bengaluru Skill Summit 2025 held in Bengaluru, India. The Summit brought together stakeholders from across the skilling ecosystem including Government leaders, industry experts, global organisations, educators, training providers, and youth, to develop and drive actionable strategies for reskilling, upskilling, digital transformation, and employability in emerging sectors. The Minister participated in a Roundtable session on Global Best Practices in Skilling and delivered a key-note address. The Minister had a series of meetings with high dignitaries of the Karnataka Government including the Chief Minister; the Deputy Chief Minister; the Minister for Skill Development, Entrepreneurship & Livelihood, and Medical Education; the Minister for Rural Development & Panchayat Raj, and Information Technology & Biotechnology; the Minister for Small Scale Industries and Public Enterprises; and the Chairman of Karnataka Skill Development Authority.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the 5th Türkiye-Africa Business and Economic Forum (TABEF 2025), held in Istanbul. The Junior Minister made a statement at the opening session of the TABEF and called on combining the prospects between Africa and Türkiye to build on the emerging regional value chains that make a significant contribution to the industrialisation and socio-economic development of the African Continent. He delivered a keynote speech during a panel on Pharmaceuticals and Medical Supplies. The Junior Minister emphasised that Government-Industry cooperation is vital for securing domestic pharmaceutical supplies and fostering innovation. He highlighted the potential of Mauritius in the medical, biotechnology, and pharmaceutical sectors, noting the advantages provided by the Economic Development Board's Investment Certificate and Premium Investor Certificate for facilitating re-export opportunities to African markets. The Junior Minister also held bilateral meetings with the Minister of Economy, Industry and Economic Integration of the Union of the Comoros, and the Minister of Trade and Entrepreneurship of Botswana, on the sidelines of the Forum.
