filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent virtual participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the continuation session of the 17th Meeting of the Council of Ministers of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). The Meeting addressed several issues including market access/tariff liberalisation, rules of origin, and intellectual property rights. It also took note of the financial situation of the AfCFTA Secretariat. During the meeting, the Minister highlighted the need to make information on the applicable rules of origin accessible to the private operators through an online platform to facilitate preferential trade under the AfCFTA regime and also the urgency for Member States to put in place their national legislation once the rules are adopted by the Assembly of Heads of State and Government of the African Union.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Extraordinary meeting of the SADC Council of Ministers and Extraordinary Summit of SADC Heads of State and Government which were both held virtually. Following Madagascar's renouncement of its position as chair of SADC, the Summit resolved that South Africa chairs SADC in the interim until August 2026. The Extraordinary Council took note of the outcome of the re-computation exercise of the SAMIDRC, namely that the Assessed Contributions for the SAMIDRC have been revised downwards to USD311,904,006. The shared Assessed Contribution of Mauritius now stands at USD7,663,993 instead of USD15,463,954.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 14 November 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mrs Marie Joëlle Coret as part- time Chairperson of the Board of the Mauritius Society of Authors.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that yesterday Mauritius and the United Kingdom signed a historic Agreement concerning the Chagos Archipelago including Diego Garcia. Under this Agreement, the United Kingdom recognises the sovereignty of Mauritius over the entirety of the Chagos Archipelago, including Diego Garcia. The Agreement has been made public and uploaded on the website of the Prime Minister's Office. Cabinet has also taken note that arrangements would be made for a high-level delegation to visit the Chagos Archipelago shortly, and that a committee under the chair of the Prime Minister and comprising, inter alia, representatives of the Chagossian community, would be set up to look into projects to be implemented in the Chagos Archipelago. Cabinet has further taken note that the Mauritian national flag would be raised on the islands of the Chagos Archipelago, to symbolise the sovereignty of the Republic of Mauritius over the Chagos Archipelago.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the elaboration of new Fair Trading (E-Commerce) Regulations 2025, so as to establish a structured and enforceable regulatory framework for digital commerce.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to amendments being brought to the Cybersecurity and Cybercrime Act 2021, through the introduction of a new Bill, so as to address the issue of proliferation of communal incitement, hate speech, trolling, cyber bullying and disinformation on social media platforms, which poses a growing risk to national security, social cohesion and democratic integrity.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of key findings of a study commissioned by the Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning to capture the actual contribution of the Financial Services and Global Business sectors on the Mauritian economy. The Financial Services sector is a key pillar of the Mauritian economy, supporting growth, high-skilled employment, and Government revenue. The study was undertaken by the University of Mauritius with the objective of delivering a holistic view of the sector's total contribution to the Mauritian economy, beyond what was currently reflected in conventional statistics. The key findings of the study included, inter alia, – (a) an increase in direct employment to around 20,000 individuals in 2023; (b) a total output multiplier including direct, indirect and induced of 1.77, raising the Financial Services sector's real contribution to Gross Domestic Product to approximately 24.7%; and (c) significant catalytic effects, including over Corporate Social Responsibility and special levies contributed by the Financial Services sector. The Financial Services sector exhibited a strong multiplier effect, contributing significantly to high quality job creation and tax revenue well beyond its direct outputs. Its catalytic potential reinforced the need for strategic investment in key areas such as skills development, digital innovation, including emerging FinTech solutions and sustainable finance. The findings of the Economic Impact Assessment offered a robust, evidence-based rationale for continued policy focus and targeted support. Such measures were vital to ensure that the sector remains a key engine of inclusive and sustainable economic growth in Mauritius. The Ministry of Financial Services and Economic Planning would continue to engage with stakeholders and coordinate the effective implementation of these strategic reforms to secure the long-term growth and resilience of the Financial Services sector in Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of a maximum mark-up mechanism on essential food products namely, margarine, butter, canned sausages, canned poultry (Luncheon meat), canned tuna, canned prawns and all types of Pasta and fresh vegetables and maximum fixed price on salt, with a view to striking the best balance between maintaining profitability and keeping the prices within a more affordable range, in the best interest of consumers. Amendments would be brought to relevant Regulations.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting-up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee to discuss the modus operandi for the timely payment of gratuity to persons under the Portable Retirement Gratuity Fund (PRGF), which provides for the payment of a gratuity to - (a) a worker, on his retirement; (b) the legal heirs or legal representative of a worker, on the death of a worker; (c) a self-employed who has contributed to the PRGF, on his retirement; or (d) the legal heirs or legal representative of a self-employed who has contributed to the PRGF, on death of the self-employed.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the promulgation of the Road Traffic (Crop Season) Regulations 2025 in view of the sugar cane crop season for the year 2025 starting on 10 June 2025 and ending on 15 January 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a Digital Transformation Blueprint 2025-2029, which aims at digitalising Government-to-Business, Government-to-Citizens and Government-to-Government services would be launched by the Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation. The Blueprint would set out a strategic vision for a smart, inclusive and sustainable digital future. Centred around four core pillars and supported by five critical enablers, it would outline a comprehensive roadmap to modernise public services, empower citizens, stimulate economic innovation and promote environmental sustainability. The Blueprint aims to transform public service delivery through the establishment of unified data centres, the implementation of a Mobile ID system and the adoption of digital documents. These initiatives will enable seamless Government-to-Citizen, Government-to- Government, and Government-to-Business interactions, all accessible via a unified digital portal or super application.
ministries: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Ministry of Health and Wellness signing a Clinical Training Framework Agreement with Charles Telfair Company Ltd T/A Curtin Mauritius. The aim of the Clinical Training Framework Agreement would be to, inter alia, – (a) establish a structured collaboration to facilitate the clinical training of undergraduate nursing students from Curtin Mauritius at Victoria Hospital, specialised hospitals, and Primary Health Care Centres; (b) ensure that the clinical training were carried out under appropriate supervision and in accordance with guidelines of the Ministry of Health and Wellness and the Higher Education Commission; and (c) define the roles, responsibilities, and obligations of both parties and of the students in the delivery of the training programme.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting-up of a Steering Committee to coordinate the implementation of the project for the setting up and operation of two regional Integrated Waste Processing Facilities on a Public-Private-Partnership basis. In order to allow for a high-quality waste stream to the Integrated Waste Processing Facilities, waste segregation at source would be fundamental through the provision of three bins to each household namely, one for organic wastes, one for recyclable materials and the third one for residual wastes to be sent to the landfill. The Integrated Waste Processing Facilities were expected to come into operation in March 2027 and well before that time, Government would operationalise the source segregation strategy by providing bins to householders, deploying adequate logistics for separate collection of waste at the level of Local Authorities, making appropriate Source Segregation Regulations and undertaking an aggressive information/sensitisation campaign.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised to commemorate World Environment Day 2025, which is celebrated annually on 05 June. The theme retained for this year is "Ending Plastic Pollution". The Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change would reaffirm the commitment of Government to providing a clean and healthy environment for the well-being of all inhabitants, flora, fauna and the economy of Mauritius through the enhancement of environmental enforcement. A workshop on Enhancing Environmental Enforcement would be held on 03 June 2025 at the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Public Service and Innovation to bring all enforcing agencies and authorities involved in enforcement of environmental issues for improved coordination. The workshop would aim at strengthening inter-agency cooperation, reducing duplication of efforts and fostering a more efficient approach to environmental protection. The following activities would also be carried out - (a) launching of a series of 10 factsheets as well as 10 video cartoons on different environmental themes, which would be aired on TV; (b) launching of a Campaign on illegal dumping and littering; (c) holding of meetings with Chairpersons of Green Cells set up at the level of Ministries, Departments and Parastatal bodies for the implementation of the Action Plan for the Greening of the Public Sector project; (d) launching of the Observatoire de L'Environnement in collaboration with the Agence Française de Développement; and (e) launching of a pilot paper recycling project in 10 primary and 10 secondary schools.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the holding of a validation workshop by the Ministry of Industry, SME and Cooperatives on 11 June 2025 to finalise a draft Report on Technical Assistance to further enhance the development of the Leather Value Chain in Mauritius, which had been prepared by the Africa Leather and Leather Products Institute. Some 100 participants from Government institutions, Ministry of Industrial Development, SMEs and Cooperatives and operators in the leather sector of Mauritius ranging from upholstery and shoe-makers to manufacturers of leather bags would attend the validation workshop. The panellists from the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA) would hold discussions on the key findings and recommendations of the draft Report. The workshop would comprise a hands on training by the Africa Leather and Leather Products Institute and assist in the development of an action plan based on the validated recommendations.
ministries: Ministry of Industrial Development, SMEs and Cooperatives

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Financial Services and Economic Planning in the 12th Edition of the Africa CEO Forum Annual Summit held in Abidjan, Côte D'Ivoire. The event was organised by the "Jeune Afrique Media Group" and co-hosted by the International Finance Corporation, the investment arm of the World Bank. The Forum focused on driving economic growth and sustainable development in Africa by fostering dialogue between the public and private sectors. It featured discussions on leadership, digital transformation, regional economic integration, and innovative financing strategies. The theme of the Forum was "Can a New Deal Between State and Private Sector Deliver the Continent a Winning Hand?" The Minister participated as a speaker on a panel on "Cost of Capital Crisis: How Can African Business Turn Geopolitical Shifts to Its Advantage". In the margin of the Conference, the Minister had several meetings with, inter alia, the Senior Bureau Official-US Dept of State, the UK Trade Commissioner for Africa, the Head of West Africa for UK Export Finance, the CEO of KFW DEG (German DFI), the President and CEO of Africa Finance Corporation, the Chairperson of the Board of Directors of Atlantic Finance Group and the Advisor to the Minister of Investment in United Arab Emirates.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the of the outcome of the recent participation of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the Resilience in Action Summit for Small Island Developing States (SIDS) organised by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC)-Fund in Vienna. The Minister participated in a Ministerial Roundtable which fostered an actionable dialogue on financial barriers facing Small Island Developing States, including debt sustainability, currency fluctuations, and access to affordable finance. He also attended the launch of the Island Resilience Facility by the OPEC-Fund. The Island Resilience Facility aims at providing financial, technical and knowledge-based assistance to SIDS. In the margin of the event, the Minister had meetings with the President of the OPEC- Fund, the Minister for European and International Affairs of Austria and the Executive Director of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 23 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the – (a) Board of the Taxi Operators Welfare Fund, with Mr Ham Sharma Kowlessur as part-time Chairperson; (b) Board of the Institute of Technical Education and Technology, with Mr Deo Prakash Ramkalawon as part-time Chairperson; and (c) Board of the State Informatics Ltd with Mr Joyadeep Beeharry, as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has considered - (a) the draft Constitution (Amendment) Bill which will amend the Constitution so as to provide that a person can stand as a candidate at any general election without community-based classification. In line with section 41(3) of the Constitution, the comments of the Electoral Supervisory Commission and the Electoral Commissioner have been sought and are awaited; and (b) the draft Constitutional Review Commission Bill which will empower the President of the Republic of Mauritius to appoint a Constitutional Review Commission which will be responsible to examine and make recommendations on a number of constitutional reforms, including the protection of fundamental rights and the consolidation of democracy and institutions. Both draft Bills will be given further consideration at the next Cabinet Meeting, after having received the comments of the Electoral Supervisory Commission and the Electoral Commissioner, prior to introduction into the National Assembly.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the issue of Electoral Reforms is being addressed separately. A report of the public consultations held earlier this year will be circulated at the next Cabinet Meeting.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Prime Minister in the Swearing-in Ceremony of His Excellency Denis Sassou N'Guesso, President of the Republic of Congo. The Prime Minister attended the inaugural ceremony at the invitation of the President, in view of the very close ties between Mauritius and the Republic of Congo and the excellent personal relations, and also in line with the ambition to elevate our relationship within Africa. Heads of State and Government, including the President of Burundi, who is also the Chairperson of the African Union, the Presidents of Angola, Chad, Comoros, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Gabon, Rwanda and Togo, as well as several Prime Ministers and Vice Presidents, attended the event. Cabinet noted that the support Mauritius has always benefitted from Africa, especially from President Denis Sassou N'Guesso, in our struggle to exercise full sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago cannot be overstated. Furthermore, President N'Guesso has always promoted peace and security across Africa and played a significant role to resolve conflicts in Libya and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The Prime Minister had the opportunity to meet President N'Guesso just after his Swearing-in Ceremony. During discussions, they highlighted the excellent bilateral relations between the two countries and agreed to an enhanced economic and trade partnership. Discussions were held on issues of mutual interest, including - (a) the possibility of Mauritius getting a quota of petroleum products at a preferential rate, from Pointe Noire Petroleum Hub in Congo, one of the main hubs of Central Africa; (b) the participation of the Mauritius private sector in the development of the agricultural hub of Oyo, in the North of the Republic of Congo, for food production, processing and exports; and (c) triangular cooperation between India-Mauritius-Congo, namely in the financial services sector, on the channelling of investment through the Mauritius International Finance Centre. The Prime Minister also met the President of Gabon, HE Brice Oligui Nguema and they agreed to reinforce bilateral relations. The President of Gabon has expressed the wish of visiting Mauritius in the course of this year.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that on 22 April 2026, the Prime Minister had a telephone conversation with Mr Jonathan Powell, United Kingdom (UK) National Security Adviser on the Chagos issue. The latter has reaffirmed the commitment of the UK Government to the Agreement between Mauritius and the UK. The Prime Minister made it clear that Mauritius will review the situation and take a decision on the way forward after the US-Africa Business Summit scheduled from 26 to 29 July 2026 in Mauritius. Cabinet has further taken note that Mr Robbie Bulloch, Director, Overseas Territories and Polar Directorate, UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) and two other officials of the FCDO travelled to Mauritius this week, at the request of the UK Government and held talks on 22 April 2026 with the Attorney-General and officers of the Attorney-General's Office and the Prime Minister's Office with regard to the Chagos Archipelago. The delegation also assured that the UK Government remains committed to the Agreement between Mauritius and the UK concerning the Chagos Archipelago including Diego Garcia although it had decided not to proceed further with the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill during the current session of the UK Parliament. They underscored that the signature of an updated Exchange of Notes between the UK and the United States (US) in respect of the military base in Diego Garcia was a sine qua non for the Bill to be taken forward in the UK Parliament. While the updated Exchange of Notes has been approved by the US agencies, the sign off of the US President has not yet been obtained. The Attorney-General was informed that the UK continues to engage regularly with the US on the matter. Mr Bancoult and other Chagossians based in Mauritius, including one from Seychelles, propose to undertake an engagement mission to the UK in order to make known their stand on the Agreement between Mauritius and the UK on the Chagos Archipelago. The UK side welcomed the idea of such a mission and considered that it would be helpful in showing that there are different views among Chagossians on the Agreement on the Chagos Archipelago.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade to Washington DC during which several meetings with key United States (US) officials and representatives from the US business community were held. The Minister met Mr Christopher Landau, US Deputy Secretary of State with whom he raised the issue of strengthening of Mauritius-US trade relations, security in the Indian Ocean region and the Chagos Archipelago. Together with the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade had a meeting with Officials of the African Bureau at the State Department focusing on strengthening the bilateral trade and promoting investments in Mauritius. The Minister highlighted the importance of expediting the ratification of the Agreement on the Chagos Archipelago between Mauritius and the United Kingdom. The Minister discussed with a team from Broadcom on the proposal for the setting up of a National Sovereign Cloud under the Digital Transformation Blueprint 2025-2029 through the VMware Cloud Foundation that will ensure data sovereignty, jurisdictional control and adherence to local legal frameworks. The Minister had a meeting with the President and Chief Executive Officer of Corporate Council of Africa and discussed issues pertaining to the organisation of the 18th US-Africa Business Summit to be held in Mauritius in July 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent mission of the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives to Washington D.C., in the context of Mauritius' ongoing efforts to advance discussions with the United States (US) under the proposed Agreement on Reciprocal Trade and move towards a comprehensive bilateral trade agreement. The Mauritian delegation met with Ambassador Jamieson Greer, United States Trade Representative and a member of President Trump's Cabinet, together with Ambassador Jeffrey Goettman, Deputy United States Trade Representative. The Minister outlined Mauritius' position, namely the need for certainty and predictability in the bilateral trade framework, with the objective of securing, first, an Agreement on Reciprocal Trade and, thereafter, a comprehensive bilateral trade agreement with the US, while preserving benefits equivalent to those under the African Growth and Opportunity Act and maintaining duty-free and quota-free access for Mauritian exports to the US market. The Minister held a number of meetings and engagements with the – (a) US Department of State; (b) US Congress and Congressional Staff; (c) Export-Import Bank of the United States; (d) US Trade and Development Agency; (e) World Bank Group and International Finance Corporation; (f) US International Development Finance Corporation; (g) Agricultural and Trade Support Stakeholders; (h) US Department of Agriculture; (i) US Africa Trade Desk Event; (j) Private Sector Engagements; and (k) Corporate Council on Africa

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the Prime Minister chaired the first meeting of the National Drug Control Commission on 23 April 2026. The National Drug Control Master Plan 2026-2030, elaborated by the National Drug Control Commission (NADC), was presented to the Prime Minister. The Master Plan will be accessible on Government's digital platforms in the coming days. The Prime Minister underlined that the current situation regarding synthetic drugs was central to discussions, and indicated that the NADC has enlisted the support of the Forensic Science Laboratory to strengthen its response. The Prime Minister further noted the need for the NADC to step up awareness of legal sanctions for drug-related offences in high-risk areas, such as the airport and the port.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the activities being organised by the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations from 27 April to 01 May 2026 in the context of the International Labour Day, which is celebrated globally on 01 May each year to commemorate the historic struggles and gains made by workers and the labour movement, with a view to improving the working conditions and promoting decent work. The following activities will be organised - (a) a job fair at Cascavelle on 27 April 2026 to share employment opportunities with job-seekers; (b) meetings with representatives of workers' organisations and employers' organisations on 29 April 2026 to receive feedback on services offered by the Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations and on suggestions for improvement; (c) a one-day symposium on 30 April 2026 with some 200 tripartite stakeholders and social partners on the theme "Inclusive, safe and productive workplaces: building tomorrow's Mauritius together"; (d) a wreath laying ceremony on 01 May 2026 at the Anjalay Coopen statue at the Children's Court, followed by a visit to a dormitory of migrant workers; and (e) a concert on 01 May 2026 at the City Council of Port Louis.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Energy Efficiency (Control of Non- Essential Grid-Powered Activities) Regulations 2026 to impose restrictions on usage of grid power for activities that are not required for safety or security. The Regulations will address non-residential Central Electricity Board customers and will seek to prohibit certain non- essential activities that use grid-power, including - (a) decorative and ornamental lighting; (b) illumination of building façades for aesthetic purposes; (c) operation of illuminated advertising displays; (d) operation of air-conditioning in unoccupied commercial spaces; (e) inefficient operation of air-conditioning; (f) operation of decorative fountains or similar installations; and (g) lighting of outdoor sports facilities when not in use. The Regulations explicitly exempt essential services such as – (a) hospitals, clinics and medical facilities; (b) emergency services including police, fire and disaster response services; (c) public street lighting necessary for public safety; (d) airport, port, air navigation and maritime safety systems; (e) water supply, wastewater treatment and other essential public utilities; (f) power generation sites; and (g) radio and Television Terrestrial Transmission services. The Regulations are expected to reduce unnecessary electricity consumption, and develop a culture of energy efficiency at all levels with time. The Regulations shall come into operation as from 01 May 2026 and be valid up to 01 November 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Energy Efficiency (Minimum Energy Performance Standards for Regulated Machinery) (Refrigerating Appliances) Regulations 2026 for the introduction of mandatory Minimum Energy Performance Standards (MEPS) for refrigerating appliances. The Regulations will be applicable to refrigerating appliances intended for households and light commercial use. Refrigerating appliances being used in the commercial and industrial sector will not be concerned for the time being in the absence of a harmonised MEPS for refrigerating appliances used in commercial and industrial sectors by the Southern African Development Community. The Regulations will not apply to refrigerating appliances that have been placed in a bonded warehouse within five months from the commencement date, or that have been imported or manufactured in accordance with Regulations 20 and 21. The Energy Efficiency (Minimum Energy Performance Standards for Regulated Machinery) (Refrigerating Appliances) Regulations 2026 shall come into operation on 01 October 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Financial Reporting (Mauritius Institute of Professional Accountants) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2026 which will provide for the increase of the annual fee payable by Professional Accountants to the Mauritius Institute of Professional Accountants from Rs2,000 to Rs3,000.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to review the existing legislation for labelling with a view to bringing visibility on the subsidy and maximum mark-up measures implemented by the Government and to review the effective date of the unit pricing requirements to 01 July 2026. Presently, there are three major measures being implemented, namely - (a) the Maximum Fixed Price Mechanism where the prices of 13 essential commodities are fixed; (b) the Maximum Mark-Up Mechanism where the retail prices of 30 commodities are determined on consignment basis; and (c) the Fixed-Quantum Subsidy Mechanism on 10 essential commodities under the Price Stabilisation Fund to cushion households against rising prices. Cabinet noted that in spite of a comprehensive structured framework for price control and subsidy measures in respect of essential commodities, consumers do not necessarily identify products which are subject to Government interventions such as fixed quantum subsidy and maximum mark-up mechanism, as presently there is no visual indication to capture their attention. In order to address this gap, retailers will have to mandatorily affix clear and standardised identifiers such as "SP" for subsidised products and "MRP" for price-controlled products on the price label of relevant commodities. Such a measure will enhance transparency in the retail market, facilitate informed purchasing decisions and improve the visibility and effectiveness of Government interventions. The Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection will amend the existing legislation, accordingly.
ministries: Ministry of Commerce and Consumer Protection

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recommendations made by Consultant COWI-Luxconsult (Mauritius) Ltd on the poor performance of the Contractor Joint Venture SOTRAVIC/STRATA following persistent non-compliance with its core obligations under the Contract for the Vertical Expansion of the Mare Chicose landfill. In the light of the recommendations, the Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change is issuing a letter of warning to the said contractor.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that an Inter-Ministerial Committee on Eid-Ul-Adha has been set up under the chair of the Minister of Housing and Lands and comprising – (a) the Minister of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries; (b) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (c) the Minister of Land Transport (d) the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; and (e) the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the pre-consultative dialogues held in the context of Les Assises de l'Océan 2025. Ten pre-consultative dialogues were held on ten different themes. These ocean dialogues served as a forum for knowledge exchange and a mechanism for consensus building and strategic alignment geared towards the development of the Blue Economy. The dialogues also provided an opportunity to identify priority actions, highlighting synergies among stakeholders and innovative solutions which will set forth the agenda of Les Assises de l'Océan. Following the ocean dialogues, and with the support of the World Bank, a Report was drafted and validated with stakeholders.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the activities being organised to mark World Day for Safety and Health at Work 2026 on the theme "Let's Ensure a Healthy Psychosocial Working Environment". The Ministry of Labour and Industrial Relations will organise a workshop for around 120 participants on 28 April 2026 to raise awareness, share knowledge and build capacity. The workshop will be an opportunity to – (a) raise awareness among participants on psychosocial risks in the workplace and their impact on employee well-being, productivity, and organisational performance; (b) enhance knowledge of preventive strategies, policies, and good practices to manage psychosocial risks effectively; (c) build capacity of Chairpersons of Safety and Health Committees and officers of the Safety and Health Cadre to identify, assess, and address psychosocial hazards within their Ministries and Departments; and (d) promote a culture of prevention and well-being, reinforcing the principles of a healthy psychosocial working environment across the Public Service.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 April 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the constitution of the Board of the STC Petroleum Company Ltd, with Mr Tejanand Dewoo as part-time Chairperson and the reconstitution of - (a) the National Tripartite Council under the chair of the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations and Mrs Manisha Dookhony and Mr Mohammad Hisham Ahmed Oozeer as Vice-Chairpersons; (b) the Boiler and Machinery Inspectors Board, with Mr Gawtum Jawaheer as part- time Chairperson; and (c) the Information and Communication Technologies Advisory Council, with Mr Prabhakar Bhoyroo as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, on the invitation of the Government of the Republic of Seychelles, the Prime Minister will proceed, on Saturday 25 October 2025, to Seychelles to attend the Swearing in Ceremony of HE Dr Patrick Herminie, President-elect of the Republic of Seychelles, to be held on Sunday 26 October 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Criminal Code (Amendment No. 2) Bill into the National Assembly. The United Kingdom and Mauritius have, in May 2025, signed a treaty relating to the Chagos Archipelago in which Mauritius is unequivocally recognised as being sovereign over the Chagos Archipelago. Accordingly, the object of the Bill is to repeal section 76B of the Criminal Code whereby the wrongful acts of misrepresenting the sovereignty of Mauritius by producing, distributing, supply or marketing any coin, stamp, official map or other official object or document have become inexistant.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of the following measures by the Ministry of Agro-Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries with a view to mitigating the risk of introduction of the Foot and Mouth Disease (FMD) in Mauritius from livestock imported from South Africa - (a) 100% Non-Structural Protein ELISA testing for the FMD virus; (b) 10% Solid Phase Competition ELISA testing for the FMD virus; (c) a quarantine period of 14 days in South Africa; and (d) a further quarantine period of 14 days upon arrival in Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the recommendations contained in the Master Plan for the Recreation Zone on Île aux Bénitiers. The objective of the Masterplan is to promote socio-economic development through touristic activities on the islet while at the same time preserving its biodiversity. The Masterplan caters for amenities such as walkways and access routes, off-grid solar power supply, water tanks for rainwater harvesting, waste management facilities, interactive signage, CCTV surveillance and controlled entry through a ticketing and visitor management system. An embarkation point has been identified at La Gaulette for safe boarding and disembarkation of visitors. The recommendations will be implemented in phases, with the collaboration and involvement of concerned Ministries, the Tourism Authority, the District Council of Black River, the Forestry Services, the National Parks and Conservation Service, law enforcement agencies, as well as through Public-Private Partnerships.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the newly constructed sports complex at Camp Motee Road, Triolet will be named as the "Sir Seewoosagur Ramgoolam Sports Complex" and will be inaugurated on Saturday 15 November 2025.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of representations received regarding the decision announced in the Budget Speech 2025/2026 for a new Grant-in-Aid Scheme for pre- primary schools and has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee under the chair of the Minister of Education and Human Resource which will comprise - (a) the Minister of National Infrastructure; (b) the Minister of Social Integration, Social Security and National Solidarity; (c) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (d) the Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare; and (e) the Minister of Tertiary Education, Science and Research.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the setting up of an Inter-Ministerial Committee to look into activities undertaken by hawkers during the festive season under the chair of the Minister of Housing and Lands and which will comprise - (a) the Minister of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change; (b) the Minister of Youth and Sports; (c) the Minister of Labour and Industrial Relations; (d) the Minister of Land Transport; (e) the Minister of Commerce and Consumer Protection; (f) the Minister of Industry, SME and Cooperatives; and (g) the Minister of Local Government.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of a Floating Solar Project at Tamarind Falls Reservoir, by the National Thermal Power Corporation Ltd, India, pursuant to a Government-to-Government arrangement between Mauritius and India. The project involves the development of a 17.5MW Floating Solar Plant on the Tamarind Falls Reservoir and a 12MW Battery Energy Storage System (BESS). The project will utilise approximately 27% of the reservoir's total area and will be connected to Henrietta Substation through an underground 22kV line. The Floating Solar plant is expected to generate approximately 36GWh of electricity annually as per the feasibility study. The BESS will provide firm power during evening peak for four hours at 10MW.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius ratifying the Samoa Partnership Agreement, between the Members of the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States on the one hand and the European Union and its Member States on the other hand; and the inclusion of an interpretative declaration in the instrument of ratification.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the suspension of operations of the light rail vehicles along Line 2 (Rose Hill Central to Mahatma Gandhi, Réduit) on Sundays, as from 02 November 2025, as a cost-saving measure in the long term, in view of the challenging financial position of the Metro Express Limited and the markedly low ridership on Sundays.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that a team of the Maharashtra Cyber Department will be in Mauritius from 30 October 2025 to 04 November 2025. During the visit, workshops and discussions will be centred around the following areas - (a) emerging threats in the field of cybersecurity and cybercrime; (b) appropriate Legal and Policy frameworks for combating cybercrime; (c) strengthening multistakeholder coordination and Public-Private Partnerships; (d) leveraging technology and reinforcing institutional capacity; and (e) effective awareness campaign and community engagement.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that, following the intervention of the Prime Minister at the 45th SADC Ordinary Summit of Heads of State and Government in August 2025, an Extraordinary Meeting of the SADC Finance Sub-Committee was chaired by Mauritius virtually on 23 October 2025 to discuss amongst others, the outcome of the recomputation exercise of Member States' assessed additional contribution towards the SADC Standby Force mission to the Democratic Republic of Congo. Following this exercise, the contribution for Mauritius was brought down from USD15,463,954 to USD7,663,993. An amount of USD596,596 will, therefore, be remitted to Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the recent participation of the Minister of Gender Equality and Family Welfare in the Global Summit of Women held in Beijing. The Summit, which commemorated the 30th Anniversary of the Fourth World Conference on Women, provided a high-level platform to review progress achieved under the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and to strengthen global commitment to gender equality and women's empowerment. Held under the theme "Carrying Forward the Spirit of the Beijing World Conference on Women and Promoting the New and Accelerated Process for Women's All-Round Development", the Summit reaffirmed that gender equality and women's development are shared responsibilities of the international community and key drivers of sustainable development. In her address at the Plenary Session, the Minister reaffirmed Mauritius' commitment to the Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action and to Sustainable Development Goal 5 on achieving gender equality and empowering all women and girls. She outlined progress made in gender parity in education, women's leadership, gender-responsive budgeting, implementation of the National Gender Policy 2022- 2030, as well as initiatives supporting women entrepreneurs through training, digital literacy, and financial inclusion. She also highlighted national efforts to integrate gender perspectives in climate action and to address challenges such as the gender wage gap, underrepresentation of women in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) and decision-making positions.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 24 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the outcome of the recent participation of the Junior Minister of Foreign Affairs, Regional Integration and International Trade in the 24th Summit of Heads of State and Government of the Common Market for the Eastern and Southern Africa held in Nairobi, Republic of Kenya. The theme of the Summit was "Leveraging digitalisation to deepen Regional Value Chains for Sustainable and Inclusive Growth". On the sidelines of the Summit, the Junior Minister held bilateral talks with various dignitaries, including the Minister of the Economy, Industry, Investments, Responsible for Economic Integration of Comoros; the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Tunisia; the Principal Secretary, Ministry of Foreign and Diaspora Affairs of Kenya; and the Manager Policy and Research, Shelter Afrique Development Bank, based in Nairobi.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the status of discussions between Mauritius and United Kingdom (UK) on the implementation of the Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Mauritius and the Government of the United Kingdom of the Great Britain and Northern Ireland concerning the Chagos Archipelago including Diego Garcia. Cabinet noted that the debates on the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill in the UK, which were to resume in the House of Lords on Monday 26 January 2026, have been postponed. Cabinet has also taken note of the statement made by the President of the United States of America on the Chagos issue and the subsequent stand taken by the UK Prime Minister on the matter, including the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory Bill. The Government of Mauritius is following up on the matter closely. Cabinet has further taken note that a high-level delegation from the United States of America (USA) is proposing to visit Mauritius on 24 and 25 February 2026 for talks on a proposed USA-Mauritius Security Partnership Agreement and enhanced cooperation in the Western Indian Ocean.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the recommendation of the standing High-Powered Committee chaired by the Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service, for the payment of a Sunday allowance to officers working on shift/roster on Sundays to be restored at a reviewed rate equivalent to one day pay in addition to the normal pay derived by that category of officer. Cabinet has, in reaching the above decision, taken note of the observations made by the High-Powered Committee, with regard to the issue of payment of allowance to officers on shift/roster working on Sundays, as follows - (a) according to the Pay Research Bureau (PRB), Sunday has always been considered as a normal working day for public officers working in grades classified as shift/roster. This recommendation of the PRB is supported by sections 30(1) and 30(2) of the Workers' Rights Act, which provides for a distinction between Sundays and Public Holidays for the purpose of remuneration; (b) the PRB has always, while determining the salary of grades classified as shift/roster, granted a higher salary scale as compared to those working normal hours to compensate for the fact that those workers on shift/roster may be required to work on Sundays. This has been reflected in the PRB Report 2026; (c) those shift workers who are required to perform night shift are also eligible for additional payment in terms of Night Duty Allowance; and (d) the decision taken by the former Government in 2024 on the basis of which public officers working in grades classified as shift/roster have been benefitting from one day normal salary plus two days' additional salary with effect from January 2024, lacked a legal basis for implementation. Cabinet has further taken note that 40,577 officers of 337 grades are concerned by this measure, and the financial implication for the restoration of the reviewed Sunday allowance is estimated at around Rs1 billion per annum. These officers on shift/roster working on a public holiday will continue to be paid at a rate equivalent to one day normal salary plus two days' additional salary.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the proposed composition of the Constitutional Review Commission chaired by former Chief Justice Y. K. J. (Bernard) Yeung Sik Yuen, GOSK and the following members - (a) Mrs Yanilla Moonshiram, SC; (b) Mr Satyajit Boolell, SC; (c) Dr Amar Roopanand Mahadew; (d) Dr Jonathan Ravat, OSK; (e) Dr Milan J.N. Meetarbhan, GOSK; (f) Dr Amenah Jahangeer Chojoo; (g) Mr Joël Edouard; (h) Mrs Anne-Sophie Jullienne; (i) Mrs Danisha Sornum; and (j) Mr Sabir Kadel (Secretary of the Constitutional Review Commission). A Bill is currently being drafted by the Attorney-General's Office to give a legal framework to the Commission and set out its mandate, powers, functions, among others, and will be circulated to Cabinet upon finalisation for introduction into the National Assembly. Cabinet has further taken note that the mandate of the Constitutional Review Commission will not include electoral reforms, which are being addressed separately.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the modalities for the introduction of Kreol Morisien as a main subject at Higher School Certificate (HSC) level as from academic year 2026. The eligibility criterion to study Kreol Morisien as a main subject at HSC level pertains to students having been awarded a Cambridge School Certificate/ Cambridge General Certificate of Education. For the Cambridge School Certificate, the students must have at least a pass in English and a minimum of three credits at one and the same sitting, including a credit in Kreol Morisien at National School Certificate (NSC) level. For the Cambridge General Certificate of Education, the students must have passed in at least five subjects with a minimum of grade C (credit) in three subjects, including a credit in Kreol Morisien at NSC level and at least grade E (pass) in English at one and the same sitting.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that following the outcome of six cases entered by employees of local authorities before the Supreme Court to challenge the decision taken in May 2025 to terminate their appointment, whereby their applications for judicial review were dismissed, the Local Government Service Commission has directed all local authorities to terminate the employment of all the 1,463 workers concerned. A fresh recruitment process has already been initiated by the Local Government Service Commission and pending the finalisation thereof, local authorities are making alternative arrangements to ensure continuity of services. The 1,463 workers whose employment has been terminated will be entitled to apply for vacant posts.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that sections 3, 4, 8 to 10 and 13 to 25 of the Road Traffic (Amendment) Act 2025 have been proclaimed. The Penalty Points System will, therefore, become operational as from Saturday 31 January 2026. Cabinet has further taken note that the Penalty Points System alongside the Super App KOREK is being launched at a Joint Press Conference by the Minister of Land Transport and the Minister of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation, today 30 January 2026. Drivers will be able to access their Penalty Points Certificates as well as their details on the Super App KOREK.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Income Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2026. Amendments were brought to the Income Tax Act to extend the partial exemption regime of 80% to a company holding a - (a) Payment Intermediary Services licence; (b) Robotic and Artificial Intelligence Enabled Advisory Services licence; or (c) Virtual Asset Service Provider licence. In order to benefit from the incentive regime, a company is required to meet conditions relating to substance in Mauritius. One of the conditions is that it should carry out its core income generating activity in Mauritius. The Income Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2026 accordingly specify the core income generating activities for each licence.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the National Pensions (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2026 amending the Second Schedule of the National Pensions Act with a view to giving effect to the increase of 3.7% in the rates of the Basic Retirement Pension, Invalid's Basic Pension, Widow's Basic Pension and Orphan's Pension, with effect from 01 January 2026. The increase in basic pensions concerns around 324,033 beneficiaries and payment has already been effected for the month of January 2026.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to Mauritius - (a) participating in the multinational nomination of "Chaath Mahaparv", led by the Republic of India, for inscription on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity; and (b) submitting the Nomination Dossier, which has been prepared by the National Heritage Fund, in consultation with the concerned communities and practitioners in Mauritius. "Chaath Mahaparv" is a traditional Hindu festival originating in the eastern regions of India, particularly in the States of Bihar, Jharkhand and eastern Uttar Pradesh. It is observed annually during the months of October and November and is dedicated to the worship of the Sun God. Over time, the practice has spread beyond India and is now observed by communities in several countries, including Mauritius, Fiji and Suriname, where it continues to be celebrated as part of the living cultural traditions of the Indian diaspora.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note that the UN SIDS Partnership Award in the Economic Category has been conferred to the SIDS Pooled Procurement Program during an award ceremony held in December 2025 at the United Nations in New York. The SIDS Pooled Procurement Program is a collaborative initiative led by the World Health Organization (WHO) Regional Office for Africa regrouping SIDS of the African Region, and Madagascar, having as objective to procure quality-assured medical supplies at most economical prices, through economies of scale and collective bargaining. Mauritius was selected as the host country for the SIDS Pooled Procurement Secretariat at the 8th Meeting of the SIDS Ministers of Health of the African Region.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Financing Agreement between the Government of Mauritius and the European Union to support Anti-Money Laundering and Combatting the Financing of Terrorism and Proliferation Financing (AML/CFT/CPF) activities in Mauritius. The European Union will provide financial assistance of EUR500,000 to ensure the effectiveness of the AML/CFT/CPF framework. Technical assistance, through Expertise France, will be provided over a period of 48 months, particularly to assist Mauritius in carrying out the following activities - (a) development of a comprehensive national strategy to combat the financing of terrorism; (b) strengthening risk-based supervision of financial institutions, designated non-financial businesses and professions, and virtual asset service providers; (c) enhancing the compilation of national AML/CFT/CPF statistics; and (d) reinforcing public-private sector cooperation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to a strategic collaboration with the Government of the United Kingdom under its Growth Gateway Programme to provide technical assistance for Vision 2050 for Mauritius. The UK support under the Growth Gateway Programme is within the UK–Mauritius Strategic Partnership Framework, which provides the overarching structure for bilateral cooperation. As part of this initiative, the UK will provide technical expertise, inter alia, for the following - (a) strengthening economic planning and coordination functions; (b) supporting evidence-based policymaking and long-term planning; (c) enhancing institutional capacity to deliver inclusive and sustainable growth; (d) improving coherence across financial services, economic planning and development policy; (e) supporting reforms that promote resilience, opportunity and shared prosperity; (f) co-designing, facilitation, analysis and insights report from over 35 regional and sectoral consultations to be conducted between January and April 2026; (g) conducting baseline economic diagnostic and assist in drafting of Vision 2050 ambitions; and (h) prioritising engines of growth.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the implementation of a project, undertaken in line with provisions of the Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer. The project relates to energy efficiency by aiming to further reduce the use of refrigerants with high ozone depleting potential in the air conditioning sector. The project involves the development and adoption of Minimum Energy Performance Standards (MEPS), for air conditioning equipment of capacity beyond 12kW. The activities include - (a) provision of guidance in developing the MEPS regulations; (b) conceptualising a draft monitoring and reporting system and enforcement plan; and (c) developing green procurement guidelines. The Multilateral Fund will provide funding to the tune of USD135,600 and the German implementing agency, Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) will technically support implementation.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the Climate Change Reporting Guidelines for Mauritius and the Report on the structure for monitoring, compliance and supervision; and to the implementation thereof. The Climate Change Reporting Guidelines align the climate reporting framework for Mauritius with the International Financial Reporting Standards. The Guidelines will ensure a structured approach to transparent and consistent disclosures, including progress towards any climate-related targets which an organisation has set, and any targets it is required to meet by law. To ensure an effective implementation of the standards, the Climate Reporting Framework will adopt a phased approach targeting organisations based on their size and listing status, which comprise large listed companies, large non-listed companies, listed SMEs and non-listed SMEs. The Report on the structure for monitoring, compliance and supervision, makes use of the current institutional mechanism, namely through the creation of a Climate Reporting Sub-Committee under the Climate Change Committee. The Climate Reporting Sub-Committee will comprise representatives from the public and private sectors, regulators, technical experts, and the civil society to ensure compliance with the Climate Change Reporting Guidelines. The Climate Reporting Sub-Committee will work closely with the Financial Reporting Council to ensure coherence with the broader requirements of sustainability reporting as per its mandate.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of an Agreement "Amending the Charter establishing the Centre for Coordination of Agriculture Research and Development for Southern Africa (CCARDESA)", which is a subsidiary of the Southern African Development Community (SADC). Its goal is to contribute to the sustainable reduction in food insecurity and poverty in the SADC Region. At the Joint meeting of SADC Ministers responsible for Agriculture and Food Security, and Fisheries and Aquaculture, held in May 2024, Member States approved the amendments to the Charter establishing the CCARDESA.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Open University of Mauritius and the African Regional Labour Administration Centre to foster working relationships geared towards conducting capacity building and talent development activities for the upgrading of skills through the development and dissemination of up-to-date, relevant, flexible and high-quality academic/training programmes. The objective of the Memorandum of Understanding is to facilitate collaboration in the following areas - (a) collaborative supervision of dissertations and projects; (b) creation and/or sharing of academic information, materials and resources; (c) publication of academic research in areas of mutual interest; (d) delivery of training and capacity development on agreed areas of collaboration; (e) promotion of collaboration in fields of mutual interest; and (f) promotion of other academic cooperation as mutually agreed.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the submission of Mauritius' First Country-Level Report and Data Matrix on the implementation of the Second Ten-Year Implementation Plan of the African Union Agenda 2063 (2024-2033) to the African Union Secretariat.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the proposed appointment of Mr Georget Mandehatsara as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of the Republic of Madagascar to the Republic of Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday 30 January 2026.pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the reconstitution of the - (a) Mental Health Board with Dr Mridula Naga, as part-time Chairperson; (b) Board of Directors of the Financial Services Institute Co. Ltd with Dr Vimal Thakoor, as part-time Chairperson; and (c) Board of the National Property Fund Ltd, with Mr Dhanraj Conhye, Permanent Secretary, as part-time Chairperson.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Electronic Transactions (Amendment) Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to amend the Electronic Transactions Act to modernise and expand the legal framework governing electronic transactions in Mauritius. The Bill updates the provisions relating to electronic signatures by adopting a reliability-based and technology-neutral standard, recognises the validity of contracts formed or performed through automated message systems and introduces a framework for the legal recognition and use of electronic transferable records. The provisions relating to electronic transferable records are based on internationally recognised principles of functional equivalence and reliability and are intended to facilitate the secure dematerialisation of transferable documents and instruments traditionally issued in paper form. These amendments aim to enhance legal certainty, facilitate paperless trade and cross-border electronic transactions and promote business facilitation in the digital economy.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Environment (Standards for Ambient Air Quality and Stack Emission) Regulations 2026 which provide for the following three environmental air standards – (a) Environmental Standards for Ambient Air Quality These standards will be used as benchmark on the maximum allowable limits of pollutants in the ambient air quality to safeguard public health and the environment; (b) Environmental Standards for Stack Emissions New parameters such as dioxins and furans, heavy metals, volatile organic compounds and mercury will be regulated in addition to the existing parameters. The Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change will be empowered to request a factory to take any air pollution abatement measures deemed appropriate, where the limit of any pollutant is being exceeded; and (c) Environmental Standards for Incinerator Stack Emissions New provision is being made to regulate emissions from incinerators burning solid wastes, medical wastes and dead body (at crematoria). Incinerators will be required to operate at an optimum temperature of 1,100 degrees Celsius to prevent the release of dioxins and furans which are extremely hazardous for public health and which can cause reproductive and developmental problems, damage the immune system, interfere with hormones and also cause cancer. The Regulations make provision for a moratorium to be extended to any public department to be in compliance with the environmental standards, where it is in the national interest to do so. The Regulations also make provision to grant industries a six- month's compliance period to meet the new air standards.
ministries: Ministry of Environment, Solid Waste Management and Climate Change

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the implementation of the National Strategy Plan (NSP)/Roadmap for a Plastic Pollution-Free Mauritius. The NSP defines a ten-year Roadmap to shift from a linear to a circular economy model and propels Mauritius towards a plastic pollution-free country. It outlines national priorities, measurable targets and concrete actions rooted in international best practices and adapted to the country's socio-economic realities. The plan encompasses policies and strategies as well as 60 short, medium and long term activities revolving around 11 targets falling under six thematic areas, namely Knowledge, Refuse, Reduce, Reuse/Repurpose, Recycle and End-of-Life Management and four Cross-Cutting Actions. The NSP includes a five-year costed Action Plan for implementation. The main recommendations of the NSP are to – (a) develop a robust inventory database of plastic and plastic waste; (b) introduce a new Plastic Governance and Control Act to strengthen governance and enforcement mechanism; (c) undertake phase-wise reduction of non-essential and problematic single use plastics; (d) achieve at least 80% phase-wise collection of plastic waste by 2035; (e) set phase-wise reduction of plastic packaging consumption by 2035; (f) reduce importation of virgin plastic pellets; (g) formalise producer accountability through Extended Producer Responsibility Schemes by 2035; (h) adopt a phased approach towards building plastic waste recycling capacity; (i) manage end-of-life non-recyclables and contaminated plastic wastes in an environmentally sound manner; (j) encourage research and development; and (k) conduct education and awareness campaigns to promote behavioural change across all segments of society to curb down plastic pollution.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Consumer Protection (Control of Imports) (Amendment of Schedule) Regulations 2026 to prohibit the importation of any toy or device which can be operated with explosive caps containing a mixture of potassium chlorate and red phosphorus due to the potential risks associated with their use.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the promulgation of the Competition Commission (Trade Association and Cooperatives Amnesty Programme) Regulations 2026, to allow trade associations and cooperatives to voluntarily disclose anti-competitive practices in return for immunity from penalties. The Trade Associations and Cooperatives Amnesty Programme will be implemented as a one-off and time-bound measure, initially for a period of six months and will be open to trade associations, professional bodies and cooperatives operating in Mauritius. Under the programme, eligible entities will be granted full immunity from financial penalties where they voluntarily disclose their involvement in specified anti-competitive conduct. Access to the programme will be subject to strict procedural and substantive conditions through undertakings. The programme is expected to improve the detection of anti-competitive practices, reduce enforcement costs, and increase compliance with competition law across sectors. It will also contribute to more competitive markets, better pricing outcomes for consumers, and stronger governance within trade associations and cooperatives.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of representations made by taxi operators regarding the recent coming into operation of the Ala-lila platform powered by Uber. The Minister of National Infrastructure, the Minister of Health and Wellness and the Minister of Land Transport will examine the representations received and hold consultative meetings with taxi operators and other stakeholders.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the University of Mauritius and the Centennial College of Applied Arts and Technology, Toronto, Canada. The Memorandum of Understanding establishes a formal framework for academic co-operation across strategically significant areas, including – (a) academic collaboration in research, teaching and learning; (b) exchange of educational resource materials; (c) the assessment of units for credit for prior learning; (d) student and staff mobility programmes; and (e) design and delivery of special short-term academic programmes and projects of mutual benefit.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the University of Technology, Mauritius and Bursa Uludağ University, Türkiye, which provides for the following avenues of collaboration – (a) student and staff exchanges; (b) faculty exchange on equal and reciprocal terms, with visiting academics serving for an academic semester or shorter period; (c) the exchange of teaching tools, textbooks, and monographs; (d) organisation of lectures, joint meetings, seminars, and webinars to develop the pedagogical competencies of faculty members; and (e the applied academic training of students on the basis of mutual exchange.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between the Higher Education Commission and the Zimbabwe Council for Higher Education, which provides for the following avenues of collaboration - (a) the assessment and accreditation of Higher Education Institutions, their internal systems of quality and courses and/or programmes assurance; (b) the enhancement of understanding of each institution's approach to ensuring quality in higher education and the respective processes in place; (c) the exchange of information, including capacity building; and (d) sharing of best practices in the execution of the functions of the Parties that will help them become more efficient and effective towards achieving their objects and potentially, mutual recognition of qualifications and standards.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the salient features and key findings of the 2024 Report on "Statistics in Mauritius - A Gender Approach", prepared by the Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare in collaboration with Statistics Mauritius. The Report is compiled on annual basis and provides a comprehensive statistical overview of gender-related indicators in the Republic of Mauritius. The Report is based on the most recent sex-disaggregated data from administrative records, household surveys, and national censuses. It provides insights into key areas, namely population, health, education, employment, decision-making, poverty and social welfare, and gender-based violence.
ministries: Ministry of Gender Equality and Family Welfare

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Friday17 April 2026 .pdf
text: Cabinet has taken note of the organisation of the International Indentured Labour Route Project Academic Conference and the Indian Ocean Workshop on Indenture by the Aapravasi Ghat Trust Fund, scheduled from 13 to 15 May 2026 and 27 to 29 May 2026 respectively, under the Indentured Labour Route Project. The International Conference will bring together scholars, researchers and members of the International Scientific Committee from countries including Australia, India, Fiji, Reunion Island, Trinidad and Tobago and Suriname. The Conference will provide a platform for dissemination of current research, interdisciplinary exchanges and the strengthening of international academic collaboration, and is expected to yield key outputs including recommendations, an updated three-year action plan and a strategic research roadmap for the Indentured Labour Route Project. The Indian Ocean Workshop on Indenture will on its part assemble representatives and experts form eight countries, namely India, Tanzania, South Africa, Kenya, Reunion Island, Malaysia, Mozambique and Seychelles. 12 Cabinet has taken note of the appointment of Mr Mohammad Djouneid Fatemamode as new Chairperson of the Mauritius Sports Council, following the resignation of Mr Iqbal Calcateea.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting Monday 06 October 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Senior Counsel and Senior Attorney Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill is to provide for the setting up of a Recommendation Panel which shall be responsible to make recommendations to the President of the Republic for the appointment of a barrister as Senior Counsel for the Republic of Mauritius and an attorney as Senior Attorney for the Republic of Mauritius.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting on Friday 16 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Revenue Tribunal Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill would be to provide for the establishment of the Revenue Tribunal which would take over the functions and powers of the Assessment Review Committee under the Mauritius Revenue Authority Act. Accordingly, any person aggrieved by a revenue determination of the Director-General of the Mauritius Revenue Authority or of the Registrar-General would now appeal to the Revenue Tribunal instead of the Assessment Review Committee. The Revenue Tribunal would also conduct its proceedings in a more simplified manner and would, inter alia, have the power to - (a) make such order requiring the attendance of a person or the production of any record, document or exhibit as it thinks necessary or expedient for the disposal of an appeal; and (b) take evidence on oath and, for that purpose, administer an oath. The Bill would impose a deadline of 120 days on the Revenue Tribunal to fix an appeal for preliminary hearing after the date the appeal was lodged and, thereafter, the Revenue Tribunal would have to give its determination not later than 90 days after the close of the hearing of the appeal. In addition, a person aggrieved by the final decision of the Revenue Tribunal would now be eligible to appeal to the Supreme Court against that decision, including any evidence assessed by the Revenue Tribunal, unlike an appeal from the decision of the Assessment Review Committee to the Supreme Court which was only limited to such decision as being erroneous in law.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting on Friday 16 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to the introduction of the Legal Aid and Legal Assistance Bill into the National Assembly. The object of the Bill would be to repeal the existing Legal Aid and Legal Assistance Act and re-enact a modern legislation with a view to making it more accessible for persons with low income and who could not afford legal representation to have access to justice in civil and criminal proceedings before any Court. The Bill would, inter alia – (a) provide for an increase in the ceiling of the monthly earnings and the assets, of a person seeking to obtain legal aid or legal assistance; (b) widen the offences for which legal aid and legal assistance might be obtained; and (c) impose an obligation on – (i) the Court to inform a person, in a language that he was conversant with, of the possibility to obtain legal aid; and (ii) a police officer who would intend to record the statement of a person under warning to inform the person, in case the person was not represented by a barrister, that he might make an application for legal assistance for legal advice and counselling during the recording of the statement.

filename: Highlights of Cabinet Meeting on Friday 16 May 2025.pdf
text: Cabinet has agreed to drafting instructions being conveyed to the Attorney-General's Office for the finalisation of the Organic Agriculture Bill. The draft Bill would aim at promoting the production of quality organic products, set the minimum requirements for and regulate the production, handling, processing and labelling of organic products. The key provisions of the Bill would include – (a) the establishment of a National Organic Office within the Ministry of Agro- Industry, Food Security, Blue Economy and Fisheries to, inter alia, register accredited certification bodies, assist in the development of a domestic and export market for organic products, and diminish the reliance of organic producers on chemical fertilisers, pesticides and other inputs; (b) the constitution of a National Organic Advisory Committee to advise on policy formulation regarding organic farming, the protection and promotion of organic products, and trade, and the minimum requirements for the production, handling, processing and labelling of organic products amongst others; (c) the principles of organic production, farming, processing, and storage to be complied with by operators engaged in organic crop and livestock production, organic beekeeping as well as in organic food and feed processing; (d) the modalities for the registration and accreditation of 'certification service providers', that is, those bodies which would be responsible for verifying that organic products were produced, processed, prepared and handled in accordance with provisions of the Bill; (e) prohibitions on the use of Genetically Modified Products, ionising radiation and sewage sludge in organic production, and the parameters for the storage, transportation, import and export of organic products; (f) precautionary measures for maintaining product integrity during storage, transportation, import and export.
